job_title: Sales & Marketing Hub Setup for Property Managers
job_description:
I am looking for an experienced HubSpot Expert to collaborate with me in building a curated HubSpot template setup designed specifically for property management companies. This role will involve configuring Sales Hub Professional and Marketing Hub Professional to create an optimised, repeatable framework that will streamline onboarding and CRM adoption for multiple customers.

Our goal is to develop a scalable HubSpot implementation that property managers with small teams can adopt seamlessly, leveraging Supered.io for in-app training and process standardisation. This project is part of our Grand Slam Offer, ensuring that every customer gets a structured, high-value HubSpot setup tailored to their business needs.

Who We Are & Our Ideal Customer Profile (ICP):

We only work with student housing operators, build-to-rent (BTR) landlords, and property management firms. Our clients often struggle with CRM adoption, fragmented sales pipelines, and inefficient lead management. This HubSpot setup will serve as a plug-and-play framework, allowing them to quickly get up and running with minimal friction, support or delay.

This isn’t just about configuring HubSpot. It’s about building a framework that makes our customers say: 
“Wow, this is exactly what we needed.” 

Key Responsibilities:

1. Build a Repeatable HubSpot Template Setup for Property Managers
  • Structure custom properties, contact lifecycles, and deal pipelines specific to property management.
  • Define pre-set dashboards and reports to track lead-to-lease conversion and revenue attribution.
  • Optimise user roles and permissions to align with property management workflows.

2. Implement Lead Capture, Sales Automation & Pipeline Optimisation
  • Configure lead forms, chatbots, and automation workflows to qualify inbound leads.
  • Build lead scoring models to prioritise high-intent prospects.
  • Automate deal stage transitions and task assignments for sales teams.
  • Develop pre-built sequences and playbooks for outbound prospecting and follow-ups.

3. Develop a Scalable Customer Onboarding & Training Process Using Supered.io
  • Set up in-app training guides within Supered.io to onboard users seamlessly.
  • Create a guided HubSpot walkthrough covering CRM navigation, sales workflows, and reporting.
  • Design SOPs and automated workflows for onboarding customers 3, 4, 5, etc., ensuring rapid deployment.

4. Reporting & Performance Monitoring
  • Implement revenue attribution reports to track marketing ROI.
  • Build custom dashboards for property managers to monitor sales, occupancy, and lead conversion.
  • Set up tracking for source attribution (e.g., PPC, organic, referrals, direct inquiries).

Ideal Candidate:
We’re not just looking for a HubSpot expert. We’re looking for someone who sees problems as opportunities, complexity as a challenge, and software as a tool for empowering people, not just processing data. 
 
You believe that systems should work for people, not the other way around. When you see inefficiencies, you don’t just fix them—you ask why they exist in the first place. You challenge assumptions, explore possibilities, and think beyond what HubSpot can do to what it should do for our customers. 
 
You’re comfortable in the unknown, figuring things out as you go, testing, iterating, and improving. You don’t wait for instructions—you create clarity. You ask the hard questions, challenge ideas (including your own), and focus on outcomes, not just outputs. 
 
Most importantly, you believe that technology is only as good as the people using it. That’s why you’ll work with us, not just for us. You’ll share ideas, debate the best solutions, and co-create something scalable, intuitive, and genuinely valuable. 

Project Scope & Timeline:
  • 6-12 week contract, with potential for ongoing support.
  • Fully remote role, flexible working hours.
  • Looking to onboard within the next 1-2 weeks.
  • GMT +5/-5 timezone range

If you have HubSpot expertise, a background in property management CRM setups, and experience with scalable customer onboarding, I would love to hear from you!
Desired Time Frame: 6 to 12 weeks
proposal_description:
Jean Christofferson
San Francisco Bay Area


Sales & Marketing Hub Setup for Property Managers


Why Jean Christofferson


Prior to HubSpot my background is as a start up founder and software developer in Silicon Valley.   It sounds like you are building a startup to offer a solution addressing  a known problem in the property management space.  I have seen the state of a number of these platforms and they are terrible for everyone involved..  

Building systems is what I do and working in HubSpot is a joy.  Prior to HubSpot I was partner for another CRM (Infusionsoft)  and customized the CRM for a large Real Estate Broker.  While it is not exactly the same as the property management, my background with that project is applicable.

SPECIFICATIONS

I am looking for an experienced HubSpot Expert to collaborate with me in building a curated HubSpot template setup designed specifically for property management companies. This role will involve configuring Sales Hub Professional and Marketing Hub Professional to create an optimised, repeatable framework that will streamline onboarding and CRM adoption for multiple customers.

Our goal is to develop a scalable HubSpot implementation that property managers with small teams can adopt seamlessly, leveraging Supered.io for in-app training and process standardisation. This project is part of our Grand Slam Offer, ensuring that every customer gets a structured, high-value HubSpot setup tailored to their business needs.

I have extensive experience with both Sales and Marketing Hubs working in both Enterprise and Pro environments.  For a project with one of my clients we rolled out training to 20 sales locations with over 100 sales representatives. Most importantly this included HubSpot usage metrics to measure adoption and enable correction as needed,.


Who We Are & Our Ideal Customer Profile (ICP):

We only work with student housing operators, build-to-rent (BTR) landlords, and property management firms. Our clients often struggle with CRM adoption, fragmented sales pipelines, and inefficient lead management. This HubSpot setup will serve as a plug-and-play framework, allowing them to quickly get up and running with minimal friction, support or delay.

This isn’t just about configuring HubSpot. It’s about building a framework that makes our customers say: 
“Wow, this is exactly what we needed.” 

Key Responsibilities:

1. Build a Repeatable HubSpot Template Setup for Property Managers
  • Structure custom properties, contact lifecycles, and deal pipelines specific to property management.
  • Define pre-set dashboards and reports to track lead-to-lease conversion and revenue attribution.
  • Optimise user roles and permissions to align with property management workflows.


In building a system like this my approach is to always start with more than one profile type to ensure the system has flexibility.  While it is easy to add or customize properties throughout the process is it important not to box yourself in at the start by building for one profile instead of several.  In this case I would think that this would mean different rental types, payment structures, lease terms, tenant requirements, and approval processes.


2. Implement Lead Capture, Sales Automation & Pipeline Optimisation
  • Configure lead forms, chatbots, and automation workflows to qualify inbound leads.
  • Build lead scoring models to prioritise high-intent prospects.
  • Automate deal stage transitions and task assignments for sales teams.
  • Develop pre-built sequences and playbooks for outbound prospecting and follow-ups.


Lead forms are easily implemented with lead scoring and workflow qualifications. I am assuming that different property managers will have different requirements for qualifying leads?  That needs to be baked into the development process so that the requirements are easily customized, not hard coded into the workflow.

Automating deal stage transitions and task assignments is something that I have extensive experience with.  

My philosophy of task creation from a workflow is to structure the task title and description with as much actionable data as possible.

One issue with tasks can be getting HubSpot users to mark them as complete.  Many times they will do the action but not mark the task complete.  Workflows can’t complete tasks (although I need to check it as HubSpot implements changes so fast perhaps that has been implemented - I along with others requested it.)  For this reason in other built outs we have used tickets.  That does require Service Hub.  We did not have the sales reps actually use Service Hub - we sent email notifications and then measured their actions and automatically moved the ticket stages based on  results.  Something to think about if task completion is an issue. although here are other ways to ensure reps complete tasks.


3. Develop a Scalable Customer Onboarding & Training Process Using Supered.io
  • Set up in-app training guides within Supered.io to onboard users seamlessly.
  • Create a guided HubSpot walkthrough covering CRM navigation, sales workflows, and reporting.
  • Design SOPs and automated workflows for onboarding customers 3, 4, 5, etc., ensuring rapid deployment.


Full disclosure -  I have not worked with Suerped.io before but it looks great.  I would welcome the opportunity to implement a guided HubSpot tour with Supered.io.

SOPs are a standard part of my deliverables.  

Workflows to onboard customers are also standard.


4. Reporting & Performance Monitoring
  • Implement revenue attribution reports to track marketing ROI.
  • Build custom dashboards for property managers to monitor sales, occupancy, and lead conversion.
  • Set up tracking for source attribution (e.g., PPC, organic, referrals, direct inquiries).


I love custom reports and dashboards!  In an existing HubSpot instance this also usually involves a data integrity component as with bad data you get bad reports.  But building a new system you can build in data integrity guard rails from the start.  So for example if you get spam submissions for rental applications they need to be automatically marked and deleted from the system so they don’t skew the reporting.

Tracking for source attribution is standard but there are multiple issues depending on the source.  If you want to measure conversion from Google or Meta ads they do not share all of the data that I want.  If the ad creates a contact we can easily measure that.  What is trickier to measure is the action that creates the conversion.  

We can build reports that offer good actionable data but there is some fuzziness as to what drives the conversion, such as a successful rental application.  For example for the student rental market you may have more than one ‘buyer’ - the student and the parent. 

Ideal Candidate:
We’re not just looking for a HubSpot expert. We’re looking for someone who sees problems as opportunities, complexity as a challenge, and software as a tool for empowering people, not just processing data. 
 
You believe that systems should work for people, not the other way around. When you see inefficiencies, you don’t just fix them—you ask why they exist in the first place. You challenge assumptions, explore possibilities, and think beyond what HubSpot can do to what it should do for our customers. 
 
You’re comfortable in the unknown, figuring things out as you go, testing, iterating, and improving. You don’t wait for instructions—you create clarity. You ask the hard questions, challenge ideas (including your own), and focus on outcomes, not just outputs. 
 
Most importantly, you believe that technology is only as good as the people using it. That’s why you’ll work with us, not just for us. You’ll share ideas, debate the best solutions, and co-create something scalable, intuitive, and genuinely valuable. 

I love building successful systems.  For all of my clients I am a team member and dive deep to understand their business. I do not just work 9-5 (when was that ever a thing?) I run on a software developer pace and schedule. 

Project Scope & Timeline:
  • 6-12 week contract, with potential for ongoing support.
  • Fully remote role, flexible working hours.
  • Looking to onboard within the next 1-2 weeks.
  • GMT +5/-5 timezone range

If you have HubSpot expertise, a background in property management CRM setups, and experience with scalable customer onboarding, I would love to hear from you!


I have HubSpot expertise, software/system development and Real Estate Brokerage expertise.

I just wrapped up a major client and am ready to dive in.  This is my first proposal in Profoundly.  Normally I would schedule a discovery call before doing any quotes.  The only way I can quote this with a monthly retainer of 18,000 per month.  Given what I currently understand about the requirements three months is realistic. 

Given more information I may be able to refine this quote.  I would welcome a discovery call.

job_title: HubSpot Marketing Hub Implementation
job_description:
HubSpot Implementation & Pardot Migration – Project Brief


Client Overview:

Client currently uses Salesforce Sales Cloud + Pardot (Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Advanced) for marketing automation. They plan to migrate to HubSpot Marketing Hub (Enterprise/Pro) while maintaining Salesforce as the CRM system with two-way sync.


  1. Salesforce & Pardot Environment



  • Pardot Edition: MCAE Advanced
  • Salesforce objects in scope: Leads, Contacts, Accounts, Opportunities
  • Current sync: Pardot → Salesforce (~20-min delay), want real-time sync in HubSpot
  • No major custom integrations beyond standard marketing sync



  1. Marketing Assets Inventory



  • Email Templates: ~5–10 active templates
  • Forms: ~5 standard reusable forms used across ~30–35 website forms
  • Landing Pages: None in Pardot today; hosted on WordPress; want ability to build/manage landing pages in HubSpot
  • Automation Programs: ~9 nurture/drip workflows and ~44 automation rules (webinar follow-up, lead nurture, A/B testing)
  • Lists: Hundreds of static lists (retain 5 active), a few dynamic lists to be migrated
  • Content Assets: ~2,500 total (PDFs, images, gated resources); migration scope to be confirmed



  1. Data Scope & Structure



  • Contacts: ~70K active in Pardot; total HubSpot volume ~100K (including Salesforce sync)
  • New Leads: ~12–15K per year
  • Accounts: Few thousand
  • Data Quality: Generally clean; deduplication handled in Salesforce
  • Custom Fields: Mostly standard demographic/firmographic; minimal custom logic
  • Retention: 18 months of historical engagement data (emails, forms, campaigns) to be migrated if possible



  1. Integrations
     To be configured/validated in HubSpot: Salesforce, GoToWebinar, Qualified Chat, Aloware, Propensity, Google Analytics, Dataraid (HubSpot connector for reporting)
     To be deprecated or shifted: Freescale integration (moved to Salesforce)
  2. Automation & Lead Management



  • Lead Attribution: Multi-touch (First Touch = Lead Source, Last Touch = Campaign)
  • Lead Routing: Complex logic handled in Salesforce (based on email domain, geography, industry, solution type); HubSpot must trigger lead routing in Salesforce
  • Follow-up SLAs: Email immediately; call/LinkedIn task assignments
  • Scoring: Not used today; may explore in HubSpot
  • Desired Automations: Inbound nurture, webinar follow-up, API trial drips, multi-channel alerts



  1. Data Enrichment & Tools



  • Using: ZoomInfo, Qualified, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, 6sense, Clearbit
  • Data enrichment logic currently runs outside Pardot — will need HubSpot integration points
  • Deduplication & record merge handled in Salesforce



  1. Compliance & Permissions



  • Regulations: CCPA + select US state laws
  • Right-to-Forget Workflow: ~50/month, currently manual (JIRA-based); explore automation within HubSpot
  • Consent Management: Opt-in checkboxes, email preferences, unsubscribe sync with Salesforce
  • Users: 5 Marketing Hub users, 2–3 Sales Hub users
  • Approval Workflows: Basic user roles, no advanced approval hierarchy



  1. Reporting & Analytics



  • Reporting Needs: Campaign ROI, lead attribution, source tracking, funnel conversion, sales-marketing performance
  • Current State: Minimal reporting from Pardot; expect to use HubSpot standard dashboards



  1. Key Pain Points with Pardot



  • Limited automation and personalization capabilities
  • Manual campaign creation and nurture setup
  • Weak reporting and analytics
  • Desire for integrated content tools and better user experience



  1. Desired Outcomes in HubSpot



  • Easier campaign setup and automation
  • Improved lead nurturing and personalization (AI-enabled tools)
  • Centralized reporting and attribution dashboards
  • Seamless sync with Salesforce and third-party apps
  • Simplified compliance and subscription management



  1. Timeline

  • Start ASAP

  • Key driver: Outreach license expires March 1
  • Pardot license valid through next year (no hard cutoff)



  1. Internal Capacity

  • No recent in-house HubSpot experience
  • Expect Bluvium + HubSpot expert to lead implementation and training



  1. Expected Deliverables

  • HubSpot–Salesforce Integration Blueprint
  • Configured HubSpot Marketing Hub (core modules, properties, compliance setup)
  • Data migration plan, mapping sheet, and validation report
  • Rebuilt marketing assets (email templates, forms, landing page framework)
  • Migrated automation workflows (~9 nurtures, 44 rules)
  • Migrated content and active dynamic lists
  • Connected integrations (GoToWebinar, Qualified Chat, Aloware, Propensity, Google Analytics, Dataraid)
  • Automated lead routing and notification workflows aligned with Salesforce logic
  • User training sessions and documentation
  • Reporting dashboards for lead attribution, source tracking, and ROI
  • UAT sign-off, go-live checklist, and post-launch support plan
proposal_description:

job_title: No More Proposals Please (HubSpot Optimization & Customer Journey Enablement)
job_description:
Phase 1: Audit & Discovery
Objective
To engage a certified HubSpot Expert or Agency Partner to audit, optimize, and evolve our HubSpot instance to fully support a unified, data-driven customer journey — from pre-lead awareness through client renewals.
The engagement will begin with a comprehensive audit and discovery phase, leading to a detailed scope of work, implementation roadmap, and pricing proposal for the recommended initiatives.
Our organization currently operates on HubSpot Professional and is considering an upgrade to Enterprise. The consultant will evaluate the potential ROI and operational impact of this upgrade and help us articulate the value to executive stakeholders.

Phase 2: Implementation & Optimization
Objective
Execute the agreed-upon improvements and optimizations identified in Phase 1 to create a cohesive, automated, and measurable customer journey within HubSpot.

Key Focus Areas
  • Lifecycle Definition & Automation – Standardize and automate stages from pre-lead through renewal with clear criteria and progression rules.
  • Data Hygiene & Enrichment – Clean, de-duplicate, and enrich contact and company data for accuracy and segmentation.
  • Lead Capture & Scoring – Centralize and streamline all forms, implement consistent routing rules, and establish a behavior- and firmographic-based scoring model.

job_title: HubSpot RevOps Consultant (Enterprise)
job_description:
We’ve just moved from SugarCRM to HubSpot and purchased 10 Sales Hub Enterprise seats and 4 Marketing Hub Pro seats. We need an expert to migrate data, set up best-practice RevOps processes, and implement key integrations (Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana).

Objectives
- Migrate all SugarCRM data to HubSpot with clean mapping, dedupe, and validation.
- Build enterprise-grade automations for sales, marketing, and handoffs.
- Integrate Xero (billing), PhoneBurner (dialer/activities), and Asana (projects/tasks).
- Enable the team with permissions, playbooks, sequences, reports, and dashboards.

Scope of Work (deliverables)

1) Discovery & Design
Audit SugarCRM objects, fields, picklists, activities, attachments, and custom logic.
  • Define HubSpot data model: standard + custom objects, associations, required fields.
    Process design: Lead → MQL/SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won/Lost, pipeline stages, entry/exit criteria.
    Security model: teams, roles, permissions, record ownership rules.

2) Data Migration (SugarCRM → HubSpot)
Field mapping doc (1:1 + transformations), deduplication rules, lookup resolution.
Import activities (emails/calls/notes/meetings where supported), files, and lists.
Validation run + reconciliation report (record counts, error log, exceptions).
Delta cutover plan with blackout window and rollback.

3) Sales Hub Enterprise Setup
Pipelines (primary + any sub-pipelines), stage properties, required reasons.
Sequences, templates, snippets, playbooks, call outcomes, meeting links.
Lead scoring (behavioral + firmographic), SLAs and task queues.
Custom quotes, products, deal workflows (create tasks, set owners, update fields).
Forecasting setup, goals, and weighted pipeline definitions.

4) Marketing Hub Pro Setup
Lifecycle model, MQL definition, subscription types, double opt-in where needed.
Forms, CTAs, lists (smart/static), nurture workflows, and lead routing.
Email + landing page brand modules, compliance (GDPR/CCPA).
UTM + source tracking, campaign hierarchy.

5) Integrations
Xero: deal→invoice sync, invoice status back to HubSpot, revenue fields for reporting.
PhoneBurner: activity logging, call outcomes, contact sync, list handoff.
Asana: deal/contract approval → Asana project creation with key fields synced.
Webhooks/middleware (if needed) with retry + error handling.

6) Reporting & Enablement
Executive dashboards: pipeline health, velocity, forecast vs. quota, win/loss, campaign attribution.
Rep dashboards: tasks due, sequence performance, meetings booked, CAC/LTV basics.
Training: live sessions + Loom library; admin runbook; change log.

It's a long term engagement and you will be working with Sales, marketing & CS teams.
proposal_description:
Hi there, 

Thanks for outlining such a clear and well-organized scope. It’s refreshing to see this level of structure, and it absolutely reflects the complexity of what you’re taking on. We’ve supported similar migrations and full RevOps implementations for B2B companies moving into HubSpot Enterprise from systems like Sugar, Salesforce, and Zoho. 


A Few Quick Notes on Fit and Approach 

You’re not just “setting up HubSpot” — you’re designing a cross-functional revenue system. This is the right approach, and it does require more than a one-and-done migration. 

Given your mix of Sales Enterprise, Marketing Pro, and integrations (Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana), this needs to be handled in phases, ideally with a monthly retainer or milestone-based model for clarity and flexibility. 

We typically engage on these implementations over a 3–4 month period, starting with discovery and architecture, then data migration and system buildout, followed by training and optimizations. 


How We’d Propose Structuring the Engagement 

Phase 1 – Discovery & Design
Process mapping, data model design, security model, field mapping plan, integration planning.

Phase 2 – Migration & Configuration
Full SugarCRM to HubSpot migration, pipeline setup, lifecycle stages, properties, sequences, lists.

Phase 3 – Integrations & Workflows
Build and test Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana connections (with middleware if needed), and associated automation.

Phase 4 – Enablement & Rollout
Dashboards, reports, permissions, training materials, Loom library, and admin runbook. 


Budget & Engagement Model

Given the depth and complexity of this engagement — including data migration, enterprise-level automation, multi-team enablement, and integrations with Xero, PhoneBurner, and Asana — we’d recommend a structured implementation retainer of $7,500/month for 4 months, totaling $30,000. This pacing allows for thorough discovery, thoughtful execution, and adequate time for testing, documentation, and adoption.

If you'd prefer to start with a lower-risk entry point, we can begin with a fixed-scope discovery and planning phase for $3,500–$5,000. This phase would cover full systems auditing, data and field mapping, integration planning, and a delivery roadmap with final pricing for the remaining phases. From there, we can confidently move into execution with full alignment.

Note: Profoundly requires selecting either "None," "Half up-front," or "All up-front" for payment terms. While we've selected Half up-front to proceed, please note that our standard billing practice is monthly invoicing. We’ll reflect this in our actual agreement and invoices. 

A Few Quick Questions 

To ensure an accurate proposal, can you clarify a few things? 

  1. Do you already have middleware in place (like Zapier, Make.com, or a custom integration layer), or should we plan for that? 
  2. Are there any other tools involved (like CPQ, chat, or analytics platforms)? 
  3. Is the sales process already documented, or would we be facilitating that? 

We’d love to support your team and can begin with a short discovery sprint or jump straight into Phase 1.

If you’d like to talk through the path and structure together, feel free to book a quick call here: https://www.eternalworks.com/meetings/timjones/discovery-call


Let me know if you have any questions.



Thanks & Much Respect,
Tim
HubSpot Platinum Certified Partner

You can also view our client reviews and HubSpot certifications on our Solutions Partner profile here: https://ecosystem.hubspot.com/marketplace/solutions/eternal-works

job_title: HubSpot Optimization for Data Integrity, Sales Process, and Reporting
job_description:

Project Scope

·       Audit and adjust existing deal, contact, and company properties 
·       Rename or restructure existing picklists (e.g. Business Line) 
·       Create new properties to support reporting and process needs, including: 
o   Date of Initial Contact 
o   Type of Inquiry (Formal RFP / Informal) 
o   Deal Source (Campaign, Website, Referral, Conference, etc.) 
o   Business Line (New picklist: MCG Power Ops, Paragon, Versify, InnoTap, Unco) 
o   Product Pick List (Grouped under Business Line)

·       Optionally introduce workflows or validation logic to guide users toward complete entries

We want to eliminate our dependence on Excel by building robust, filterable reports inside HubSpot. Reporting goals include: 
·       Lead activity: Formal/informal RFPs over trailing 12 months 
·       Sales velocity: Time from inquiry → quote → close 
·       Pipeline analysis: Proposals made, prospects qualified, contracts closed

·       Product-level and business line performance

o   Manual deal creation (no automation from forms)

·       A clean, consistent data structure that supports our sales and reporting needs 
·       Native HubSpot reports that replace Excel for pipeline, lead, and upsell tracking 
·       Improved internal knowledge so we can build additional reports or workflows ourselves 
Clear documentation and a system we can own confidently going forward

This ensures your teams feel confident, aligned, and empowered to use the newly optimized environment. 

Budget Fit 

The full three-phase engagement (audit, refactor, and team training) is scoped at $26,250 USD. This reflects the depth of work required across multiple hubs and teams, including property cleanup, lifecycle redesign, workflow rebuilding, data hygiene, and end-user training. 

Timeline 

We’re ready to begin within your desired timeframe. 
Typical sequence:

A Few Questions to Finalize Scope 

How many pipelines exist today across Sales, CS, and Support? Roughly how many custom properties are in the system? Are lifecycle stages currently being used, or should we rebuild them? job_title: HubSpot Implementation Expert – Consolidated Client Communications
job_description:
Living With Lolo is a fast-growing, award-winning luxury interior design and construction firm based in Scottsdale, AZ. We’re in the middle of a big internal upgrade: creating a consolidated client communications hub so every client has one email address, one phone number, and a consistently white-glove experience across design and construction.

We’re looking for an experienced HubSpot expert to lead the implementation of this communications hub and turn our detailed business requirements into a real, live system our team loves using.

About the project

The goal of this project is to create a single operational “home” for all client-facing communication using HubSpot as the primary interface.

Phase 1 of this initiative is focused on client communications only. All email, SMS, voicemail, and internal notes for active projects will live in HubSpot, be routed to the correct point of contact, and be fully visible to leadership and the broader team. Gmail remains our enterprise email backbone; HubSpot becomes the day-to-day workspace for communication.

LWL Consolidated Communications

You will be collaborating closely with our CEO, Director of Operations, Director of Construction, and key team members across design and construction. Our team is remote-friendly, fast-paced, and human. We care a lot about how things feel to our clients and to our team, not just whether they technically “work.”

What we want you to build (at a high level)

Centralized email and SMS for clients, using one shared email address and one shared phone number that all communication flows through, while still honoring Gmail as the underlying email platform.

Shared inbox and routing in HubSpot so every inbound message (email, text, voicemail transcript) is automatically associated with the correct client, project, and project phase, and routed to that project’s primary point of contact (POC), with clear backup and escalation rules.

Configurable SLAs, business hours, and escalation so we can confidently say “we respond within one business day,” with timers that pause outside business hours and nudges that escalate from primary to backup to leadership when something sits too long.

Dashboards and visibility so any team member can open HubSpot and instantly see: open conversations by project, by POC, and by status; unmatched communications that need to be linked to a client/project; and escalated items that need leadership attention.

Internal notes and @-style collaboration replacing scattered Google Chat threads, so discussions, decisions, and “behind the scenes” commentary live with the client communication thread but are never visible to clients.

Unmatched communication handling so any message from a new email or phone number lands in an “unmatched” queue where it can be triaged, linked to an existing client/project, or dismissed as spam, with future messages automatically associated correctly.

Telephone integration using a solution like OpenPhone, Aircall, or similar, including voicemail routing, voicemail transcription, and SMS, aligned with HubSpot and our routing logic. We expect your help selecting and integrating the right platform for our volume and use case.

Clean, scalable data structures that respect a unique Project ID across tools (HubSpot, Airtable, QuickBooks, and future systems), and keep us “AI-ready” for future phases like AI search, summarization, and sentiment detection.

How we’ll work together:
We already have a detailed, 40+ page Business Requirements Document that outlines business objectives, functional and non-functional requirements, user scenarios, routing and escalation logic, governance, and a proposed 6-phase rollout (Discovery, Configuration, Integration & Testing, UAT, Training & Deployment, Stabilization & Handover). Your first job is to pressure-test, refine, and then implement against that document.

You will:
  • Lead or co-lead discovery and configuration planning, validating assumptions and calling out any gaps, risks, or trade-offs.
  • Design the HubSpot configuration (objects, properties, shared inboxes, pipelines or equivalent, workflows, routing and escalation, user roles, dashboards) to match the requirements and our real-life workflow.
  • Own or guide integration between HubSpot and Gmail, Airtable, and the chosen telephone platform, using native integrations and/or middleware like Zapier.
  • Set up SLA and escalation logic based on business hours, including vacation/OOO handling for POCs, with clear auditability.
  • Build usable, clean dashboards for different roles (POCs, Ops, Construction, Leadership) that highlight what actually matters: open conversations, unresolved items, escalations, and unmatched messages.
  • Create a practical UAT plan, guide our team through testing using realistic scenarios, and iterate based on their feedback.
  • Deliver clear documentation and lightweight training so non-technical, very busy team members can quickly feel confident using the system day-to-day.
  • Be a thought partner, not just a ticket-taker: propose smarter ways to do things when you see them, but respect the business constraints and non-negotiables already documented.


Who you are
You have deep, hands-on HubSpot implementation experience, ideally including complex shared inbox setups, multi-channel routing (email + SMS + voicemail), SLAs, and escalation logic in service or operations contexts.
  • You’ve integrated HubSpot with Gmail and at least one telephone platform (OpenPhone, Aircall, or comparable), and you understand the nuances of voicemail transcription, SMS deliverability, and routing in a service-oriented environment.
  • You’re comfortable designing and implementing integrations between HubSpot and tools like Airtable or similar databases using Zapier, n8n, or native connectors, and you think in terms of data models, not one-off zaps.
  • You communicate clearly, calmly, and with high emotional intelligence. You know how to explain technical trade-offs in plain language, draw out stakeholders’ real needs, and keep people feeling informed and respected, even when you have to push back or say “no” to something.
  • You enjoy training and change management. You’re patient, you anticipate where people will get confused, and you design interfaces and processes that feel intuitive, not overwhelming.
  • You are organized and reliable. You’re comfortable with a governance structure that includes change requests, documented assumptions, and explicit sign-offs. You keep good records and don’t “configure and disappear.”
  • You appreciate brands that care about experience. The idea of supporting a luxury, high-touch design firm appeals to you, and you understand that the way a system feels to clients and staff matters just as much as the underlying logic.


Nice to have (but not required):
Experience with construction or home services firms, CRMs for project-based businesses, or high-touch professional services.
  • Experience designing systems with future AI capabilities in mind (AI search, summarization, sentiment/urgency detection), even if you’re not implementing AI in this first phase.
  • Understanding of compliance considerations around email/SMS communication (CAN-SPAM, TCPA, etc.).


What success looks like:
Clients always use one email and one phone number, and feel genuinely cared for and clearly communicated with.
  • No client communication goes unanswered beyond the agreed SLA during business hours.
  • Our team spends their client-communication time inside HubSpot, not bouncing between Gmail, phones, and chat, and they feel that HubSpot is helping them, not adding busywork.
  • Leadership can log in at any time and see, in seconds, whether clients are being responded to, what’s open, and where escalation is happening.
  • The implementation is well-documented, stable, and ready for future enhancements, including AI-assisted search and analytics, without ripping anything apart.


How to apply:
If this sounds like the kind of project you do best, please send:
A short introduction that highlights why this project is a fit for you.
  • 2–3 examples of similar HubSpot implementations you’ve led, particularly ones involving shared inboxes, routing/escalation, and telephony/SMS.
  • A brief summary of your proposed approach to Discovery and Phase 1–3 work (Configuration, Integration & Testing), including rough timing and budget for a Time & Materials engagement with a not-to-exceed cap.
  • Any relevant certifications or partner status with HubSpot or telephone vendors.


We’re excited to meet someone who can help us bring this system to life in a way that feels as thoughtful, calm, and elevated as the spaces we design.
proposal_description:
You've clearly done the hard work most firms skip - a 40-page BRD puts you way ahead. But I've noticed something: companies with detailed requirements documents often nail the technical build and still struggle six months later because their team quietly reverted to Gmail and personal phones.

The question isn't whether HubSpot can do what your BRD outlines (it can). It's whether your designers and project managers will actually live in it when they're juggling demanding clients and tight deadlines.

Three questions:
1. When this is live and working, what specific business outcome changes? (Client retention? Revenue per project? Hours saved per week?)
2. Right now, what does the fragmented communication actually cost you - lost client details, delayed responses, leadership blind spots?
3. What happens if we build exactly what's in your BRD, but six months post-launch your team has found workarounds back to their old tools?

On budget: projects like this typically run $15-25K depending on integration complexity and training depth. 

Would a 30-minute call to pressure-test the BRD and map the real implementation?

Answering a handful of your questions:
Dashboards and visibility so any team member can open HubSpot and instantly see: open conversations by project, by POC, and by status; unmatched communications that need to be linked to a client/project; and escalated items that need leadership attention.
We've done this in every project we've taken on, but you can immediately point to a software company called WasteNot that we just wrapped up the initial implementation with, and their team is loving their reports. Happy to make an introduction. 

Clean, scalable data structures that respect a unique Project ID across tools (HubSpot, Airtable, QuickBooks, and future systems), and keep us “AI-ready” for future phases like AI search, summarization, and sentiment detection.
I built this exact system at a company called Hampton where I was the first employee in charge of a HubSpot Enterprise account, Airtable, Zapier, and all the other big automation platforms like Stripe (which would have replaced QuickBooks). Now my agency creates AI-ready and AI-forward companies regularly. 

Telephone integration using a solution like OpenPhone, Aircall, or similar, including voicemail routing, voicemail transcription, and SMS, aligned with HubSpot and our routing logic. We expect your help selecting and integrating the right platform for our volume and use case.
We've also integrated JustCall and now CloudTalk with HubSpot for our marketing agency with over 80 employees. I can also tell you why one platform is better than the other and where you should be looking for your technology vendors.

job_title: HubSpot Campaign Standardization, Segmentation, and Workflow Optimization
job_description:

Our goal is to standardize campaign setup, segmentation, and workflows so that our internal teams can operate more efficiently and confidently within HubSpot.

We’re looking for a Certified Pro who can help us identify where to start, guide the cleanup and standardization process, and leave us with a system that’s consistent, scalable, and easy to maintain.

> Salesforce Integration Alignment: Ensure Salesforce ↔ HubSpot sync behavior supports the new campaign and workflow logic without creating duplicates or misaligned data.

  • > Deep experience in HubSpot Marketing Hub and Salesforce integration
  • > Proven track record in data hygiene, workflow optimization, and campaign automation
  • > Strong understanding of segmentation strategy, lead routing, and attribution modeling
  • > Ability to design repeatable campaign frameworks and templates
  • > Excellent documentation and communication skills to support internal enablement


What Success Looks Like
  • > Teams can quickly and confidently launch campaigns using standardized templates
  • > Segmentation is consistent and channel assignment is reliable
  • > Workflow automation is simplified and error-free
  • > Data is clean, complete, and accurate across HubSpot and Salesforce
  • > Clear documentation and best practices empower internal teams to self-serve

job_title: HubSpot RevOps Consultant (Enterprise)
job_description:
We’ve just moved from SugarCRM to HubSpot and purchased 10 Sales Hub Enterprise seats and 4 Marketing Hub Pro seats. We need an expert to migrate data, set up best-practice RevOps processes, and implement key integrations (Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana).

Objectives
- Migrate all SugarCRM data to HubSpot with clean mapping, dedupe, and validation.
- Build enterprise-grade automations for sales, marketing, and handoffs.
- Integrate Xero (billing), PhoneBurner (dialer/activities), and Asana (projects/tasks).
- Enable the team with permissions, playbooks, sequences, reports, and dashboards.

Scope of Work (deliverables)

1) Discovery & Design
Audit SugarCRM objects, fields, picklists, activities, attachments, and custom logic.
  • Define HubSpot data model: standard + custom objects, associations, required fields.
    Process design: Lead → MQL/SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won/Lost, pipeline stages, entry/exit criteria.
    Security model: teams, roles, permissions, record ownership rules.

2) Data Migration (SugarCRM → HubSpot)
Field mapping doc (1:1 + transformations), deduplication rules, lookup resolution.
Import activities (emails/calls/notes/meetings where supported), files, and lists.
Validation run + reconciliation report (record counts, error log, exceptions).
Delta cutover plan with blackout window and rollback.

3) Sales Hub Enterprise Setup
Pipelines (primary + any sub-pipelines), stage properties, required reasons.
Sequences, templates, snippets, playbooks, call outcomes, meeting links.
Lead scoring (behavioral + firmographic), SLAs and task queues.
Custom quotes, products, deal workflows (create tasks, set owners, update fields).
Forecasting setup, goals, and weighted pipeline definitions.

4) Marketing Hub Pro Setup
Lifecycle model, MQL definition, subscription types, double opt-in where needed.
Forms, CTAs, lists (smart/static), nurture workflows, and lead routing.
Email + landing page brand modules, compliance (GDPR/CCPA).
UTM + source tracking, campaign hierarchy.

5) Integrations
Xero: deal→invoice sync, invoice status back to HubSpot, revenue fields for reporting.
PhoneBurner: activity logging, call outcomes, contact sync, list handoff.
Asana: deal/contract approval → Asana project creation with key fields synced.
Webhooks/middleware (if needed) with retry + error handling.

6) Reporting & Enablement
Executive dashboards: pipeline health, velocity, forecast vs. quota, win/loss, campaign attribution.
Rep dashboards: tasks due, sequence performance, meetings booked, CAC/LTV basics.
Training: live sessions + Loom library; admin runbook; change log.

It's a long term engagement and you will be working with Sales, marketing & CS teams.

job_title: HubSpot Marketing Hub Implementation
job_description:
HubSpot Implementation & Pardot Migration – Project Brief


Client Overview:

Client currently uses Salesforce Sales Cloud + Pardot (Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Advanced) for marketing automation. They plan to migrate to HubSpot Marketing Hub (Enterprise/Pro) while maintaining Salesforce as the CRM system with two-way sync.


  1. Salesforce & Pardot Environment



  • Pardot Edition: MCAE Advanced
  • Salesforce objects in scope: Leads, Contacts, Accounts, Opportunities
  • Current sync: Pardot → Salesforce (~20-min delay), want real-time sync in HubSpot
  • No major custom integrations beyond standard marketing sync



  1. Marketing Assets Inventory



  • Email Templates: ~5–10 active templates
  • Forms: ~5 standard reusable forms used across ~30–35 website forms
  • Landing Pages: None in Pardot today; hosted on WordPress; want ability to build/manage landing pages in HubSpot
  • Automation Programs: ~9 nurture/drip workflows and ~44 automation rules (webinar follow-up, lead nurture, A/B testing)
  • Lists: Hundreds of static lists (retain 5 active), a few dynamic lists to be migrated
  • Content Assets: ~2,500 total (PDFs, images, gated resources); migration scope to be confirmed



  1. Data Scope & Structure



  • Contacts: ~70K active in Pardot; total HubSpot volume ~100K (including Salesforce sync)
  • New Leads: ~12–15K per year
  • Accounts: Few thousand
  • Data Quality: Generally clean; deduplication handled in Salesforce
  • Custom Fields: Mostly standard demographic/firmographic; minimal custom logic
  • Retention: 18 months of historical engagement data (emails, forms, campaigns) to be migrated if possible



  1. Integrations
     To be configured/validated in HubSpot: Salesforce, GoToWebinar, Qualified Chat, Aloware, Propensity, Google Analytics, Dataraid (HubSpot connector for reporting)
     To be deprecated or shifted: Freescale integration (moved to Salesforce)
  2. Automation & Lead Management



  • Lead Attribution: Multi-touch (First Touch = Lead Source, Last Touch = Campaign)
  • Lead Routing: Complex logic handled in Salesforce (based on email domain, geography, industry, solution type); HubSpot must trigger lead routing in Salesforce
  • Follow-up SLAs: Email immediately; call/LinkedIn task assignments
  • Scoring: Not used today; may explore in HubSpot
  • Desired Automations: Inbound nurture, webinar follow-up, API trial drips, multi-channel alerts



  1. Data Enrichment & Tools



  • Using: ZoomInfo, Qualified, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, 6sense, Clearbit
  • Data enrichment logic currently runs outside Pardot — will need HubSpot integration points
  • Deduplication & record merge handled in Salesforce



  1. Compliance & Permissions



  • Regulations: CCPA + select US state laws
  • Right-to-Forget Workflow: ~50/month, currently manual (JIRA-based); explore automation within HubSpot
  • Consent Management: Opt-in checkboxes, email preferences, unsubscribe sync with Salesforce
  • Users: 5 Marketing Hub users, 2–3 Sales Hub users
  • Approval Workflows: Basic user roles, no advanced approval hierarchy



  1. Reporting & Analytics



  • Reporting Needs: Campaign ROI, lead attribution, source tracking, funnel conversion, sales-marketing performance
  • Current State: Minimal reporting from Pardot; expect to use HubSpot standard dashboards



  1. Key Pain Points with Pardot



  • Limited automation and personalization capabilities
  • Manual campaign creation and nurture setup

job_title: HubSpot Cleanup, Segmentation & Automation for Multi-Brand, Multi-Location Business
job_description:
We’re a multi-location entertainment company operating 26 locations across multiple brands, and we’ve inherited a complex HubSpot setup from previous employees and acquisitions. While HubSpot is core to our CRM and marketing efforts, our current configuration has become difficult to manage and scale.

We’re looking for an experienced HubSpot expert to help us clean up our existing setup, recommend best practices, and create a clear go-forward model that our internal teams can maintain confidently.

Key focus areas include:

CRM & Segmentation
  • Audit and clean up existing contacts, lists, and segments.
  • Recommend a clear structure for how contacts should be organized and segmented going forward.
  • Define best practices for how new contacts are created and managed across different channels and teams (e.g., reservations system, sales team, marketing).
  • Reduce confusion and duplication while ensuring contacts route to the correct teams and locations.

Marketing & Email Automation
  • Design a scalable approach for managing marketing efforts across 26 individual brands/locations.
  • Advise on how to structure campaigns and automated emails at both the brand and portfolio level.
  • Help determine best practices around marketing contact status (including when and how to use it).
  • Reduce form sprawl by redesigning forms into a more intentional structure (e.g., fewer, smarter forms that properly segment leads into sales, events, or marketing workflows).
  • Explore options for efficiently creating, managing, or uploading emails across multiple brands.

Integrations, Automation & Reporting
  • Review and advise on the integration between HubSpot and our reservations system
  • Provide guidance on understanding and improving existing workflows.
  • Help us better utilize HubSpot reporting so stakeholders can clearly see performance across brands and locations.

Critical Requirement

Training and procedure documentation is a must.
  • We want clear documentation and guidance so internal teams understand how HubSpot is structured, why decisions were made, and how to operate the system correctly going forward.

Overall, success for us looks like a clean, well-structured HubSpot instance with clear segmentation, scalable automation, reliable reporting, and documented best practices that support growth across all locations without creating ongoing operational friction.
proposal_description:
Hey, thanks for posting the project to Profoundly.

This is exactly the type of HubSpot challenge we specialise in.

We have worked with complex, multi-brand and multi-location organisations that have inherited messy HubSpot portals through growth, acquisitions, and staff turnover. In almost every case, the underlying issue is not just “cleanup”, it is the lack of a clear operating model that teams can actually follow and maintain.

Our experience
We are senior HubSpot specialists who work hands-on across the full CRM and Hubs. We do not drop in junior teams or generic templates. We have delivered similar work for organisations with:
  • Multiple brands and locations
  • High contact volumes and duplicate data
  • Conflicting lifecycle stages, lists, and workflows
  • Overbuilt forms and fragmented email automation
  • Reporting that stakeholders do not trust

Our work consistently centres on clarity, governance, and scalability, not just technical fixes. Client feedback regularly highlights confidence, simplicity, and long-term usability as the biggest outcomes.

How we would approach this
We use a structured, proven process that avoids ripping everything apart and instead creates a stable, maintainable model.

1. Audit and diagnosis
  • Review contact data, lists, segments, lifecycle stages, and workflows
  • Identify duplication, routing issues, and points of confusion
  • Assess form sprawl, marketing contact usage, and email architecture
  • Review integrations, including the reservations system
  • Assess current reporting and data reliability

2. Define the go-forward model
  • Clear segmentation framework across brands and locations
  • Agreed rules for contact creation, ownership, and routing
  • Scalable approach to brand-level and portfolio-level marketing
  • Simplified form and automation strategy
  • Clear guidance on marketing contact status usage

3. Implement and simplify
  • Clean up and rationalise data, lists, and workflows
  • Rebuild automation where needed, remove what is not serving you
  • Align integrations and reporting to the agreed structure

4. Documentation and training
This is a non-negotiable part of how we work.
  • Clear written documentation explaining how HubSpot is structured and why
  • Practical operating procedures for internal teams
  • Training sessions tailored to real day-to-day usage, not generic demos

The goal is that your team can run HubSpot with confidence without ongoing dependency.

Why a conversation is essential
While your brief is clear and well thought through, the scope, effort, and sequencing will depend on things we cannot reliably assess without speaking to you, for example:
  • How brands and locations are currently represented in HubSpot
  • The complexity of the reservations system integration
  • Contact volumes and data quality
  • The level of automation already in place versus what should be retained
  • Internal team structure and ownership

Because of this, we do not provide fixed pricing or proposals without a discovery conversation. That conversation allows us to confirm scope, avoid assumptions, and produce a proposal that is realistic and aligned to how you actually operate.

Next step
The right next step is a focused call to walk through your current setup, priorities, and constraints. From there, we would provide a clear proposal covering scope, approach, timelines, and investment.

If this sounds aligned you can book time with me here: https://app.reclaim.ai/m/rikki/profoundly

Hello! I’m Rikki, a Growth Consultant and HubSpot expert with over 13 years of hands-on experience helping companies unlock growth with HubSpot.

I’ve worked directly with hundreds of businesses from scale-ups to global brands to transform their marketing, sales, CRM and operations using HubSpot. 

Unlike agencies that delegate work to juniors or admins, I’m a strategic, hands-on partner who knows the platform inside out. I help companies:
  • Align strategy with execution across marketing, sales, and technology
  • Implement and optimise HubSpot (all Hubs)
  • Build scalable CMS solutions
  • Enable sales teams with tools, coaching, and automation

My focus is simple: to help ambitious companies grow profitably, sustainably, and with confidence using HubSpot.

Customer References
"As a CMO running a lean marketing operation, partnering with Rikki has been transformative. His strategic insights and HubSpot expertise have been invaluable in shaping our growth."
— Ingunn Bjøru, CMO, IXT

Proof & Credibility
Over 12+ years I've been trusted by leading brands including: HubSpot, IBM, Toyota, GovNet, Shoes for Crews, Fulfilmentcrowd, DMG Events, Towergate, Newcastle University, Spark TSL and many more.

Certifications

proposal_description:
Jean Christofferson Certified Profoundly Pro

I am a HubSpot Architect and Admin with a software engineering background. I have worked in multiple enterprise and pro HubSpot accounts, re-architecting the environment and setting up effective information flow with reporting for leadership and data quality monitoring.  
I work as a fractional member of your team for the duration of the project and typically am on your communications channels such as Slack or Teams to facilitate fast turn around of questions (both my questions and yours. 

I recently completed a major project with a large HubSpot Enterprise client with multiple sales pipelines, 20 sales locations and over 100 sales representatives. This engagement started with a detailed audit addressing both strategy and technical issues. The audit examined all aspects of the HubSpot Enterprise Sales, Marketing and Service Hubs including data model, sourcing and integrity.  The audit resulted in a detailed document that was linked to reports to  illustrate specific problem areas. Structural changes and steps required were specified and prioritized. 

Thank you for detailing the core user groups.  Sales representatives, sales managers and executives are the stakeholders that I typically work with along with your current HubSpot admin (if available) and technical staff. 

I start with an audit of your HubSpot instance, ideally in combination with several discovery calls with your primary stakeholders. The audit includea a security audit of your users, teams and permissions.  Current workflows will be examined along with property usage for contacts, leads, companies, deals, and tickets (if in use.) The pipeline(s) configuration will be reviewed along with required properties and conditional logic for moving deals through stages. The audit and roadmap will be presented for review along with an audit dashboard.

Building custom reporting and setting up dashboards for stakeholders is a speciality of mine.  Reporting is an integral part of each of the phases to measure implementation progress in addition to sharing data with stakeholders.

I have inserted the project cost of each of the desired phases. The audit is included as that start of Phase 1. Based on the audit it is possible that the costs for some of the phases may be reduced. Snowflake integration in particular may require less time depending on the number of properties that need to be mapped..  Costs will not go up.  Workflows will be setup (and tested) throughout the process, including in the audit phase.  

Phase 1: Audit and Fix the Basics

$8000

  • Audit 
  • Clean up existing data mess.  Data flagged for deletion is captured in a list and exported just in case.
  • Set up custom drop-down fields and properties they actually need across Leads, Company Records, Contacts, Accounts, etc. This will include syncing properties from one object to another as required.
  • Clean up existing & configure role-based access for sellers, sales leaders, marketing, and contractors. Primary and secondary teams will be reviewed and setup if not property utililzed.
  • Configure sales pipeline stages - with required fields and conditional logic.  Customize cards and views.
  • Build basic automations.  I can built coimplicated automations as well - whatever is required.
  • Test


Phase 2: Build Reports

$3500

  • Sales rep scorecards (This can also include setting up of goals if they have not been used)
  • Manager dashboards for team performance.  The audit will identify the metrics required.
  • Executive dashboards (for 5 specific leaders) This will include KPIs, Pivot tables and extensive custom reports pulling data from multiple source.
  • Basic sales metrics everyone needs.  HubsSpot provides many of these but they are dependent upon proper adoption and usage of HubSpot.


Phase 3: Connect to Snowflake

$4000

  • One-way sync from Snowflake to HubSpot (two-way sync could be considered later)
  • Map the right fields
  • Test everything works
  • Document how to maintain it


Phase 4: Train the Team

$3500

  • Virtual training for sales users.  I have extensive experience training.  Training of sales users required sales leadership support.  In some cases sales users have been excused if they were exceeding their goals.  This compromises HubSpot data and leadership reporting.
  • Admin (how to make changes) training for whoever maintains it - full documentation will be included in workflows and in a comprehensive document.  Workflows and lists will be organized into well named folders.  Workflows will have standard naming conventions.
  • Written guides for reference (documentation will be produced as part of implementation)

I am located in the San Francisco Bay Area and have extended hours online.

Here is my meeting link: https://meetings.hubspot.com/jean-christofferson

I look forward to meeting with you. Let me know if you don't find a good opening on my calendar and I will adjust my availability to match your schedule.

Thank you, Jean Christofferson

1. Project Overview
This engagement aims to optimize the Leads, Deals, and Sales Processes within Cargas's HubSpot CRM. The focus will be on streamlining deal management, standardizing processes across Sales, CX, and Marketing, and improving CRM usability through best practices, workflow automation, and training.
Cargas has different teams with varied processes and deal management approaches. The goal of this engagement is to simplify, standardize, and optimize these processes while ensuring each team’s unique requirements are met. Additionally, we will create executive-level HubSpot reporting, addressing any platform limitations by embedding external reports where necessary.

This project will include a discovery phase, process design, implementation, reporting setup, training, and ongoing support for unforeseen challenges.

2. Objectives
  •  Conduct discovery meetings with key stakeholders to analyze the current lead, deal, and reporting processes.
  • Unify and standardize deal pipeline structures while ensuring necessary flexibility for different sales teams.
  • Optimize data properties across teams to improve usability and reporting.
  • Implement best practices for lead management, deal tracking, and reporting.
  • Reevaluate and optimize existing workflows while creating new workflows as needed to align with new processes.
  • Define an efficient lifecycle management approach for Contacts and Companies.
  • Create executive-level reporting in HubSpot and embed external reports via iFrames when HubSpot reporting limitations arise.
  • Deliver end-user and admin training for seamless adoption.
  •  Include a buffer to address unexpected complexities uncovered during discovery.

3. Scope of Work

Phase 1: Discovery & Strategy

1. Conduct deep-dive discovery sessions with Sales, CX, and Marketing teams to:
• Map out current lead and deal processes.
• Identify inconsistencies, inefficiencies, and reporting gaps.
• Understand how different sales teams use properties and what can be unified.

2. Develop a Discovery Findings Report & Process Design Document, including:
• Proposed unified pipeline structures with flexibility for unique team needs.
• Recommendations for data standardization across teams.
• Strategy for executive reporting, with HubSpot dashboards and external reports where needed.
• Workflow optimization recommendations.

3. Provide an updated project timeline after discovery findings.

Phase 2: Implementation

1. Deal Pipeline Optimization
• Implement the revised pipeline structures based on approved strategy.
• Define clear, meaningful deal stages that align with sales processes.
• Standardize deal record properties across teams for better reporting.

2. Workflow Enhancements
• Review existing workflows, optimize where needed, and build new automations.
• Improve lifecycle management for Contacts and Companies to align with new processes.

3. Executive Reporting Setup
• Configure HubSpot dashboards for CEO-level insights.
• Create custom reports for sales performance tracking.
• Where HubSpot reporting falls short, embed iframe reports using external BI tools.

Phase 3: Training & Enablement

1. End-User Training for sales teams covering:
• New deal pipeline and lead management processes.
• Best practices for data entry and CRM hygiene.
• How to leverage new reports and dashboards.

2. Admin Training for RevOps/CRM managers covering:
• Managing pipeline changes and workflows.
• Customizing reports and dashboards.
• Best practices for ongoing CRM maintenance.

Phase 4: Buffer & Ongoing Support
• Allow for additional hours to address unexpected challenges uncovered during implementation.
• Make adjustments based on user feedback after the initial rollout.

4. Deliverables
  • Discovery & Process Design Document
  • Optimized Deal Pipeline with meaningful stages and standarized properties and stages
  • Implemented workflows and process automations aligned with new processes
  • Training sessions (End-user & Admin)
  • Final documentation with best practices
  • Executive Reporting (HubSpot dashboards + iFrame reports)
  • Buffer for additional refinements post-implementation

5. Estimated Timeline & Milestones

Estimated Completion

Phase 2: Design | Process Design Document | April 11, 2025
Phase 3: Implementation | Pipeline, Workflows, Reporting Setup | May 30, 2025

Phase 5: Buffer & Refinements | Refinements, Adjustments, Ongoing Support | June 30, 2025
Phase 6: Extended Buffer | Final tweaks, additional support, and unforeseen challenges | July 31, 2025

6. Assumptions
• Client will provide access to HubSpot CRM and necessary data.
• Stakeholders will be available for scheduled discovery meetings and feedback sessions.
• Any additional scope outside of the agreed-upon Process Design Document may require a change request and additional fees.


7. Pricing & Payment Terms

Payment Schedule

Estimated Due Date

Reforge Solutions is a boutique RevOps agency founded by HubSpot alumni with deep expertise in HubSpot ↔ Salesforce integrations and B2B marketing automation.

This covers the entire project from initial scoping through go-live, plus a two-week post-launch hypercare period for optimization and troubleshooting.

We need someone to help us integrate HubSpot with our WordPress site that uses WooCommerce and the MakeWebBetter plug-in. We're having challenges with our shopping cart page because it's not a HubSpot form and therefore certain fields aren't transferring properly to HS. We also have issues with contacts we imported from ConstantContact that weren't high-quality so now our email reputation has been damaged. We need to clean/purge our contacts and organize them into better Lists in order to create successful Workflows. 
Desired Time Frame: asap
proposal_description:
Project Scope


  1. HubSpot – WooCommerce Integration
    • Audit existing WordPress/WooCommerce setup with MakeWebBetter plugin
    • Identify missing shopping‑cart fields and form triggers
    • Build or embed a custom HubSpot form on the cart/checkout page, mapping all required fields (e.g. products, order total, coupon codes)
    • Test end‑to‑end data flow from cart to HubSpot Contact & Deal records
  2. Contact Cleanup & List Organization
    • Audit contact records records for bounces, unsubscribes and low-engagement profiles
    • Purge or suppress invalid/low‑quality contacts to restore email sending reputation
    • Define segmentation criteria (e.g. lifecycle stage, purchase history, engagement level)
    • Build targeted HubSpot Lists based on those criteria
  3. Workflow Design & Implementation
    • Outline 2–3 key automation workflows (e.g. abandoned‑cart reminders, welcome/nurture series)
    • Configure workflow logic, email templates and enrollment triggers
    • Test all workflows with live data to verify correct list membership and timing

job_title: HubSpot Technical Consultant
job_description:
The Alberta Real Estate Association (AREA) is seeking a part-time independent contractor to support our organization by configuring, customizing, and maintaining the HubSpot platform to align with our business needs. This role is ideal for a HubSpot expert who excels at problem-solving, technical support, and system optimization.
 
The contractor will provide services remotely, with some in-office time as required. 
Hours will vary; anticipate 15-20 hours per week.
Services to Commence:  As soon as possible
End of Contract Services: October 31, 2025
  
AREA Overview
The Alberta Real Estate Association (AREA) is the provincial professional organization that represents the interests of 10 regional real estate boards/associations and more than 15,000 REALTORS® provincewide.  Based in Calgary, the association’s mission is to provide strategic leadership through AREA’s member-centric services, advocacy and professional development.
 
Services Required
§ Configure, customize, and manage HubSpot for optimal performance
§ Coordinate user groups, roles, and permissions across teams
§ Oversee paid licenses and system credential documentation
§ Diagnose and resolve technical issues related to HubSpot, integrations (e.g., Stripe), and data flows
§ Develop and implement HubSpot integrations to enhance workflows
§ Ensure data integrity, segmentation, and compliance
§ Provide training, documentation, and ongoing support to teams
§ Optimize business processes using HubSpot automation, workflows, and reporting
§ Develop dashboards and analytics for data-driven decision-making
§ Stay updated on HubSpot trends, best practices, and compliance
§ Collaborate with other departments and IT to align HubSpot with organizational goals
 
Ideal Service Provider
The contractor will have technical expertise and analytical thinking, enabling them to communicate complex solutions in a user-friendly manner. With a proactive problem-solving mindset, they will anticipate challenges, identify solutions in advance, and optimize HubSpot processes for efficiency and scalability. The contractor will excel at diagnosing and resolving technical issues related to HubSpot and its integrations while working cross-functionally to align the platform with organizational goals. Additionally, they can build and maintain effective relationships with both internal and external stakeholders to support seamless collaboration.
 
Required Qualifications
§ Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems or a related field with minimum 5 years relevant service experience (equivalent industry-related expertise will be considered)
§ Advanced CRM experience, preferably with HubSpot, including system architecture, integrations, and automation + API integration experience (e.g., integration architecture, endpoint guidance)
§ Experience managing data integrity, segmentation, reporting, and analytics within a CRM
§ Ability to effectively train and support users of varying technical skill levels, translating complex concepts into practical applications
 
To Apply
Please submit your CV and a brief statement outlining your HubSpot experience and remuneration expectation.
Desired Time Frame: As soon as possible
proposal_description:
Project: Complete HubSpot Sales Hub Rebuild & Automation Implementation

Executive Summary
We understand your current pain points with spreadsheet dependency, misfiring automations, and disconnected booking logic. As a HubSpot Certified Partner with expertise across every Hub, GTM Ops Agency specializes in exactly this type of system rebuild—transforming fragmented processes into a unified, scalable revenue engine within HubSpot.
This isn't a consulting engagement; it's a complete implementation project where we'll rebuild your system from the ground up, ensuring your team can work efficiently with minimal manual intervention.

Understanding Your Current Challenges
Based on your description, we've identified these critical pain points:
Process Fragmentation
  • Booking logic isolated outside HubSpot
  • Heavy reliance on Google Sheets for business logic
  • Manual data entry creating bottlenecks for sales reps
System Inefficiencies
  • Broken workflows and misfiring triggers
  • Slow deal creation processes
  • Automation overload causing reminder conflicts
  • No centralized system for group booking management
Team Productivity Issues
  • Sales reps spending time on manual tasks instead of selling
  • Lack of streamlined processes for complex scenarios (group bookings)
  • Disconnected systems creating data silos

Our Implementation Approach

Phase 1: Discovery & System Audit (Week 1)
Deep-dive into your current state:
  • Map existing spreadsheet logic and business rules
  • Audit current HubSpot workflows and identify failure points
  • Document booking processes and group booking requirements
  • Analyze data flow between systems
  • Interview key users to understand daily workflows
Deliverable: Comprehensive system blueprint and implementation roadmap

Phase 2: Foundational Architecture (Weeks 2-3)
Rebuild core infrastructure:
  • Design new deal creation automation framework
  • Restructure contact and company data architecture
  • Create custom properties and pipeline stages optimized for your processes
  • Establish data validation rules and standardization protocols
Deliverable: Clean, optimized HubSpot foundation ready for automation

Phase 3: Automation Implementation (Weeks 4-5)
Build intelligent workflows:
  • Smart Deal Creation: Automated deal generation based on booking types and triggers
  • Google Sheets Migration: Migrate critical logic into HubSpot using custom properties, workflows, and calculated fields
  • Group Booking Logic: Sophisticated automation handling multiple attendees without trigger conflicts
  • Intelligent Reminder System: Context-aware notifications that prevent automation overload
Deliverable: Fully functional automation suite with comprehensive testing

Phase 4: Advanced Logic & Optimization (Week 6)
Implement complex business rules:
  • Advanced group booking management with dynamic assignment
  • Sales rep task automation and priority scoring
  • Revenue tracking and forecasting improvements
  • Integration testing and performance optimization
Deliverable: Polished system with advanced business logic

Phase 5: Training & Handoff (Week 7)
Ensure sustainable adoption:
  • Team training on new processes and workflows
  • Documentation of all custom implementations
  • Admin training for ongoing maintenance
  • Performance monitoring setup
Deliverable: Trained team and comprehensive system documentation


Key Implementations

Automated Deal Creation System
  • Trigger-based deal generation from multiple sources (forms, bookings, referrals)
  • Dynamic deal properties based on booking type and customer segment
  • Automated assignment logic for sales reps
  • Pipeline progression automation with stage-specific actions

Integrated Booking Logic
  • Migrate Google Sheets logic into HubSpot using: 
    • Custom calculated properties
    • Workflow automation

    • Custom API integrations where necessary

Group Booking Management
  • Parent-child deal structure for group bookings
  • Intelligent reminder scheduling to prevent conflicts
  • Automated attendee tracking and management
  • Consolidated reporting for group vs. individual bookings

Sales Efficiency Tools
  • Automated data entry for standard fields
  • Smart task creation based on deal stage and type
  • Streamlined follow-up sequences
  • Integrated communication tracking

Technical Expertise
HubSpot Certifications: Complete certification across Sales Hub, Marketing Hub, Service Hub, and Operations Hub
Core Competencies:
  • Advanced workflow architecture and automation design
  • Custom property creation and calculated field implementation
  • API integrations and data migration
  • Complex business logic translation into CRM workflows
  • Performance optimization and system scalability
Implementation Philosophy: We build solutions centered on HubSpot as your single source of truth, ensuring all business logic lives within your CRM for maximum efficiency and transparency.

Investment & Timeline
Project Duration: 7 weeks
Start Date: Early August 2025
Hourly Rate: $125/hour
Estimated Hours: 120-150 hours
Total Investment: $15,000 - $18,750
Payment Structure:
  • 50% upfront to begin discovery and planning
  • 25% at Phase 3 completion (automation implementation)
  • 25% upon final delivery and training completion

Why GTM Ops Agency
Proven Implementation Track Record: We specialize in transforming fragmented operations into unified HubSpot systems for early-stage companies
Technical + Strategic Expertise: Beyond technical implementation, we understand the business impact of each automation and workflow
Post-Implementation Support: Our foundational builds are designed for long-term scalability, with optional ongoing optimization retainers

job_title: Hubspot Refactor
job_description:
We Use Hubspot for a large percentage of our company operations. Our Sales, Marketing, CS and Support Teams all use it for their day-to-day. We've been on the tool for years, and it's gotten very unwieldy with out of date properties, unconnected data, etc.

We're looking for a partner to A. Map out our current instance in a way that helps us decide where to go next, and B. Then will help us refactor the instance to empower our teams to do their best work.
proposal_description:
I'm a HubSpot Certified Solution Partner based in Boston, MA. It sounds like your HubSpot portal has reached that stage where years of growth, layer after layer of quick fixes, and team-by-team adaptations have created a system that is powerful but also chaotic.

Based on what you shared, I already see two strong opportunities. First, consolidating property groups and removing stale fields will immediately reduce noise and help your teams avoid inaccurate data entry. Second, mapping data flow across Deals, Contacts, Tickets, and any custom objects will reveal breakpoints that currently slow down handoffs between Sales, CS, and Support.

I would want to understand a few practical details. For example, which objects carry the bulk of your operational logic right now, and where are you seeing conflicts between Sales, CS, and Support? Are there legacy properties feeding active workflows or reports that no one is owning anymore?

I recently worked with a fast-growing B2B SaaS company where HubSpot had become messy after years of rapid expansion. Their Sales, Marketing, CS, and Support teams were all stepping on each other's workflows, relying on outdated properties, and dealing with data conflicts that slowed everything down. I audited the entire portal, mapped how data actually moved across objects, and cleaned up pipelines, properties, and automation to match their real processes. The result was a much clearer system, cleaner reporting, and teams who finally felt confident using HubSpot again.

Please schedule a call at a time that suits you best. https://cal.com/uttamm/30-min-meeting-profoundly 

Best,
Uttam M.
HubSpot Certified Solution Partner
