job_title: Hubspot Refactor
job_description:
We Use Hubspot for a large percentage of our company operations. Our Sales, Marketing, CS and Support Teams all use it for their day-to-day. We've been on the tool for years, and it's gotten very unwieldy with out of date properties, unconnected data, etc.

We're looking for a partner to A. Map out our current instance in a way that helps us decide where to go next, and B. Then will help us refactor the instance to empower our teams to do their best work.
proposal_description:
Hello,

I am writing to propose a partnership with DataOps Group to map, audit, and refactor your HubSpot instance.

Your description of a system that has become "unwieldy" with "outdated properties" and "unconnected data" is a challenge I encounter often. When a HubSpot portal grows organically over years without strict governance, it often transforms from a productivity tool into a source of friction.

As the founder of DataOps Group and a HubSpot Certified Consultant (and winner of the "Inbound 2023 Rising Star" award), I specialize in exactly this type of remediation. My focus is helping organizations like yours—where Sales, Marketing, CS, and Support all rely on one system—to scale their operations with data-driven precision.

Here is how I propose we address your two core objectives, utilizing my established methodology.

Part A: Mapping Your Current Instance
Objective: Create a clear visualization of your data landscape to inform strategic decisions. You cannot fix what you do not know is broken. To decide "where to go next," we must first establish a comprehensive audit trail of your current data reality.
  • Data Lineage Audit: We will map the "lineage" of your data—tracking exactly where data enters your system (web forms, third-party integrations, manual entry) and how it flows through your Sales, Marketing, and CS pipelines. This ensures we identify not just where the data is messy, but why it is getting messy.
  • Bottleneck Identification: We will visualize the flow of data between your departments to identify "silos" where data is getting stuck or lost. This includes mapping out your current "minimally viable" stack and identifying where manual workarounds are causing delays.
  • Property & Field Audit: We will audit your property list to distinguish between "explicit data" (critical business info) and outdated/unused legacy properties. This allows us to deprecate what you don't need and standardize the formatting for what you do.
Outcome: A complete "Data Flow Map" and "Health Assessment" of your portal, providing the clarity needed to make architectural decisions.

Part B: Refactoring for Team Empowerment
Objective: Rebuild the instance to serve as a reliable "Single Source of Truth." Once mapped, we will move to refactoring. My goal is to transform your HubSpot instance from a perceived pain point into an indispensable revenue driver.
  • Establishing Data Governance: We will implement strict data hygiene rules and validation processes. This ensures that the cleanup we perform is permanent, rather than a temporary fix that degrades over time.
  • Process Automation: We will replace manual workarounds with automated data routing. This ensures that when Marketing hands a lead to Sales, or Sales hands a customer to CS, the data transfer is seamless, formatted correctly, and triggers the right notifications.
  • Cross-Departmental Alignment: A major cause of "unwieldy" data is misaligned definitions. We will unify your teams by standardizing definitions for Lifecycle Stages (MQL, SQL, Opportunity) across Sales and Marketing. This ensures your Sales and Support teams are looking at the same reality as your Marketing team.

job_title: HubSpot RevOps Consultant (Enterprise)
job_description:
We’ve just moved from SugarCRM to HubSpot and purchased 10 Sales Hub Enterprise seats and 4 Marketing Hub Pro seats. We need an expert to migrate data, set up best-practice RevOps processes, and implement key integrations (Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana).

Objectives
- Migrate all SugarCRM data to HubSpot with clean mapping, dedupe, and validation.
- Build enterprise-grade automations for sales, marketing, and handoffs.
- Integrate Xero (billing), PhoneBurner (dialer/activities), and Asana (projects/tasks).
- Enable the team with permissions, playbooks, sequences, reports, and dashboards.

Scope of Work (deliverables)

1) Discovery & Design
Audit SugarCRM objects, fields, picklists, activities, attachments, and custom logic.
  • Define HubSpot data model: standard + custom objects, associations, required fields.
    Process design: Lead → MQL/SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won/Lost, pipeline stages, entry/exit criteria.
    Security model: teams, roles, permissions, record ownership rules.

2) Data Migration (SugarCRM → HubSpot)
Field mapping doc (1:1 + transformations), deduplication rules, lookup resolution.
Import activities (emails/calls/notes/meetings where supported), files, and lists.
Validation run + reconciliation report (record counts, error log, exceptions).
Delta cutover plan with blackout window and rollback.

3) Sales Hub Enterprise Setup
Pipelines (primary + any sub-pipelines), stage properties, required reasons.
Sequences, templates, snippets, playbooks, call outcomes, meeting links.
Lead scoring (behavioral + firmographic), SLAs and task queues.
Custom quotes, products, deal workflows (create tasks, set owners, update fields).
Forecasting setup, goals, and weighted pipeline definitions.

4) Marketing Hub Pro Setup
Lifecycle model, MQL definition, subscription types, double opt-in where needed.
Forms, CTAs, lists (smart/static), nurture workflows, and lead routing.
Email + landing page brand modules, compliance (GDPR/CCPA).
UTM + source tracking, campaign hierarchy.

5) Integrations
Xero: deal→invoice sync, invoice status back to HubSpot, revenue fields for reporting.
PhoneBurner: activity logging, call outcomes, contact sync, list handoff.
Asana: deal/contract approval → Asana project creation with key fields synced.
Webhooks/middleware (if needed) with retry + error handling.

6) Reporting & Enablement
Executive dashboards: pipeline health, velocity, forecast vs. quota, win/loss, campaign attribution.
Rep dashboards: tasks due, sequence performance, meetings booked, CAC/LTV basics.
Training: live sessions + Loom library; admin runbook; change log.

It's a long term engagement and you will be working with Sales, marketing & CS teams.

job_title: HubSpot Campaign Standardization, Segmentation, and Workflow Optimization
job_description:
Penn Foster is seeking an experienced HubSpot expert to help us bring order, scalability, and efficiency to our marketing operations. We currently manage multiple channels in HubSpot (Enterprise, Partner, and Inside Sales) with over 200,000 contacts—many of which are not yet properly segmented by channel. Our Salesforce integration is active, but some logic and data flow issues remain.

Our goal is to standardize campaign setup, segmentation, and workflows so that our internal teams can operate more efficiently and confidently within HubSpot.

We’re looking for a Certified Pro who can help us identify where to start, guide the cleanup and standardization process, and leave us with a system that’s consistent, scalable, and easy to maintain.

What We Need Help With
> Campaign & Workflow Standardization: Develop reusable templates for campaigns, workflows, and emails so teams can “pull off the shelf,” clone, and execute campaigns faster—with consistent naming conventions and folder structures.
> Segmentation Framework: Establish clear best practices for creating, maintaining, and cleaning up campaign segments across channels.
> Data Cleanup: Audit and organize existing records to ensure data integrity, reliable segmentation, and accurate reporting. Address issues such as incomplete or inconsistent data, duplicate records, and unclear channel attribution.
> Standard Campaign Frameworks: Build a few go-to campaign types (for example, nurture, referral, product launch, seasonal) with pre-built assets, workflows, and automation logic.
> HubSpot Workflow and Automation Optimization: Streamline lead assignment, channel attribution, and workflow logic. We currently have roughly 100 overlapping workflows and need to reduce conflicts and redundancy.
> Salesforce Integration Alignment: Ensure Salesforce ↔ HubSpot sync behavior supports the new campaign and workflow logic without creating duplicates or misaligned data.

Preferred Skills & Experience
  • > Deep experience in HubSpot Marketing Hub and Salesforce integration
  • > Proven track record in data hygiene, workflow optimization, and campaign automation
  • > Strong understanding of segmentation strategy, lead routing, and attribution modeling
  • > Ability to design repeatable campaign frameworks and templates
  • > Excellent documentation and communication skills to support internal enablement


What Success Looks Like
  • > Teams can quickly and confidently launch campaigns using standardized templates
  • > Segmentation is consistent and channel assignment is reliable
  • > Workflow automation is simplified and error-free
  • > Data is clean, complete, and accurate across HubSpot and Salesforce
  • > Clear documentation and best practices empower internal teams to self-serve
proposal_description:
Hi Penn Foster team,

5,000 weekly Reddit viewers and nearly 1,500 Trustpilot reviews. You've built serious brand reach and student trust. That makes fixing your marketing operations even more critical: we need to make sure your systems are buttoned up, tested, and documented because every workflow misfire or segmentation error costs you real leads.

I've seen this pattern before: 100 overlapping workflows, 200K unsegmented contacts, and teams afraid to launch campaigns because they don't know what will break. The issue isn't technical debt - it's organizational governance that broke down as you scaled. Don't worry, it's fixable.

Most HubSpot cleanups fail because they standardize the tech without addressing why teams created 100 workflows in the first place. I worked with one of the nation's largest education companies where we built beautiful templates, but six months later they were back to chaos because we never solved the underlying process issues.

Two questions before we go further:

What's the current business cost of this dysfunction? (Leads falling through cracks, campaigns delayed, misattributed revenue, team hours wasted on manual workarounds?)

How did you get to 100 workflows? Understanding whether this was organic growth, siloed teams, or lack of process governance will determine whether standardization alone solves this. more than likely, we have some root causes to address first.

The technical work you've outlined is common among agencies. The harder question is: will your teams actually use the standardized system, or will they revert to building workarounds because the new process doesn't fit how they actually work?

It would be best to get on a call to map out what's actually broken and whether cleanup alone fixes it. Does that sound like a good next step?

Client Overview:

Client currently uses Salesforce Sales Cloud + Pardot (Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Advanced) for marketing automation. They plan to migrate to HubSpot Marketing Hub (Enterprise/Pro) while maintaining Salesforce as the CRM system with two-way sync.


  1. Salesforce & Pardot Environment



  • Pardot Edition: MCAE Advanced
  • Salesforce objects in scope: Leads, Contacts, Accounts, Opportunities
  • Current sync: Pardot → Salesforce (~20-min delay), want real-time sync in HubSpot
  • No major custom integrations beyond standard marketing sync



  1. Marketing Assets Inventory



  • Email Templates: ~5–10 active templates
  • Forms: ~5 standard reusable forms used across ~30–35 website forms
  • Landing Pages: None in Pardot today; hosted on WordPress; want ability to build/manage landing pages in HubSpot
  • Automation Programs: ~9 nurture/drip workflows and ~44 automation rules (webinar follow-up, lead nurture, A/B testing)
  • Lists: Hundreds of static lists (retain 5 active), a few dynamic lists to be migrated
  • Content Assets: ~2,500 total (PDFs, images, gated resources); migration scope to be confirmed



  1. Data Scope & Structure



  • Contacts: ~70K active in Pardot; total HubSpot volume ~100K (including Salesforce sync)
  • New Leads: ~12–15K per year
  • Accounts: Few thousand
  • Data Quality: Generally clean; deduplication handled in Salesforce
  • Custom Fields: Mostly standard demographic/firmographic; minimal custom logic
  • Retention: 18 months of historical engagement data (emails, forms, campaigns) to be migrated if possible



  1. Integrations
     To be configured/validated in HubSpot: Salesforce, GoToWebinar, Qualified Chat, Aloware, Propensity, Google Analytics, Dataraid (HubSpot connector for reporting)
     To be deprecated or shifted: Freescale integration (moved to Salesforce)
  2. Automation & Lead Management



  • Lead Attribution: Multi-touch (First Touch = Lead Source, Last Touch = Campaign)
  • Lead Routing: Complex logic handled in Salesforce (based on email domain, geography, industry, solution type); HubSpot must trigger lead routing in Salesforce
  • Follow-up SLAs: Email immediately; call/LinkedIn task assignments
  • Scoring: Not used today; may explore in HubSpot
  • Desired Automations: Inbound nurture, webinar follow-up, API trial drips, multi-channel alerts

  • Deduplication & record merge handled in Salesforce

  • Consent Management: Opt-in checkboxes, email preferences, unsubscribe sync with Salesforce
  • Users: 5 Marketing Hub users, 2–3 Sales Hub users
  • Approval Workflows: Basic user roles, no advanced approval hierarchy



  1. Reporting & Analytics



  • Reporting Needs: Campaign ROI, lead attribution, source tracking, funnel conversion, sales-marketing performance

  1. Key Pain Points with Pardot



  • Limited automation and personalization capabilities
  • Manual campaign creation and nurture setup
  • Weak reporting and analytics
  • Desire for integrated content tools and better user experience



  1. Desired Outcomes in HubSpot



  • Easier campaign setup and automation
  • Improved lead nurturing and personalization (AI-enabled tools)
  • Centralized reporting and attribution dashboards
  • Seamless sync with Salesforce and third-party apps
  • Simplified compliance and subscription management



  1. Timeline

  • Key driver: Outreach license expires March 1
  • Pardot license valid through next year (no hard cutoff)



  1. Internal Capacity

  • No recent in-house HubSpot experience
  • Expect Bluvium + HubSpot expert to lead implementation and training



  1. Expected Deliverables

  • HubSpot–Salesforce Integration Blueprint
  • Configured HubSpot Marketing Hub (core modules, properties, compliance setup)
  • Data migration plan, mapping sheet, and validation report
  • Rebuilt marketing assets (email templates, forms, landing page framework)
  • Migrated automation workflows (~9 nurtures, 44 rules)
  • Migrated content and active dynamic lists
  • Connected integrations (GoToWebinar, Qualified Chat, Aloware, Propensity, Google Analytics, Dataraid)
  • Automated lead routing and notification workflows aligned with Salesforce logic
  • User training sessions and documentation
  • Reporting dashboards for lead attribution, source tracking, and ROI
  • UAT sign-off, go-live checklist, and post-launch support plan

  • Configure GDPR/CCPA-compliant consent management and email preference centers.
  • Automate Right-to-Forget requests (~50/month) directly in HubSpot to replace manual JIRA processes.
  • Set up user roles and permissions for 5 Marketing Hub users and 2–3 Sales Hub users, aligned with operational responsibilities.

Why Work With Me

End-to-End Implementation:

Data-Driven Growth Focus:

Scalable, Future-Ready Setup:

Engagement Model

  • Data capture + property setup
  • Deal + pipeline configuration
  • Reporting + dashboards
  • Automation to support sales motion
  • Optimization for brokerage-style workflows

Outcome:
A HubSpot CRM tailored for a brokerage business that:

  • captures the right data
  • routes leads correctly
  • supports brokers through the sales cycle
  • tracks deals + performance
  • provides visibility to leadership

Looking to get started ASAP. Pros with brokerage or similar industry experience preferred.
proposal_description:
Hi there,
Thanks for the detailed overview. This is a great use case for HubSpot, and your description clearly points to a Set up, Implementation & Optimization engagement rather than basic onboarding.

We’ve helped several brokerage-style teams configure HubSpot so leads route cleanly to individual reps, brokers are supported throughout the sales cycle, and leadership gets real visibility into performance. Your goals around routing, pipelines, automation, and reporting are exactly where a thoughtful Sales Hub implementation makes the biggest impact. 

How we’d approach this

We’d recommend structuring this as a focused Sales Hub implementation tailored to a brokerage sales motion, covering:

  • CRM & data model setup
    Defining the right contact, company, and deal properties to capture buyer, seller, and asset-specific details without overcomplicating the system.
  • Lead routing & broker assignment
    Designing routing logic for web and event leads based on rules like broker assignment, territory, asset type, or other criteria that matter to your business.
  • Deal pipelines & sales process
    Configuring pipelines and stages that reflect how brokerage deals actually move, not a generic SaaS funnel.
  • Automation to support brokers
    Notifications, task creation, stage-based automation, and guardrails that help brokers follow a consistent process without adding friction.
  • Reporting & dashboards
    Dashboards for leadership visibility into deal flow, broker performance, and pipeline health, plus practical views for brokers themselves.

This keeps the initial scope tight, usable, and scalable, with room to layer on additional optimization later.

Budget & timeline fit

Based on what you’ve outlined, your $5,000–$15,000 budget is realistic for this phase of work. Most brokerage-style Sales Hub implementations land comfortably in that range when focused on setup, implementation, and core optimization rather than advanced marketing or custom development.

We typically recommend a half up-front project structure, with the remainder billed as milestones are completed. From a delivery standpoint, we’d expect roughly 4–6 weeks depending on routing complexity and feedback cycles, with work scheduled during standard business hours.

A few clarifying questions

To make sure we scope this correctly, we’d want to confirm a few things early:

  • How should leads be assigned to brokers (round-robin, territory, asset type, price range, exceptions)?
  • Do brokers need full record ownership, shared visibility, or both?
  • Are website and event leads already connected to HubSpot?
  • Will you be using one primary pipeline or multiple pipelines?
  • What metrics does leadership want to see on day one?

(You can answer below in t he comments for ease)

What usually comes next

Many brokerage teams start with this implementation and then move into:
  • Ongoing CRM optimization
  • Advanced reporting and forecasting
  • Sales enablement and broker training
  • Marketing or nurture automation once the foundation is solid

There’s no pressure to do that upfront, but it’s a natural next step once the system is in place and being used.

If this sounds aligned, the best next step is a short discovery call so we can confirm routing logic, reporting needs, and timeline before finalizing scope.

You can schedule time here: https://www.eternalworks.com/meetings/timjones/discovery-call

You can also review our HubSpot certifications and client feedback on our Solutions Partner profile: https://ecosystem.hubspot.com/marketplace/solutions/eternal-works

Looking forward to learning more about your brokerage model and seeing if we’re the right fit.

job_title: HubSpot Optimization for Data Integrity, Sales Process, and Reporting
job_description:
Project Overview 
We are seeking a HubSpot expert to help us restructure and optimize our current setup. The project centers on improving data consistency, enhancing reporting capabilities, and enforcing standardized input at key stages in our sales process. 
This will be a defined project engagement, with the potential for follow-on phases. Our top priorities are data structure, property and workflow improvements, and reporting dashboards. 
Project Scope

·       Audit and adjust existing deal, contact, and company properties 
·       Rename or restructure existing picklists (e.g. Business Line) 
·       Create new properties to support reporting and process needs, including: 
o   Date of Initial Contact 
o   Type of Inquiry (Formal RFP / Informal) 
o   Deal Source (Campaign, Website, Referral, Conference, etc.) 
o   Business Line (New picklist: MCG Power Ops, Paragon, Versify, InnoTap, Unco) 
o   Product Pick List (Grouped under Business Line) 
o   Solution Configuration (Single or bundled products; identify which) 
o   Quality of Opportunity (Scored 1–5 based on defined factors) 
 2. Enforcing Data Capture 
·       Configure field-level requirements to ensure key data is captured when a deal is created or modified 
·       Add guidance to ensure consistency across teams 
·       Optionally introduce workflows or validation logic to guide users toward complete entries 
3. Reporting & Dashboards 
We want to eliminate our dependence on Excel by building robust, filterable reports inside HubSpot. Reporting goals include: 
·       Lead activity: Formal/informal RFPs over trailing 12 months 
·       Sales velocity: Time from inquiry → quote → close 
·       Pipeline analysis: Proposals made, prospects qualified, contracts closed 
·       Upsell tracking: Quoted vs Won 
·       Product-level and business line performance 
·       Customer segmentation (e.g. geography, industry, solution type) 
4. Internal Enablement 
We want our internal team to understand what’s been implemented and how to work with it. This includes: 
·       Walkthroughs of the new data structure and reports 
·       Guidance for our finance lead (Andy) and commercial lead (Dave) to become more self-sufficient 
·       Documentation to help us maintain the system long-term 
5. Optional Phase Two (Future) 
Depending on the outcome of this initial project, we may explore: 
·       Integrating quote and invoicing workflows 
·       Manual or automated handoff to QuickBooks Desktop (note: we are not using QuickBooks Online) 
·       Deeper analytics or customized dashboards 
Pricing 
We would prefer a fix price proposal. 
Timeline 
We are ready to begin immediately and expect to review proposals and select a Pro by Monday, July 22. Our aim is to complete the core project within 4–6 weeks. Timing for any optional follow-on work will be addressed separately. 
Our HubSpot Setup 
·       In use since: 2020 
·       Primary Hubs: Sales (recently added Marketing Hub) 
·       User Base: ~20 active users 
·       Current Limitations: 
o   Manual deal creation (no automation from forms) 
o   Inconsistent use of properties 
o   Reporting reliant on Excel 
·       Desired Outcome: Structured deal input, real-time dashboards, scalable data model 
What Success Looks Like 
·       A clean, consistent data structure that supports our sales and reporting needs 
·       Native HubSpot reports that replace Excel for pipeline, lead, and upsell tracking 
·       Improved internal knowledge so we can build additional reports or workflows ourselves 
Clear documentation and a system we can own confidently going forward

job_description:
We’ve just moved from SugarCRM to HubSpot and purchased 10 Sales Hub Enterprise seats and 4 Marketing Hub Pro seats. We need an expert to migrate data, set up best-practice RevOps processes, and implement key integrations (Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana).

Objectives
- Migrate all SugarCRM data to HubSpot with clean mapping, dedupe, and validation.
- Build enterprise-grade automations for sales, marketing, and handoffs.
- Integrate Xero (billing), PhoneBurner (dialer/activities), and Asana (projects/tasks).
- Enable the team with permissions, playbooks, sequences, reports, and dashboards.

Scope of Work (deliverables)

1) Discovery & Design
Audit SugarCRM objects, fields, picklists, activities, attachments, and custom logic.
  • Define HubSpot data model: standard + custom objects, associations, required fields.
    Process design: Lead → MQL/SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won/Lost, pipeline stages, entry/exit criteria.
    Security model: teams, roles, permissions, record ownership rules.

2) Data Migration (SugarCRM → HubSpot)
Field mapping doc (1:1 + transformations), deduplication rules, lookup resolution.
Import activities (emails/calls/notes/meetings where supported), files, and lists.
Validation run + reconciliation report (record counts, error log, exceptions).
Delta cutover plan with blackout window and rollback.

3) Sales Hub Enterprise Setup
Pipelines (primary + any sub-pipelines), stage properties, required reasons.
Sequences, templates, snippets, playbooks, call outcomes, meeting links.
Lead scoring (behavioral + firmographic), SLAs and task queues.
Custom quotes, products, deal workflows (create tasks, set owners, update fields).
Forecasting setup, goals, and weighted pipeline definitions.

4) Marketing Hub Pro Setup
Lifecycle model, MQL definition, subscription types, double opt-in where needed.
Forms, CTAs, lists (smart/static), nurture workflows, and lead routing.
Email + landing page brand modules, compliance (GDPR/CCPA).
UTM + source tracking, campaign hierarchy.

5) Integrations
Xero: deal→invoice sync, invoice status back to HubSpot, revenue fields for reporting.
PhoneBurner: activity logging, call outcomes, contact sync, list handoff.
Asana: deal/contract approval → Asana project creation with key fields synced.
Webhooks/middleware (if needed) with retry + error handling.

6) Reporting & Enablement
Executive dashboards: pipeline health, velocity, forecast vs. quota, win/loss, campaign attribution.
Rep dashboards: tasks due, sequence performance, meetings booked, CAC/LTV basics.
Training: live sessions + Loom library; admin runbook; change log.

It's a long term engagement and you will be working with Sales, marketing & CS teams.

job_title: HubSpot Expert Needed to Audit, Restructure, and Optimize Sales & Marketing Hubs
job_description:
Wavicle, a data and analytics consultancy, is looking for a HubSpot expert to help us clean up and reconfigure our CRM, improve usability and adoption, and align the platform with our current business processes across sales and marketing with focus on clean reporting to leadership to be utilized in business-level decision making.

We’re on Sales Hub Enterprise and Marketing Hub Pro, but our implementation was DIY, and key HubSpot focused staff have recently left. Adoption is poor, reporting isn’t actionable, and the CRM no longer reflects how we work.

Key challenges include:
• Lack of clearly defined sales and marketing processes inside HubSpot
• Over-customization causing confusion and poor UX
• Manual data entry and Excel reliance
• Reports that don’t drive decision-making
• Disconnected marketing and sales efforts—no clear ownership of the customer journey
• Onboarding materials are ineffective and outdated
• Martech/salestech stack bloated and underused

Short-term goals:
• Audit existing CRM setup and tech stack
• Recommend and implement a streamlined structure
• Improve adoption and usability for the sales and marketing teams
• Fix broken workflows, integrations (including ZoomInfo, Sales Navigator, etc), and reporting

Long-term goals:
• Hubspot becomes the centralized single place of truth for Marketing, Presales, Sales (and Partner Sales), Finance (Forecasting), Staffing (from Forecast), and Customer Management and Expansion
• Strong onboarding for new hires and backfills to reduce risk from attrition

Budget & Engagement:
We’re looking for a HubSpot Solutions Partner with strong expertise who can deliver strategic guidance and hands-on execution.
Desired Time Frame: We are going to start this project after the next two weeks.
proposal_description:
Good afternoon Wavicle Team! Thank you for taking some time to review my proposal, catered directly to your HubSpot needs. I want to avoid focusing solely on my experience and what I've done, and more about what that means for your challenges and goals. 

To best accomplish this I have built this proposal using the same structure as your request outline. My hope is that my 10+ years working with HubSpot, assisting many clients in a variety of industries will be self-evident throughout my proposal. Thank you again and I hope to speak with in more detail about your project.


Before we address the details of this project I wanted to lay out my vision for how this should be structured. In the past I have found success starting with a project that has clearly defined tasks and outcomes, then transferring into a retainer/hourly agreement to keep practices in place, administer HubSpot, work with team members and continue to improve the HubSpot experience.

Let's first address Wavicle's "Key Challenges"

To summarize, HubSpot is currently setup in a way that lacks standard practices, has a clunky interface (leading to lack of adoption), poor CRM architecture (leading to bad reporting and goal management), silo'd marketing and sales efforts and no resources to guide users for a better overall experience. Here is how I would directly address these issues:

  • Clearly developed reporting based on key metrics and goals
    • Having a system in place is only half the battle, being able to understand what is working and not within that system provides leaders with the ability to adapt and grow over time. Define sucess, then measure it.
  • Capturing only what is needed to succeed – how are users measured on engagement and success? How is information segmented?
    • More is not always better and in this instance, potentially destructive. Identify the key data points that must be captured, limit it to those points and ensure HubSpot is built in a way that captures those points, every time.
  • Create internals SLAs for both Marketing and Sales teams to ensure an understanding of ownership and handoffs. This understanding is established around a set a standard practices.
    • Standard practices usually focus on the basics. How to enter a new lead manually, when to create a deal, how/when to book a meeting, how to mark a lead unqualified. These are simple, yet essential activities to ensure success.

Now onto the "Short-term Goals (The Project)"

  • Review all current: worfklows, manual processes (data entry), record layouts, key property types, essential dashboards and integrations.
    • Making HubSpot easy to use is the first step to ensuring adoption and accurate data. HubSpot can always be modified, but let us first ensure it meets the most basic needs of the regular users.
    • When it comes to certain items, workflows, for example, it is essential that each workflow has a clearly defined set of actions and outcomes. Anyone tasked with working on this system in the future can then use those to easily understand the purpose behind those strategies.
  • Develop a clear task list and those responsible.
    • Transparency is key to any successful project. What are we gonna do and when are we gonna do it? This approach also ensures meaningful check-ins and meetings.

Lastly, Long-term Goals (The Hourly/Retainer)

  • The key to success in the long term is ensuring HubSpot is a source of truth. Each team leader can log into HubSpot and get an accurate picture from reviewing team specific dashboards. 
    • As data is uncovered, often times curiousity grows. What else can we see? What other info are we gathering? How can we dig deeper? These are on-going questions that generally do not have a "finish line". 
  • Ongoing training sessions ensure users are aware of system changes and updates. These trainings should be supported with updated training materials for easy reference. Create accountability within each team and let them find success.
  • Create an "onboarding-class". This class will test the ability of users to accomplish their tasks under the framework created. All users, new and old, must pass this test to access to HubSpot.

I have broken this down into 2 phases centered around short- and long-term goals. The pricing will reflect the short-term project, the long-term phase can be supported at $100/hr but is NOT reflected in the project price listed in this proposal. 

Thank you again for taking time to review this proposal. If you have any questions or would like to speak in greater detail, please let me know!

Best
Tim Hunter
Owner + Lead Consultant
Hunter-Evans

I need help from a sophisticated HubSpot CRM Admin

I’m the CRM admin at a global manufacturing company with 5 facilities — 2 in the U.S. and the rest in Europe. We’ve been in HubSpot since 2022, and our entire global sales team operates from one HubSpot instance.

Things are messy.

We’re dealing with complex, overlapping sales team data. It’s been tough to keep domains and territories clean and separate. We have missing data, unclear lead qualification, and no reliable way to know who we should market to, who’s qualified, or who owns what account. We just rolled out sales territories and it’s been a real challenge.

My role has expanded fast, and I need a real HubSpot expert to step in and help me get this right.

Here’s what I need:

  • A full audit of our HubSpot instance to show me where we’re exposed and what needs to be fixed
  • Cleanup of existing data and setup improvements
  • Long-term structure that future-proofs our system so incoming data stays clean
  • Clear account ownership rules so every sales rep knows exactly who and what they’re responsible for — no more record crossover

I’m open to working with an individual or an agency. I do not want hourly billing — I want clear project pricing with an all-in cost and time frame. I’m also open to breaking this into smaller phases to get started and build momentum.

Target budget: $8,000–$10,000 total for initial scope

Ultimately, I want to find our HubSpot person — someone who understands complex sales orgs and knows how to design a CRM that actually supports the team using it.

Let’s fix this together.
proposal_description:
• A full audit of our HubSpot instance to show me where we’re exposed and what needs to be fixed (20 hours)
  • Long-term structure that future-proofs our system so incoming data stays clean (20 hours)
HubSpot Solutions Architect/Subject Matter Expert with 17 years experience on the platform. Independent HubSpot Consulting firm since May, 2018.  My engagement with a new client begins with a conversation about the clients business. Understanding the clients business model is the key to optimum implementation of the HubSpot solution. Once I have an understanding of the client's business model, sales process and rev-op's structure I perform an in-depth Audit on the entire HubSpot account. Upon completion of the audit a detailed report is created outlining the results of the audit based on the Hubs the client has implemented. The report will contain comments on what in HubSpot is setup correctly according to the clients business processes (learned in the discovery meeting) and make recommendation on what to change or improve upon and optimize HubSpot going forward. I have completed more than 60 audits of this nature on the HubSpot platform including Marketing, Sales, Service, Operations and Content Hubs. 

  • Cleanup of existing data and setup improvements (40 hours)
Data cleanup can be accomplished utilizing HubSpot's built-in duplicate management tool which uses machine learning to identify potential duplicate contacts and companies which can be reviewed and merge duplicates or delete to ensure data accuracy. In addition, Breeze, HubSpot's AI tools can be used to enrich contact and company data by pulling information from various sources. This will help fill in missing information and improve data accuracy of both Contacts and Company data. There are also 3rd Party applications such as Insycle, Sweephy and Zuar Runner which can be tremendously productive in the data cleaning efforts.

  • Long-term structure that future-proofs our system so incoming data stays clean (20 hours)
Using the information gained in the Discovery Meeting on how leads come into HubSpot, how leads are assigned, how leads are scored and how leads are associated with sales reps and territories the most effective method to manage all of this is with the use of Workflows and Sequences. If you organization is like most every client I have worked with, your data is most likely incomplete, spotty and a mess. Sales people all work at varying levels of details which makes STANDARDIING DATA ENTRY a key priority once the Instance is cleaned up. 

+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
This task is outside the scope of this proposal and will need additional funding
  • Clear account ownership rules so every sales rep knows exactly who and what they’re responsible for — no more record crossover (80 hours)
The best way I've found to clean up account ownership is by implementing a 3rd party Territory Management solution from the HubSpot Marketplace.  These apps such as Map My Customers, Badger Maps, Mapsly, GeoMapper, Lead Mapper and others help visualize and manage sales territories, optimize routes (at one point I had 12 Servpro Franchises as clients and implemented these solutions to help route planning for all their Marketing Reps) identify new leads based on location data. These tools can help visualize leads, Deals and territories on a map, produce heatmaps based on HubSpot data. Implementing one of these tools will provide a completely different view of your HubSpot Instance and I can assure you, by adding a visualization tool to HubSpot will completely change how your Sales Teams function on a daily basis. In addition to my 17 years on the HubSpot platform I also have 20 plus years of outside sales and sales management experience working at various Silicon Valley high tech companies which provides me with a thorough understanding of the sales process which in turn enables me to streamline and optimize the HubSpot Sales Hub component.   

Thank you for the opportunity to work with your company. 
Best Regards, Michael Motyka Principal.

job_title: No More Proposals Please (HubSpot Optimization & Customer Journey Enablement)
job_description:
Phase 1: Audit & Discovery
Objective
To engage a certified HubSpot Expert or Agency Partner to audit, optimize, and evolve our HubSpot instance to fully support a unified, data-driven customer journey — from pre-lead awareness through client renewals.
The engagement will begin with a comprehensive audit and discovery phase, leading to a detailed scope of work, implementation roadmap, and pricing proposal for the recommended initiatives.
Our organization currently operates on HubSpot Professional and is considering an upgrade to Enterprise. The consultant will evaluate the potential ROI and operational impact of this upgrade and help us articulate the value to executive stakeholders.

Phase 2: Implementation & Optimization
Objective
Execute the agreed-upon improvements and optimizations identified in Phase 1 to create a cohesive, automated, and measurable customer journey within HubSpot.

Key Focus Areas
  • Lifecycle Definition & Automation – Standardize and automate stages from pre-lead through renewal with clear criteria and progression rules.
  • Data Hygiene & Enrichment – Clean, de-duplicate, and enrich contact and company data for accuracy and segmentation.
  • Lead Capture & Scoring – Centralize and streamline all forms, implement consistent routing rules, and establish a behavior- and firmographic-based scoring model.
  • Attribution & Reporting – Build dashboards that show marketing and sales performance across the funnel, including lead sources, conversion rates, deal velocity, and ROI.
  • Nurture & Sales Alignment – Develop nurture sequences, follow-up SLAs, and workflows to support faster MQL→SQL conversion and better visibility for sales.
  • Customer Success & Renewal Automation – Create workflows and alerts for onboarding, renewal, and advocacy tracking.
  • Integrations & Scalability (TBD) – Connect supporting tools (e.g., Slack, Rev.ai, Breeze AI, Finance/ERP) and prepare HubSpot for scale and Enterprise functionality.
proposal_description:
Hey there, 

Your post instantly caught my attention! It’s not every day you see someone approach HubSpot optimization with such strategic clarity. The way you’ve defined the journey from pre-lead awareness to renewals tells me you’re thinking beyond “fixing workflows” and aiming for true lifecycle alignment. That’s exactly where our team thrives. 

I lead HubSpot transformation projects at OneMetric, a HubSpot Elite Partner, where we help revenue teams evolve from fragmented tools to unified, insight-driven systems. Over the last few years, we’ve run similar “Audit → Enablement → Optimization” programs for companies using HubSpot Pro and scaling into Enterprise, where the goal wasn’t just operational cleanup, but turning the CRM into a single source of truth for marketing, sales, and success. 

Here’s how I’d approach this project: 

Phase 1: Audit & Discovery
We’ll begin with a 360° audit of your HubSpot instance — covering objects, properties, workflows, lifecycle definitions, and reporting infrastructure. Alongside that, we’ll analyze your lead flow, attribution model, and renewal data. The goal is to identify where friction exists in your funnel and how to evolve toward a unified customer journey.
Deliverables include:
• A detailed audit report with identified gaps and improvement areas
• Lifecycle stage redefinition and automation recommendations
• Data hygiene, enrichment, and segmentation strategy
• ROI impact assessment for moving to HubSpot Enterprise
• Implementation roadmap with hours, cost, and technical dependencies 

Phase 2: Implementation & Optimization
Based on the approved plan, we’ll execute the optimization roadmap — aligning your teams around a connected data structure and measurable automation engine.
This will cover:
• Lifecycle automation from MQL to Renewal
• Contact & company data cleanup with standardized enrichment logic
• Form & lead scoring model setup (behavioral + firmographic)
• Attribution dashboards and conversion visibility across the funnel
• Nurture workflows and Sales-Success handoff automation
• Renewal and advocacy automation for Customer Success 

If integrations (Slack, Rev.ai, Breeze AI, ERP/Finance) are part of the stack, we’ll plan the architecture to ensure data flow remains traceable and audit-ready.

job_title: HubSpot Optimization for Data Integrity, Sales Process, and Reporting
job_description:
Project Overview 
We are seeking a HubSpot expert to help us restructure and optimize our current setup. The project centers on improving data consistency, enhancing reporting capabilities, and enforcing standardized input at key stages in our sales process. 
This will be a defined project engagement, with the potential for follow-on phases. Our top priorities are data structure, property and workflow improvements, and reporting dashboards. 
Project Scope 
 1. Data Model & Property Enhancements 
·       Audit and adjust existing deal, contact, and company properties 
·       Rename or restructure existing picklists (e.g. Business Line) 
·       Create new properties to support reporting and process needs, including: 
o   Date of Initial Contact 
o   Type of Inquiry (Formal RFP / Informal) 
o   Deal Source (Campaign, Website, Referral, Conference, etc.) 
o   Business Line (New picklist: MCG Power Ops, Paragon, Versify, InnoTap, Unco) 
o   Product Pick List (Grouped under Business Line) 
o   Solution Configuration (Single or bundled products; identify which) 
o   Quality of Opportunity (Scored 1–5 based on defined factors) 
 2. Enforcing Data Capture 
·       Configure field-level requirements to ensure key data is captured when a deal is created or modified 
·       Add guidance to ensure consistency across teams 
·       Optionally introduce workflows or validation logic to guide users toward complete entries 
3. Reporting & Dashboards 
We want to eliminate our dependence on Excel by building robust, filterable reports inside HubSpot. Reporting goals include: 
·       Lead activity: Formal/informal RFPs over trailing 12 months 
·       Sales velocity: Time from inquiry → quote → close 
·       Pipeline analysis: Proposals made, prospects qualified, contracts closed 
·       Upsell tracking: Quoted vs Won 
·       Product-level and business line performance 
·       Customer segmentation (e.g. geography, industry, solution type) 
4. Internal Enablement 
We want our internal team to understand what’s been implemented and how to work with it. This includes: 
·       Walkthroughs of the new data structure and reports 
·       Guidance for our finance lead (Andy) and commercial lead (Dave) to become more self-sufficient 
·       Documentation to help us maintain the system long-term 
5. Optional Phase Two (Future) 
Depending on the outcome of this initial project, we may explore: 
·       Integrating quote and invoicing workflows 
·       Manual or automated handoff to QuickBooks Desktop (note: we are not using QuickBooks Online) 
·       Deeper analytics or customized dashboards 
Pricing 
We would prefer a fix price proposal. 
Timeline 
We are ready to begin immediately and expect to review proposals and select a Pro by Monday, July 22. Our aim is to complete the core project within 4–6 weeks. Timing for any optional follow-on work will be addressed separately. 
Our HubSpot Setup 
·       In use since: 2020 
·       Primary Hubs: Sales (recently added Marketing Hub) 
·       User Base: ~20 active users 
·       Current Limitations: 
o   Manual deal creation (no automation from forms) 
o   Inconsistent use of properties 
o   Reporting reliant on Excel 
·       Desired Outcome: Structured deal input, real-time dashboards, scalable data model 
What Success Looks Like 
·       A clean, consistent data structure that supports our sales and reporting needs 
·       Native HubSpot reports that replace Excel for pipeline, lead, and upsell tracking 
·       Improved internal knowledge so we can build additional reports or workflows ourselves 
Clear documentation and a system we can own confidently going forward

This project is part of our Grand Slam Offer, ensuring that every customer gets a structured, high-value HubSpot setup tailored to their business needs.

Who We Are & Our Ideal Customer Profile (ICP):

We only work with student housing operators, build-to-rent (BTR) landlords, and property management firms. Our clients often struggle with CRM adoption, fragmented sales pipelines, and inefficient lead management. This HubSpot setup will serve as a plug-and-play framework, allowing them to quickly get up and running with minimal friction, support or delay.

This isn’t just about configuring HubSpot. It’s about building a framework that makes our customers say: 
“Wow, this is exactly what we needed.” 

Key Responsibilities:

1. Build a Repeatable HubSpot Template Setup for Property Managers
  • Structure custom properties, contact lifecycles, and deal pipelines specific to property management.
  • Define pre-set dashboards and reports to track lead-to-lease conversion and revenue attribution.
  • Optimise user roles and permissions to align with property management workflows.

2. Implement Lead Capture, Sales Automation & Pipeline Optimisation
  • Configure lead forms, chatbots, and automation workflows to qualify inbound leads.
  • Build lead scoring models to prioritise high-intent prospects.
  • Automate deal stage transitions and task assignments for sales teams.

Ideal Candidate:
We’re not just looking for a HubSpot expert. We’re looking for someone who sees problems as opportunities, complexity as a challenge, and software as a tool for empowering people, not just processing data. 
 
You believe that systems should work for people, not the other way around. When you see inefficiencies, you don’t just fix them—you ask why they exist in the first place. You challenge assumptions, explore possibilities, and think beyond what HubSpot can do to what it should do for our customers. 
 
You’re comfortable in the unknown, figuring things out as you go, testing, iterating, and improving. You don’t wait for instructions—you create clarity. You ask the hard questions, challenge ideas (including your own), and focus on outcomes, not just outputs. 
 
Most importantly, you believe that technology is only as good as the people using it. That’s why you’ll work with us, not just for us. You’ll share ideas, debate the best solutions, and co-create something scalable, intuitive, and genuinely valuable.

If you have HubSpot expertise, a background in property management CRM setups, and experience with scalable customer onboarding, I would love to hear from you!

job_title: HubSpot CRM Audit, clean-up and future proofing project!
job_description:
Hey friends (this is Jason) - posting this on behalf of customer referred by HubSpot. I just got off a call with their CRM Admin and typed up the following description. She is looking for "her HubSpot partner" that she can lean on anytime they need help. Global manufacturing org. I have a proposal review meeting with her next Weds so get proposals in ASAP! I'll make sure she comes out of that meeting having booked calls with her top choices. 

Details (cleaned up by GPT):

I need help from a sophisticated HubSpot CRM Admin

I’m the CRM admin at a global manufacturing company with 5 facilities — 2 in the U.S. and the rest in Europe. We’ve been in HubSpot since 2022, and our entire global sales team operates from one HubSpot instance.

Things are messy.

We’re dealing with complex, overlapping sales team data. It’s been tough to keep domains and territories clean and separate. We have missing data, unclear lead qualification, and no reliable way to know who we should market to, who’s qualified, or who owns what account. We just rolled out sales territories and it’s been a real challenge.

My role has expanded fast, and I need a real HubSpot expert to step in and help me get this right.

Here’s what I need:

  • A full audit of our HubSpot instance to show me where we’re exposed and what needs to be fixed
  • Cleanup of existing data and setup improvements
  • Long-term structure that future-proofs our system so incoming data stays clean
  • Clear account ownership rules so every sales rep knows exactly who and what they’re responsible for — no more record crossover

I’m open to working with an individual or an agency. I do not want hourly billing — I want clear project pricing with an all-in cost and time frame. I’m also open to breaking this into smaller phases to get started and build momentum.

Target budget: $8,000–$10,000 total for initial scope

Ultimately, I want to find our HubSpot person — someone who understands complex sales orgs and knows how to design a CRM that actually supports the team using it.

Let’s fix this together.
proposal_description:
Hi, I’m a Certified HubSpot Partner based in Boston, MA. To make this audit most effective, could you help me understand a few key things first: How is your team currently assigning ownership for leads and companies? Are you using custom territory properties, round-robin workflows, or manual reassignment? Do reps have reliable, consistent views into their accounts and pipelines, or are there issues with visibility and reporting? And finally, how do you currently define a qualified lead, and where do you feel your process breaks down at the form level, during assignment, or further down the funnel?

Once I have this context, I’ll conduct a detailed audit of your HubSpot CRM setup, including how leads and accounts are routed, how territories are defined and enforced, and how data is segmented across your U.S. and European teams. This will include a deep dive into lifecycle stages, lead status logic, property usage, ownership overlaps, workflows, permission sets, and deal pipeline configurations. I’ll also assess your data structure and identify areas where inconsistent inputs or missing values are undermining reporting, sales productivity, or marketing targeting.

Based on the audit findings, I’ll create a prioritized cleanup and optimization plan. This will include removing or merging redundant properties, restructuring lifecycle logic, designing rules-based ownership workflows, and building a scalable CRM framework that supports future growth. I’ll also prepare a clear, board-ready summary that outlines what’s currently working, where the gaps are, and how we can close them to build a clean, future-proof CRM that your reps actually trust.

If you have any specific questions or additional details to share, I’m all ears. Please pick a time that works best for you using my calendar here: https://cal.com/uttamm.

Looking forward to collaborating with you!

Best,
Uttam M.

job_title: HubSpot Optimization for Data Integrity, Sales Process, and Reporting
job_description:
Project Overview 
We are seeking a HubSpot expert to help us restructure and optimize our current setup. The project centers on improving data consistency, enhancing reporting capabilities, and enforcing standardized input at key stages in our sales process. 
This will be a defined project engagement, with the potential for follow-on phases. Our top priorities are data structure, property and workflow improvements, and reporting dashboards. 
Project Scope 
 1. Data Model & Property Enhancements 
·       Audit and adjust existing deal, contact, and company properties 
·       Rename or restructure existing picklists (e.g. Business Line) 
·       Create new properties to support reporting and process needs, including: 
o   Date of Initial Contact 
o   Type of Inquiry (Formal RFP / Informal) 
o   Deal Source (Campaign, Website, Referral, Conference, etc.) 
o   Business Line (New picklist: MCG Power Ops, Paragon, Versify, InnoTap, Unco) 
o   Product Pick List (Grouped under Business Line) 
o   Solution Configuration (Single or bundled products; identify which) 
o   Quality of Opportunity (Scored 1–5 based on defined factors) 
 2. Enforcing Data Capture 
·       Configure field-level requirements to ensure key data is captured when a deal is created or modified 
·       Add guidance to ensure consistency across teams 
·       Optionally introduce workflows or validation logic to guide users toward complete entries 
3. Reporting & Dashboards 
We want to eliminate our dependence on Excel by building robust, filterable reports inside HubSpot. Reporting goals include: 
·       Lead activity: Formal/informal RFPs over trailing 12 months 
·       Sales velocity: Time from inquiry → quote → close 
·       Pipeline analysis: Proposals made, prospects qualified, contracts closed 
·       Upsell tracking: Quoted vs Won 
·       Product-level and business line performance 
·       Customer segmentation (e.g. geography, industry, solution type) 
4. Internal Enablement 
We want our internal team to understand what’s been implemented and how to work with it. This includes: 
·       Walkthroughs of the new data structure and reports 
·       Guidance for our finance lead (Andy) and commercial lead (Dave) to become more self-sufficient 
·       Documentation to help us maintain the system long-term 
5. Optional Phase Two (Future) 
Depending on the outcome of this initial project, we may explore: 
·       Integrating quote and invoicing workflows 
·       Manual or automated handoff to QuickBooks Desktop (note: we are not using QuickBooks Online) 
·       Deeper analytics or customized dashboards 
Pricing 
We would prefer a fix price proposal. 
Timeline 
We are ready to begin immediately and expect to review proposals and select a Pro by Monday, July 22. Our aim is to complete the core project within 4–6 weeks. Timing for any optional follow-on work will be addressed separately. 
Our HubSpot Setup 
·       In use since: 2020 
·       Primary Hubs: Sales (recently added Marketing Hub) 
·       User Base: ~20 active users 
·       Current Limitations: 
o   Manual deal creation (no automation from forms) 
o   Inconsistent use of properties 
o   Reporting reliant on Excel 
·       Desired Outcome: Structured deal input, real-time dashboards, scalable data model 
What Success Looks Like 
·       A clean, consistent data structure that supports our sales and reporting needs 
·       Native HubSpot reports that replace Excel for pipeline, lead, and upsell tracking 
·       Improved internal knowledge so we can build additional reports or workflows ourselves 
Clear documentation and a system we can own confidently going forward
proposal_description:
Summary 
Your sales and marketing data is the backbone of your decision-making — but right now, it’s being slowed down by inconsistencies, manual processes, and Excel workarounds. At HUSHinbound, we specialize in turning HubSpot into a single source of truth for sales, marketing, and reporting, so teams can move faster and make better decisions. 

We will restructure your HubSpot data model, enforce standardized inputs, and build robust reporting dashboards tailored to your workflows. Our approach ensures your team — from Andy in finance to Dave in commercial — has the clarity and tools needed to self-serve insights long after the project ends.  

Understanding of Needs 
You’re seeking more than a HubSpot cleanup — you want: 
  • Data integrity: A consistent and scalable structure for deals, contacts, and companies.
  • Process alignment: Enforced data capture at critical sales stages.
  • Reporting freedom: Replace Excel with dynamic, filterable HubSpot dashboards.
  • Team empowerment: Training and documentation so you can own the system moving forward.

You also value speed to delivery (4–6 weeks), a fixed price, and the flexibility for future enhancements like QuickBooks Desktop integration and deeper analytics.


Proposed Solution

Phase 1 – Core Project (4–6 weeks)

1. Data Model & Property Enhancements
 
  • Audit all current deal, contact, and company properties.
  • Standardize and rename picklists (e.g., Business Line, Product Pick List).
  • Add key properties to improve tracking: Date of Initial Contact, Type of Inquiry, Deal Source, Solution Configuration, Quality of Opportunity, etc.
  • Group products under the appropriate business line for clearer reporting.

2. Enforcing Data Capture
 
  • Configure field-level requirements for deal creation/modification.
  • Add inline property descriptions for user guidance.
  • Optionally implement workflow logic to prompt users for missing data.

3. Reporting & Dashboards
 
  • Build native HubSpot dashboards for:
    • Lead activity by type (Formal vs. Informal RFPs).
    • Sales velocity from inquiry → quote → close.
    • Pipeline analysis: proposals, qualified prospects, contracts closed.
    • Upsell tracking: quoted vs. won.
    • Product-level and business line performance.
    • Customer segmentation by geography, industry, or solution type.

4. Internal Enablement
 
  • Live walkthroughs of the new data structure and dashboards.
  • Role-specific guidance for Andy (finance) and Dave (commercial).
  • Written documentation covering structure, workflows, and reporting tips.

 
Phase 2 – Optional Future Work
 
  • Quote-to-invoice workflow design.
  • Manual/automated QuickBooks Desktop integration.
  • Advanced analytics and custom dashboard enhancements.

 
Why HUSHinbound 
  • HubSpot Platinum Partner with deep CRM optimization experience.
  • Proven record of replacing Excel reporting with HubSpot-native solutions.
  • 5 star rated on HubSpot Directory.
  • Structured, documented approach for lasting results.

job_title: No More Proposals Please (HubSpot Optimization & Customer Journey Enablement)
job_description:
Phase 1: Audit & Discovery
Objective
To engage a certified HubSpot Expert or Agency Partner to audit, optimize, and evolve our HubSpot instance to fully support a unified, data-driven customer journey — from pre-lead awareness through client renewals.
The engagement will begin with a comprehensive audit and discovery phase, leading to a detailed scope of work, implementation roadmap, and pricing proposal for the recommended initiatives.
Our organization currently operates on HubSpot Professional and is considering an upgrade to Enterprise. The consultant will evaluate the potential ROI and operational impact of this upgrade and help us articulate the value to executive stakeholders.

Phase 2: Implementation & Optimization
Objective
Execute the agreed-upon improvements and optimizations identified in Phase 1 to create a cohesive, automated, and measurable customer journey within HubSpot.

Key Focus Areas
  • Lifecycle Definition & Automation – Standardize and automate stages from pre-lead through renewal with clear criteria and progression rules.
  • Data Hygiene & Enrichment – Clean, de-duplicate, and enrich contact and company data for accuracy and segmentation.
  • Lead Capture & Scoring – Centralize and streamline all forms, implement consistent routing rules, and establish a behavior- and firmographic-based scoring model.
  • Attribution & Reporting – Build dashboards that show marketing and sales performance across the funnel, including lead sources, conversion rates, deal velocity, and ROI.
  • Nurture & Sales Alignment – Develop nurture sequences, follow-up SLAs, and workflows to support faster MQL→SQL conversion and better visibility for sales.
  • Customer Success & Renewal Automation – Create workflows and alerts for onboarding, renewal, and advocacy tracking.
  • Integrations & Scalability (TBD) – Connect supporting tools (e.g., Slack, Rev.ai, Breeze AI, Finance/ERP) and prepare HubSpot for scale and Enterprise functionality.
proposal_description:
Everyone reading this is going to be licking their lips at your budget and focus areas. They're thinking like vendors, not partners. 

You're considering going to enterprise which means things are going well in the business right now or we've just raised money. In either case, we need to look at your company wholistically to ensure that the you are getting the most bang for your buck at the current HubSpot tier. HubSpot will always take your money and upgrade you to enterprise, but 
  1. The best deals are at the end of the quarter
  2. It's easy to upgrade and unlock new features, it's not easy to get deep team adoption. 

This project is our sweet spot. So much so that we have a page dedicated to the assessment we do with our partners. In that assessment, we'll determine exactly what you pointed out: is now the right time to upgrade? Are our processes ready for enterprise growth?

The assessment is 2 weeks and carries an upfront, strategy-only investment of $10,000.

My name is Grant Hushek (LinkedIn) - this is my company and I will personally be your process consultant to make sure we get the right answers for you now, in 12 months, and in 12 years if we're ready to start thinking that far in advance. 

Looking forward to learning more, and hope we can get a call on the calendar soon.

job_title: Restore Email, HubSpot Optimization & Team Enablement
job_description:
HubSpot Re-Implementation & Enablement (CREDE)

Goal: Fix email deliverability and re-start marketing campaigns; simplify and standardize our HubSpot CRM for adoption (including an acquired team); and enable reliable forecasting and attribution—then train our team to sustain it.
Background & Pain Points
  • Marketing emails paused after poor sender reputation (“HubSpot jail”): high bounces, list quality issues.
  • CRM is bloated (deal cards/fields, processes), causing low adoption—especially on the newly acquired team.
  • Inconsistent data capture from conferences/meetings; weak source attribution; limited visibility for forecasting.
  • Former in-house HubSpot owner left; we need an embedded expert/agency to steady the system and upskill us.
What We’re Looking For (Scope)
  1. Audit & Action Plan (phase 0): Quick technical/operational audit with a prioritized roadmap and effort estimate.
  2. Email Deliverability Recovery: Repair domain reputation (e.g., alias domains + warming tool), DNS auth (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), list health, segmentation, and sending best practices—so monthly nurture resumes.
  3. CRM Simplification & Governance: Streamline pipelines and deal cards (reduce required fields), standardize contact/account fields, list-upload rules, and create admin guardrails.
  4. Adoption & Training: Role-based training (BD, marketing, “doer-seller” teams), playbooks, and short Looms; show-and-do enablement.
  5. Attribution & Activity Capture: Improve meeting/note capture (Fathom already used; open to lightweight voice/capture tools), conference lead workflows, and reliable source attribution.
  6. Reporting & Forecasting: Clean dashboards for pipeline health and stage-based probability forecasting; executive roll-ups.
  7. Documentation & Handoff: Admin guide, governance doc, field dictionary, and 30/60-day operating checklist.
Deliverables
  • Audit + remediation plan; DNS/auth + warming implemented; cleaned/segmented send lists.
  • Simplified pipelines/deal cards and governed list-upload process.
  • Adoption toolkit: playbooks, role trainings, and recorded how-tos.
  • Standard attribution model + dashboards for pipeline/forecast accuracy.
  • Admin documentation and ongoing recommendations.
Success Looks Like
  • Marketing domain warmed and campaigns relaunching with healthy metrics.
  • BD and acquired team actively using HubSpot (measurable weekly activity uplift).
  • Fewer fields, faster data entry, cleaner data; clear visibility into pipeline and forecast.
  • Team can run the playbook without external help.
Engagement Model
  • Start ASAP with a paid audit, then execute in phases (email → CRM simplification → adoption/reporting).
  • Open to a vetted solo consultant or small agency; we value hands-on execution + teach-us-to-fish enablement.
  • Indicative budget: $5k–$15k depending on depth; begin with the audit and expand based on outcomes.
Key Tools/Context
  • HubSpot (Marketing & Sales), Fathom for meeting notes; considering simple voice/capture for in-person meetings.
  • Must keep all comms/activities tracked in HubSpot.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

Your post really stood out — it’s clear you have a strong vision for where you want your HubSpot system to be, and you’re looking for a partner who can combine strategy with hands-on execution. That’s exactly what we do at Evenbound.

We’ve helped many B2B teams re-implement HubSpot after incomplete or messy setups, addressing challenges like yours: email deliverability issues, CRM complexity, low team adoption, and unclear attribution. Our approach starts with a focused audit and roadmap (your “Phase 0”), followed by a structured rollout that rebuilds your HubSpot for scalability and sustainability.

Here’s what we’d bring to your project:

  • Email deliverability recovery: Repairing domain reputation, implementing SPF/DKIM/DMARC, cleaning lists, and re-establishing healthy send patterns.
  • CRM simplification: Streamlined pipelines, standardized data capture, and governance guardrails for long-term adoption.
  • Attribution and forecasting: Configuring clean dashboards and accurate reporting for leadership visibility.
  • Enablement: Role-based training, playbooks, and Loom videos so your team can confidently maintain everything after launch.

If you’re ready to stabilize and relaunch your HubSpot systems confidently, I’d love to discuss your goals and share how our team could support each phase — from audit to adoption. You can easily book a discovery call with me here:

 👉 Book a Discovery Call

Looking forward to connecting and helping your team build a HubSpot system that genuinely works for you.

Cheers,

Stephen

job_title: HubSpot RevOps Consultant (Enterprise)
job_description:
We’ve just moved from SugarCRM to HubSpot and purchased 10 Sales Hub Enterprise seats and 4 Marketing Hub Pro seats. We need an expert to migrate data, set up best-practice RevOps processes, and implement key integrations (Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana).

Objectives
- Migrate all SugarCRM data to HubSpot with clean mapping, dedupe, and validation.
- Build enterprise-grade automations for sales, marketing, and handoffs.
- Integrate Xero (billing), PhoneBurner (dialer/activities), and Asana (projects/tasks).
- Enable the team with permissions, playbooks, sequences, reports, and dashboards.

Scope of Work (deliverables)

1) Discovery & Design
Audit SugarCRM objects, fields, picklists, activities, attachments, and custom logic.
  • Define HubSpot data model: standard + custom objects, associations, required fields.
    Process design: Lead → MQL/SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won/Lost, pipeline stages, entry/exit criteria.
    Security model: teams, roles, permissions, record ownership rules.

2) Data Migration (SugarCRM → HubSpot)
Field mapping doc (1:1 + transformations), deduplication rules, lookup resolution.
Import activities (emails/calls/notes/meetings where supported), files, and lists.
Validation run + reconciliation report (record counts, error log, exceptions).
Delta cutover plan with blackout window and rollback.

3) Sales Hub Enterprise Setup
Pipelines (primary + any sub-pipelines), stage properties, required reasons.
Sequences, templates, snippets, playbooks, call outcomes, meeting links.
Lead scoring (behavioral + firmographic), SLAs and task queues.
Custom quotes, products, deal workflows (create tasks, set owners, update fields).
Forecasting setup, goals, and weighted pipeline definitions.

4) Marketing Hub Pro Setup
Lifecycle model, MQL definition, subscription types, double opt-in where needed.
Forms, CTAs, lists (smart/static), nurture workflows, and lead routing.
Email + landing page brand modules, compliance (GDPR/CCPA).
UTM + source tracking, campaign hierarchy.

5) Integrations
Xero: deal→invoice sync, invoice status back to HubSpot, revenue fields for reporting.
PhoneBurner: activity logging, call outcomes, contact sync, list handoff.
Asana: deal/contract approval → Asana project creation with key fields synced.
Webhooks/middleware (if needed) with retry + error handling.

6) Reporting & Enablement
Executive dashboards: pipeline health, velocity, forecast vs. quota, win/loss, campaign attribution.
Rep dashboards: tasks due, sequence performance, meetings booked, CAC/LTV basics.
Training: live sessions + Loom library; admin runbook; change log.

It's a long term engagement and you will be working with Sales, marketing & CS teams.
proposal_description:
I need to be direct with you: the scope you've outlined is comprehensive and technically sophisticated – but it's also exactly what every other HubSpot partner will deliver. Where you'll succeed or fail has nothing to do with field mapping or integration middleware (unless you choose someone inept which I don't get the feeling you'll do based on this write up).

Let me explain why.

I recently worked with a legal marketing agency that came to us with almost identical language: "We're moving from Pipedrive to HubSpot, need help with migration and setup." They wanted clean data, proper automations, integrations – all the technical deliverables you've listed + simple, effective reporting for better decision making.

Deals were tracked in spreadsheets. The founder couldn't coach because reps bypassed the procedures. The founder was trapped in the same oversight loop he'd been trying to escape.

What we did differently: We started by asking what they actually wanted to achieve. Not "a working HubSpot instance" – but the business outcome. They wanted to scale from 3 to 8+ sales reps with confidence that new hires would succeed. That required metrics, not migrations. Visibility, not integrations. A system that made it frictionless for their team to do the right thing.

Eight months later: quarter over quarter sales at record highs, team scaled to 8+ reps, show-up rates measurably improved, and the founder completely removed from day-to-day sales operations. He's actually hiring a CRO and CMO now.

Before we discuss your scope further, I need to understand a few things:

1. What business outcome is driving this move from SugarCRM to HubSpot? (I'm not asking "why HubSpot" – I'm asking what changes in your P&L when this is done right. Faster sales cycles? Better forecast accuracy? More revenue per rep? Something else?)

2. What happened with SugarCRM that led to this decision? (This tells me what we absolutely cannot repeat. Was it an adoption problem? A functionality gap? A vendor relationship issue? Understanding the failure pattern is critical.)

3. You've got Sales, Marketing, and CS teams who all need to embrace new workflows. How are you planning to ensure they actually adopt the new system vs. defaulting back to spreadsheets, Slack updates, and their old habits when things get busy?

4. When you picture "success" 6 months post-launch, what does that look like? Is it "HubSpot is configured correctly" or "we can see exactly where deals are stalling and our close rate has improved by X%"?

5. If we execute everything in your scope perfectly – pristine data migration, flawless integrations, beautiful dashboards – but 6 months from now only half your team is using it consistently, what does that cost you? (Lost deals, forecast chaos, manager frustration, the 12-18 months you just burned before trying again.)

I'm asking these questions because every other proposal you'll receive will focus on your deliverables list. They'll quote the technical work, promise timelines, maybe showcase certifications. What they won't tell you is that the technical work is the easy part.

Where RevOps transformations fail – and I've seen this repeatedly at enterprise scale – is in three places:
1. Misalignment on outcomes (you think you're buying "a working HubSpot," they think they're building "faster deal velocity")
2. Change management underinvestment (80% of effort on setup, 20% on adoption – should be reversed)
3. No measurement framework (you can't tell if it's working until it's too late to course-correct)

Here's what I'd suggest as a next step:

A 30-minute call where we:
- Map your current revenue constraints (what's actually limiting growth today)
- Pressure-test your scope against your business outcomes (is this the right sequence of work?)
- Determine if you have the organizational readiness for this level of transformation
- Identify where adoption will break down if we don't plan for it explicitly

Let's make sure you're set up beyond go-live, no matter who you pick

Two other things worth mentioning:

On the monthly retainer: You're smart to structure this as ongoing vs. one-and-done. Systems like this need to evolve as your business does. The companies that win with RevOps treat it like infrastructure, not a project. We've been embedded with clients for over a year specifically because this approach works.

On the ASAP timeline: I understand urgency, but I've also seen enterprises rush implementations and create more chaos than they solve. Speed is good. Speed with the wrong foundation is expensive. Let's make sure we're optimizing for "time to business impact," not "time to technical go-live."

Are you available this week or next for that diagnostic conversation? Here are my calendar options.

- Grant Hushek, Founder @ Grantbot

Hi there! 

This sounds like a huge step forward for you, congratulations on the decision. 

That being said, the next few months will be a bit crazy. Let's get you up and running. 

My name is Justin, I founded RevOps Hub to help small companies get their Sales, Marketing and Finance Operations up and running, we've done a number of migrations recently ranging from 30-100 people orgs. We're based in Hampshire and have people in South Africa, the US and India who can cover a range of needs. 

It sounds like you've got a very clear picture of what HubSpot can do and what you need set up, which is fantastic. I'd love to speak to you in more detail about your needs and give you a feel for the timelines and costs involved. 

Typically the timelines would be:

Pre-Project: Agree on scope of project, timelines, cost, and key stakeholders

Weeks 1-2 : Scoping of requirements:
  • Objects & Fields required
  • Process definition for MQL & SQL
  • Define Lead Scoring criteria (though this typically iterates throughout the project)
  • Define Sales Process & Handoff to CS

Weeks 2-3: Data Migration & Definition of roles/permissions, Set up basic Marketing bits like DNS & Website Tracking

Week 4: User Set Up:
  • Licenses
  • Permissions
  • Teams
  • Email/Calendar Connection
  • Meeting Scheduling Links

Week 5: Build out Goals/Forecasting, and start Admin Enablement

Week 6: Marketing Set Up
  • Brand kits
  • Ideal Customer Profiles
  • Company Scoring
  • Email/Landing Page Templates {Usually best if your Marketing team leads this bit, as they have all the assets & know the brand guidelines}

Week 7-8: Integrations & Report/Dashboard building


Other things to note:
  • Key processes will be documented throughout the processes
  • Processes will be built for simplicity and efficiency - we aren't interested in locking you in with technical complexity
  • The quoted price is the total project cost. 20% of the project cost will be dependant on your Customer Satisfaction at the end of the initial project.

I really hope you'll consider us as a partner for your journey, if you'd like to discuss the project further please book some time with me here.

There are some fantastic partners on Profoundly, so whoever you pick will do a great job. 

All the best
Justin
