job_title: Hubspot CPQ and Revenue Recognition
job_description:
Context:
Greenshades currently uses HubSpot as our primary CRM for marketing, sales, and pipeline management, and Microsoft Dynamics 365 for customer management, renewals, billing, and entitlements. PandaDoc is used for quotes and signatures but is not fully connected to ARR tracking or invoicing. 

Our goal is to determine whether HubSpot’s Commerce Hub can meet our CPQ and revenue recognition needs — or if an external system (e.g., PandaDoc CPQ) should be layered in.  

Current State 
  • HubSpot manages all funnel, deal, and opportunity tracking.
  • Dynamics 365 manages invoices, renewals, and customer entitlements.
  • PandaDoc used for quote generation and signatures, but ARR and bookings are not captured in HubSpot.
  • ARR calculations currently run via SQL and Excel, not within CRM.

 
Desired State
  • HubSpot becomes the single source of truth for accounts, opportunities, and ARR.
  • Automated quote-to-invoice process using HubSpot Commerce Hub or PandaDoc CPQ.
  • Product catalog, pricing, term, and discount logic managed directly within HubSpot.
  • Closed-won deals automatically sync to Finance for billing and entitlement provisioning.
  • ARR, MRR, and Bookings visible directly in HubSpot dashboards and reports.

 
What We Need
 
  1. Guidance on whether Commerce Hub can serve as our unified CPQ + revenue management tool.
  2. Prescriptive recommendations on:
    • Setting up product catalog + ARR calculations in HubSpot.
    • Integrating PandaDoc (if retained) for quoting/invoicing workflows.
    • Ensuring data sync and reporting accuracy between HubSpot and Dynamics 365.
  3. Suggested implementation partner or HubSpot resource for setup and configuration.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

I see you're working through the HubSpot/Dynamics/PandaDoc fragmentation challenge. What I'm curious about is what's driving this beyond just "cleaner data" – most companies I talk to in this situation are trying to solve something more specific around speed, accuracy, or confidence in their numbers.

The technical implementation of Commerce Hub or PandaDoc is straightforward after we do our workflow assessment. Where these projects typically succeed or fail is in the adoption side – getting Sales, Finance, and CS to actually trust and use the new system instead of reverting to their Excel backups and off-system quotes.

Two questions that would help me understand if we're the right fit:

1. What's the quantified impact of your fragmented system today? For example: hours per week on manual ARR reconciliation, average time from closed-won to invoice, quote errors that delay deals, forecast accuracy issues. Without this baseline, how will you measure if a new system actually improves the target metrics?

2. What's driving the ASAP timeline right now? Board mandate, audit finding, scaling bottleneck, or something else? Understanding the why now helps determine if we're solving the right problem first.

I'd suggest we spend 30 minutes on a quick call where I can walk you through how to quantify your current state cost, determine whether Commerce Hub or a different approach fits your actual process, and share what I've seen work (and not work) for companies in similar situations.

No pitch, just exploration. You can decide from there if our approach aligns with what you need.

Does a call sound like the right next step?

Best,
Grant Hushek, Founder @ Grantbot
HubSpot Gold Partners

job_title: HubSpot Implementation Expert – Consolidated Client Communications
job_description:
Living With Lolo is a fast-growing, award-winning luxury interior design and construction firm based in Scottsdale, AZ. We’re in the middle of a big internal upgrade: creating a consolidated client communications hub so every client has one email address, one phone number, and a consistently white-glove experience across design and construction.

We’re looking for an experienced HubSpot expert to lead the implementation of this communications hub and turn our detailed business requirements into a real, live system our team loves using.

About the project

The goal of this project is to create a single operational “home” for all client-facing communication using HubSpot as the primary interface.

Phase 1 of this initiative is focused on client communications only. All email, SMS, voicemail, and internal notes for active projects will live in HubSpot, be routed to the correct point of contact, and be fully visible to leadership and the broader team. Gmail remains our enterprise email backbone; HubSpot becomes the day-to-day workspace for communication.

LWL Consolidated Communications

You will be collaborating closely with our CEO, Director of Operations, Director of Construction, and key team members across design and construction. Our team is remote-friendly, fast-paced, and human. We care a lot about how things feel to our clients and to our team, not just whether they technically “work.”

What we want you to build (at a high level)

Centralized email and SMS for clients, using one shared email address and one shared phone number that all communication flows through, while still honoring Gmail as the underlying email platform.

Shared inbox and routing in HubSpot so every inbound message (email, text, voicemail transcript) is automatically associated with the correct client, project, and project phase, and routed to that project’s primary point of contact (POC), with clear backup and escalation rules.

Configurable SLAs, business hours, and escalation so we can confidently say “we respond within one business day,” with timers that pause outside business hours and nudges that escalate from primary to backup to leadership when something sits too long.

Dashboards and visibility so any team member can open HubSpot and instantly see: open conversations by project, by POC, and by status; unmatched communications that need to be linked to a client/project; and escalated items that need leadership attention.

Internal notes and @-style collaboration replacing scattered Google Chat threads, so discussions, decisions, and “behind the scenes” commentary live with the client communication thread but are never visible to clients.

Unmatched communication handling so any message from a new email or phone number lands in an “unmatched” queue where it can be triaged, linked to an existing client/project, or dismissed as spam, with future messages automatically associated correctly.

Telephone integration using a solution like OpenPhone, Aircall, or similar, including voicemail routing, voicemail transcription, and SMS, aligned with HubSpot and our routing logic. We expect your help selecting and integrating the right platform for our volume and use case.

Clean, scalable data structures that respect a unique Project ID across tools (HubSpot, Airtable, QuickBooks, and future systems), and keep us “AI-ready” for future phases like AI search, summarization, and sentiment detection.

How we’ll work together:
We already have a detailed, 40+ page Business Requirements Document that outlines business objectives, functional and non-functional requirements, user scenarios, routing and escalation logic, governance, and a proposed 6-phase rollout (Discovery, Configuration, Integration & Testing, UAT, Training & Deployment, Stabilization & Handover). Your first job is to pressure-test, refine, and then implement against that document.

You will:
  • Lead or co-lead discovery and configuration planning, validating assumptions and calling out any gaps, risks, or trade-offs.
  • Design the HubSpot configuration (objects, properties, shared inboxes, pipelines or equivalent, workflows, routing and escalation, user roles, dashboards) to match the requirements and our real-life workflow.
  • Own or guide integration between HubSpot and Gmail, Airtable, and the chosen telephone platform, using native integrations and/or middleware like Zapier.
  • Set up SLA and escalation logic based on business hours, including vacation/OOO handling for POCs, with clear auditability.
  • Build usable, clean dashboards for different roles (POCs, Ops, Construction, Leadership) that highlight what actually matters: open conversations, unresolved items, escalations, and unmatched messages.
  • Create a practical UAT plan, guide our team through testing using realistic scenarios, and iterate based on their feedback.
  • Deliver clear documentation and lightweight training so non-technical, very busy team members can quickly feel confident using the system day-to-day.
  • Be a thought partner, not just a ticket-taker: propose smarter ways to do things when you see them, but respect the business constraints and non-negotiables already documented.


Who you are
You have deep, hands-on HubSpot implementation experience, ideally including complex shared inbox setups, multi-channel routing (email + SMS + voicemail), SLAs, and escalation logic in service or operations contexts.
  • You’ve integrated HubSpot with Gmail and at least one telephone platform (OpenPhone, Aircall, or comparable), and you understand the nuances of voicemail transcription, SMS deliverability, and routing in a service-oriented environment.
  • You’re comfortable designing and implementing integrations between HubSpot and tools like Airtable or similar databases using Zapier, n8n, or native connectors, and you think in terms of data models, not one-off zaps.
  • You communicate clearly, calmly, and with high emotional intelligence. You know how to explain technical trade-offs in plain language, draw out stakeholders’ real needs, and keep people feeling informed and respected, even when you have to push back or say “no” to something.
  • You enjoy training and change management. You’re patient, you anticipate where people will get confused, and you design interfaces and processes that feel intuitive, not overwhelming.
  • You are organized and reliable. You’re comfortable with a governance structure that includes change requests, documented assumptions, and explicit sign-offs. You keep good records and don’t “configure and disappear.”
  • You appreciate brands that care about experience. The idea of supporting a luxury, high-touch design firm appeals to you, and you understand that the way a system feels to clients and staff matters just as much as the underlying logic.


Nice to have (but not required):
Experience with construction or home services firms, CRMs for project-based businesses, or high-touch professional services.
  • Experience designing systems with future AI capabilities in mind (AI search, summarization, sentiment/urgency detection), even if you’re not implementing AI in this first phase.
  • Understanding of compliance considerations around email/SMS communication (CAN-SPAM, TCPA, etc.).


What success looks like:
Clients always use one email and one phone number, and feel genuinely cared for and clearly communicated with.
  • No client communication goes unanswered beyond the agreed SLA during business hours.
  • Our team spends their client-communication time inside HubSpot, not bouncing between Gmail, phones, and chat, and they feel that HubSpot is helping them, not adding busywork.
  • Leadership can log in at any time and see, in seconds, whether clients are being responded to, what’s open, and where escalation is happening.
  • The implementation is well-documented, stable, and ready for future enhancements, including AI-assisted search and analytics, without ripping anything apart.


How to apply:
If this sounds like the kind of project you do best, please send:
A short introduction that highlights why this project is a fit for you.
  • 2–3 examples of similar HubSpot implementations you’ve led, particularly ones involving shared inboxes, routing/escalation, and telephony/SMS.
  • A brief summary of your proposed approach to Discovery and Phase 1–3 work (Configuration, Integration & Testing), including rough timing and budget for a Time & Materials engagement with a not-to-exceed cap.
  • Any relevant certifications or partner status with HubSpot or telephone vendors.


We’re excited to meet someone who can help us bring this system to life in a way that feels as thoughtful, calm, and elevated as the spaces we design.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

Your post immediately stood out — this is precisely the kind of high-touch, system-driven implementation we specialize in at Evenbound. We’ve worked extensively with construction, design, and home services firms, helping them streamline client communication, unify sales and project workflows, and create seamless client experiences within HubSpot.

Our team has delivered similar large-scale HubSpot implementations that blend luxury client service with robust operational automation, including:

  • How Evenbound Helped a Healthcare Technology Company Unify Multi-Brand Operations With HubSpot Service Hub Enterprise
  • How Evenbound Helped MDI Eliminate Manual Work With a HubSpot + Aircall Integration
  • How Evenbound Helped Unlock Build a Scalable HubSpot System for 3x Growth

We’re proud to be among fewer than 100 HubSpot partners worldwide (out of 7,000+) to earn the HubSpot CRM Implementation Accreditation, recognizing our expertise in complex HubSpot architecture and deployment. Evenbound is also a HubSpot Diamond Solutions Partner, with multiple HubSpot Certified Trainers on staff who collectively hold over 300 individual certifications — and we’ve earned 80+ 5-star reviews on the HubSpot Solutions Partner Directory for our commitment to excellence and client experience.

We understand the importance of white-glove communication and visibility — especially when working with clients who expect consistency, responsiveness, and clarity. Our RevOps and integration teams can help you configure HubSpot as your centralized communications hub, integrating Gmail, telephony, Airtable, and future AI-ready systems with precision.

The next best step would be a discovery call, where we can review your Business Requirements Document, align on your priorities, and use that to create a detailed scope of work (SOW) with accurate pricing and realistic timelines. This ensures we tailor every part of the solution to your firm’s exact needs.

Stephen

Our current setup lacks integration and automation, resulting in manual work and unreliable forecasting.   • Mixed Sales Models: We have both ARR (annual recurring revenue for software licensing) and fixed-price sales.
  • Disconnected Systems: QuickBooks (finance), HubSpot (CRM), and manual Excel sheets for revenue tracking are not integrated, causing significant manual reconciliation.
  • Recurring Revenue Tracking: We struggle with quoting and tracking recurring revenue accurately.
Objectives:

  • Configure HubSpot to handle both ARR and fixed-price sales models.
  • Integrate HubSpot with QuickBooks and/or other tools to reduce manual reconciliation.
  • Create a reliable process for quoting and managing recurring revenue.
  • Establish a single source of truth for sales and revenue data.
Deliverables:
  • Audit of current HubSpot setup and recommendations.
  • Implementation of improved forecasting and revenue tracking processes.
  • Integration between HubSpot, QuickBooks, and revenue tracking tools.
  • Documentation and training for internal team.

job_title: HubSpot Deals Platform, to be hosted on WebPress page
job_description:
We're planning out a new idea, and wanted some help in building out a virtual marketplace for accessing special deals from vendors we work with

We want to build something very similar to this: https://ecosystem.hubspot.com/marketplace/apps

Same concept -- Apps listed in squares, you click on it, it then opens up a page with more details, and a 'contact page' instead of the install page.

1). The original page would be gated to login. Think of it like how we do content.the5thingredient.com/beer30marketplace (similar to our other Hubspot approved URLs).

Here are the user stories:

1). Current Beer30 customers can 'login for free' and see all the details. We already have in our Hubspot all of the Beer30 customer emails with an "isActive" flag... so in theory, they just need to register to get access to this page.

2). A non-Beer30 customer can sign-up to become a member.
i.e. I would want Regina / Marlise / Allison to just fill out "First / last / brewery name / email / phone / password" -> add to cart "Join Beer30 Marketplace" at $29 USD / month - credit card details. -- Sign up -- They can also cancel at any time. It'll be a recurring fee which goes through our Hubspot Payments side. (In a separate chart of account ultimately)

Once you login --

1). You can see all of the apps --> we need to be able to have an interface that allows us to easily add or remove apps.

2). Clicking on an app shows you more details about it. https://ecosystem.hubspot.com/marketplace/listing/gmail 

3). When you click on the 'reach out' it pops-up a Husbpot form that goes to us and the app supplier. We have that tracked for all data capture. (Similar to the "Sign In to Install") 

That would be the v1 side. From my quick googling around, I think that the Hubspot Knowledge Base being registered users gated could work + Cart Object Functionality to build this out? Each of the 'apps' could then be a Knowledge Base article...?

We would eb looking for someone to assist in the buildout of the web page itself, in the format described above.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

Thanks for sharing this — it’s a fascinating idea, and absolutely something we can help you design and build. At Evenbound, we’ve worked on numerous custom HubSpot experiences, and your marketplace concept is an excellent match for what HubSpot’s newer tools can support.

Based on your requirements — including gated access, member login, recurring payments, and a dynamic grid of apps — a more effective long-term approach might be to leverage HubSpot’s Content Hub with Membership functionality, paired with HubDB to power the app directory. This setup would allow you to:
  • Gate the marketplace to logged-in users (both free and paid tiers)
  • Manage apps dynamically via a HubDB table (add/remove/edit without touching code)
  • Build individual app detail pages automatically
  • Support recurring membership fees through HubSpot Payments
  • Tie form submissions and “reach out” actions directly into CRM workflows

This approach would be more scalable, more flexible, and easier to maintain than trying to force-fit a Knowledge Base or portal workaround. Your user stories fit nicely into this architecture:
  • Active Beer30 customers automatically gain free access based on their isActive flag
  • Non-customers can register, subscribe for $29/mo via HubSpot Payments, and access gated content
  • Admins can easily manage apps and suppliers in HubSpot, no custom CMS required
  • App detail pages can display dynamically, with forms routing inquiries to both your team and vendors

There are multiple ways we can execute this. Still, the right approach depends on several technical details related to your current HubSpot setup, your customer data structure, and how you want memberships to behave.

A quick discovery call would allow us to clarify those pieces and then build a precise scope of work with accurate pricing and timelines: 👉 Book a Discovery Call

Looking forward to exploring this marketplace concept with you — I think we can build something robust, clean, and very future-proof.

Stephen

We would like to improve how inbound leads are organized, associated, and communicated by:

  • Creating reports that capture key criteria from HubSpot form submissions and allow for segmentation by county, state, or partner
  • Automating emails to the appropriate company or county point of contact showing the leads associated with their program or region

Success looks like having a structured, automated system where inbound form submissions are properly categorized, associated with the right entities, and surfaced through reports and automated emails without manual effort, while keeping partner contacts and end users clearly distinct.

job_title: HubSpot CRM Data Cleanup & Hygiene Specialist
job_description:
We are seeking a detail-oriented HubSpot CRM specialist to support data cleanup, normalization, and hygiene following (or in parallel with) a strategic HubSpot audit. This role is execution-focused and operational. The goal is to restore data reliability, consistency, and usability so HubSpot can function as a trusted system of record.

The specialist will:

  • Reduce duplicate, incomplete, and unreliable records
  • Normalize properties so reporting and workflows function correctly
  • Support long-term data health through basic guardrails

     Scope of Work
    1. Data Cleanup & Normalization
    2. Property & Field Hygiene
    3. Lifecycle & Deal Stage Hygiene
    4. Marketing Data Cleanup (Operational)
    5. Reporting Support (Post-Cleanup)

    Deliverables

    • Standardized property values across key objects

      • What was cleaned
      • What could not be fixed without process changes

proposal_description:
Project Proposal: HubSpot CRM Data Cleanup & Hygiene Optimization


Introduction

We deliver best-in-class Go-To-Market operations to transform tech stack efficiencies, unify cross-functional teams, and enable sustainable growth.

Data inconsistencies in HubSpot often arise from rapid scaling, siloed data entry, and process shifts—flaws that erode revenue momentum if ignored. With 15+ years supporting VC- and PE-backed firms, we apply refined methods to deduplicate, normalize, and protect data, converting chaos into reliable operations.

Our method respects your system's unique evolution while using proven tools: audits for insights, phased executions for control, and guardrails for longevity. Drawing from wabi-sabi, we embrace imperfections to restore data trust efficiently.
We deliver collaborative execution and measurable hygiene improvements. Thank you for considering this proposal.

We are committed to deliver collaborative execution and measurable hygiene improvements. Thank you for considering this proposal.

Strategy Brief

Client

A HubSpot customer seeking data reliability.

Goals: Restore Data Reliability to Optimize Operations

  • Reduce duplicates by 90-100% across contacts and companies for clean insights.
  • Standardize properties, deal stages, and lifecycle stages for effective workflows and reporting.
  • Establish guardrails to prevent regression and support long-term data health.
  • Provide summaries of cleaned data, unresolved issues, and prevention recommendations.

Tech Stack: HubSpot

We conduct a targeted audit to map integrations and vulnerabilities, ensuring HubSpot-focused changes minimize operational disruptions.

Scope of Services

As a HubSpot Partner, we offer execution-focused data cleanup and hygiene using tools like Koalify for scalable deduplication. Includes weekly recaps and final summary of actions completed, open issues, and recommendations.

Total: 60 hours.

1. Data Cleanup and Normalization

  • Deduplicate contacts and companies via HubSpot and Koalify.
  • Normalize critical properties (e.g., domain, lifecycle stages) without data loss.
  • Fix incomplete or unreliable records.

2. Property and Field Hygiene

  • Audit and standardize values across objects (contacts, companies, deals).
  • Implement validation rules for ongoing data quality.

3. Lifecycle and Deal Stage Hygiene

  • Map and define lifecycle stages with processes.
  • Map and define deal stages with sales processes.

4. Marketing Data Cleanup (Operational)

  • Reduce marketing asset clutter (e.g., lists, emails, workflows) and suggest naming conventions.
  • Prepare data for AI initiatives, supporting segmentation and content.

5. Reporting Support (Post-Cleanup)

  • Audit dashboards and reports for usage and ownership.

6. Collaboration and Enablement

  • Train on data governance.
  • Develop documentation and release notes.

Risks and Proactive Mitigations

  • Data Loss or Inaccurate Merges: Risk of overwriting key attributes (e.g., domain, email). Mitigation: Backup HubSpot data; test in sandbox; log changes for review.
  • Sync Disruptions with Integrations: Record merges and data updates could trigger data syncs and errors. Mitigation: Target HubSpot-only duplicates; monitor logs before/after batches.
  • Workflow/Reporting Breaks: Data transforms may trigger automations. Mitigation: Map dependencies; phase rollouts with rollbacks; validate 10% of records post-cleanup.
  • Scalability Overruns: Time extensions for large volumes. Mitigation: Batch 500-1,000 records; automate where possible; buffer hours.
  • User Resistance/Regression: Users may revert to old habits. Mitigation: Train users; implement data quality checks; create data monitoring dashboards.
  • Tool Limitations: Koalify may not cover all edge cases. Mitigation: Initial audit for anomalies; use alternatives; recommend process fixes in summary.

Timeline

Engagement Duration: 4-6 weeks
Start Date: Thursday, January 15, 2026
End Date: Thursday, February 26, 2026

Responsibilities

  • Client: Grant HubSpot Partner Access, provide tech stack details, designate a point of contact, and timely reviews.
  • Consultant: Meet timelines, ensure data quality, and flag risks proactively.

Investment

Estimated 40-60 hours based on scope.
Total: $10,000 USD (depending on record volume)

Terms and Conditions

Payment: 50% upfront, 50% upon deliverables. Changes to scope via mutual addendum. All work is confidential and follows HubSpot best practices.

Next Steps 

  1. Schedule discovery call with Ian Shields (https://cal.com/ian-shields/chat) 
  2. Review proposal 
  3. Sign agreement 
  4. Schedule kick-off call on start date 
  5. Install Koalify (free trial) 
  6. Set weekly check-ins 

About Kintsugi Marketing 

Kintsugi /”kin-TSOO-gi”/ 

Poetically translated as “golden joinery,” is the ancient Japanese method of mending broken pottery with precious metals, turning breaks into beauty. 

Kintsugi Marketing 

As a consultancy, we join Go-To-Market strategy with technical execution to help B2B SaaS revenue and marketing leaders and their teams transform tech stack efficiencies, unify cross-functional teams, and drive sustainable growth. 

Ian Shields, Founder & GTM Solutions Architect

With over 15 years of experience, Ian specializes in developing and executing Go-To-Market strategies that drive measurable growth. 

Madelynn Johnson, Marketing Operations Apprentice 

“Maddie” excels in marketing operations and ensures smooth execution of engagements. 

Thank You 

We look forward to working with you.

job_title: HubSpot Workflow Audit, Optimization, and Lead Scoring Improvements
job_description:
Agiloft is a provider of enterprise contract lifecycle management (CLM) software, helping organizations streamline, automate, and gain visibility into complex contract processes. The company works with mid-market and enterprise customers and relies on HubSpot to support its marketing and sales operations.

We’re looking for an experienced HubSpot expert to help us assess and optimize our existing workflows and lead scoring model. Over time, workflows have grown in number and complexity, and we want to ensure they are still necessary, efficient, and aligned with our current go-to-market strategy.

Specifically, we need support to:
  • Review and evaluate existing HubSpot workflows to determine what should be kept, adjusted, consolidated, or retired
  • Make recommended workflow changes directly in HubSpot
  • Clearly document what changes were made and the reasoning behind each decision, so our internal team can understand and maintain them going forward
  • Audit and improve our lead scoring model to better reflect buying intent and help sales prioritize the right leads

Success for this project looks like a cleaner, more intentional workflow setup, improved transparency through documentation, and a more effective lead scoring system that better supports our sales and marketing teams.
proposal_description:
Agiloft's in a spot we see often with mature B2B SaaS companies: workflows that made sense when they were built have accumulated over years, and now nobody's sure what's still relevant to your current GTM motion. Same with lead scorin, what signaled buying intent two years ago probably doesn't match how your mid-market and enterprise buyers actually engage today.

What we'd do:
  • Full workflow audit: map every active workflow, identify redundancies, flag what's broken or orphaned, and recommend keep/consolidate/retire decisions
  • Lead scoring rebuild: interview your sales team on what actually indicates a qualified buyer, then restructure scoring to reflect real buying intent for your CLM buyers (legal, procurement, sales ops personas behave differently)
  • Execute changes directly in HubSpot—not just a recommendations doc
  • Document everything with clear reasoning so your team can maintain it

On documentation: We use Supered to embed workflow logic and lead scoring criteria directly inside HubSpot. Your team sees why a lead scored the way it did and what triggers each workflow without digging through a wiki nobody updates.

Pricing
  • Workflow Audit & Cleanup: $8K–$12K
  • Lead Scoring Rebuild: $2K–$4K
  • Combined: $10K–$16K depending on workflow volume

Next step: Book a call and give me a quick tour of your workflow library-I can tell you in 20 minutes how tangled it actually is and whether this is a cleanup or a rebuild.

  • Lead routing + distribution (web + events → correct broker)
  • Data capture + property setup
  • Deal + pipeline configuration
  • Reporting + dashboards
  • Automation to support sales motion
  • Optimization for brokerage-style workflows

Outcome:
A HubSpot CRM tailored for a brokerage business that:

  • captures the right data
  • routes leads correctly
  • supports brokers through the sales cycle
  • tracks deals + performance
  • provides visibility to leadership

Looking to get started ASAP. Pros with brokerage or similar industry experience preferred.
proposal_description:
Hi there, thanks for submitting a project here. Excited to dive in.

Yacht brokerage is interesting territory for HubSpot. The deal dynamics are similar to luxury real estate: high-value, low-frequency sales where broker relationships and listing ownership matter more than typical SaaS pipeline metrics.

Having 20 seats sitting unconfigured since June tells me something shifted priorities. Before we dig into lead routing and pipelines, I'd want to understand:

What's the actual bottleneck right now? Is it leads getting lost between web/events and brokers? Brokers working deals without visibility? Or leadership not being able to forecast?

What does "correct broker" mean for your business? Territory, listing ownership, relationship history, specialty?

I don't want you to get 6 months down the road and have to rebuild this whole thing because someone didn't ask enough questions. 

The technical setup is straightforward. What makes or breaks brokerage CRMs is whether the system reflects how deals actually move, not how you wish they moved.

Would a call be a good next step for you to make sure we get answers to these questions?

job_title: Hubspot Sales Hub "Re-implementation" + Revenue Data Integration
job_description:
HubSpot Re-Implementation
We are looking for a top-tier, highly consultative HubSpot Solutions Partner to lead a full "re-implementation" of our existing HubSpot instance, starting with Sales Hub. We are not starting from scratch—we already use HubSpot—but it was never implemented with strategic guidance, standardized definitions, or optimized workflows. We're seeking a partner who will proactively guide us toward best-in-class usage—for sales, reporting, and service—by understanding our business and telling us what to build and why.

High-Level Goals
  1. Redesign CRM usage for field sales reps to improve pipeline activity tracking, call/email/text logging (especially from mobile devices), and ease of use.
  2. Integrate revenue data from our cloud-based Azure SQL database into HubSpot, then use that data to build insightful business and sales activity reporting.
  3. Strategically allow for a power dialer integration for mass outbound calls, texts, and emails. (Lead lists come in, get scrubbed against Hubspot data, have statuses, and then ultimately become a real lead/deal to work at some point)

You don’t need to handle the technical integration yourself—our in-house full-stack dev team will build any custom connectors or workflows needed. But we’ll rely on you to clearly define what data should flow into HubSpot, how it should be structured, and how it will be used & reported on.

CRM Use Case Highlights
  • 11 field-based sales reps who primarily use personal smartphones to communicate with customers—calls and texts.
  • CRM must be extremely easy to use for tracking calls, emails, and notes. Texting through the CRM (or integrations) is preferred to consolidate communication visibility.
  • We are interested in adding some sort of power dialer for mass outreach, but also need a solution (could be same or different) for tracking day-to-day calls, texts, emails.
  • We are open to rebuilding processes if they optimize usability and tracking.

Data Integration & Reporting Needs
  • Azure SQL Server (cloud) contains all customer, product, revenue, and renewal data.
  • Our internal dev team will handle syncing this data into HubSpot—preferably in near real time, but nightly is acceptable.
  • We need your help defining:
    • What data needs to be pulled in, 
    • How it should be structured in HubSpot, 
    • What dashboards, metrics, and automations should be built from it.

Required reporting (again our internal dev team can help build with guidance):
  • Business performance metrics (e.g. customer retention, LTV, product adoption, account growth/decline, upcoming renewals).
  • Sales productivity & conversion analytics (e.g. activities vs. deals won, how many touches to convert, rep performance benchmarks).

************************************************************************************************
PHASE TWO POSSIBILITY! Not for initial quote, but for deeper knowledge of our instance of Hubspot and how we may add to this project if Phase 1 (Sales Hub / Data) goes well.

Service Hub Optimization
  • Currently using HubSpot Service Hub for basic ticketing via phone, email, and web forms.
  • Have an existing CSAT/NPS process but want recommendations on industry best practices.
  • Need to improve reporting and visibility around service SLAs, volume, satisfaction trends, and team productivity.
  • Right now, Service Hub is just for us to communicate to customers. We have other internal tools to "do the work" that our customers are requesting. How much work could we do right within Hubspot Service Hub with proper workflows and data feeding back to SQL?

Marketing Hub Optimization
  • Currently using HubSpot Marketing Hub to run marketing campaigns
  • Data is disjointed, and hard to measure ROI on any given campaign.
    • Did this lead come as a referral, or from a marketing campaign?
    • Did the Sales rep get this lead, or did Marketing give this lead to the rep?
  • Much of the success here probably largely depends on optimization and consistency with how Sales Hub gets used, but then we would want to move to Marketing Hub to ensure we can properly measure ROI on campaigns and see how much business is generated by Marketing vs. Sales.

job_description:

______________

Custom HubSpot Quote Template + Approval Process Automation

We’re looking for an experienced HubSpot developer with strong HubL and JavaScript skills to help us customize a quote template and build a smarter approval workflow for our sales process.

Project Scope

1. Quote Template Customization

  • We have a specific design template in mind and need:

    • A custom “Bill To” address module
    • Start and End Dates added to each line item
    • Proper display of future payments, due dates, and payment terms
  • We’re working within a child theme. While modules are nesting correctly, our current dev is running into issues with:

    • Complex HubL logic (null errors, broken tables, etc.)
    • Managing nested content and formatting in the quote layout

2. Approval Process Development

  • We need to build a more sophisticated quote approval process based on:

    • Deal size
    • Discount thresholds
  • Once complete, the flow should prevent quotes from being published without the appropriate approvals.
  • We’ll provide examples of the quote layout and design references.


💼 Requirements

  • Advanced HubSpot CMS + Enterprise Sales Hub experience
  • Strong HubL and JavaScript skills
  • Comfortable working with custom themes and quote template logic
  • Able to advise on scalable approval workflows in HubSpot


📅 Start: ASAP
proposal_description:
Hey Jason,

Thanks for posting this opportunity, this project is within my wheelhouse.

I have hands on experience building custom coded quote templates within the CMS using HubL and JavaScript. I’ve worked inside child themes, built modular quote structures, and solved the exact types of issues your current dev is facing: nested content rendering, null value handling. A quick previous Custom quote project I worked on was to embed a HubSpot form and use js to prevent the quote from being submitted by the customer until the form was filled out. 

Here’s how I’d approach this project:

                                                   Quote Template Customization
Here’s how I’d tackle your requirements:
  • Build a clean, scalable quote layout based on your design references
  • Develop a custom “Bill To” module with fallback logic and proper token handling
  • Add Start/End Dates per line item using dynamic HubL + JS
  • Display future payments, due dates, and payment terms clearly and consistently
  • Harden the HubL logic to prevent null errors and ensure stable rendering across edge cases

                                                           Approval Process Automation
While HubSpot doesn’t support native quote-level approvals, I can implement a scalable deal-stage-based approval logic. For your workflow, I can:
  • Gate quote rendering based on deal approval status using conditional HubL
  • Prevent the quote from displaying full content unless the associated deal has cleared specific thresholds
  • Build a logic layer around deal size, discount levels, and approval checkpoints: fully aligned with your team’s process

                                                              Deliverables
  • Fully custom quote template (HubL + JavaScript)
  • Dynamic modules for billing, line items, and payment terms
  • Approval enforcement logic tied to deal-level conditions
  • Documentation + 1-2 weeks of post-launch support
  Timeline & Budget

                          I can begin immediately. Based on the scope, I propose a fixed rate of $8,570, accounting for:
  • Discovery + Architecture
  • Development + QA
  • Iterations + Final Review
  • Handoff + Light Support

If this aligns with what you’re looking for, I’d be happy to jump on a quick call to sync on final specs and expectations.
Looking forward to helping your team.
Best,

 Khristian R.
HubSpot Partner | HubL | HS Developer | Automation Specialist

job_title: Hubspot GTM - Deals Automation and Cleanup
job_description:
We're a SaaS Business that somewhat recently have migrated over from SalesForce to Hubspot.  After a very poor implementation and self taught learning exercise, we're looking for some help with a few automations and setup for our deals & renewals process.  

We're looking for someone to come in and help get our engine past idling.
proposal_description:
I've seen this movie before – company makes the move from Salesforce to HubSpot, implementation partner treats it like a technical migration instead of a revenue transformation, and six months later the team is worse off than before. Now you're stuck with a shiny new tool that's just creating different problems.

Here's what concerns me about your situation: you're a SaaS business with a broken renewals process. That's not an "automation problem" – that's a revenue leak. Every day your renewals engine is idling, you're losing customers you shouldn't lose, missing expansion opportunities, and probably flying blind on churn risk.

The difference between a renewals process that works and one that doesn't isn't 5% – it's often 15-20% of your annual recurring revenue. So my first question is blunt: what's this poor implementation actually costing you in lost renewals and expansion revenue over the last few months?

Before we talk about automations, I need to understand a few things:

1. What drove the move from Salesforce to HubSpot in the first place – was it cost, complexity, or something else? (This matters because it tells me if you're solving the right problem)

2. When you say "very poor implementation," what specifically broke – was it the data migration, the workflows, the team adoption, or all of the above?

3. Right now, how are renewals actually being managed – spreadsheets, manual tasks, tribal knowledge? And what's falling through the cracks?

4. If your renewals process stays in its current state for another 3-6 months, what does that cost you in real dollars – both from customers who churn and from the opportunity cost of your team doing manual work instead of strategic revenue activities?

5. Beyond "getting past idling," what does success actually look like for you – faster renewal cycles, better expansion visibility, predictable revenue forecasting, or something else?

The reason I'm asking these questions: most companies that come to us after a failed implementation are thinking tactically ("fix the automations") when they need to be thinking strategically ("fix the revenue engine"). The technical work you need is table stakes – where we're different is ensuring the system actually drives revenue outcomes, not just cleaner data.

I'd suggest a 30-minute call where we map the actual business impact of your current state, identify what caused the first implementation to fail, and determine whether this is a "fix and optimize" situation or whether you need a more significant intervention. If we're not the right fit, I'll tell you directly.

Does that make sense as a next step? If so, here's my calendar.

- Onward and Upward,
Grant Hushek, Founder @ Grantbot

job_title: No more proposals needed
job_description:
Hey Pro's - this is Jason posting on behalf of a customer. This is a referral from a HubSpot CS rep, they are a software company, using Salesforce and HubSpot....the HubSpot part of that equation is in tough shape. Looking to engage a Pro ASAP.

My short summary of the need:
  1. This is only open to individuals consultants (or very small teams) - this is "retained consultant" and less "engage an agency". They want to work with 1 person who can devote 10-15 hours per week to working with them. 
  2. They estimate 10-15 hours per week of close collaborative work
  3. Healthcare experience is the dream (NOT a requirement) - largely about some of the complexities in selling into the space. They sell to hospitals, providers, pharma and there are complex account hierarchies 
  4. They asked me to send 3 options to select from, they want to have calls this week / early next week and make their choice. 

Call notes from the call where they detailed their pain points:

Background:

  • The client previously ran separate, unintegrated HubSpot and Salesforce instances, manually updating both.
  • About a year ago, they began attempting to integrate the two systems (with your ongoing support), but have faced persistent technical and operational issues. Recently tried again but with not great results and data cleanliness issues
  • Key internal stakeholder leading these efforts has since left the company, compounding the complexity of troubleshooting and system understanding.
  • Current workflows include a functioning push of MQLs (based on meetings, form submissions, lead status/score) from HubSpot to Salesforce as opportunities.
Core Issues Identified:

  • Data Sync & Associations: Contact and account data is not consistently or accurately syncing between systems. Associations are frequently lost or incorrect, and reporting is now unreliable.
  • Campaign, Attribution, & Prospecting Gaps: Campaign event sync, attribution tracking, upload/report functionality for event lists, and contact associations are not functioning as needed.
  • Lifecycle & Funnel Inconsistency: Lifecycle stages and funnel data tracking are no longer reliable, creating challenges for lead management and sales process transparency.
  • Account Hierarchies and Data Integrity: Loss of Salesforce account IDs complicates cross-platform account association. Hierarchy maintenance is an ongoing issue.
  • System Usability: Underlying issues have rendered HubSpot’s reporting and marketing tools difficult—sometimes impossible—to use, consuming significant client bandwidth and limiting adoption.
  • Recent Troubleshooting: The client turned off “create and associate companies with contacts” in an effort to resolve some syncing issues, but results remain unsatisfactory.
Customer’s Stated Goals:

  1. Assess the current state of our instance and identify what’s been overengineered or improperly configured
  2. Regain access to native HubSpot functionality wherever possible
  3. Design a scalable and sustainable approach to Salesforce integration that doesn’t require constant workarounds
  4. Reclaim visibility into MQLs, campaign performance, and contact behaviors within HubSpot
Immediate Needs:

  • The client requires a comprehensive evaluation of their current state, and actionable recommendations to restore reliable, scalable integration.
  • Their top priorities are accurate reporting, restored campaign and funnel visibility, and a simplified, sustainable integration setup.

THIS IS HOURLY - please only submit hourly rates between $110 - $140/hr
proposal_description:

Rate: $110/hr
Estimated Hours: 10–15 per week 
Scope of Work: 
  • Audit current HubSpot + Salesforce integration setup
  • Identify key configuration issues, broken syncs, and over-engineered workflows
  • Restore foundational HubSpot reporting, campaign, and lifecycle functionality
  • Provide clear documentation and knowledge transfer for internal stakeholders

Best for: Teams that need fast stabilization and a reliable foundation without over-investment in advanced customizations.

 
Option 2 – Strategic Optimization
 
Rate: $125/hr
Estimated Hours: 10–15 per week

Scope of Work:
  • Full evaluation of HubSpot + Salesforce integration with root cause analysis of sync errors and account hierarchy issues
  • Redesign data flow for lifecycle stages, MQL/SQL handoff, and funnel reporting
  • Re-enable and optimize native HubSpot tools (campaign attribution, event tracking, associations)
  • Deliver structured documentation and training for long-term sustainability

Best for: Client that wants both remediation and optimization, balancing stability with growth readiness.


Option 3 – Advanced Integration Partner

Rate: $140/hr
Estimated Hours: 10–15 per week

Scope of Work:
 
  • Everything in Option 2, plus:
    • Design and implement a scalable Salesforce account hierarchy strategy for healthcare selling complexity
    • Advanced reporting buildouts: cross-platform campaign attribution, MQL visibility, multi-touch attribution models
    • Ongoing advisory on Salesforce <> HubSpot best practices, data governance, and adoption strategies
  • Serve as a dedicated extension of your team with proactive recommendations and change management support

Best for: Client looking for a long-term strategic partner who can future-proof HubSpot + Salesforce alignment and reduce reliance on constant troubleshooting.

job_title: HubSpot RevOps Consultant (Enterprise)
job_description:
We’ve just moved from SugarCRM to HubSpot and purchased 10 Sales Hub Enterprise seats and 4 Marketing Hub Pro seats. We need an expert to migrate data, set up best-practice RevOps processes, and implement key integrations (Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana).

Objectives
- Migrate all SugarCRM data to HubSpot with clean mapping, dedupe, and validation.
- Build enterprise-grade automations for sales, marketing, and handoffs.
- Integrate Xero (billing), PhoneBurner (dialer/activities), and Asana (projects/tasks).
- Enable the team with permissions, playbooks, sequences, reports, and dashboards.

Scope of Work (deliverables)

1) Discovery & Design
Audit SugarCRM objects, fields, picklists, activities, attachments, and custom logic.
  • Define HubSpot data model: standard + custom objects, associations, required fields.
    Process design: Lead → MQL/SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won/Lost, pipeline stages, entry/exit criteria.
    Security model: teams, roles, permissions, record ownership rules.

2) Data Migration (SugarCRM → HubSpot)
Field mapping doc (1:1 + transformations), deduplication rules, lookup resolution.
Import activities (emails/calls/notes/meetings where supported), files, and lists.
Validation run + reconciliation report (record counts, error log, exceptions).
Delta cutover plan with blackout window and rollback.

3) Sales Hub Enterprise Setup
Pipelines (primary + any sub-pipelines), stage properties, required reasons.
Sequences, templates, snippets, playbooks, call outcomes, meeting links.
Lead scoring (behavioral + firmographic), SLAs and task queues.
Custom quotes, products, deal workflows (create tasks, set owners, update fields).
Forecasting setup, goals, and weighted pipeline definitions.

4) Marketing Hub Pro Setup
Lifecycle model, MQL definition, subscription types, double opt-in where needed.
Forms, CTAs, lists (smart/static), nurture workflows, and lead routing.
Email + landing page brand modules, compliance (GDPR/CCPA).
UTM + source tracking, campaign hierarchy.

5) Integrations
Xero: deal→invoice sync, invoice status back to HubSpot, revenue fields for reporting.
PhoneBurner: activity logging, call outcomes, contact sync, list handoff.
Asana: deal/contract approval → Asana project creation with key fields synced.
Webhooks/middleware (if needed) with retry + error handling.

6) Reporting & Enablement
Executive dashboards: pipeline health, velocity, forecast vs. quota, win/loss, campaign attribution.
Rep dashboards: tasks due, sequence performance, meetings booked, CAC/LTV basics.
Training: live sessions + Loom library; admin runbook; change log.

It's a long term engagement and you will be working with Sales, marketing & CS teams.
proposal_description:
Hey there, congrats on moving to HubSpot! We've helped dozens of companies through this exact transition, and it's always an exciting chance to rethink how revenue flows through your business.

Here's what most agencies miss
They treat migrations like a data dump. Move everything over, set up some workflows, done. But you're sitting on years of customer data that tells a story. There are deals that went cold, customers who bought once and disappeared. This migration is your chance to wake all that up.

We recently helped a consulting firm through a similar move and discovered $300K in deals that had just gone quiet. No one was working them. That's what we look for.

What we'd actually do
First two weeks: dig into your SugarCRM to understand what you've built and how your team actually sells. What's working that we need to preserve? What drives everyone crazy that we can fix?

Then we architect HubSpot around how you want to work, not how SugarCRM forced you to work. Your Sales Hub Enterprise gives us incredible flexibility. Marketing Hub Pro means marketing qualified leads will actually mean something.

For your integrations (Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana), we've done these before. Xero needs proper revenue visibility. PhoneBurner is straightforward but the activity tracking feeds everything downstream. And that Asana handoff? That's where deals go to die if it's not automated properly.

Making it stick
Your teams are probably mix of excited and terrified. We build with adoption in mind using one of our tools that enforces processes and puts documentation right inside of HubSpot where they're working. No PDFs they'll never read. It's a browser extension and free for your team to help them adopt everything that's built.

We run this over 6 months. First three months: migration, cleaning, integrations, core processes. Next three months: finding money through systematic revenue plays that resurface opportunities and catch deals before they stall.

Investment
For Enterprise seats with these integrations, we typically range from $8k-$12k/month depending on the details. You're already investing heavily in HubSpot licenses. Let's make sure they deliver.

Next step?
We could do a Revenue Leak Diagnostic for $2k first. We'll dig into your SugarCRM and show you exactly what opportunities are hiding. Think of it as a home inspection before renovation.

Or just hop on a call and tell me what's driving this change. Scaling? SugarCRM limits? Team frustration?

dan@mergeyourdatateam.com

job_title: Building custom objects for each business unit
job_description: We currently have three business units in one account. These business units represent three portfolio companies. These companies are competitors and need seperate custom objects for our sales team to be able to work their deals without being about to see if that contact is also speaking with one of the other businesses.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

I appreciate the clarity. What you're describing, custom objects for three portfolio companies in one HubSpot account, is technically possible, but I'm wondering if you're solving the right problem.

Custom objects won't actually prevent visibility issues between competitors. Sales reps with access to contact records will still see activity history across all three companies unless you rebuild permissions at the user/team level. That's a much deeper (and costlier) fix than object architecture.

Two questions: 
1. When you say sales teams "can't see" cross-company contacts, do you mean legally can't (compliance issue) or operationally shouldn't (preference)? The answer changes everything about the right solution.
2. What happens if you spend $1-5K on custom objects, launch it, and six months later realize your sales team still has visibility problems or can't actually use the new structure?

The budget-to-scope mismatch is a problem given we need to deeploy understand your business needs and workflows before building something out like this. Custom objects for three business units typically runs $8-12K when done properly (data modeling, migration, permissions, training). If budget is genuinely capped at $5K, let's talk about a diagnostic phase that pressure-tests whether custom objects are even the right move.

I'd recommend a quick 30 minute call to make sure we're on the same page. Does that sound like a good next step?

job_description:
HubSpot Re-Implementation
We are looking for a top-tier, highly consultative HubSpot Solutions Partner to lead a full "re-implementation" of our existing HubSpot instance, starting with Sales Hub. We are not starting from scratch—we already use HubSpot—but it was never implemented with strategic guidance, standardized definitions, or optimized workflows. We're seeking a partner who will proactively guide us toward best-in-class usage—for sales, reporting, and service—by understanding our business and telling us what to build and why.

High-Level Goals
  1. Redesign CRM usage for field sales reps to improve pipeline activity tracking, call/email/text logging (especially from mobile devices), and ease of use.
  2. Integrate revenue data from our cloud-based Azure SQL database into HubSpot, then use that data to build insightful business and sales activity reporting.
  3. Strategically allow for a power dialer integration for mass outbound calls, texts, and emails. (Lead lists come in, get scrubbed against Hubspot data, have statuses, and then ultimately become a real lead/deal to work at some point)

You don’t need to handle the technical integration yourself—our in-house full-stack dev team will build any custom connectors or workflows needed. But we’ll rely on you to clearly define what data should flow into HubSpot, how it should be structured, and how it will be used & reported on.

CRM Use Case Highlights
  • 11 field-based sales reps who primarily use personal smartphones to communicate with customers—calls and texts.
  • CRM must be extremely easy to use for tracking calls, emails, and notes. Texting through the CRM (or integrations) is preferred to consolidate communication visibility.
  • We are interested in adding some sort of power dialer for mass outreach, but also need a solution (could be same or different) for tracking day-to-day calls, texts, emails.
  • We are open to rebuilding processes if they optimize usability and tracking.

Data Integration & Reporting Needs
  • Azure SQL Server (cloud) contains all customer, product, revenue, and renewal data.
  • Our internal dev team will handle syncing this data into HubSpot—preferably in near real time, but nightly is acceptable.
  • We need your help defining:
    • What data needs to be pulled in, 
    • How it should be structured in HubSpot, 
    • What dashboards, metrics, and automations should be built from it.

Required reporting (again our internal dev team can help build with guidance):
  • Business performance metrics (e.g. customer retention, LTV, product adoption, account growth/decline, upcoming renewals).
  • Sales productivity & conversion analytics (e.g. activities vs. deals won, how many touches to convert, rep performance benchmarks).

************************************************************************************************
PHASE TWO POSSIBILITY! Not for initial quote, but for deeper knowledge of our instance of Hubspot and how we may add to this project if Phase 1 (Sales Hub / Data) goes well.

Service Hub Optimization
  • Currently using HubSpot Service Hub for basic ticketing via phone, email, and web forms.
  • Have an existing CSAT/NPS process but want recommendations on industry best practices.
  • Need to improve reporting and visibility around service SLAs, volume, satisfaction trends, and team productivity.
  • Right now, Service Hub is just for us to communicate to customers. We have other internal tools to "do the work" that our customers are requesting. How much work could we do right within Hubspot Service Hub with proper workflows and data feeding back to SQL?

Marketing Hub Optimization
  • Currently using HubSpot Marketing Hub to run marketing campaigns
  • Data is disjointed, and hard to measure ROI on any given campaign.
    • Did this lead come as a referral, or from a marketing campaign?
    • Did the Sales rep get this lead, or did Marketing give this lead to the rep?
  • Much of the success here probably largely depends on optimization and consistency with how Sales Hub gets used, but then we would want to move to Marketing Hub to ensure we can properly measure ROI on campaigns and see how much business is generated by Marketing vs. Sales.

proposal_description:
Hi there,

I appreciate the clarity on your three-channel setup - D2C, agent network, and enterprise partnerships. That's a complex go-to-market for a seed-stage company.

I'm going to be candid with you because you've got a lot of proposals to sift through: Not only am I a HubSpot expert, I also love the game of business. If you want an upwork-style order taker that will build these three pipelines without playing a game of 20 questions first, we are not the right fit.

Here's what I'm seeing: multiple pipelines for three channels isn't just a HubSpot configuration challenge - it's a strategic attribution and decision-making challenge. I worked with a legal agency scaling across multiple sales motions; they built their pipelines well, but six months later still couldn't answer "which channel should we invest in?" because they'd focused on tracking everything instead of identifying what mattered.

The Auth0 integration tells me you're technical, but "may need Tableau/Zapier" suggests you're not sure what level of complexity you actually need. Most seed-stage fintechs over-engineer their stack when what they really need first is clarity on which channel is working and why.

Two questions before we talk solution:

1. Which of these three channels drives the most revenue today, and where are deals falling through cracks right now? (Agent onboarding, enterprise contract delays, D2C drop-off points?)

2. What business decisions are you making, or NOT making, because you can't see performance across channels? Are you over-investing in underperforming channels, or missing opportunities in high-performing ones?

Since you're seed-stage and budget is uncertain, we should talk about phasing this: start with the highest-impact channel and reporting that drives actual decisions, then expand.

Would a 30 minute call make sense as a next step? Goal: stop guessing which channel deserves your attention.

Thanks for the clarity - looking forward to connecting.

job_title: HubSpot Implementation & Revenue Integration Specialist
job_description:
Project Overview:
We are seeking an experienced HubSpot implementation partner to help us optimize our CRM and revenue management processes. Our current setup lacks integration and automation, resulting in manual work and unreliable forecasting. We need a solution that connects HubSpot, QuickBooks, and our revenue tracking workflows to create a single source of truth for sales and revenue data.
Key Challenges We Need to Solve:
  • Unreliable Sales Forecasting: Current HubSpot forecasts are not trusted by the team.
  • Revenue Management Gaps: HubSpot is not being used effectively to manage and track revenue.
  • Mixed Sales Models: We have both ARR (annual recurring revenue for software licensing) and fixed-price sales.
  • Disconnected Systems: QuickBooks (finance), HubSpot (CRM), and manual Excel sheets for revenue tracking are not integrated, causing significant manual reconciliation.
  • Recurring Revenue Tracking: We struggle with quoting and tracking recurring revenue accurately.
Objectives:
  • Implement best practices in HubSpot for forecasting and revenue tracking.
  • Configure HubSpot to handle both ARR and fixed-price sales models.
  • Integrate HubSpot with QuickBooks and/or other tools to reduce manual reconciliation.
  • Create a reliable process for quoting and managing recurring revenue.
  • Establish a single source of truth for sales and revenue data.
Deliverables:
  • Audit of current HubSpot setup and recommendations.
  • Implementation of improved forecasting and revenue tracking processes.
  • Integration between HubSpot, QuickBooks, and revenue tracking tools.
  • Documentation and training for internal team.
Additional Info:
  • Looking for a partner who can provide strategic guidance and hands-on implementation.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

I appreciate the clarity on what's not working. Most companies aren't looking for HubSpot help because they love project work—they're losing deals to bad forecasts, burning finance team hours on reconciliation, or can't answer basic questions like "what's our actual ARR?"

Here's what I'm seeing: QuickBooks + HubSpot + Excel disconnects aren't really integration problems. They're revenue governance problems. You probably have sales closing deals that finance doesn't know about for weeks, or invoices that don't match what's in the CRM, or recurring revenue that just... gets lost.

I worked with a SaaS company last year in similar shape. Before we got involved, they spent months trying to "fix HubSpot" before realizing the real issue was no one owned the handoff between sales and finance. Once we established clear revenue ownership and built the right workflows, the tech integration was straightforward.

Before we talk solution, I need to understand two things:

1. Right now, when a deal closes in HubSpot, what happens next? How long until it's in QuickBooks, who owns that handoff, and where does it break down?

2. What's the business cost of this dysfunction? Are you missing revenue targets because forecasts are wrong, or burning 20+ hours monthly on manual reconciliation?

Would a 30-minute call make sense as a good next step?

Thanks for the detail. Looking forward to sorting this out together.

Starting with a Scoping & Architecture engagement ensures the full project is defined accurately before any design or development begins. With a website of this scale, complexity, and integration depth, this step protects both teams from assumptions, scope gaps, and misaligned expectations.

Scoping allows us to confirm the content model, data requirements, template needs, functionality, integrations, and technical constraints early, so nothing is built twice and nothing is missed. job_title: HubSpot Lead Association and Automated Reporting by County and Company/Partner
job_description:
***We are seeking a HubSpot Solutions Partner (with a partner seat) for this project***

Intuition Robotics is a mission-driven AI and robotics company focused on empowering older adults with empathetic digital companions, notably the ElliQ social companion robot that promotes independence and engagement for aging populations.

We use HubSpot to capture inbound interest in ElliQ through free-form forms on our website. These form submissions currently create contact records, but they are not associated with any company, county, or funding partner, and different types of people are not clearly differentiated within HubSpot.

Our ecosystem includes state and county partners that fund and distribute ElliQ, as well as individual end users who request or use the product. Today, those parties are not clearly separated in HubSpot, which creates confusion between partner contacts and end users coming from the same organization or region.

We would like to improve how inbound leads are organized, associated, and communicated by:

  • Clearly distinguishing end users from partner or company contacts within HubSpot
  • Associating individual leads with the appropriate county or regional entity
  • Avoiding confusion between a company or county point of contact and the individual end users connected to that entity
  • Creating reports that capture key criteria from HubSpot form submissions and allow for segmentation by county, state, or partner
  • Automating emails to the appropriate company or county point of contact showing the leads associated with their program or region

Success looks like having a structured, automated system where inbound form submissions are properly categorized, associated with the right entities, and surfaced through reports and automated emails without manual effort, while keeping partner contacts and end users clearly distinct.
proposal_description:
Hey thanks for submitting a proposal here!

The challenge you're describing (partner contacts vs. end users, county attribution, automated reporting) is a data model problem, not just a reporting problem. If the foundation is wrong, every automation you build becomes a workaround. We want to avoid that.

I've worked with multi-stakeholder HubSpot setups where the distinction between "who distributes" and "who uses" creates exactly this type of confusion. The fix requires rethinking your object relationships before touching any workflows. That is a lot of technical jargon to tell you that we need to get closer to the metal and see how this was set up at the most basic level so we can build to your ideal outcome: clear reports that help you make decisions.

Two questions:
1. When a county partner asks "how many leads came through our region," how long does that take today and who's doing that work manually?
2. What happens when a partner contact and an end user share the same email domain or organization name, but you need them routed completely differently?

We’ll rebuild all 10 landing page templates (5 primary + 5 thank-you pages) inside HubSpot’s drag-and-drop builder. Everything will be modular, reusable, and fully brand-adaptive.

Deployment Plan
We’re proposing two possible deployment paths - depending on how you want to scale this internally.

Option 1: HubSpot CLI Deployment (Recommended)

The most efficient and scalable way to install these templates across multiple client portals. 
This approach allows us to:
  • Push templates directly via the HubSpot CLI 
  • Maintain Git-based version control for cleaner updates 
  • Deploy or update templates in minutes across all accounts

This is our standard deployment process for partner templates and works seamlessly for teams managing multiple portals.

Executive Summary 

Eventual is redesigning its HubSpot architecture to support three distinct distribution channels, unify its customer data model, and fully connect CRM operations to its Auth0 based infrastructure. You have an early stage but rapidly scaling environment, with distributors, homeowners, and embedded partners all generating revenue in different ways. 
This two month engagement focuses on building the correct multi channel HubSpot foundation, ensuring clean data flow from Auth0 into HubSpot, and giving your BDR team the tools, reporting, and workflows required to support real revenue operations across your Direct to Consumer, Independent Agent, and Embedded Partnership channels. 
This is a hands on build where you work directly with me, not a team. I will architect the system, configure the pipelines, design the data model, build required automations, and support your engineering team on Auth0 field mapping and events. At the end of two months you will have a fully aligned, multi channel HubSpot system that can scale.  

Objective

 
Create a unified, scalable HubSpot architecture that supports Eventual’s three distribution channels, integrates cleanly with Auth0 events, standardizes deal creation around the homeowner, and provides clear attribution, reporting, and lifecycle automation across the full revenue engine.
 
 

Scope of Work

 
1. Multi Channel Pipelines and Deal Architecture
 
  • Create three purpose built pipelines for DTC, Independent Agent, and Embedded Partner channels
  • Align deal stages to your quote, bind, activation, and renewal process
  • Standardize a single homeowner deal object with all required custom properties
  • Implement association rules to tie deals to homeowners, agents, agencies, embedded partners, and owners
  • Configure BDR attribution properties and labels for clean reporting

2. Auth0 Integration and Automated Deal Creation
 
  • Define the full field mapping strategy between Auth0 and HubSpot
  • Identify triggering events for new deal creation and deal updates
  • Align address, monthly fee, product selection, homeowner details, channel attribution, and status into HubSpot
  • Work directly with your engineering team to support their API implementation and validate successful processing across all three channels

3. Reporting, Attribution and Dashboards
 
  • Build BDR attribution logic across DTC, Agent, and Embedded channels
  • Create dashboards for BDR level performance, pipeline value, deal progression, and conversion rates
  • Build executive dashboards for recurring revenue, Premium Lock monthly fees, and channel performance

4. Email Automation and Lifecycle Workflows
 
  • Replace scattered sales sequences with centralized, branded HubSpot workflows
  • Build logic for agent onboarding, partner enablement, and nurture journeys
  • Build or refine homeowner lifecycle workflows triggered by deal or Auth0 events
  • Connect the correct branded emails and ensure the right people enter the right workflow at the right time

5. Data Migration, Documentation and Training
 
  • Refactor and migrate existing contacts, companies, and deals into the new multi channel architecture
  • Document all key components of the build including architecture diagrams, property definitions, integration notes, and reporting structure
  • Create role based dashboards and provide abbreviated BDR training for ongoing use and maintenance

 

Deliverables

 
  • Three complete pipelines with standardized deal object
  • Custom properties, association rules, and attribution logic
  • Auth0 field mapping and integration plan
  • Automated deal creation and update logic
  • Channel specific dashboards and executive reporting
  • Centralized lifecycle workflows with auto enrollment logic
  • Documentation playbook and BDR facing training assets

 

Timeline

 
This is a structured two month engagement broken into the following phases:
 
Weeks 1 to 2
Architecture design, property creation, pipeline build, association strategy
Auth0 mapping definition and integration plan with engineering team

Weeks 3 to 4
Workflow logic, agent onboarding journeys, homeowner lifecycle automations
BDR attribution modeling, reporting framework, initial dashboards

Weeks 5 to 8
Data migration into new architecture
Refinements, QA, documentation playbook, and BDR training sessions
 
 

Investment

 
Total Cost for Two Months: 9,500

job_title: HubSpot Optimization for Data Integrity, Sales Process, and Reporting
job_description:
Project Overview 
We are seeking a HubSpot expert to help us restructure and optimize our current setup. The project centers on improving data consistency, enhancing reporting capabilities, and enforcing standardized input at key stages in our sales process. 
This will be a defined project engagement, with the potential for follow-on phases. Our top priorities are data structure, property and workflow improvements, and reporting dashboards. 
Project Scope 
 1. Data Model & Property Enhancements 
·       Audit and adjust existing deal, contact, and company properties 
·       Rename or restructure existing picklists (e.g. Business Line) 
·       Create new properties to support reporting and process needs, including: 
o   Date of Initial Contact 
o   Type of Inquiry (Formal RFP / Informal) 
o   Deal Source (Campaign, Website, Referral, Conference, etc.) 
o   Business Line (New picklist: MCG Power Ops, Paragon, Versify, InnoTap, Unco) 
o   Product Pick List (Grouped under Business Line) 
o   Solution Configuration (Single or bundled products; identify which) 
o   Quality of Opportunity (Scored 1–5 based on defined factors) 
 2. Enforcing Data Capture 
·       Configure field-level requirements to ensure key data is captured when a deal is created or modified 
·       Add guidance to ensure consistency across teams 
·       Optionally introduce workflows or validation logic to guide users toward complete entries 
3. Reporting & Dashboards 
We want to eliminate our dependence on Excel by building robust, filterable reports inside HubSpot. Reporting goals include: 
·       Lead activity: Formal/informal RFPs over trailing 12 months 
·       Sales velocity: Time from inquiry → quote → close 
·       Pipeline analysis: Proposals made, prospects qualified, contracts closed 
·       Upsell tracking: Quoted vs Won 
·       Product-level and business line performance 
·       Customer segmentation (e.g. geography, industry, solution type) 
4. Internal Enablement 
We want our internal team to understand what’s been implemented and how to work with it. This includes: 
·       Walkthroughs of the new data structure and reports 
·       Guidance for our finance lead (Andy) and commercial lead (Dave) to become more self-sufficient 
·       Documentation to help us maintain the system long-term 
5. Optional Phase Two (Future) 
Depending on the outcome of this initial project, we may explore: 
·       Integrating quote and invoicing workflows 
·       Manual or automated handoff to QuickBooks Desktop (note: we are not using QuickBooks Online) 
·       Deeper analytics or customized dashboards 
Pricing 
We would prefer a fix price proposal. 
Timeline 
We are ready to begin immediately and expect to review proposals and select a Pro by Monday, July 22. Our aim is to complete the core project within 4–6 weeks. Timing for any optional follow-on work will be addressed separately. 
Our HubSpot Setup 
·       In use since: 2020 
·       Primary Hubs: Sales (recently added Marketing Hub) 
·       User Base: ~20 active users 
·       Current Limitations: 
o   Manual deal creation (no automation from forms) 
o   Inconsistent use of properties 
o   Reporting reliant on Excel 
·       Desired Outcome: Structured deal input, real-time dashboards, scalable data model 
What Success Looks Like 
·       A clean, consistent data structure that supports our sales and reporting needs 
·       Native HubSpot reports that replace Excel for pipeline, lead, and upsell tracking 
·       Improved internal knowledge so we can build additional reports or workflows ourselves 
Clear documentation and a system we can own confidently going forward

My partner Bryan and I help B2B companies turn their HubSpot instances into the efficient hubs of your business that you always intended it to be. We're particularly focused on: 
  • Building HubSpot architectures that enforce data consistency

  • Enabling teams to confidently maintain their systems

We typically work with clients in 3 stages - Discovery, Architecting, and Implementing/Training. To meet your timeline, we'll aim to have this all done within 5 weeks giving ourselves a week buffer
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Timeline

job_title: Hubspot Cleanup Project & Setting Up Proper Data Structure Processes To Limit Future Issues
