proposal_description:
Hi there,
Thanks for laying this out so clearly. What you’re describing is very common after multiple CRM migrations, especially moving from Salesforce to Dynamics and then into HubSpot. Data issues tend to compound over time, and without clear ownership and governance, reporting becomes unreliable fast.

This is less about a one-time cleanup and more about resetting your CRM foundation so it can actually support sales, marketing, and leadership decisions moving forward.

How We’d Recommend Approaching This

We typically structure engagements like this in phases, followed by ongoing support.

Phase 1: Audit and Data Foundation
We start by understanding what’s actually happening inside your portal today. That includes:
  • Auditing contacts, companies, deals, lifecycle stages, and custom properties
  • Identifying duplication, outdated fields, and inconsistent usage
  • Reviewing reporting and dashboards to pinpoint why insights are unreliable
  • Mapping where data breaks down across Sales and Marketing

This gives us a clear picture of what needs to be cleaned, standardized, or retired before making changes.

Phase 2: Cleanup and Structural Fixes
Based on the audit, we then:
  • Clean and deduplicate records
  • Standardize lifecycle stages, lead statuses, and required fields
  • Align Sales and Marketing on what data matters and when it should be updated
  • Rebuild key reports so leadership can trust what they’re seeing

Phase 3: SOPs and Data Governance
Cleanup alone doesn’t stick without process. We document and implement:
  • Clear SOPs for data entry and updates
  • Rules around ownership and accountability
  • Guardrails in HubSpot to prevent future degradation
  • Simple enablement so teams actually follow the process

Ongoing Support
Given your history and ongoing needs, this works best as a monthly retainer where we:
  • Monitor data quality
  • Handle ongoing cleanup and optimization
  • Adjust processes as your team evolves
  • Support reporting, workflows, and RevOps improvements over time

Budget and Engagement Model

A monthly retainer is the right fit here. Most teams in a similar situation land in the $3,000 to $6,000 per month range, depending on data volume, urgency, and reporting complexity.

We’d suggest starting with an initial audit and cleanup phase, then rolling directly into ongoing support so the improvements don’t erode over time.

While Profoundly requires selecting a payment option, our standard approach is monthly invoicing once we align on scope.

A Few Quick Questions Before We Start

To scope this accurately, we’d want to confirm:
  1. Roughly how many contacts, companies, and deals are in HubSpot today?
  2. Which reports are currently the biggest pain point for leadership?
  3. Are lifecycle stages and lead statuses clearly defined today?
  4. Do you have Ops Hub or any deduplication tools enabled?
  5. Are there active integrations feeding data into HubSpot?
  6. Who currently owns data quality internally?

Next Step 

The best next step is a short discovery call so we can confirm fit, scope the initial phase, and recommend the right retainer level.

proposal_description:
HubSpot Optimization & Customer Journey Enablement — Phase 1 Audit & Discovery

Your challenge is real: HubSpot Pro works, but fragmented data silos, manual processes, and inconsistent automation across pre-lead through renewal are creating bottlenecks. The Enterprise upgrade question adds pressure—you need to know if it's the right move for your specific operations, not just because the tier exists.

I specialize in exactly this: architecting unified data models and automations that turn HubSpot from a tool you maintain into an ops backbone that scales with you.

Here's what I've seen go wrong in similar situations:
Most audits and implementations stop at feature recommendations. That leaves you guessing about: (1) Which fragmented systems are actually killing your efficiency? (2) Is Enterprise ROI-positive for your use case, or should you maximize Pro first? (3) Will your teams actually adopt the new workflows once built?

I approach it differently: I audit for system impact, not features—then build a clear roadmap that shows you exactly what's worth investing in.

Here's what I recommend as your first step:

Discovery & Diagnostic Sprint: 2 weeks, $5,500
Before committing to a full Phase 2 implementation (which could be $25-40K+), you need three specific things:

Deliverable 1: Data Architecture Audit
  • Current state diagram showing where data lives across your tech stack
  • Integration fault tree: which silos are blocking what outcomes
  • Data quality assessment: deduplication needs, enrichment gaps, ownership issues

Deliverable 2: Enterprise ROI Analysis (Specific to Your Ops)
  • Does Pro→Enterprise upgrade pay for itself in operational gains? (Quantified)
  • What capabilities are you actually missing in Pro today?
  • Timeline & cost comparison: maximize Pro now vs. upgrade + optimize later

Deliverable 3: 90-Day Phase 2 Implementation Roadmap
  • Sequenced priorities: lifecycle automation first, then dashboards, then integrations
  • Technical dependencies: which builds unlock the next
  • Resource requirements, risk register, realistic timeline

How this works:
Week 1: Discovery calls + audit of current setup (I'll need access to your HubSpot instance, brief walkthrough of integrations, and 2-3 stakeholder conversations). You'll see early findings daily.
Week 2: Deliver all three artifacts + 1 strategy call to review + answer questions.
Outcome: You have a clear picture. If you proceed to Phase 2, you're funding a known, sequenced roadmap—not a guessing game. If you decide to optimize Pro first or pause, you still have the architecture blueprint.

What happens after the Diagnostic Sprint:
If you want to move to Phase 2 implementation, I transition to embedded retainer model: $8-12K/month, 3-4 agreed deliverables per week, unlimited support, and strategic monthly planning sessions. Average engagement 2-5 years as I become your ops infrastructure partner.

Why I'm the right fit for this:
I don't do basic HubSpot configuration or marketing campaign setup. I specialize in custom architecture—complex data models, multi-system integrations (ERP, billing, inventory), custom UI extensions, and workflows that native HubSpot can't handle.

I've built: inventory management systems that reduced proposal time from 2 hours to 15 minutes, multi-location association logic for scaling companies, Stripe integrations providing real-time subscription visibility, and architected systems that grew with clients from $10M to $500M+ revenue.

I work best with ops/revenue leaders who understand that HubSpot is a system, not a tool—and are willing to invest in getting the architecture right upfront so it scales without constant firefighting.

I'm based in Roseville, CA, available to start within 48 hours of agreement, and I maintain ongoing partnerships with clients over years (not quick fixes).

Ready to move forward?
Let's schedule a 45-minute discovery call this week. I'll ask five specific questions about your current state and timeline, you'll see if I'm the right partner, and if we're aligned, we can kick off the Diagnostic Sprint by end of week.

Calendar: https://calendly.com/unified-support-solutions/meeting

Or feel free to message me any questions on the platform first or you can call me at 818-645-5832.

Ryan Ginsberg

job_description:
Project Overview 
We are seeking a HubSpot expert to help us restructure and optimize our current setup. The project centers on improving data consistency, enhancing reporting capabilities, and enforcing standardized input at key stages in our sales process. 
This will be a defined project engagement, with the potential for follow-on phases. Our top priorities are data structure, property and workflow improvements, and reporting dashboards. 
Project Scope 
 1. Data Model & Property Enhancements 
·       Audit and adjust existing deal, contact, and company properties 
·       Rename or restructure existing picklists (e.g. Business Line) 
·       Create new properties to support reporting and process needs, including: 
o   Date of Initial Contact 
o   Type of Inquiry (Formal RFP / Informal) 
o   Deal Source (Campaign, Website, Referral, Conference, etc.) 
o   Business Line (New picklist: MCG Power Ops, Paragon, Versify, InnoTap, Unco) 
o   Product Pick List (Grouped under Business Line) 
o   Solution Configuration (Single or bundled products; identify which) 
o   Quality of Opportunity (Scored 1–5 based on defined factors) 
 2. Enforcing Data Capture 
·       Configure field-level requirements to ensure key data is captured when a deal is created or modified 
·       Add guidance to ensure consistency across teams 
·       Optionally introduce workflows or validation logic to guide users toward complete entries 
3. Reporting & Dashboards 
We want to eliminate our dependence on Excel by building robust, filterable reports inside HubSpot. Reporting goals include: 
·       Lead activity: Formal/informal RFPs over trailing 12 months 
·       Sales velocity: Time from inquiry → quote → close 
·       Pipeline analysis: Proposals made, prospects qualified, contracts closed 
·       Upsell tracking: Quoted vs Won 
·       Product-level and business line performance 
·       Customer segmentation (e.g. geography, industry, solution type) 
4. Internal Enablement 
We want our internal team to understand what’s been implemented and how to work with it. This includes: 
·       Walkthroughs of the new data structure and reports 
·       Guidance for our finance lead (Andy) and commercial lead (Dave) to become more self-sufficient 
·       Documentation to help us maintain the system long-term 
5. Optional Phase Two (Future) 
Depending on the outcome of this initial project, we may explore: 
·       Integrating quote and invoicing workflows 
·       Manual or automated handoff to QuickBooks Desktop (note: we are not using QuickBooks Online) 
·       Deeper analytics or customized dashboards 
Pricing 
We would prefer a fix price proposal. 
Timeline 
We are ready to begin immediately and expect to review proposals and select a Pro by Monday, July 22. Our aim is to complete the core project within 4–6 weeks. Timing for any optional follow-on work will be addressed separately. 
Our HubSpot Setup 
·       In use since: 2020 
·       Primary Hubs: Sales (recently added Marketing Hub) 
·       User Base: ~20 active users 
·       Current Limitations: 
o   Manual deal creation (no automation from forms) 
o   Inconsistent use of properties 
o   Reporting reliant on Excel 
·       Desired Outcome: Structured deal input, real-time dashboards, scalable data model 
What Success Looks Like 
·       A clean, consistent data structure that supports our sales and reporting needs 
·       Native HubSpot reports that replace Excel for pipeline, lead, and upsell tracking 
·       Improved internal knowledge so we can build additional reports or workflows ourselves 
Clear documentation and a system we can own confidently going forward

job_description:

Time management: We would need a rough idea of how many hours each "project" (e.g., building out an agreed number of reports and associated dashboards) would take once scoped but prior to launching (e.g., 10 reports for 3 dashboards would take 3 hours - making numbers up here). Also would need a regular update (probably weekly) of how many hours we've used that month. 

About us: We have an enterprise subscription. Our culture is collaborative, creative, and hard-working (wow, bunch of alliteration there). We think we're a pretty fun group of humans to work with. We have two full time employees who are acting as a part-time admin and a part-time project manager; our head of dev and head of sales/marketing is pretty involved as well. We're used to having a weekly meeting to talk through current initiatives and work through challenges. This is not a meeting that could be an email; we have a specific, detailed agenda where each topic has a specific goal and status updates are more talking through challenges that came up when somebody was handling their part that need some collaboration. 

Full transparency: we have been working with a firm for about two years, but they recently raised their prices ($172/hour up from $152/hour) so looking to do a price check. This is not a procurement, check-the-box type request - we are seriously considering a switch. If we decide to switch, we are looking to implement in early January. 

Happy to answer any other questions
proposal_description:
Hi there, 
Thanks for the detailed and thoughtful overview. This is exactly the kind of relationship we tend to work best in, ongoing, enterprise-level HubSpot support where priorities evolve and the value comes from both execution and strategic guidance.

Based on what you shared, this clearly fits an ongoing HubSpot optimization and platform enablement retainer. The mix of reporting, workflows, data cleanup, CMS support, product updates, and custom APIs is very familiar territory for us, and we regularly support teams with a similar internal structure of a part-time admin, PM, and technical leadership. 

A few important notes up front, in the spirit of transparency. 

On budget and scope 
The “under $1,000” selection looks more like a platform constraint than a true reflection of scope. At 35 hours per month of senior, cross-hub HubSpot support, this is firmly an enterprise retainer. For context, our standard rate is $150 per hour, which would put a 35 hour engagement at $5,250 per month. That is also meaningfully below the $172 per hour you are currently paying, while still allowing us to staff the work appropriately. 

If it’s helpful, we are also open to starting slightly smaller, for example 20 to 25 hours per month, as a transition period if you want to validate fit before fully switching partners.

 How we typically run retainers like this 
We put a lot of structure around time management and visibility, since that is clearly important to you. 

What this looks like in practice: 

  • We scope and estimate discrete work items before starting them, including rough hour ranges for things like reports, dashboards, or workflow builds. 
  • We track time against those scoped items so you always know where hours are going. 
  • We provide a weekly update with hours used, remaining hours, and what is in progress or queued. 
  • We help you prioritize when requests exceed available hours, rather than just burning time. 

This tends to work especially well in environments where monthly scope evolves. 

On the CMS rebuild you mentioned 
Moving from custom code to HubSpot building blocks so marketing can self-serve is a very smart goal. That type of CMS rebuild is typically best handled as a separately scoped project, even if it is delivered alongside a retainer. Once we understand page count, templates, custom modules, and migration needs, we can give you a clear hour range and timeline without it unexpectedly consuming your monthly support hours. 

Fit and experience 
We are a HubSpot Solutions Partner with hands-on experience across Marketing, Sales, Service, CMS, and Operations Hubs, including Enterprise accounts. Our team includes strategists, developers, RevOps specialists, and reporting experts, so you are not dependent on a single generalist for everything from APIs to dashboards. 

Next step 
The easiest way to see if this is a good mutual fit is a short discovery call where we can: 
  • Sanity-check the 35 hour monthly scope 
  • Talk through how hours are currently being spent 
  • Identify any immediate red flags or quick wins 
  • Discuss what a clean transition from your current partner would look like 

You can grab time here: https://www.eternalworks.com/meetings/timjones/discovery-call

And if helpful, you can also review our HubSpot certifications and partner profile here: https://ecosystem.hubspot.com/marketplace/solutions/eternal-works

Happy to answer any questions in the meantime, and appreciate the candid context you shared.

Thanks & Much Respect,
Tim - CEO + Founder
Eternal Works
HubSpot | AI | Marketing | Web Design | Branding | Sales Enablement
www.eternalworks.com

job_title: Custom Card Development
job_description:
We are looking to develop a custom integration card that connects PandaDoc with a custom object in HubSpot. This integration should enable seamless synchronization between the two platforms, ensuring efficient document handling and status tracking.
Key requirements for the integration include:
  1. Property Synchronization
    The integration must automatically sync custom properties from the specified HubSpot custom object to a pre-defined PandaDoc template. This ensures that all relevant data is accurately reflected in the generated document without manual input.
  2. Status Updates Based on Approval Workflow
    The system should monitor the approval status of the PandaDoc document. Based on this status (e.g., sent, viewed, approved, declined), the integration must update the corresponding pipeline stage of the custom object in HubSpot to reflect the current progress.
  3. PDF Generation and Storage
    Once the document reaches its final state (e.g., approved or completed), a PDF version should be generated and automatically stored within the associated custom object record in HubSpot for reference and recordkeeping.
The end goal is a fully integrated and automated workflow between PandaDoc and HubSpot that minimizes manual data entry, maintains up-to-date pipeline statuses, and ensures easy access to finalized documents.
Desired Time Frame: Within 2 weeks
proposal_description:
Proposal: Custom PandaDoc–HubSpot Integration Card
Submitted by: Walter Cervantes / Reforge Solutions

🔧 Project Overview

You’re looking to streamline document workflows between PandaDoc and a custom object in HubSpot, and I can help you build a custom integration card that enables seamless synchronization of data, status updates, and PDF storage.

I’ve worked extensively with HubSpot custom objects, automation, and external integrations, and I understand the importance of reducing manual effort while ensuring reliability in deal workflows and document handling.

✅ Deliverables
1. 🔄 Property Synchronization
• Automatically map and sync key custom properties from the HubSpot custom object into a PandaDoc template.
• Support for dynamic field population to minimize user effort and human error.
2. 📊 Status-Driven Pipeline Updates
• Monitor PandaDoc document status in real time (e.g., sent, viewed, approved, declined).
• Automatically update the associated custom object’s pipeline stage in HubSpot to reflect the latest document status.
3. 📁 PDF Generation + Storage
• Once a document is finalized (approved or completed), the integration will generate a PDF version.
• The PDF will be automatically stored in the file attachments of the related custom object record in HubSpot for easy retrieval.

🛠️ Technical Approach
• Tech Stack: HubSpot Custom CRM Cards + Webhooks + PandaDoc API
• Authentication: OAuth/Private App tokens for secure access
• Hosting: Serverless (e.g., AWS Lambda or Render.com) or hosted Node.js/Python backend
• Error Handling: Built-in logging + retry logic for reliability

Deliverable

ETA

• $2,500 upon completion and delivery of final working integration

Includes testing, handoff documentation, and post-launch support for 7 days.

🤝 Why Me
• 7+ years in RevOps, HubSpot consulting, and API-based integrations
• Deep experience working with custom objects and PandaDoc
• I focus on clean, scalable architecture that’s easy to maintain and enhance later

🚀 Next Steps

If this proposal aligns with what you’re looking for, I’d be happy to:
1. Schedule a quick alignment call
2. Finalize scope and field mapping
3. Send over a kickoff invoice and start building

Looking forward to helping you streamline your HubSpot ↔ PandaDoc workflow!

RevOps Consultant | HubSpot Expert

job_title: HubSpot ↔ Abtrac Integration Build
job_description:
Hi team,

Apologies, I'd located a contractor for this job but they have since become unavailable. 

I have two clients looking for this integration currently with urgency:
 
Objective
Automate the workflow from HubSpot Deals to Abtrac Jobs:
  1. When a HubSpot Deal reaches the “Quoting” stage:
    • Generate a unique Quote Number.
    • Create a HubSpot Quote linked to the Deal, Company, and Contact.
    • Write the Quote Number back to both the Deal and the Quote.
  2. When the Quote is signed:
    • Create a Job in Abtrac.
    • Map and upsert related Company → Client, Contact → Contact, Deal → Job, Line Items → Tasks.
    • Update the HubSpot Deal with the Abtrac Job ID and status.

Core Requirements
  • Tech Stack: Prefer Node.js/TypeScript serverless function.
  • HubSpot:
    • Auth via Private App Token.
    • Use CRM v3 APIs for Deals, Quotes, Line Items, Companies, Contacts.
    • Implement Workflows to trigger webhooks for middleware.
  • Abtrac:
    • Use API to upsert Clients/Contacts, create Jobs and Tasks.
    • Confirm required fields, enums, rate limits.
  • Mapping:
    • Company → Client
    • Contact → Contact
    • Deal → Job
    • Line Item → Task
  • Idempotency: Prevent duplicate object creation.

  • Google Sheet mapping table provided for data object mapping.

  • Documentation: Endpoint references, environment setup, runbook.

Ideal Candidate
  • Proven track record building HubSpot integrations.
  • Familiarity with Abtrac API or similar job management systems.
  • Strong understanding of API authentication, associations, and mapping.
  • Ability to communicate clearly and document thoroughly.
proposal_description:
Hi there, 

Thanks for the detailed overview—and for circling back after your original contractor fell through. 

This HubSpot ↔ Abtrac integration aligns closely with work I’ve done helping teams automate clean handoffs between CRM and project systems. 

From what you’ve outlined, the flow looks like: 

  1. At the “Quoting” stage in HubSpot, generate a unique quote number, 
  2. Create a Quote associated with the Deal, Company, and Contact, Write that quote number back into HubSpot, 
  3. And once the quote is signed, create a Job in Abtrac—upserting the related Client, Contact, Job, and Tasks—then update HubSpot with the Abtrac Job ID and status. 

That’s a thoughtful architecture—and very much in my lane. 

There are a few details I’d want to clarify together:
 
  1. How are you detecting when a quote is signed—via HubSpot, a connected e-signature tool, or something else? 
  2. What does Abtrac’s API authentication look like (OAuth2, API key)? 
  3. Are there any known rate limits, required fields, or enums that need to be mapped early? 
  4. Any constraints around quote number generation or deduplication logic? 

I don’t approach this work as an outside agency—I embed directly within teams, co-designing solutions that are efficient, scalable, and built to last.

The best next step is a 60-minute discovery call.  This is where we’ll get clear on what’s working, where things get stuck, and what a right-sized solution could look like.

You can book a time here: https://calendly.com/unified-support-solutions/60-minute-discovery-call or reach me directly at (818) 645-5832

·         Provide targeted training for our supervisors and admins (live + documented + videos)
·         Offer strategic guidance on user roles, permissions, and settings to ensure smooth adoption

·         Audit of our existing Zendesk ticketing setup
·         Design and configuration of HubSpot’s Service Hub ticketing tools
·         Creation of reusable macros, workflows, and automations
·         Development of performance and team activity reports
·         Supervisor/admin training sessions (live and recorded)
·         Documentation outlining configurations, settings, and best practices

Timeline

Qualifications
·         Proven experience implementing HubSpot Service Hub (Help Desk)
·         Strong understanding of Zendesk workflows and support operations
·         Demonstrated ability to replicate or enhance Zendesk functionality in HubSpot
·         Experience building automations, macros, and reporting dashboards
·         Excellent communication and training skills
·         (Preferred) HubSpot Solutions Partner or Certified HubSpot Consultant

Questions for You

At Ambs Call Center, we care deeply about service quality and the people who deliver it. You’ll be working with a collaborative, detail-oriented team that values clear communication, practical solutions, and long-term partnerships.

proposal_description:
Hello,

Your agent onboarding automation requires connecting multiple systems that each need to complete successfully before the next step begins. I've built similar multi-step processes that handle payment confirmation → account setup → system activation smoothly, even when things don't go as planned.

Here's what typically breaks (and how I prevent it)

Payment timing: Agent pays but the system doesn't know = they're stuck waiting
→ I ensure payment confirmation triggers the next steps automatically

System overload: When you jump from 2 to 20 agents, Google and other tools can't keep up
→ I build in smart pacing so everything processes smoothly

Process gaps: Agent is set up but not getting leads (or getting leads before they're ready)
→ I make sure every step completes before moving to the next

Your specific build would include
✓ HubSpot as command center - Track every agent from application to active status
✓ Native HubSpot workflows first - I maximize built-in tools before adding complexity
✓ Stripe payment automation - Seamless payment tracking when HubSpot Payments isn't enough
✓ Account setup automation - Google Workspace + website access created automatically
✓ Smart lead assignment - New agents enter the rotation only when fully ready
✓ Complete visibility - One dashboard showing every agent's current status

Behind the scenes, I carefully map out each connection point with proper logging, error handling, automatic retries, and notifications for both successes and failures. You'll always know what's working and what needs attention.

How I'm different
I start in a sandbox—prototyping solutions, gathering your feedback, and refining until it's production-ready. I prioritize native HubSpot functionality wherever possible (it's more reliable and easier to maintain), only building custom integrations when truly needed.

You're not hiring an agency or dealing with account managers—you get direct access to me. I become part of your team, monitoring these systems and catching issues before they affect your agents. When your needs change (and they will), I'm already there to adapt the system.

job_title: HubSpot Data Cleanup, Best Practices, and Affinity CRM Integration
job_description:
Company Overview:
We’re Partners for Growth (PFG) — a global growth-debt and asset-backed financing partner supporting innovative companies around the world. With our CMO’s recent departure, I’ve stepped in to manage our HubSpot account and am looking for expert guidance to ensure it’s set up, maintained, and used effectively.

Project Description:
Our CMO recently left, and I’ve taken over responsibility for managing HubSpot. I’m new to the platform and need help understanding what we have, what’s working, and what needs improvement.

We currently have around 55,000 contacts in Affinity and need guidance on how best to organize, clean, and manage this data going forward. We also use Affinity CRM for relationship and deal tracking and would like advice on how to align or integrate it with HubSpot in a way that’s efficient and sustainable.

I’m looking for a HubSpot expert who can:

  • Audit our existing HubSpot setup across Marketing, Sales, and CRM


  • Lead a comprehensive data cleanup (contacts, companies, properties, lists, and workflows)


  • Provide consultancy on HubSpot best practices for data structure, automation, and reporting


  • Advise on or implement integration with Affinity CRM


  • Offer hands-on help and 1:1 guidance so I feel confident managing HubSpot going forward



Deliverables:
  • Full audit of current HubSpot setup and data


  • Action plan for data cleanup and optimization


  • Recommendations on how to manage 55,000 existing contacts


  • Best practice framework for future use (segmentation, automation, reporting)


  • Guidance or implementation support for HubSpot ↔ Affinity CRM integration


  • Optional ongoing consultation or coaching sessions



Ideal Pro:
We’re looking for a Profoundly Certified Pro who:
  • Has deep experience cleaning and structuring HubSpot data at scale


  • Understands CRM integrations, especially with platforms like Affinity


  • Can clearly explain both the “why” and “how” behind their recommendations


  • Balances strategy and hands-on execution


  • Is approachable, patient, and great at helping non-technical users gain confidence
proposal_description:
I can see why this feels urgent – losing your CMO and suddenly being responsible for HubSpot is like being handed the keys to a car you've never driven. But here's what I'm noticing: you're asking for a HubSpot cleanup when what you actually have is a revenue infrastructure problem.

Most companies in your situation have Affinity for relationships and HubSpot for... something else? That's the first question that needs answering. When I work with PE-backed or growth-stage financial firms, tool redundancy is usually a symptom of unclear go-to-market strategy. You're spending money on two systems, neither of which is probably delivering the insights you need to make decisions.

Here's the pattern I see play out: Company does a big HubSpot cleanup, spends $5-10K getting data pristine, builds beautiful dashboards... and six months later, nobody's using it because there was never clarity on WHO owns what data, what business decisions it needs to support, or how it connects to your actual revenue model.

A few questions that would help me understand if we're the right fit:

1. Beyond having "clean data," what business outcome are you unable to achieve right now? (For example: Can't execute ABM campaigns? Can't forecast pipeline accurately? Sales team working blind? Marketing ROI is invisible?)

2. Why do you have both Affinity and HubSpot? Which system is your source of truth, and which one is creating duplicate work or confusion?

3. When your CMO was in place, was HubSpot driving meaningful business results, or was it already a question mark? This tells me whether we're fixing something broken or building something new.

4. Who internally will own your marketing and revenue operations function long-term? Because if you're the interim owner and someone else is getting hired, we could be building for the wrong person's preferences.

5. If you spend the next 6 months operating with fragmented data across two systems and no clear marketing leadership, what does that cost you in terms of deals you can't effectively nurture or market insights you can't act on?

Now, let me be direct about scope and budget: What you've described – comprehensive audit, data cleanup of 55K contacts, integration work, best practice implementation, AND ongoing consultation – is realistically $15-30K worth of work if done properly. Your $1-5K budget might cover a strategic assessment and roadmap, but not the execution.

What I'd suggest: Let's spend 30-45 minutes on a diagnostic call where I can:
- Help you understand what's actually broken vs. what just feels overwhelming
- Give you a realistic view of what different levels of investment would buy you
- Potentially save you money by showing you what you DON't need to do
- Point you toward the highest-impact starting point given your constraints

If after that conversation you want to work together, great. If you decide you need a different type of help, or want to handle pieces internally, I'll tell you exactly how to do that.

No charge for the diagnostic – I just need to understand the problem before I can tell you if I'm the right person to solve it.

Does that approach make sense?

job_title: Enrich Contact records in Hubspot from Wealth Engine API
job_description: We need to create an integration between HubSpot and Wealth Engine to enrich a filtered set of contact records.
proposal_description:
Hi,

A few quick questions to ensure the HubSpot ⇄ WealthEngine enrichment is precise and compliant. Which contacts do you want enriched (saved filters/static lists, lifecycle stage, or all net-new)? What are the primary match keys you’d like to use, email only, or name + postal address/phone for higher confidence? How frequently should enrichment run (one-time backfill + daily delta, or event-driven on create/update)? On the WealthEngine side, which API/version and endpoints do you have access to, and are there rate-limit constraints we should design around? For HubSpot, do you already have custom properties defined for WealthEngine attributes (e.g., P2G score, net worth bands, giving capacity, affinity segments), or should I provision a namespaced property set and mapping? Finally, any consent/GDPR rules, field-level visibility needs, or audit logging requirements we should adhere to?

I’ve led several HubSpot enrichment and scoring builds for finance and nonprofit teams, where we connected external prospecting/wealth data sources into HubSpot, implemented deterministic/heuristic match logic, and wrote back normalized properties for lists, workflows, and lead scoring, plus dashboards and error logs so RevOps could trust the data pipeline.

My approach here would be: perform a quick audit of your HubSpot contact model and WealthEngine response schema; confirm match keys, rate limits, and required attributes; then deliver a phased implementation. Phase 1 sets up a secure HubSpot Private App, creates a property map for WealthEngine fields, and runs a controlled backfill on a filtered list. Phase 2 adds ongoing enrichment via either (a) a HubSpot Workflow + Custom Code action calling WealthEngine or (b) a lightweight middleware (AWS Lambda/Cloudflare Workers) to handle retries, queuing, and rate-limit smoothing.

Throughout, I’ll include confidence scoring, duplicate protection, and opt-in governance; and I’ll provide Ops-friendly monitoring (custom object log + report) so you can see successes, skips, and errors in one place.

If you have sample contacts or a sandbox API key, I can begin with a small proof on a 100–200 record list to validate match rates and property writes before we scale.

If you have any specifics to add or preferred WealthEngine attributes you want prioritized, I’m all ears. Please pick a time that works best for you here: https://cal.com/uttamm/30-min-meeting-profoundly 

Looking forward to collaborating with you! 

Best,
Uttam.

proposal_description:
Hey there, 

This project sounds like a fantastic opportunity, and your Dec 2025 – Mar 2026 window is perfect for doing it right: discovery, design-dev alignment, and a clean, modular HubSpot build that lasts for years. 

You’re already thinking the right way by pairing a design partner with a HubSpot CMS developer. Your design firm can focus on visual excellence, while our role can be to translate that design system into fast, reusable, marketer-friendly components inside HubSpot.

That’s exactly what we do every day at OneMetric, a HubSpot Elite Partner with a dedicated CMS engineering team. 
Here are some ideas worth exploring as you shape the plan: 

1. Build / Buy / Modify strategy 

We can either: 
  • Build a custom modular theme – gives you full control, lean code, and scalability.
  • Buy & harden a marketplace theme – faster start, then optimize for performance and accessibility.
  • Modify your current theme – quick win, but limited lifespan.

    Most enterprises end up choosing a hybrid: start from a proven base, then evolve it into a design-system-driven custom theme.

2. Design-to-development handshake
 
We’ll turn your design tokens (color, typography, spacing) into global theme variables and a library of 20-30 drag-and-drop modules like hero, grids, data tables, proof bars, tab blocks, timelines, resource cards, CTAs, etc.
 Your marketing team will be able to spin up new pages without touching code, while the design system stays consistent.
 
3. Content model & migration
 
We’ll define structured content types (Solutions, Products, Resources, Webinars, Team, Regions) and use HubDB for repeatable data like integrations or events. All URLs, redirects, and metadata will be mapped to maintain SEO authority when you cut over from the old site.

4. Performance, SEO, and accessibility baked-in
 
Every template ships with Lighthouse 90+ targets, responsive images, deferred JS, and semantic HTML. We include structured data (Organization, Article, FAQ) and ensure WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance out of the gate.

5. Editor experience & governance

Role-based editing, scheduled publishing, and locked “safe” module controls. We’ll also build page blueprints so marketers can clone pre-approved layouts for campaigns in minutes.

6. Analytics & integrations

HubSpot analytics + GA4 events built into modules, plus optional content gating tied to HubSpot lists for lead nurturing.

How this fits your sprint plan (Dec – Mar)
 
  • Weeks 1-3: Technical discovery + design token alignment with your vendor.
  • Weeks 4-8: Theme build – globals, header/footer, first 10 core modules, homepage, key templates.
  • Weeks 9-11: Secondary modules, migration prep, accessibility QA.
  • Weeks 12-13: Content loading, performance hardening, training, go-live rehearsal.

We’ve managed similar multi-sprint CMS rebuilds alongside outside design agencies, acting as the “HubSpot side” that keeps code clean, performance high, and collaboration friction-free.

> Segmentation Framework: Establish clear best practices for creating, maintaining, and cleaning up campaign segments across channels.
> Data Cleanup: Audit and organize existing records to ensure data integrity, reliable segmentation, and accurate reporting. Address issues such as incomplete or inconsistent data, duplicate records, and unclear channel attribution.
> Standard Campaign Frameworks: Build a few go-to campaign types (for example, nurture, referral, product launch, seasonal) with pre-built assets, workflows, and automation logic.
> HubSpot Workflow and Automation Optimization: Streamline lead assignment, channel attribution, and workflow logic. We currently have roughly 100 overlapping workflows and need to reduce conflicts and redundancy.
> Salesforce Integration Alignment: Ensure Salesforce ↔ HubSpot sync behavior supports the new campaign and workflow logic without creating duplicates or misaligned data.

Preferred Skills & Experience
  • > Deep experience in HubSpot Marketing Hub and Salesforce integration
  • > Proven track record in data hygiene, workflow optimization, and campaign automation
  • > Strong understanding of segmentation strategy, lead routing, and attribution modeling
  • > Ability to design repeatable campaign frameworks and templates
  • > Excellent documentation and communication skills to support internal enablement


What Success Looks Like
  • > Teams can quickly and confidently launch campaigns using standardized templates
  • > Segmentation is consistent and channel assignment is reliable
  • > Workflow automation is simplified and error-free
  • > Data is clean, complete, and accurate across HubSpot and Salesforce
  • > Clear documentation and best practices empower internal teams to self-serve

proposal_description:
Proposal Title: Strategic HubSpot Multi-Brand Implementation & Optimization

1. Executive Summary / Personal Statement

As the founder of Elevated Sprocket Solutions and a Profoundly Certified HubSpot Pro, I specialize in transforming business operations through expert CRM implementation, sales enablement, and marketing automation. With over five years of dedicated experience and a comprehensive suite of HubSpot certifications, I have a proven track record of helping businesses across diverse industries streamline their processes, enhance sales efficiency, and achieve significant growth. I am confident that my strategic approach to the HubSpot ecosystem can help [Client Name] overcome the challenge of managing three distinct brands that market to the same customer base and achieve its goal of a seamlessly integrated and efficient multi-brand CRM. This proposal outlines how we can configure your HubSpot instance to build a more efficient and profitable system for your business.

2. About Elevated Sprocket Solutions LLC

Elevated Sprocket Solutions LLC is a dedicated HubSpot Solutions Provider focused on empowering businesses to fully leverage their HubSpot investment. Our mission is to deliver end-to-end HubSpot solutions tailored to the unique needs of each client. From initial CRM setup and migration to advanced marketing automation and API integrations, we ensure seamless adoption and long-term success. We act as a primary HubSpot liaison for our clients, staying ahead of platform advancements to ensure you always receive the maximum benefit. Our commitment is reflected in our 100% 5-star client satisfaction rating.

3. Project Scope of Work

This project will focus on the successful integration of your two additional brands into your existing HubSpot portal, leveraging the multi-brand feature. The primary objective is to create a unified CRM environment that allows for brand-specific marketing and sales activities while maintaining a shared, comprehensive view of your customers.

Phase 1: Asset Migration and Brand Attribution

  • Objective: To ensure all existing assets are correctly associated with your primary brand.

  • Tasks:

    • Conduct a full audit of your current HubSpot assets (e.g., landing pages, emails, forms, workflows).

    • Migrate and attribute all identified assets to your currently on-boarded brand, ensuring a clean foundation for the multi-brand setup.

Phase 2: Configuration of New Brands

  • Objective: To establish the technical and marketing foundation for your two new brands within HubSpot.

  • Tasks:

    • Domain Configuration: Connect and verify the website and email sending domains for each of the two new brands. This includes the setup of DNS records (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) to ensure optimal email deliverability.

    • Ad Account Integration: Connect the respective ad accounts (e.g., Google Ads, Facebook Ads) for each new brand to enable accurate tracking and attribution.

    • Newsletter & Subscription Management: Create brand-specific newsletter sign-up forms and subscription types. Configure unsubscribe processes to be brand-specific, ensuring compliance and a positive user experience.

Phase 3: Team & Permission Structuring

  • Objective: To configure user access and visibility to align with your multi-brand operational needs.

  • Tasks:

    • Team Setup: Create distinct teams within HubSpot for each brand.

    • Permission Configuration: Define and implement user roles and permissions to control access to contacts, assets, and reporting based on brand alignment. This will ensure that teams can focus on their respective brand's activities while allowing for desired levels of cross-brand visibility for management.

4. My Skills & Expertise

My expertise lies in designing and executing custom business and marketing strategies that drive client acquisition and revenue growth. I offer a comprehensive suite of services to optimize your entire operational workflow.

Strategic & Implementation Skills:

  • CRM Implementation, Migration & Re-implementation: Leading full-scale HubSpot implementations, including complex migrations from other CRM platforms and strategic re-implementations to overhaul and optimize existing, underperforming HubSpot instances.

  • Multi-Brand HubSpot Architecture: Expertly structuring and managing multiple brands or business units within a single HubSpot instance, ensuring proper data partitioning, brand identity, and user permissions.

  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partnering closely with Sales, Marketing, and Operations teams to ensure strategic alignment, streamline end-to-end processes, and build solutions that serve the entire business.

  • Lead Lifecycle & Pipeline Management: Architecting the HubSpot Lead Object and defining clear MQL (Marketing Qualified Lead) and SQL (Sales Qualified Lead) criteria to ensure a seamless, data-driven handoff from marketing to sales.

  • Sales Enablement & Workflow Optimization: Designing automated workflows, building high-performing Sales Hub sequences, and providing the tools your sales team needs to close deals more efficiently.

Technical & Platform Skills:

job_title: Membership Portal
job_description: We are migrating this over to HubSpot: https://taxadvisornetwork.markjkohler.com/ We want our 'members' that pay a monthly fee to be able to login & update their profile page on the tax advisor network. We want them to have access only to their page and to be able to update image, bio, etc and publish. In Hubspot, it is my understanding that the membership portal is the best way to do this? We are looking to get a developer to help us build this out quickly. We've created the pages and moved over the existing data to a HubDb Table.  more: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fo0YMRMpWILrxN4dl38lC2QzKahRHihYPbQoJHkaxEg/edit?tab=t.0
proposal_description:
Hey there,

When I read through your brief, I could recognize the situation you’re in pages migrated over, HubDB structured, but now you need an actual membership portal experience that’s secure, scalable, and doesn’t create operational chaos for your team. We’ve stepped into this exact scenario many times: a business has the structure ready but needs someone who deeply understands HubSpot CMS + Memberships + Custom Objects + HubDB to bring it all together without duct tape or workarounds.

And you’re right when members can’t manage their own profiles, or when internal teams need to manually update HubDB rows, HubSpot quickly becomes more of a chore than a tool. That’s usually a sign that the membership framework isn’t architected correctly.

At OneMetric (HubSpot Elite Partner), we’re usually brought in when teams need HubSpot to function as a true system especially when memberships, HubDB, and custom objects need to work together. We’ve built secure member portals, editable directories, and modular CMS experiences that internal teams can manage without dev help.

You can see an example of our structured approach here: https://www.onemetric.io/hubspot/content-hub-onboarding

Both projects had the same requirement you have now: a member-only environment tied tightly to HubSpot’s CRM + CMS + HubDB architecture.

Here’s how I’d approach your membership portal:

Phase 0 - Blueprint & Architecture
Before writing a line of code, we map the flow:
  • How members authenticate
  • How their CRM record ties to their HubDB row
  • How updates should sync (direct publish vs. review workflow)
  • How internal notifications and permissions will be governed

Phase 1 - Core Build (Membership + Custom Object + Pages)
This is where everything comes to life:
  • Set up the custom object that stores submissions and profile updates
  • Build secure, gated membership pages (Sign-In, Dashboard, Access Denied, Password Reset, Registration, etc.)
  • Set up access groups ensuring each member can only view and edit their own profile
  • Build the edit profile form with terms & conditions acceptance
  • Connect the form to your HubDB table using serverless functions or workflows
  • Implement internal notification processes when a new profile or update is submitted

Phase 2 — QA, Optimization & Handover
We test the full end-to-end cycle: onboarding → login → update → publish → notify.
 Then we document everything and give your team short Looms so they can operate the system with confidence.

A Few Things I’d Love to Clarify
To design the portal correctly, can you help me understand:
  1. Are member accounts already created in HubSpot, or should account creation be triggered via the new registration flow?
  2. Do you want updates to auto-publish to the HubDB directory, or should they go through an approval workflow?
  3. Will members have different roles/tiers, or is this a single-level membership?
  4. Are images being stored directly in HubDB today, or do you want members to upload into the file system via the portal?

Our goal isn’t just to build the portal  it’s to give you a HubSpot architecture that your team can run without developer intervention. Happy to walk you through similar portal builds we’ve done or jump on a quick 15-min call to review your current setup. Let's Talk!

Looking forward to collaborating on this.

Cheers,
Todd!

job_title: HubSpot CRM Design for Inventory-Driven Equipment Sales  HubSpot Sales Architecture for Heavy Equipment Dealer (NetSuite ERP)
job_description:
We are a heavy equipment dealer evaluating and implementing HubSpot as our primary CRM.
Our goal is to design HubSpot correctly for an inventory-driven, dealer-style sales model, not a generic SaaS setup.
Context:
  • Business model: Used heavy equipment sales with financing
  • Sales motion: High-volume inbound leads + negotiated, machine-specific deals
  • ERP: NetSuite (system of record)
  • Lead sources: Listing sites, inbound email, phone, website forms
  • Reps are mobile and speed-to-lead is critical
What we need help with:
  • HubSpot data model and architecture (deals, equipment/machines, financing context)
  • Lead routing, SLAs, and rep accountability
  • Modeling machine-specific deals (unit, hours, attachments, condition)
  • Preserving inquiry history even after inventory turns
  • Pipeline design, forecasting, and reporting
  • Clean integration strategy with NetSuite (prefer configuration over heavy custom code)
  • Clear guidance on what HubSpot can vs cannot do, and recommended workarounds
What we are not looking for:
  • Generic HubSpot onboarding
  • Marketing-only CRM implementations
  • Heavy custom development without a strong rationale
  • Junior teams following a template
We are starting with architecture and design, followed by phased implementation. We value partners who understand dealer/distributor sales models and can think independently.
proposal_description:
Hey there,

Reading through your description, I know exactly what this feels like. When HubSpot is brought into a dealer-style, inventory-driven business and treated like a generic SaaS CRM, it quickly turns into data clutter, low rep adoption, inconsistent reporting, and integration gaps with the ERP. Reps stop trusting it, leadership stops using forecasts, and the CRM becomes more of a chore than a tool. We’ve stepped into this situation many times before usually right at the point where speed-to-lead matters, inventory turns fast, and the system simply isn’t reflecting how deals actually happen on the ground. 

I’m Todd, a Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric. We’re a HubSpot Elite Partner, and a large part of our work is “steadying the system” after partial implementations or setups that didn’t account for real-world sales motion. 

A few relevant examples to give you context: 
  •  Case Study: HubSpot–Salesforce Integration for a Unified Revenue Engine 
 The integration approach, data standardisation, and cross-system governance here are directly applicable to defining your HubSpot–NetSuite integration strategy
 
  •  Case Study: Building a Scalable Data Architecture in HubSpot 
 This is highly relevant to your need to design a CRM model that supports machine-specific deals, clear associations, and reliable forecasting

How I’d approach this with you

Phase 0 - Architecture & Blueprint (the most important part)
We start by mapping how your business actually sells: inventory lifecycle, inquiry sources, rep workflows, financing steps, and where NetSuite must remain authoritative. From there, we design a HubSpot data model that supports machine-specific deals (units, hours, attachments, condition) without bloating the CRM or breaking reporting. You’ll leave this phase with a clear blueprint and trade-off decisions documented including what HubSpot can and cannot do cleanly.

Phase 1 - Core Implementation
This is where we implement the architecture:
 
  • Deal and object structure for equipment + financing context
  • Lead routing, SLAs, and speed-to-lead workflows for mobile reps

Our goal isn’t just to set up HubSpot it’s to make it feel obvious, fast, and trustworthy for your reps and leadership.

Happy to start with a short architecture conversation or walk you through similar examples so you can see how this looks in practice. Let's Talk!

Todd

job_title: HubSpot Cleanup, Segmentation & Automation for Multi-Brand, Multi-Location Business
job_description:
We’re a multi-location entertainment company operating 26 locations across multiple brands, and we’ve inherited a complex HubSpot setup from previous employees and acquisitions. While HubSpot is core to our CRM and marketing efforts, our current configuration has become difficult to manage and scale.

We’re looking for an experienced HubSpot expert to help us clean up our existing setup, recommend best practices, and create a clear go-forward model that our internal teams can maintain confidently.

Key focus areas include:

CRM & Segmentation
  • Audit and clean up existing contacts, lists, and segments.
  • Recommend a clear structure for how contacts should be organized and segmented going forward.
  • Define best practices for how new contacts are created and managed across different channels and teams (e.g., reservations system, sales team, marketing).
  • Reduce confusion and duplication while ensuring contacts route to the correct teams and locations.

Marketing & Email Automation
  • Design a scalable approach for managing marketing efforts across 26 individual brands/locations.
  • Advise on how to structure campaigns and automated emails at both the brand and portfolio level.
  • Help determine best practices around marketing contact status (including when and how to use it).
  • Reduce form sprawl by redesigning forms into a more intentional structure (e.g., fewer, smarter forms that properly segment leads into sales, events, or marketing workflows).
  • Explore options for efficiently creating, managing, or uploading emails across multiple brands.

Integrations, Automation & Reporting
  • Review and advise on the integration between HubSpot and our reservations system
  • Provide guidance on understanding and improving existing workflows.
  • Help us better utilize HubSpot reporting so stakeholders can clearly see performance across brands and locations.

Critical Requirement

Training and procedure documentation is a must.
  • We want clear documentation and guidance so internal teams understand how HubSpot is structured, why decisions were made, and how to operate the system correctly going forward.

Overall, success for us looks like a clean, well-structured HubSpot instance with clear segmentation, scalable automation, reliable reporting, and documented best practices that support growth across all locations without creating ongoing operational friction.
proposal_description:
Hi there, 
Thanks for the detailed overview. What you described is extremely common in multi-location, multi-brand organizations that have grown through acquisitions and turnover, and it is exactly the type of HubSpot work I specialize in.
At a high level, the issues you are seeing are not tool problems, they are architecture and governance problems. HubSpot is doing what it was configured to do, but without a clear data model, ownership rules, and operating standards, complexity compounds very quickly.

Here is how I would approach this and what I would focus on.

First, I would start with a full CRM and data model audit. That includes contacts, companies, lists, lifecycle stages, lead sources, location and brand attribution, and how records are being created from each channel, including your reservations system, sales activity, and marketing forms. The goal here is not just cleanup, but defining a single, opinionated model for how contacts should exist, how they are segmented, and how ownership and routing should work across 26 locations without duplication or confusion.

Second, we would design a scalable marketing and automation framework. This typically means separating portfolio-level logic from location or brand-level execution. Campaign structure, form strategy, marketing contact status, and automated email usage all need clear rules so teams are not reinventing the wheel or creating form and workflow sprawl. I would help you consolidate forms into fewer, smarter entry points, design reusable automation patterns, and define how emails and campaigns should be managed across brands in a way that is both flexible and controlled.

Third, I would review your integrations, workflows, and reporting as a system, not in isolation. That includes validating how data flows from your reservations platform into HubSpot, identifying brittle or outdated workflows, and simplifying automation where possible. On the reporting side, the focus is on making brand and location performance visible and trusted, so leadership can answer questions without pulling data into spreadsheets or second-guessing the numbers.

Finally, and most importantly based on your requirement, everything would be documented. I place a heavy emphasis on training, written procedures, and decision rationale. That means clear documentation of the data model, segmentation rules, automation logic, and day-to-day operating guidance so your internal teams understand not just what to do, but why the system is structured the way it is. The goal is long-term maintainability, not consultant dependency.

Success, from my perspective, looks exactly like what you described: a clean, well-governed HubSpot instance that scales across locations, supports growth, and reduces operational friction instead of creating it.

If it makes sense, I would suggest an initial call to make sure everything is addressed so that I can create an accurate proposal 

Looking forward to the conversation.

job_description:
We’re looking for a highly experienced HubSpot specialist to help us clean up, structure, and operationalize our HubSpot portal.

This role is hands-on and strategic. It involves data hygiene, CRM architecture, automation, reporting, and campaign execution—not just email design.

We have a large and growing dataset and need someone who understands how to make HubSpot work properly at scale.
Scope of Work

1. HubSpot Data Cleanup & Structure
  • Audit existing contacts, companies, and properties
  • Deduplicate and normalize data
  • Create and refine custom properties (e.g., provider type, role, specialty, account tier, etc.)
  • Establish and enforce parent–child relationships between companies and contacts
  • Ensure data is structured to support automation, reporting, and segmentation

2. Campaigns, Email & Sequences
  • Assist with campaign setup and structure
  • Build and manage email campaigns, sequences, and enrollment logic
  • Ensure campaigns are properly tracked and attributed
  • Help define best practices for ongoing campaign execution

3. Workflows & Automation
  • Build HubSpot workflows to automate:
    • Lead routing
    • Lifecycle stage updates
    • Property updates
    • Internal notifications
  • Maintain clean, scalable automation logic (no fragile spaghetti workflows)

4. Lead Scoring (Advanced)
  • Implement lead scoring models based on:
    • Contact engagement (email, site activity, form fills, etc.)
    • Imported CPT usage data (provided via CSV or integration)
  • Create transparent, explainable scoring logic sales can trust
  • Ensure scores are usable in workflows, views, and dashboards

5. Dashboards & Reporting
  • Build dashboards showing:
    • Individual salesperson performance
    • Campaign progress by rep
    • Lead status and conversion metrics
  • Ensure reports are accurate, actionable, and easy for leadership to read

Ideal Candidate

You are a strong fit if you:
  • Have deep HubSpot experience (Sales Hub, Marketing Hub, Workflows, Reporting)
  • Understand CRM data modeling and RevOps best practices
  • Are comfortable working with large datasets and imports
  • Know how to translate business logic into clean HubSpot automation
  • Can explain why a workflow or scoring model is built a certain way
  • Have experience supporting sales teams and leadership reporting
Experience in B2B healthcare, medical, or provider-based sales is a plus, but not required.

Project Details
  • HubSpot portal is already live with existing data
  • Initial focus: cleanup, structure, and foundation
  • Ongoing support likely after initial setup
  • Timeline: Flexible, but expecting consistent weekly involvement
  • Budget: Hourly or milestone-based (please propose)
proposal_description:
Thanks for sharing the scope. This is exactly the type of work I do.

For context, I have helped 125+ organizations design, clean up, and operationalize HubSpot at scale. This includes complex, live portals supporting active sales and marketing teams, large datasets, and ongoing revenue motion. My work spans high-growth B2B organizations and well-known operators, including teams in the Alex Hormozi / Acquisition.com ecosystem, as well as multi-team and multi-entity environments.

I am a senior RevOps and HubSpot specialist. I do not focus on surface-level execution. My work is centered on CRM architecture, data modeling, automation, scoring, and reporting that leadership can rely on.
Here is how I would approach your scope.

Data model and cleanup first
I start with a full audit of contacts, companies, properties, lifecycle stages, and associations. This includes deduplication, normalization, and rationalizing custom properties so they directly support automation, segmentation, and reporting. Parent–child company relationships and account structure are designed intentionally, not retrofitted.

Automation built on stable logic
Workflows are built only after the data model is correct. Lead routing, lifecycle updates, property updates, and notifications are designed to be explainable and resilient. I avoid fragile workflow chains and automation that only one person understands.

Campaigns and attribution that hold up
I help structure campaigns, enrollment logic, and attribution standards so reporting remains accurate as volume increases. The goal is consistent measurement across reps and campaigns, not just activity tracking.

Advanced lead scoring sales actually trusts
I have implemented advanced scoring models using engagement data, behavioral signals, and imported usage data. Scoring logic is transparent and usable across workflows, views, and dashboards. Sales teams know why a score changed and how to act on it.

Reporting designed for leadership
Dashboards focus on rep performance, campaign contribution, lead status, and conversion metrics. Reports are built to be read quickly and used for decisions, not interpretation.
I work hands-on and directly. There is no delegation to junior resources. I am comfortable making structural changes inside live portals without disrupting sales or marketing operations.

In terms of engagement structure, I typically recommend:
  • An initial audit and foundation phase scoped as a milestone
  • Followed by consistent weekly execution
  • I prefer project based fees so that there is no limitation on hours, as well as ensuring you get everything you want out of the project and a set fee.

Happy to review your current portal and data volume and then propose a concrete plan and pricing approach.

job_title: Building custom objects for each business unit
job_description: We currently have three business units in one account. These business units represent three portfolio companies. These companies are competitors and need seperate custom objects for our sales team to be able to work their deals without being about to see if that contact is also speaking with one of the other businesses.
proposal_description:
I've worked through your requirements and want to be direct with you: the approach you're considering—using custom objects to separate the three portfolio companies within your current account won't actually solve the problem. 

WHY: HubSpot's architecture treats a single portal as one shared database. You can use Teams and permissions to restrict who sees which deals, and you can partition assets so sales teams only access their own lists and reports. But the fundamental issue is the contact record itself. When the same person is talking to two of your portfolio companies, that's one contact in HubSpot. All the emails, calls, meetings, and deal associations live on that contact's timeline.  Team-based permissions can't hide the fact that engagement exists, they can only limit who owns or edits the record. 

For competing portfolio companies where sales teams genuinely can't know if a prospect is also talking to a sister company, the only architecturally sound solution is separate HubSpot portals. Each company gets its own CRM database, its own contact records, its own pipelines. There's no risk of cross-visibility because the data doesn't exist in the same system. 

I know that sounds like a bigger investment, three subscriptions instead of one, but HubSpot does offer Multi-Account Management for situations like this. If you have Marketing Hub Enterprise on one portal, you can get centralized admin access across all three, and you can pull consolidated reporting for portfolio-level visibility without compromising the operational separation. 

If you'd like, I can take a closer look at your current setup and map out what a migration would look like. Sometimes these transitions are simpler than they appear, especially if the data is already somewhat siloed by business unit. Happy to jump on a call and walk through it. We've done a few of these so familiar with roll-ups, competing subsidiaries, etc.

job_title: HubSpot Implementation & Revenue Integration Specialist
job_description:
Project Overview:
We are seeking an experienced HubSpot implementation partner to help us optimize our CRM and revenue management processes. Our current setup lacks integration and automation, resulting in manual work and unreliable forecasting. We need a solution that connects HubSpot, QuickBooks, and our revenue tracking workflows to create a single source of truth for sales and revenue data.
Key Challenges We Need to Solve:
  • Unreliable Sales Forecasting: Current HubSpot forecasts are not trusted by the team.
  • Revenue Management Gaps: HubSpot is not being used effectively to manage and track revenue.
  • Mixed Sales Models: We have both ARR (annual recurring revenue for software licensing) and fixed-price sales.
  • Disconnected Systems: QuickBooks (finance), HubSpot (CRM), and manual Excel sheets for revenue tracking are not integrated, causing significant manual reconciliation.
  • Recurring Revenue Tracking: We struggle with quoting and tracking recurring revenue accurately.
Objectives:
  • Implement best practices in HubSpot for forecasting and revenue tracking.
  • Configure HubSpot to handle both ARR and fixed-price sales models.
  • Integrate HubSpot with QuickBooks and/or other tools to reduce manual reconciliation.
  • Create a reliable process for quoting and managing recurring revenue.
  • Establish a single source of truth for sales and revenue data.
Deliverables:
  • Audit of current HubSpot setup and recommendations.
  • Implementation of improved forecasting and revenue tracking processes.
  • Integration between HubSpot, QuickBooks, and revenue tracking tools.
  • Documentation and training for internal team.
Additional Info:
  • Looking for a partner who can provide strategic guidance and hands-on implementation.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

You're asking for HubSpot-QuickBooks integration, but that's not actually the problem. The problem is you don't have a reliable revenue data model, and connecting broken systems just creates an expensive, automated mess.

Evidence: "Current forecasts not trusted," "manual Excel reconciliation," "struggle with recurring revenue tracking." These aren't integration failures—they're data architecture failures.

What Actually Needs to Happen
Before connecting HubSpot to QuickBooks, you need:
  1. Revenue Data Model Design - How do ARR and fixed-price sales actually flow through your business? What's the single source of truth for each revenue type?
  2. HubSpot Architecture Validation - Is your current setup even capable of handling mixed revenue models? Most aren't without custom objects or Commerce Hub.
  3. Integration Strategy - Once the architecture exists, what actually syncs where, when, and why?
Most consultants skip straight to #3 (integration), which is why you're still using Excel spreadsheets despite having HubSpot and QuickBooks.

How Scoping Works
3-week discovery project that answers the strategic questions before building anything:
Week 1: Current State Mapping
  • Document your actual revenue flow (ARR subscriptions, fixed-price projects)
  • Analyze HubSpot's current data structure and gaps
  • Identify QuickBooks requirements and integration constraints
Week 2: Architecture Design
  • Build revenue data model (objects, properties, relationships)
  • Map data flow between systems with clear ownership rules
  • Design recurring revenue tracking and forecasting methodology
Week 3: Implementation Blueprint
  • Integration specification with sync rules and timing
  • Forecasting framework that sales actually trusts
  • Documentation and training plan for sustainable operation

What You Get
  • Revenue Data Architecture: Exact HubSpot configuration for ARR and fixed-price tracking
  • Integration Specification: Complete sync design for dev team or integration partner
  • Forecasting Framework: Process that produces reliable projections
  • Implementation Roadmap: Phased approach with effort estimates and resource requirements
  • 90-Day Support: Questions answered as you implement

Why This Works
5+ Years Custom HubSpot Development: Specialist in complex revenue models, system integration strategy, and sustainable data architecture design.
HubSpot Platform Expertise: Deep experience with custom objects, Commerce Hub implementation, and revenue tracking optimization across SaaS, distribution, and professional services companies.
QuickBooks Integration: Multiple implementations connecting financial systems with CRM platforms while maintaining clean data governance.
Proven Track Record: Long-term partnerships with companies like Streamline (5+ years), Adams Outdoor, and GoRout. Average client engagement: 2-5+ years.

Investment
$4,995 for comprehensive scoping
Delivered over 3 weeks with live collaboration sessions, detailed documentation, and post-scoping support.
Guarantee: If the scoping doesn't provide clear direction for your implementation, full refund.

Next Step
60-minute call to confirm fit and schedule sessions.

Here is my link --> https://calendly.com/unified-support-solutions/profoundly?back=1&month=2025-11

Best,

Ryan Ginsberg
Unified Support Solutions

P.S. I know this isn't the "integrate HubSpot and QuickBooks" proposal you expected. But after working with companies building complex revenue systems, I've learned this truth: Organizations that skip strategic architecture always end up rebuilding. The ones that invest 3 weeks in scoping save months of expensive rework and actually get forecasts their teams trust.

job_title: HubSpot Data Structure Transformation: Deals + Line Items
job_description:
HubSpot Deal Data Structure & Reporting Project


Objective

We’re a mid-size SaaS company looking for a HubSpot expert to validate, build, test, and implement a new Deal Data Structure that will scale with us for years to come. The goal is to enable accurate, reliable reporting around ARR, retention, and product-level bookings.

This is a short-term project (2–3 weeks) where you’ll collaborate with our RevOps team to ensure our proposed structure works in practice and is future-proof.

Current Situation

  • -- We use HubSpot Sales Professional and HubSpot Marketing Enterprise today.
  • -- Products span multiple regions, subscription and one-time revenue types.
  • -- Sales contracts can include multiple products, with terms spanning months or years.

  • Problem today: To manage multi-year and multi-product contracts, our sales team has been splitting them into multiple deals. This causes serious issues:

    • -- Renewal reporting is distorted (hard to separate true renewals vs. contractual renewals).
      -- Upsells/downsells are inaccurate.
      -- ARR and product-level reporting requires messy manual work.

Our Goal

Move to a “1 Contract = 1 HubSpot Deal” setup, leveraging Deals + Line Items to capture the right level of detail. Attached you’ll find a layout of our current vs. future state.

We already have a proposed data structure (drafted by our Sr. RevOps Manager and attached to this job posting), and historical contracts are being prepared for migration. Now we need an expert to help us:

-- Validate the proposed structure within HubSpot. Be honest with us – will it work, or should we go about this in a different way?

-- Build and test the setup with HubSpot reporting in a sandbox environment.

-- Push to production with historical data migrated.

-- Advise on process and automation to ensure ongoing accuracy and scalability.

Deliverables

-- Review and validate our proposed HubSpot Deal & Line Item structure. Will it enable us to answer questions like:
  • ----- How much of X product was sold in Y geographies?
  • ----- Which ARR, by product, is up for renewal next year?
    -- Build test deals and reporting in a HubSpot sandbox.
    -- Confirm the structure supports reporting on ARR, renewals, and product-level bookings.
    -- Help roll historical contracts into production under the new format.
    -- Recommend permissions, data entry rules, and automations to ensure data quality moving forward.

Scope Notes

Out of scope: Moving to HubSpot Quotes (all contracting is currently done outside HubSpot).
  • Future consideration: Must be compatible with future NetSuite integration for financials and revenue recognition.

Access & Support
You’ll have full access to our HubSpot sandbox.
  • Our RevOps team will collaborate closely and provide historical data for testing.
  • Work can be asynchronous, but we’d like short kickoff and handoff calls.

Timeline
Start: Within 1 week
  • Duration: 2–3 weeks

About Us
This project is posted by Kyleigh Andries, Sr. RevOps Manager at Opensignal (LinkedIn).
Opensignal is the leading global provider of independent insights into consumers’ connectivity experiences and choice of carrier. Learn more at opensignal.com.

  • Keyword Analysis: Identify high-impact keywords relevant to Maine Home Connection’s target audience and industry.

job_title: Help Rebuilding Newsletter Template to Prevent Gmail Clipping
job_description:
Hi, This is Brian Garvey from Profoundly. HubSpot asked us to take a look at this one, so I’m submitting the request on behalf of both their team and the customer. While I’m helping get things started, you’ll be working and communicating directly with the customer moving forward.  If this feels like a fit, please respond and we'll work with HubSpot and the customer to align on next steps.

***

This HubSpot customer is experiencing Gmail clipping issues with their newsletter. The customer recently migrated from ActiveCampaign to HubSpot and is now encountering email clipping due to the message size exceeding Gmail’s 102KB limit. The existing template is approximately 30 pages long and was originally built using HubSpot’s drag-and-drop editor, which may be contributing to excess HTML weight. 

They’re seeking an expert who can: 

  • Rebuild or optimize the template using lighter-weight HTML
  • Ensure proper rendering across major devices and inboxes
  • Retain as much of the original design and structure as possible

Context:
This is a high-priority issue with churn implications. HubSpot is actively working with the customer to resolve it and may subsidize part or all of the cost, pending a quote or rough estimate for the work.

Our HubSpot Products:
You'll need to ask on the discovery call.

Preferred Outcome:
A fully functioning newsletter template that avoids Gmail clipping (under 102KB), preserves critical content, and maintains a high standard of design and usability.
proposal_description:
Intro

At Unified Support Solutions, we’re here to help you tackle the Gmail clipping issue—plain and simple. When your newsletter exceeds 102 KB of underlying HTML, Gmail clips the message. That disrupts how your content is delivered, affects engagement, and creates unnecessary friction for your audience.

You’ve already done the heavy lifting by identifying the issue. Now it’s about solving it effectively. HubSpot’s drag-and-drop templates (especially long ones) tend to generate excess code that Gmail counts—even if readers never see it. Our job is to clean that up, whether by optimizing the existing structure or rebuilding a lightweight version from the ground up. 

Either way, the goal is simple: preserve your design, keep your content intact, and stay under the Gmail limit.

Our Two-Step Path

Step 1: Evaluate & Recommend

  • 30-minute call to gather the current template and brand assets
  • Run a size and structure audit to pinpoint where the excess weight lives
  • Decide the best path: streamline the current template or rebuild a leaner version
  • Deliverables:
    • 1-page summary of findings
    • Clear recommendation on optimize vs. rebuild
    • Defined success criteria (≤ 102 KB, renders cleanly across major inboxes)

Step 2: Build & Test

  • Code work: either stripping out unnecessary elements or rebuilding from scratch
  • Internal QA during development
  • Full email client testing (Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail, iOS/Android)
  • Minor tweaks after your team reviews a test send
  • Deliverables:
    • Final HubSpot-ready template
    • Testing matrix showing it meets the 102 KB limit

Timeline

Three weeks from kickoff to delivery.
  • Week 1: Discovery and decision-making.
  • Weeks 2–3: Build, test, and review.

Investment
  • Optimization of current template: $4,500 (Fixed Price of estimated 30 hours @ $150/hour)

Investment How We Measure Success

The compiled HTML is under 102 KB and renders properly across all major inboxes. That’s our definition of done.
Immediate

Next Steps
  1. Provide access – Send the latest HTML export or grant temporary access to the template in HubSpot Design Manager.
  2. Share brand guidelines – Style guide, colors, fonts, and any specific client/device requirements.
  3. Book the kickoff – Let us know a 30-minute slot that works for you this week.
  4. Budget note – Confirm whether HubSpot’s subsidy is approved, and share any internal billing references.

Once we have these, we’ll begin the audit immediately and keep the project on pace for a three-week turnaround.

Looking forward to helping you get this resolved quickly and cleanly.
