job_title: HubSpot Data Structure Transformation: Deals + Line Items
job_description:
HubSpot Deal Data Structure & Reporting Project


Objective

We’re a mid-size SaaS company looking for a HubSpot expert to validate, build, test, and implement a new Deal Data Structure that will scale with us for years to come. The goal is to enable accurate, reliable reporting around ARR, retention, and product-level bookings.

This is a short-term project (2–3 weeks) where you’ll collaborate with our RevOps team to ensure our proposed structure works in practice and is future-proof.

Current Situation

  • -- We use HubSpot Sales Professional and HubSpot Marketing Enterprise today.
  • -- Products span multiple regions, subscription and one-time revenue types.
  • -- Sales contracts can include multiple products, with terms spanning months or years.

  • Problem today: To manage multi-year and multi-product contracts, our sales team has been splitting them into multiple deals. This causes serious issues:

    • -- Renewal reporting is distorted (hard to separate true renewals vs. contractual renewals).
      -- Upsells/downsells are inaccurate.
      -- ARR and product-level reporting requires messy manual work.

Our Goal

Move to a “1 Contract = 1 HubSpot Deal” setup, leveraging Deals + Line Items to capture the right level of detail. Attached you’ll find a layout of our current vs. future state.

We already have a proposed data structure (drafted by our Sr. RevOps Manager and attached to this job posting), and historical contracts are being prepared for migration. Now we need an expert to help us:

-- Validate the proposed structure within HubSpot. Be honest with us – will it work, or should we go about this in a different way?

-- Build and test the setup with HubSpot reporting in a sandbox environment.

-- Push to production with historical data migrated.

-- Advise on process and automation to ensure ongoing accuracy and scalability.

Deliverables

-- Review and validate our proposed HubSpot Deal & Line Item structure. Will it enable us to answer questions like:
  • ----- How much of X product was sold in Y geographies?
  • ----- Which ARR, by product, is up for renewal next year?
    -- Build test deals and reporting in a HubSpot sandbox.
    -- Confirm the structure supports reporting on ARR, renewals, and product-level bookings.
    -- Help roll historical contracts into production under the new format.
    -- Recommend permissions, data entry rules, and automations to ensure data quality moving forward.

Scope Notes

Out of scope: Moving to HubSpot Quotes (all contracting is currently done outside HubSpot).
  • Future consideration: Must be compatible with future NetSuite integration for financials and revenue recognition.

Access & Support
You’ll have full access to our HubSpot sandbox.
  • Our RevOps team will collaborate closely and provide historical data for testing.
  • Work can be asynchronous, but we’d like short kickoff and handoff calls.

Timeline
Start: Within 1 week
  • Duration: 2–3 weeks

About Us
This project is posted by Kyleigh Andries, Sr. RevOps Manager at Opensignal (LinkedIn).
Opensignal is the leading global provider of independent insights into consumers’ connectivity experiences and choice of carrier. Learn more at opensignal.com.

job_title: HubSpot RevOps Consultant (Enterprise)
job_description:
We’ve just moved from SugarCRM to HubSpot and purchased 10 Sales Hub Enterprise seats and 4 Marketing Hub Pro seats. We need an expert to migrate data, set up best-practice RevOps processes, and implement key integrations (Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana).

Objectives
- Migrate all SugarCRM data to HubSpot with clean mapping, dedupe, and validation.
- Build enterprise-grade automations for sales, marketing, and handoffs.
- Integrate Xero (billing), PhoneBurner (dialer/activities), and Asana (projects/tasks).
- Enable the team with permissions, playbooks, sequences, reports, and dashboards.

Scope of Work (deliverables)

1) Discovery & Design
Audit SugarCRM objects, fields, picklists, activities, attachments, and custom logic.
  • Define HubSpot data model: standard + custom objects, associations, required fields.
    Process design: Lead → MQL/SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won/Lost, pipeline stages, entry/exit criteria.
    Security model: teams, roles, permissions, record ownership rules.

2) Data Migration (SugarCRM → HubSpot)
Field mapping doc (1:1 + transformations), deduplication rules, lookup resolution.
Import activities (emails/calls/notes/meetings where supported), files, and lists.
Validation run + reconciliation report (record counts, error log, exceptions).
Delta cutover plan with blackout window and rollback.

3) Sales Hub Enterprise Setup
Pipelines (primary + any sub-pipelines), stage properties, required reasons.
Sequences, templates, snippets, playbooks, call outcomes, meeting links.
Lead scoring (behavioral + firmographic), SLAs and task queues.
Custom quotes, products, deal workflows (create tasks, set owners, update fields).
Forecasting setup, goals, and weighted pipeline definitions.

4) Marketing Hub Pro Setup
Lifecycle model, MQL definition, subscription types, double opt-in where needed.
Forms, CTAs, lists (smart/static), nurture workflows, and lead routing.
Email + landing page brand modules, compliance (GDPR/CCPA).
UTM + source tracking, campaign hierarchy.

5) Integrations
Xero: deal→invoice sync, invoice status back to HubSpot, revenue fields for reporting.
PhoneBurner: activity logging, call outcomes, contact sync, list handoff.
Asana: deal/contract approval → Asana project creation with key fields synced.
Webhooks/middleware (if needed) with retry + error handling.

6) Reporting & Enablement
Executive dashboards: pipeline health, velocity, forecast vs. quota, win/loss, campaign attribution.
Rep dashboards: tasks due, sequence performance, meetings booked, CAC/LTV basics.
Training: live sessions + Loom library; admin runbook; change log.

It's a long term engagement and you will be working with Sales, marketing & CS teams.

Long-Term Strategic Partner: I don’t just set up your system, I help evolve it. From forecasting and attribution to automation and reporting, I partner with leadership teams to drive consistent RevOps performance.

Objective
Help your team successfully migrate from SugarCRM to HubSpot while building a scalable, RevOps-driven foundation for Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success. The engagement will ensure clean data, seamless integrations, and robust reporting across the entire revenue lifecycle.

Scope of Work
1. Discovery & Design

  • Develop quote and product structures for seamless billing and reporting.
4. Marketing Hub Pro Setup

  • Configure campaign tracking (UTMs, attribution), forms, and email compliance.
  • Implement analytics dashboards for campaign ROI and funnel conversion.

  • Xero: Sync deals to invoices, send back invoice status, and track revenue in HubSpot.

  • Asana: Auto-create projects upon Closed Won, syncing key fields and milestones.

  • Provide live training sessions and a recorded Loom library for ongoing enablement.

Our organization currently uses our internally developed app to manage the sales process. We recently purchased Hubspot Enterprise with the intention of migrating that sales process over to the Sales Hub. Currently, only 3-4 sales workflows need to be created in Hubspot. We also require foundational reporting dashboards and training enablement material (such as Loom videos of the automation buildout) to support our team in adopting the enhanced setup.

Objective
Referencing our visual workflow map, replicate the workflow within Hubspot. The specialist will leverage workflow automations and sales sequences to guide sales members through a clearly defined customer journey—from initial prospect outreach through follow-up, pipeline progression, and ultimately to closed deal. This project aims to enhance operational efficiency, ensure consistent execution, and improve visibility into sales performance. 

Create a custom sales workflow leveraging Hubspot's workflow automations and sequences to guide a salesperson through the following workflows:

Initial Outreach to Newly Created Accounts
Notification/priority on newest accounts
  • Round robin assignment 
  • Defined Lifecycle Stages 
  • Task automation based on call counts on a contact 
  • Associated email sequences
  • Reporting and visibility for sales management 

Lead Nurturing Based on New Account Spend
  • Email sequences based on account revenue (will need API integration from our internal app to receive this data)
  • Task automation based on account activity 
  • Build out of new fields to track contact’s time within a given Lifecycle stage 

Reactivation Flow (Marketing Hub Automation)
  • Review our current Reactivation Email Automation to ensure it’s setup optimally and doesn’t interfere with the newly created workflows mentioned above.

Reporting & Dashboards
  • Performance Metrics Per Sales Team Member
  • Contact Counts per Lifecycle stage
  • Avg days to Close 
  • Avg speed to lead 
  • Avg touchpoints before a prospect responds 
  • Revenue opportunity per stage

Access & Support
You’ll have full access to our Hubspot and context to the project. The EVP Of Sales will be your main point of contact with support from a Project Manager and Developer. 

About Us
NextGen Leads is a leading provider of Health Insurance leads with a focus on lead quality and customer support.

proposal_description:
Hi there,

Transitioning from Salesforce to HubSpot while building event ROI tracking is doable, but most teams I see get the technical setup right and still can't answer "which events actually drive revenue." The gap isn't usually the campaign tool configuration, it's the attribution model and data hygiene underneath it.

Quick context: I recently helped a B2B team build event-to-close tracking and discovered their "event leads" weren't even being associated to companies properly. Perfect reports, wrong data.

Two questions before we map this out:

1. When you say "all the way to sale," how are deals currently being tracked in HubSpot? (This determines whether attribution will work or need foundation work first.)

2. What decisions will event ROI data drive? Knowing whether this is for budget allocation, event selection, or proving marketing value shapes what we build.

Your budget fits a focused engagement. Let's spend 20 minutes determining if we can get you to actionable event reporting or if there's foundational work needed first.

Here's my calendar: https://meetings.hubspot.com/ghushek/profoundly

Looking forward to connecting.

job_title: HubSpot Event + Campaigns to report and see event leads + ROI
job_description: I am new to HubSpot and need support learning HubSpot (previously a SFDC user). In addition, my goal is to use the Marketing Events & Campaign tools to tag Event Leads all the way to sale, and then report on the buyers event journey alongside the event ROI.
proposal_description:
Hey,

 I understand what you’re experiencing moving from Salesforce to HubSpot can feel like stepping into a whole new world. Learning the system while tracking every event lead and measuring ROI can quickly get overwhelming. Low adoption, scattered data, and inconsistent reporting aren’t just frustrating they can slow growth. I’ve seen this many times, and when HubSpot feels more like a chore than a tool, it usually signals process drift, not a lack of skill.

At OneMetric, we’re an Elite HubSpot Partner and frequently called in to steady the system after partial implementations or migrations. For example,

- HubSpot marketing automation and lead management improvements drove a 28% increase in booked meetings, making event and campaign performance much easier to report on.

Here’s how I’d approach your project in a structured, consultative way:

Phase 0 - Discovery & Audit / Blueprint
  • Audit your current HubSpot setup and data architecture, especially focusing on Marketing Events & Campaign tools.
  • Map out the lead-to-sale journey for event-generated contacts and identify gaps in tagging, workflows, and reporting.
  • Build a prioritized roadmap highlighting quick wins and longer-term optimizations.

Phase 1 - Core Implementation / Fixes
  • Implement improved event tracking, lead segmentation, and lifecycle stage automation to ensure each event lead is tagged correctly from registration through sale.
  • Refine workflows, pipelines, and property management to reduce friction for your sales and marketing teams.
  • Integrate any key tools or data sources needed to close reporting gaps and reduce manual tracking.

Phase 2 - Optimization & Training
  • Develop dashboards that clearly visualize event ROI, lead journeys, and conversion metrics.
  • Document processes and deliver short, actionable training sessions (Loom videos or live walkthroughs) to get your team up to speed quickly.
  • Provide recommendations for ongoing improvements, so your team becomes self-reliant rather than dependent on external support.

Questions to better understand your requirement:
  1. Are you currently tracking event leads in any system, or would this be a fresh implementation in HubSpot?
  2. Which types of events are priorities virtual, in-person, or both and do you need attribution at the session or campaign level?
  3. How granular do you need the reporting on the buyer journey just lifecycle stage conversions, or detailed touchpoints?
  4. Do you have any existing integrations with marketing or event platforms we should consider?

Our goal isn’t just to fix HubSpot it’s to make your team confident and self-reliant on it.

 I’d be happy to walk you through similar before-and-after examples or start with a 15-minute discussion to pinpoint exactly where improvements will have the most impact.

Best,
Todd

job_title: Hubspot Cleanup Project & Setting Up Proper Data Structure Processes To Limit Future Issues
job_description: The data in our current Hubspot implementation is a bit of a mess. We have Sales Hub Pro and Marketing Hub Pro. The Sales team came went Salesforce to Microsoft Dynamics to Hubspot and the data is definitely not clean. It's affecting our ability to get clear insights using reporting. We need someone to help us clean up the whole thing and then create SOPs/processes for us to follow to ensure data quality is maintained. We will also need ongoing support to ensure data stays clean.
proposal_description:
HubSpot Data Governance Project Proposal

Moving from Salesforce → Dynamics → HubSpot almost always introduces structural and data quality issues, especially when reporting and insights are critical for decision-making.

Before jumping into cleanup, the most important step is to properly assess what’s broken, why it’s broken, and what’s worth fixing vs. leaving untouched. Otherwise, cleanup efforts tend to be expensive, risky, and short-lived.

Recommended Approach: HubSpot Data Audit & Governance Blueprint

This phase is designed to give you clarity and control before making any major changes.

What I’ll review:

  • Contact, Company, and Deal data model and associations
  • Pipelines, deal stages, lifecycle stages, and ownership logic
  • Property sprawl (unused, duplicated, conflicting fields)
  • Duplicate patterns and sources of ongoing data pollution
  • Reporting gaps and why current reports are unreliable
  • Light review of Sales and Marketing data entry behaviors that contribute to issues

What you’ll get:

  • A clear HubSpot Data Health Scorecard
  • A prioritized cleanup roadmap (what to fix now vs. later)
  • A Data Governance Blueprint with guardrails to prevent future issues
  • Clear recommendations for future cleanup and optional ongoing support

This phase is strategic and diagnostic — it intentionally avoids bulk cleanup or risky historical changes until there’s a clear plan in place.

Timeline

  • Approximately 2–3 weeks, depending on access and stakeholder availability

Investment

  • $4,500 fixed fee

There’s no obligation to proceed beyond these phase, but it ensures that any cleanup or ongoing support is intentional, efficient, and sustainable.

If this sounds aligned, the next step would be a short discovery call to confirm scope, access, and priorities, and then we can kick off Phase 1 immediately. https://reforgesolutions.com/meetings/walter101/profoundly-calls

Best,
Walter

proposal_description:
Hello,

Please see a proposed approach for connecting MortgageBot and Five9 to HubSpot, so your teams can operate from one system—without manual updating, duplicate records, or missing activity history.

Overview
My goal is to deliver a clean, reliable integration quickly, with a clear plan, predictable cost, and minimal disruption.

What you’ll get

✅ Five9 → HubSpot
So every call shows up where your team actually works:
  • Calls logged to the right Contact / Company / Deal
  • Call outcomes / dispositions mapped consistently
  • Optional: auto-create or update records from call activity

✅ MortgageBot → HubSpot
So loan/application progress is visible inside HubSpot:
  • Automatically create/update Contacts + Deals (or a Loan/Application object if preferred)
  • Sync key milestones and status changes
  • Ensure sales/ops can always see “where things stand” without chasing updates

Why this matters
Most integrations fail for one of two reasons:
  • Bad mapping (fields don’t match the business process)
  • No guardrails (duplicates, overwritten values, unreliable updates)

This engagement is built to avoid both—so the integration stays stable and usable long-term.

Delivery window
  • 3–4 weeks from kickoff (depends on access and scope depth)

Pricing approach (fits your budget)
Since your budget range is $1,000–$5,000, I recommend one of these options:

Option A — Fixed “Core Integration” (best fit for small project budgets)

$4,500 fixed
  • Five9 call logging + disposition mapping into HubSpot
  • MortgageBot → HubSpot contact/deal updates for key statuses/fields
  • Testing + go-live support


Option B — Hourly (best if requirements are still evolving)

$125/hr with a not-to-exceed cap you choose (ex: $5,000)
  • Same outcome, with more flexibility if scope changes during discovery

Why choose me
I’m a HubSpot Solutions Partner and operator-focused consultant—meaning I don’t build integrations that “technically sync.” I build them so your team can run the business inside HubSpot with confidence.

In past client work, I’ve:
  • Stabilized CRM foundations (properties, pipelines, dedupe strategy, governance) so reporting becomes trustworthy
  • Rebuilt workflows/automations that were creating bad data or conflicting ownership/lifecycle movement
  • Integrated external systems into HubSpot in a way that supports adoption (teams actually use it because the data is accurate)

For MortgageBot + Five9, that translates to:
  • Correct object model + mapping (so it’s reportable, not just stored)
  • Guardrails to prevent duplicates and bad overwrites
  • A clear delivery plan with quick feedback loops to hit your 3–4 week window

Please book a time to discuss.

Thank you.

Very Respectfully

job_description:

Intuition Robotics is a mission-driven AI and robotics company focused on empowering older adults with empathetic digital companions, notably the ElliQ social companion robot that promotes independence and engagement for aging populations.

We use HubSpot to capture inbound interest in ElliQ through free-form forms on our website. These form submissions currently create contact records, but they are not associated with any company, county, or funding partner, and different types of people are not clearly differentiated within HubSpot.

Our ecosystem includes state and county partners that fund and distribute ElliQ, as well as individual end users who request or use the product. Today, those parties are not clearly separated in HubSpot, which creates confusion between partner contacts and end users coming from the same organization or region.

We would like to improve how inbound leads are organized, associated, and communicated by:

  • Clearly distinguishing end users from partner or company contacts within HubSpot
  • Associating individual leads with the appropriate county or regional entity
  • Avoiding confusion between a company or county point of contact and the individual end users connected to that entity
  • Creating reports that capture key criteria from HubSpot form submissions and allow for segmentation by county, state, or partner
  • Automating emails to the appropriate company or county point of contact showing the leads associated with their program or region

Success looks like having a structured, automated system where inbound form submissions are properly categorized, associated with the right entities, and surfaced through reports and automated emails without manual effort, while keeping partner contacts and end users clearly distinct.

job_title: HubSpot CRM Design for Inventory-Driven Equipment Sales  HubSpot Sales Architecture for Heavy Equipment Dealer (NetSuite ERP)
job_description:
We are a heavy equipment dealer evaluating and implementing HubSpot as our primary CRM.
Our goal is to design HubSpot correctly for an inventory-driven, dealer-style sales model, not a generic SaaS setup.
Context:
  • Business model: Used heavy equipment sales with financing
  • Sales motion: High-volume inbound leads + negotiated, machine-specific deals
  • ERP: NetSuite (system of record)
  • Lead sources: Listing sites, inbound email, phone, website forms
  • Reps are mobile and speed-to-lead is critical
What we need help with:
  • HubSpot data model and architecture (deals, equipment/machines, financing context)
  • Lead routing, SLAs, and rep accountability
  • Modeling machine-specific deals (unit, hours, attachments, condition)
  • Preserving inquiry history even after inventory turns
  • Pipeline design, forecasting, and reporting
  • Clean integration strategy with NetSuite (prefer configuration over heavy custom code)
  • Clear guidance on what HubSpot can vs cannot do, and recommended workarounds
What we are not looking for:
  • Generic HubSpot onboarding
  • Marketing-only CRM implementations
  • Heavy custom development without a strong rationale
  • Junior teams following a template
We are starting with architecture and design, followed by phased implementation. We value partners who understand dealer/distributor sales models and can think independently.
proposal_description:
Most heavy equipment dealers come to us after a failed HubSpot implementation where consultants treated them like a SaaS company. You're smart to start with architecture instead of jumping to setup.

Your NetSuite/HubSpot boundary is the make-or-break decision here. We need to nail where data is going to live and how it will be synced. Integrations with HubSpot would be my middle name if I could hyphenate it.

Three questions before we go further:
  1. What's breaking right now that's forcing you to evaluate HubSpot? Is it lead response time, visibility into what reps are doing, or something else costing you deals?
  2. When a prospect inquires about a specific machine that sells before you close them, what happens to that relationship data today? Where does it go and how do you re-engage them on the next unit?
  3. If we architect this wrong and six months from now your reps are still keeping deal info in spreadsheets or text messages because HubSpot feels like extra work, what does that cost you in lost margin and slow turns?

We work with a heat treater and CNC shop in Phoenix, and I work with SaaS. You're not going to be labeled as either until we know your business. Then we will take the best of both to give you a well rounded system you can grow with.


Best,
Grant Hushek
Founder @ Grantbot

job_title: HubSpot Optimization for Data Integrity, Sales Process, and Reporting
job_description:
Project Overview 
We are seeking a HubSpot expert to help us restructure and optimize our current setup. The project centers on improving data consistency, enhancing reporting capabilities, and enforcing standardized input at key stages in our sales process. 
This will be a defined project engagement, with the potential for follow-on phases. Our top priorities are data structure, property and workflow improvements, and reporting dashboards. 
Project Scope 
 1. Data Model & Property Enhancements 
·       Audit and adjust existing deal, contact, and company properties 
·       Rename or restructure existing picklists (e.g. Business Line) 
·       Create new properties to support reporting and process needs, including: 
o   Date of Initial Contact 
o   Type of Inquiry (Formal RFP / Informal) 
o   Deal Source (Campaign, Website, Referral, Conference, etc.) 
o   Business Line (New picklist: MCG Power Ops, Paragon, Versify, InnoTap, Unco) 
o   Product Pick List (Grouped under Business Line) 
o   Solution Configuration (Single or bundled products; identify which) 
o   Quality of Opportunity (Scored 1–5 based on defined factors) 
 2. Enforcing Data Capture 
·       Configure field-level requirements to ensure key data is captured when a deal is created or modified 
·       Add guidance to ensure consistency across teams 
·       Optionally introduce workflows or validation logic to guide users toward complete entries 
3. Reporting & Dashboards 
We want to eliminate our dependence on Excel by building robust, filterable reports inside HubSpot. Reporting goals include: 
·       Lead activity: Formal/informal RFPs over trailing 12 months 
·       Sales velocity: Time from inquiry → quote → close 
·       Pipeline analysis: Proposals made, prospects qualified, contracts closed 
·       Upsell tracking: Quoted vs Won 
·       Product-level and business line performance 
·       Customer segmentation (e.g. geography, industry, solution type) 
4. Internal Enablement 
We want our internal team to understand what’s been implemented and how to work with it. This includes: 
·       Walkthroughs of the new data structure and reports 
·       Guidance for our finance lead (Andy) and commercial lead (Dave) to become more self-sufficient 
·       Documentation to help us maintain the system long-term 
5. Optional Phase Two (Future) 
Depending on the outcome of this initial project, we may explore: 
·       Integrating quote and invoicing workflows 
·       Manual or automated handoff to QuickBooks Desktop (note: we are not using QuickBooks Online) 
·       Deeper analytics or customized dashboards 
Pricing 
We would prefer a fix price proposal. 
Timeline 
We are ready to begin immediately and expect to review proposals and select a Pro by Monday, July 22. Our aim is to complete the core project within 4–6 weeks. Timing for any optional follow-on work will be addressed separately. 
Our HubSpot Setup 
·       In use since: 2020 
·       Primary Hubs: Sales (recently added Marketing Hub) 
·       User Base: ~20 active users 
·       Current Limitations: 
o   Manual deal creation (no automation from forms) 
o   Inconsistent use of properties 
o   Reporting reliant on Excel 
·       Desired Outcome: Structured deal input, real-time dashboards, scalable data model 
What Success Looks Like 
·       A clean, consistent data structure that supports our sales and reporting needs 
·       Native HubSpot reports that replace Excel for pipeline, lead, and upsell tracking 
·       Improved internal knowledge so we can build additional reports or workflows ourselves 
Clear documentation and a system we can own confidently going forward

proposal_description:
Proposal: HubSpot Service Hub Setup and Automation Enablement

Hi there,

Thank you for sharing your project. It sounds like you are in a great position to take your HubSpot use to the next level. You are already using Sales Hub effectively, and now it is time to extend that structure to your service and support process. That is exactly what HubSpot Service Hub was designed for, and our team specializes in helping small teams like yours get it running quickly and correctly.

We are a HubSpot Certified Solutions Partner agency with deep experience building connected customer experiences across Sales, Service, and Operations Hubs. Our goal is to help you move from manual work to measurable, automated processes that free your team to focus on customers.

What We Will Build

We will implement HubSpot Service Hub for both customer and internal support management. This will include:

  • Setting up ticket pipelines for external and internal issue tracking
  • Configuring shared inboxes and routing rules
  • Automating ticket creation, assignment, and notifications
  • Adding SLA tracking and reporting dashboards
  • Providing short training and documentation so your team is comfortable using the system

This will give you a consistent, automated process for managing issues and a clear view of how quickly they are being resolved. 

Why It Matters 

Your current process is manual, which makes it hard to track performance and improvement opportunities. Service Hub will make your support process visible and repeatable. It will shorten resolution times, ensure accountability, and help your team see where common problems occur so you can fix them at the source. 

How We Will Do It

We recommend approaching this project in two phases so that you can start quickly and then build on a working foundation.

Phase 1: Setup and Automation Sprint (3 to 4 weeks)
We will map your issue types and ticket categories, configure pipelines for both customer and internal use, connect inboxes and forms, build automation for routing and notifications, create dashboards for resolution and backlog visibility, and provide training for your team.

Phase 2: Optimization and Insights (optional)
Once your system is live, we can refine workflows and SLAs based on real data, add NPS and feedback collection, and expand reporting to include satisfaction, backlog, and agent metrics.

Optional Retainer: Ongoing Optimization
If you prefer ongoing help, we offer a $1,800 per month retainer for Service Hub improvements, automation adjustments, and reporting support.

Who It Is For

This setup is for your customer support and service team as well as your internal associates who handle recurring or post-sale issues. Everyone will use one simple system for tracking and managing requests from start to finish.

Timeline and Delivery

All work is completed within Eternal Works business hours, Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM Eastern.
 Phase 1 delivery is estimated at three to four weeks.
 Phase 2 can follow once the first stage has been in use for a few weeks.
 If you ever need a faster turnaround, rush options are available per our Delivery Policy.

Investment

Based on your needs and HubSpot’s Service Hub scope:
  • Phase 1 setup sprint: $3,000 to $5,000
  • Phase 2 optimization: $2,000 to $3,500
  • Optional ongoing retainer: $1,800 per month

Payment terms are half up front and half on completion. These estimates align with our standard Service Hub onboarding and implementation pricing. 

What We Will Need From You

We will need:
  • Admin access to your HubSpot account
  • Examples of three to five common issue types
  • One main contact for review and feedback
  • Confirmation of any integrations such as Slack or Teams

After that, we handle everything from configuration to activation.

Next Steps

We can start as soon as you are ready. To move forward:
  • Confirm whether you would like to begin with Phase 1 only or a phased engagement that includes optimization
  • Once confirmed, we will send a short scope and kickoff plan

 You can also schedule a short discovery call to review your options here:
 Book a quick call

You can view our certifications and reviews on our HubSpot Partner Profile:
View Eternal Works on HubSpot’s Partner Directory

We would love to help your team move from manual issue tracking to a structured, automated support system in HubSpot that delivers real efficiency and insight. 


Thanks & Much Respect,

Tim

job_title: HubSpot ↔ Abtrac Integration Build
job_description:
Hi team,

Apologies, I'd located a contractor for this job but they have since become unavailable. 

I have two clients looking for this integration currently with urgency:
 
Objective
Automate the workflow from HubSpot Deals to Abtrac Jobs:
  1. When a HubSpot Deal reaches the “Quoting” stage:
    • Generate a unique Quote Number.
    • Create a HubSpot Quote linked to the Deal, Company, and Contact.
    • Write the Quote Number back to both the Deal and the Quote.
  2. When the Quote is signed:
    • Create a Job in Abtrac.
    • Map and upsert related Company → Client, Contact → Contact, Deal → Job, Line Items → Tasks.
    • Update the HubSpot Deal with the Abtrac Job ID and status.

Core Requirements
  • Tech Stack: Prefer Node.js/TypeScript serverless function.
  • HubSpot:
    • Auth via Private App Token.
    • Use CRM v3 APIs for Deals, Quotes, Line Items, Companies, Contacts.
    • Implement Workflows to trigger webhooks for middleware.
  • Abtrac:
    • Use API to upsert Clients/Contacts, create Jobs and Tasks.
    • Confirm required fields, enums, rate limits.
  • Mapping:
    • Company → Client
    • Contact → Contact
    • Deal → Job
    • Line Item → Task
  • Idempotency: Prevent duplicate object creation.
  • Error Handling: Log failures, retry on API errors, create HubSpot tasks for manual follow-up.

Deliverables
  • Fully functioning middleware deployed to cloud (AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, or similar).
  • Google Sheet mapping table provided for data object mapping.
  • HubSpot custom properties created for quote_number, abtrac_job_id, and abtrac_job_status.
  • Documentation: Endpoint references, environment setup, runbook.

Ideal Candidate
  • Proven track record building HubSpot integrations.
  • Familiarity with Abtrac API or similar job management systems.
  • Strong understanding of API authentication, associations, and mapping.
  • Ability to communicate clearly and document thoroughly.
proposal_description:

I've built several different integrations with tools similar to Abtac so I'm pretty confident we can get this done, just want to understand the stack we're working with.

job_title: HubSpot Data Structure Transformation: Deals + Line Items
job_description:
HubSpot Deal Data Structure & Reporting Project


Objective

We’re a mid-size SaaS company looking for a HubSpot expert to validate, build, test, and implement a new Deal Data Structure that will scale with us for years to come. The goal is to enable accurate, reliable reporting around ARR, retention, and product-level bookings.

This is a short-term project (2–3 weeks) where you’ll collaborate with our RevOps team to ensure our proposed structure works in practice and is future-proof.

Current Situation

  • -- We use HubSpot Sales Professional and HubSpot Marketing Enterprise today.
  • -- Products span multiple regions, subscription and one-time revenue types.
  • -- Sales contracts can include multiple products, with terms spanning months or years.

  • Problem today: To manage multi-year and multi-product contracts, our sales team has been splitting them into multiple deals. This causes serious issues:

    • -- Renewal reporting is distorted (hard to separate true renewals vs. contractual renewals).
      -- Upsells/downsells are inaccurate.
      -- ARR and product-level reporting requires messy manual work.

Our Goal

Move to a “1 Contract = 1 HubSpot Deal” setup, leveraging Deals + Line Items to capture the right level of detail. Attached you’ll find a layout of our current vs. future state.

We already have a proposed data structure (drafted by our Sr. RevOps Manager and attached to this job posting), and historical contracts are being prepared for migration. Now we need an expert to help us:

-- Validate the proposed structure within HubSpot. Be honest with us – will it work, or should we go about this in a different way?

-- Build and test the setup with HubSpot reporting in a sandbox environment.

-- Push to production with historical data migrated.

-- Advise on process and automation to ensure ongoing accuracy and scalability.

Deliverables

-- Review and validate our proposed HubSpot Deal & Line Item structure. Will it enable us to answer questions like:
  • ----- How much of X product was sold in Y geographies?
  • ----- Which ARR, by product, is up for renewal next year?
    -- Build test deals and reporting in a HubSpot sandbox.
    -- Confirm the structure supports reporting on ARR, renewals, and product-level bookings.
    -- Help roll historical contracts into production under the new format.
    -- Recommend permissions, data entry rules, and automations to ensure data quality moving forward.

Scope Notes

Out of scope: Moving to HubSpot Quotes (all contracting is currently done outside HubSpot).
  • Future consideration: Must be compatible with future NetSuite integration for financials and revenue recognition.

Access & Support
You’ll have full access to our HubSpot sandbox.
  • Our RevOps team will collaborate closely and provide historical data for testing.
  • Work can be asynchronous, but we’d like short kickoff and handoff calls.

Timeline
Start: Within 1 week
  • Duration: 2–3 weeks

About Us
This project is posted by Kyleigh Andries, Sr. RevOps Manager at Opensignal (LinkedIn).
Opensignal is the leading global provider of independent insights into consumers’ connectivity experiences and choice of carrier. Learn more at opensignal.com.
proposal_description:

job_title: Enrich Contact records in Hubspot from Wealth Engine API

proposal_description:
I see you need contact enrichment from Wealth Engine – that's typically tied to either major donor prospecting or high-value sales targeting. What I'm curious about is what happens AFTER you have the wealth data.

Most organizations I work with get the enrichment right but miss the bigger opportunity: they enrich thousands of contacts but don't fundamentally change how their team prioritizes or engages with those contacts. The data sits in HubSpot, but the behavior stays the same.

A few questions that would help me determine if we're the right fit:

1. What business decision or process does having wealth indicators unlock for your team? (For example: prioritizing major donor outreach, segmenting campaigns by capacity, qualifying enterprise prospects differently)

2. After a contact record gets enriched with wealth data, what happens next? Is there a workflow, assignment rule, or campaign that triggers – or is someone manually reviewing enriched records?

3. You mentioned filtering contacts before enrichment. What's the criteria, and why not enrich your entire database? (This tells me you're being strategic about costs, which is smart.)

4. Is this a one-time enrichment project or do you need ongoing syncing as new contacts enter HubSpot?

5. If we build a perfect integration but six months from now your team's close rate or donor conversion hasn't changed, what would that cost you in missed opportunities?

The technical integration you've outlined is straightforward – that's the easy part. Where these projects typically fail is when the enriched data doesn't translate into different actions. The budget range you've mentioned would cover the integration work, but I want to make sure you're factoring in Wealth Engine's API costs and thinking through the activation strategy.

Would a 20-minute call make sense to dig into these questions? I can walk you through how we've approached data enrichment for similar use cases, and you can decide if our thinking aligns with what you need.

job_title: Enrich Contact records in Hubspot from Wealth Engine API
job_description: We need to create an integration between HubSpot and Wealth Engine to enrich a filtered set of contact records.
proposal_description:
Reforge Solutions Proposal

Project: WealthEngine API → HubSpot Contact Enrichment
Client Type: HubSpot customer (internal integration)
Timeline: Approximately 3 weeks


Why Reforge

Reforge Solutions is a HubSpot-alumni-led consultancy trusted by startups and enterprises to make complex automations work inside HubSpot.
With more than eight years of RevOps and API integration experience, we specialize in connecting systems like Salesforce, Clay, and Apollo to HubSpot, turning messy data into clean, actionable insights.

We focus on clarity, speed, and results. You get reliable delivery and documentation your team can easily maintain after handoff.


Scope of Work

Phase 1 – Discovery and Mapping (up to 1 week)

  • Define which HubSpot contacts will be enriched using filters or lists.
  • Review WealthEngine API capabilities and confirm whether Basic or Full profiles are needed.
  • Map enrichment fields to HubSpot properties

Phase 2 – Integration Build (1 week)

  • Authenticate to the WealthEngine API using a secure key.
  • Build a custom HubSpot workflow action (Ops Hub or Zapier/Make) that:
    • Pulls eligible contacts.
    • Calls WealthEngine’s /v1/profile/find_one/by_email endpoint.
    • Writes enrichment data to HubSpot contact properties.
    • Logs match status and last-enriched date.

Phase 3 – Testing, QA, and Handoff (1 week)

  • Test enrichment on a pilot batch of up to 500 contacts.
  • Validate property mappings, rate-limit control, and error handling.
  • Deliver a runbook detailing:
    • How to trigger and schedule enrichment
    • How to monitor match rates and costs
    • Recommended re-enrichment cadence (every 90–180 days)


Timeline 

Deliverable
Week 1 | Discovery, mapping, and API connection
Week 2 | Integration build and internal testing
Week 3 | QA, documentation, and handoff


Investment

Fixed Fee: $4,000 USD

Includes:

  • All three phases (Discovery, Build, and Handoff)
  • One post-launch support session within 30 days
  • Full documentation and source code handover


Assumptions

  • Integration is for internal HubSpot use only, not a public app.
  • Client provides WealthEngine API key and HubSpot Private App token.
  • WealthEngine billing for enrichment matches is handled by the client.
  • Enrichment runs via controlled batch execution because WealthEngine does not yet offer a batch endpoint.


Acceptance Criteria

✅ Successful enrichment of pilot contacts through the WealthEngine API
✅ All mapped wealth fields correctly populated in HubSpot
✅ Error handling, logging, and re-enrichment logic functioning correctly
✅ Documentation and training handoff completed

 Next Steps

Let’s schedule a quick meeting to walk through your specific goals and confirm the enrichment logic.

job_title: Fix HubSpot Tracking and Attribution for Webinar Landing Pages (Demio Form Integration)
job_description:
Background:
Our law firm hosts weekly educational webinars using Demio as our webinar platform. Registrations for these webinars are collected via landing pages that include an embedded Demio registration form (iframe). We promote these webinars through paid ads, directing leads to the landing page.
Currently, contact records created from these webinar registrations in HubSpot are missing key attribution data — specifically: Original Source Drill-Down 1, Original Source Drill-Down 2
This data loss prevents us from accurately tracking the effectiveness of our marketing campaigns and understanding the true source of leads.

Problem Statement:
The issue appears to stem from the Demio form being embedded via an iframe, which likely blocks HubSpot’s tracking scripts from capturing visitor and UTM parameters. As a result, HubSpot attributes these contacts incorrectly (or not at all) when they sync through the Demio integration.

Goal:
Ensure that all lead tracking and attribution data (UTMs, Original Source, Drill-Downs, etc.) flows correctly into HubSpot when visitors register for webinars, allowing accurate campaign performance reporting.
Additionally, ensure that the number of contacts coming from our paid (“money”) ads are all properly accounted for, so we can measure our ad performance more effectively and accurately.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

I see you're hosting weekly webinars and running paid ads, but the Demio iframe is blocking HubSpot's attribution tracking. This isn't just a technical headache - it means you're making ad budget decisions blind.

Here's what I'm seeing: most law firms in this situation either overspend on underperforming channels or kill campaigns that are actually working. I worked with a professional services firm last year with a similar Demio setup - they discovered their "best performing" LinkedIn ads were driving half the conversions they thought, while an underinvested Google campaign was their actual winner.

2. If we can't fix this, what does another 6 months of blind ad spending cost you? What's your monthly webinar ad budget?

job_title: Hubspot CPQ and Revenue Recognition
job_description:
Context:
Greenshades currently uses HubSpot as our primary CRM for marketing, sales, and pipeline management, and Microsoft Dynamics 365 for customer management, renewals, billing, and entitlements. PandaDoc is used for quotes and signatures but is not fully connected to ARR tracking or invoicing. 

Our goal is to determine whether HubSpot’s Commerce Hub can meet our CPQ and revenue recognition needs — or if an external system (e.g., PandaDoc CPQ) should be layered in.  

Current State 
  • HubSpot manages all funnel, deal, and opportunity tracking.
  • Dynamics 365 manages invoices, renewals, and customer entitlements.
  • PandaDoc used for quote generation and signatures, but ARR and bookings are not captured in HubSpot.
  • ARR calculations currently run via SQL and Excel, not within CRM.

 
Desired State
  • HubSpot becomes the single source of truth for accounts, opportunities, and ARR.
  • Automated quote-to-invoice process using HubSpot Commerce Hub or PandaDoc CPQ.
  • Product catalog, pricing, term, and discount logic managed directly within HubSpot.
  • Closed-won deals automatically sync to Finance for billing and entitlement provisioning.
  • ARR, MRR, and Bookings visible directly in HubSpot dashboards and reports.

 
What We Need
 
  1. Guidance on whether Commerce Hub can serve as our unified CPQ + revenue management tool.
  2. Prescriptive recommendations on:
    • Setting up product catalog + ARR calculations in HubSpot.
    • Integrating PandaDoc (if retained) for quoting/invoicing workflows.
    • Ensuring data sync and reporting accuracy between HubSpot and Dynamics 365.
  3. Suggested implementation partner or HubSpot resource for setup and configuration.
proposal_description:
Hey Greenshades Team,

Thank you for sharing this opportunity. I read through your situation, and my first reaction was: yes, this is exactly the kind of RevOps architecture project that OneMetric and specifically I, as a senior HubSpot consultant thrive on.

When I hear challenges like ARR and bookings are disconnected, invoices live in Dynamics, quotes in PandaDoc, spreadsheets running critical calculations, I know exactly what that feels like. You’re working with a fractured system where visibility and data trust are fragmented  and that undermines confidence in your revenue reporting. When HubSpot feels more like a dashboard mess than a single source of truth, it’s time for a systems reset.

At OneMetric, a HubSpot Elite Partner (top 1%) with deep RevOps expertise, we’ve helped enterprise and mid-market SaaS companies unify their go-to-market motion. Our team, builds scalable GTM architecture, integrates finance systems, and creates clean data models so teams can rely on one trusted source-of-truth.

Here’s how I would approach your specific goals 

Phase 0 - Blueprint & System Audit
  • Map your current quote → invoice → renewal flow across HubSpot, Dynamics, and PandaDoc.
  • Review how ARR/MRR is calculated today and what needs to shift into HubSpot.
  • Produce the decision framework: Commerce Hub vs. PandaDoc CPQ, based on complexity, pricing rules, and financial dependencies.

Phase 1 - CPQ & Revenue Architecture
If Commerce Hub is the right fit:
  • Build your product catalog, pricing logic, contract terms, and ARR formulas in HubSpot.
  • Configure Quotes + deal automation + closed-won triggers into Dynamics.
If PandaDoc remains essential:
  • Use HubSpot as the master for products/terms and push clean data into PandaDoc.
  • Sync quote outputs back into HubSpot so ARR, bookings, and contract dates live in CRM.
Either way, HubSpot becomes the single source for ARR/MRR/Bookings reporting.

Phase 2 - Reporting & Team Enablement
  • Build RevOps dashboards for bookings, ARR, renewals, and expansion.
  • Document the quote-to-cash workflow and train sales + finance via short Looms.
  • Run a QA round with finance to ensure numbers match Dynamics.

A Few Clarifying Questions
  1. Which HubSpot tier are you using Sales/Service Enterprise or Pro?
  2. How complex are your SKU rules (bundles, tiers, term-based pricing)?
  3. Does PandaDoc push any data back today, or is it one-way?
  4. Should renewals ultimately be owned by HubSpot or stay in Dynamics?
  5. Do you want ARR reporting native to HubSpot dashboards, or exported to BI?

Here are a few case studies showcasing how we have helped business to leverage Hubspot for their businesses -
1) Reengineering Sales in HubSpot for Qashio
2) Integrating Hint Health with HubSpot
3) Unifying sales, marketing & onboarding for an AI platform with HubSpot
4) Boosting Pipeline 83% with Lead Management

Our goal isn’t just to connect the tools  it’s to make HubSpot a revenue system you can actually trust. Happy to walk you through similar architectures we’ve built or begin with a short audit conversation. Let's talk!

Cheers,
Todd!

job_title: HubSpot Dashboard Buildout
job_description:
We need three advanced dashboards to support our 2026 GTM plan. These dashboards must be accurate, automated, and exec-ready.

Dashboards needed:
1. 2026 Channel Quota Pacing Dashboard
  • - Shows quota attainment vs. plan across all GTM channels
    - Tracks pacing, trends, and contribution toward revenue goals
    - Must include monthly and quarterly views

    2. Funnel Conversion & Efficiency Dashboard (CPQM Dashboard)
    - Full-funnel conversion tracking (meeting booked → qualified → closed-won)
    - Cost per qualified meeting (CPQM) by channel
    - Efficiency metrics, bottleneck analysis, and historical trending

    3. Channel Owner Scorecards
    - Per-channel owner performance view (Outbound BDR, vendors, etc.)
    - Pipeline sourced, meetings booked, opps created, revenue tied to each channel owner
    - Red/green indicators and weekly pacing

    What we need from you:
    - Expertise in HubSpot reporting, custom properties, datasets, and attribution
    - Ability to translate business requirements into clean, actionable dashboards
    - A short onboarding call to align on definitions and naming conventions
    - Delivery of dashboards that leadership can use for weekly forecasting

    Our goal:
    To consolidate all GTM performance into a single set of dashboards that allow us to see quota pacing, funnel health, channel performance, and efficiency at a glance.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

You've clearly thought through what you need, which tells me you've been living with reporting gaps that make forecasting feel like guesswork. The dashboard specs are solid. Here's what will make or break this project.

Dashboards are only as trustworthy as the data feeding them. I worked with a SaaS company last quarter that built exec-ready reporting but leadership stopped using it within two months because their attribution model was set up wrong. The dashboards looked great but the numbers didn't match reality.

The onboarding call you mentioned to align on definitions and naming conventions will likely be multiple calls. I want to set expectations correctly - this is the most important part of our engagement together. If attribution, lifecycle stages, and channel owner associations aren't bulletproof before we build anything, you'll have dashboards nobody trusts.

**Two questions:**

1. How confident are you right now that the data in HubSpot accurately reflects which channel owners sourced which pipeline? Is that clean, or is that part of what we're solving?

2. When leadership looks at these dashboards weekly, what decision are they trying to make that they can't make today?

Based on your scope, this typically runs $3-5K depending on data complexity. If we need foundational work on attribution or custom properties first, that shifts things. Worth a 30-minute call to diagnose whether this is a straightforward build or if there's upstream work first.

Does a call make sense as a next step? So we can take a look at what your channels are and how HubSpot is currently laid out?

Thanks for the detailed brief.

Grant Hushek
Founder @ Grantbot

job_title: Contact & Lead Lifecycle Best Practices Learning & Implementation
job_description: We are looking to get guidance on contact and lead lifecycle best practices for a software engineering and consulting company. The outcome is to have the correct process and fields implemented into HubSpot, preparing us for the next step in our marketing journey.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

I know exactly what this feels like. When a growing consulting or software firm gets serious about marketing, HubSpot often starts to surface issues you didn’t even realize were there data clutter, unclear lead stages, lifecycle stages being used differently by sales and marketing, and reporting that doesn’t quite tell the truth. When HubSpot becomes more of a chore than a tool, that’s usually a sign of process drift not a bad team.

We’ve stepped into this situation many times before. Especially with engineering and consulting teams, the challenge isn’t effort  it’s defining a lifecycle model that actually reflects how real conversations turn into revenue, without over-engineering things.

I’m Todd, Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric. We’re an Elite HubSpot Partner, and a big part of our work is “steadying the system” after partial implementations or well-intentioned setups that weren’t designed with long-term growth in mind.

A couple of relevant examples:
A global market intelligence company streamlined complex marketing processes using HubSpot marketing automation, resulting in a 48% reduction in page editing time and a 28% increase in meetings booked
https://www.onemetric.io/resources/case-study/global-market-intelligence

Here’s how I’d approach your engagement:

Phase 0 - Discovery & Lifecycle Blueprint
We start with a focused audit of your current HubSpot setup: lifecycle stages, lead status usage, core contact/company fields, workflows, and reporting dependencies. The goal here is to understand how your business actually moves from first touch to closed-won not how HubSpot defaults suggest it should.

Phase 1 - Core Implementation & Cleanup
Once the blueprint is validated, we implement it directly in HubSpot. That usually includes tightening lifecycle logic, cleaning or consolidating fields, updating workflows that move contacts between stages, and fixing any automation that’s creating noise or false signals.

Phase 2 - Optimization, Reporting & Enablement
Finally, we make the system usable. That means lifecycle-aligned dashboards, basic governance rules, and short Loom walkthroughs so your team knows how (and when) to use each stage and field. The aim is adoption not documentation that sits in a folder.

To make sure we’re aligned, here are a few questions I’d love to explore together:
  1. How do sales and marketing define a “qualified lead,” and where do those definitions break down?
  2. Are lifecycle stage changes happening manually, automatically, or inconsistently?
  3. Which reports do leaders rely on today and where do they feel the least confident?
  4. Are there integrations (forms, ads, product signups, CRM syncs) influencing lifecycle stages today?

Our goal isn’t just to fix HubSpot it’s to make your team self-reliant on it with a lifecycle model that scales as you grow.

Happy to start with a short audit call or walk you through similar before-and-after examples if helpful. Let's talk!

Cheers,
 Todd

Time management: We would need a rough idea of how many hours each "project" (e.g., building out an agreed number of reports and associated dashboards) would take once scoped but prior to launching (e.g., 10 reports for 3 dashboards would take 3 hours - making numbers up here). Also would need a regular update (probably weekly) of how many hours we've used that month. 

About us: We have an enterprise subscription. Our culture is collaborative, creative, and hard-working (wow, bunch of alliteration there). We think we're a pretty fun group of humans to work with. We have two full time employees who are acting as a part-time admin and a part-time project manager; our head of dev and head of sales/marketing is pretty involved as well. We're used to having a weekly meeting to talk through current initiatives and work through challenges. This is not a meeting that could be an email; we have a specific, detailed agenda where each topic has a specific goal and status updates are more talking through challenges that came up when somebody was handling their part that need some collaboration. 

Full transparency: we have been working with a firm for about two years, but they recently raised their prices ($172/hour up from $152/hour) so looking to do a price check. This is not a procurement, check-the-box type request - we are seriously considering a switch. If we decide to switch, we are looking to implement in early January. 

Happy to answer any other questions
proposal_description:
Hi there,

You've clearly built a solid working rhythm with your current partner. Two years is real tenure. The fact that you're price-checking over a $20/hour increase tells me either the value conversation has gotten stale, or the relationship has drifted from strategic to transactional.

That's actually the more interesting question than hourly rates.

Two things I'm curious about:
1. When you describe the monthly scope as "work we don't have internal resources to get done," is that how your current partner frames it? Or do they connect each initiative to a business outcome you're tracking?
2. On the website rebuild and Service Hub migration: what does "done" look like in revenue or operational terms? (Faster time-to-resolution? Lower churn? Marketing team velocity?)

We've structured something called Growth Insurance specifically for teams in your situation: ongoing coverage with a 14-day jumpstart and quarterly optimization baked in. This is our managed service option - like a HubSpot administrator. When you need larger implementations that require strategic vision, we'll do the scoping as part of this service, learn the metrics that matter, and timeline needed to move your needle.

Worth a 30-minute call to see if there's a fit? Here's my calendar. Thanks for the transparency on budget and situation. Makes the conversation easier.

job_title: HubSpot CRM Design for Inventory-Driven Equipment Sales  HubSpot Sales Architecture for Heavy Equipment Dealer (NetSuite ERP)
job_description:
We are a heavy equipment dealer evaluating and implementing HubSpot as our primary CRM.
Our goal is to design HubSpot correctly for an inventory-driven, dealer-style sales model, not a generic SaaS setup.
Context:
  • Business model: Used heavy equipment sales with financing
  • Sales motion: High-volume inbound leads + negotiated, machine-specific deals
  • ERP: NetSuite (system of record)
  • Lead sources: Listing sites, inbound email, phone, website forms
  • Reps are mobile and speed-to-lead is critical
What we need help with:
  • HubSpot data model and architecture (deals, equipment/machines, financing context)
  • Lead routing, SLAs, and rep accountability
  • Modeling machine-specific deals (unit, hours, attachments, condition)
  • Preserving inquiry history even after inventory turns
  • Pipeline design, forecasting, and reporting
  • Clean integration strategy with NetSuite (prefer configuration over heavy custom code)
  • Clear guidance on what HubSpot can vs cannot do, and recommended workarounds
What we are not looking for:
  • Generic HubSpot onboarding
  • Marketing-only CRM implementations
  • Heavy custom development without a strong rationale
  • Junior teams following a template
We are starting with architecture and design, followed by phased implementation. We value partners who understand dealer/distributor sales models and can think independently.
proposal_description:
Nice to meet you, my name is Bill Barlas, CEO of Quick2Bid. We are a technical agency focused entirely around improving Quote to Cash processes for Equipment Manufacturers and dealers.

Our team can help you with the following:
1: HubSpot data model
2: Best uses of the various hubs within HubSpot
3: System integrations (NetSuite in this case)

We also have very strong Configure, Price, Quote background which will be extremely handy when it comes to designing your inventory, and product logic within the HubSpot platform.

Quick2Bid is a small, US based technical team and we take significant pride in reducing complex problems to their core and solving them quickly with HubSpot and other technologies.

In order to ensure we are the right fit I would propose an initial 1 hour call to discuss your needs, learn a little more about your technology stack, etc.

From there we typically dive into a paid discovery session (typically 2-4k) where we will interview your users, understand how things are done today, map the process, and provide architectural guidance. We can also build the solutions as needed.

Here is my scheduling link: https://meetings.hubspot.com/bill-barlas

job_title: HubSpot CRM Design for Inventory-Driven Equipment Sales  HubSpot Sales Architecture for Heavy Equipment Dealer (NetSuite ERP)
job_description:
We are a heavy equipment dealer evaluating and implementing HubSpot as our primary CRM.
Our goal is to design HubSpot correctly for an inventory-driven, dealer-style sales model, not a generic SaaS setup.
Context:
  • Business model: Used heavy equipment sales with financing
  • Sales motion: High-volume inbound leads + negotiated, machine-specific deals
  • ERP: NetSuite (system of record)
  • Lead sources: Listing sites, inbound email, phone, website forms
  • Reps are mobile and speed-to-lead is critical
What we need help with:
  • HubSpot data model and architecture (deals, equipment/machines, financing context)
  • Lead routing, SLAs, and rep accountability
  • Modeling machine-specific deals (unit, hours, attachments, condition)
  • Preserving inquiry history even after inventory turns
  • Pipeline design, forecasting, and reporting
  • Clean integration strategy with NetSuite (prefer configuration over heavy custom code)
  • Clear guidance on what HubSpot can vs cannot do, and recommended workarounds
What we are not looking for:
  • Generic HubSpot onboarding
  • Marketing-only CRM implementations
  • Heavy custom development without a strong rationale
  • Junior teams following a template
We are starting with architecture and design, followed by phased implementation. We value partners who understand dealer/distributor sales models and can think independently.
proposal_description:
The main thing I'd solve for is that HubSpot doesn't natively handle inventory-driven sales well, it's built around contacts and deals, not equipment. For your model, I'd build Equipment as its own custom object (serial, hours, condition, attachments) with deals tied to both the buyer and the unit. That way when a machine sells, you keep the inquiry history and your reps can work interested buyers toward similar units.

I've worked with other inventory-driven businesses facing the same structural challenges, and before consulting I spent time at Siemens Energy repair centers, so I understand how equipment-centric operations actually run. This isn't theoretical for me, but something I and my team have experience with through repititions.

Beyond data architecture, I'd focus on lead routing with real SLA tracking (speed-to-lead is where dealers lose deals), pipeline stages that reflect how negotiation and financing actually work in your business, and a NetSuite integration strategy that syncs what matters without building something fragile. 

I typically start with a paid architecture engagement. A few weeks to document your process, design the data model, map the integration, and give you a blueprint. You'd own the deliverable whether we continue together or not. 

Happy to jump on a call and dig into how your team works deals today. That'll tell me whether this is a fit.

job_title: Hubspot Report + Dashboard Creation: email generated revenue and customer LTV
job_description:
We are a membership association that also sells a variety of education-related products and just hit our one-year anniversary with HubSpot. We spent the first year familiarizing ourselves with HubSpot and using the various email tools/automations. Now we are ready to dive deeper into the reporting. We've dabbled a bit and created some helpful dashboards (i.e. email performance by campaign), but we want to add revenue associated to these campaigns. We also want to roll up this data by customer to segment by key audiences (i.e members vs nonmember, early careerist, mid-careerist, legacy purchasers). Our ultimate goal is to determine, measure and compare the lifetime value of these audiences over time to help us make decisions.

HubSpot is integration with our Association Management System (AMS) called NetForum. It houses all of our membership, customer, and purchase data so we should have access to the data we need. It's just a matter of pulling it into HubSpot in a meaningful (and accurate) way.
proposal_description:
You've got the data in NetForum, now you need it structured in HubSpot so you can actually see what's working and who's worth the most.

What we'd do:
  • Review your NetForum > HubSpot integration to make sure revenue and purchase data is flowing correctly
  • Build audience segments: members vs nonmembers, career stages, legacy purchasers
  • Create dashboards tying campaign performance to actual revenue
  • Set up LTV tracking by segment so you can compare and make decisions

Deliverables:
  • Segmentation properties and lists structured for your key audiences
  • Revenue attribution dashboards by campaign
  • LTV reporting by customer segment
  • Quick Supered guide so your team knows how the segmentation logic works and can maintain it

Pricing
$3K–$5K depending on how clean your NetForum data is coming into HubSpot. If the integration needs work, that's a separate conversation.

Next step: Book a quick call. I'll take a look at what's syncing from NetForum and tell you if this is a straightforward build or if we need to fix the plumbing first.

I really loved reading your post! Especially the part about being a women-owned, inclusive mortgage business. For context, I lead CRM and RevOps projects at a HubSpot Elite Partner agency, helping teams like Mortgage Bolt and other boutique financial firms turn HubSpot into the true engine behind their client relationships. We love working with businesses that care deeply about customer experience and community impact; yours fits that perfectly.

Would love to hop on a short call to walk through your current setup and outline a roadmap. Here's my meeting link - https://meetings.hubspot.com/nishant26

job_title: Hubspot Sales Hub, marketing Hub
job_description: I’m looking for a HubSpot freelancer who can support me with CRM setup and optimization work for B2B SaaS companies. Most projects involve configuring pipelines, automations, custom properties, and reports, as well as ensuring data flows cleanly between HubSpot and other tools. I focus on helping B2B SaaS teams streamline their sales and marketing operations, so I’m looking for someone who really understands HubSpot inside out and can help keep things organized, efficient, and scalable across multiple client accounts.
proposal_description:
I see you're supporting multiple B2B SaaS clients with their RevOps – that's a solid niche, and it sounds like you're growing faster than you can deliver on your own. Before I dive into my HubSpot background, I'm curious about what's really driving this search.

Most consultants I've worked with in your position are dealing with one of three situations: either they're turning down good clients because they're at capacity, or they've had freelancers who technically knew HubSpot but couldn't represent their brand well, or they're spending more time project-managing freelancers than actually selling. Which of these resonates most with where you're at?

I ask because you're not just looking for someone who can configure pipelines – you need someone who protects your reputation with every client interaction. That's a different skill set than what most of the other proposals will offer you.

A few questions that would help me understand if I'm the right fit:

1. When you hand off a client project to a freelancer, what's your biggest concern – that they'll miss technical details, that they won't understand the business context behind the setup, or that they'll communicate poorly with your clients?

2. What's happened with previous freelancers or contractors that led you to post this? (I'm guessing this isn't your first hire, and there's usually a pattern in what hasn't worked before.)

3. You mentioned "multiple client accounts" – how do you currently maintain consistency across clients, and what role would you want me to play in that? (Are you looking for someone to follow your playbook, or help you build one?)

4. If I configure everything perfectly but your client's team doesn't actually adopt the new workflows six months later, whose problem is that – yours or mine?

The reason I ask that last question: most HubSpot freelancers are great at technical setup but don't think about adoption. I approach client delivery the same way you probably approach your sales process – the technical work is table stakes, but the real value is in making sure it drives actual behavior change.

About me specifically:
- 4+ years in HubSpot (Sales Hub, Marketing Hub, Service Hub, Operations Hub)
- I've worked with B2B SaaS companies in both direct-to-client and agency/consulting support roles
- I understand revenue operations strategy, not just tools (former sales ops background)
- I can run client calls on your behalf or stay behind the scenes – whatever protects your brand best
- I document everything and can help you systematize your delivery if that's valuable

My agency's approach is different because I'm not just executing your task list – I'm thinking about whether the client is asking for the right thing, and I'll flag it if they're not. (I'm guessing you don't want someone who just says "yes" to every request.)

Would it make sense to spend 30 minutes on a call? You can decide if our approach aligns with how you want to scale your delivery.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

job_title: Nonprofit Membership Database for Hubspot
job_description: Our small nonprofit is seeking help transitioning our current databases (in Excel format) into HubSpot. We will use the Starter Package. Most of the data is membership dues info going back to 1995. We would like project-based bids; our budget is $2,000-$5,000. Specifically, we need help designing the properties in Hubspot and migrating the Excel data into the software. We'll primarily use Hubspot as a static database to start. Ideally, the database should be completed in about a month (end of October).
proposal_description:
Hello,

Thank you for outlining your project. I’d love to support your nonprofit in transitioning from Excel to HubSpot Starter. With your historical membership data, the goal will be to create a clean, structured HubSpot database that’s easy to manage and ready for future growth.

Scope of Work
Property Design
The purpose of this phase is to ensure HubSpot is customized to reflect your nonprofit’s specific membership data. We'll identify data types, key properties, fields to merge, and object-property relationships before generating a property map.

  • Confirm the objects (e.g. Companies, Contacts) and associations being migrated.
  • Confirm primary identifiers for imports (e.g. email, customer #)
  • Define and set up custom HubSpot properties to capture your membership dues and historical data accurately.
  • Test property value input, editing, and deletion
  • Establish clear structure for member records so data remains usable and scalable.

Data Migration
The goal here is to safely transfer decades of data from Excel into HubSpot, while maintaining accuracy and integrity. We'll start with test files a cross-section of your existing data and property values, troubleshoot any issues, and then run larger imports. 

  • Finalize data map Excel --> HubSpot
  • Clean and prepare import files
    • Master files for each object
  • Migrate membership data (dating back to 1995) into HubSpot, preserving historical dues information.
  • Validate migrated records to ensure accuracy.

Configuration & Documentation
This phase makes HubSpot fully functional as your new database and equips your team with the knowledge to manage it.

job_title: Resolve HubSpot ↔ Salesforce Duplicate Company & Contact Data Issues
job_description:
Our current HubSpot–Salesforce integration does not support merging duplicate companies, leaving us with:
  • ~9,000 duplicate companies
  • ~10,000 duplicate contacts that would need to be merged manually, one by one
This volume makes manual cleanup unrealistic and risks ongoing data pollution.

Root Cause
HubSpot’s native integration does not sync merged company records to Salesforce, which prevents us from performing merges on the HubSpot side. The duplicates only live in HubSpot, as we have our own de-duplication system in Salesforce that manages all duplicates. There are no duplicates in Salesforce. 

Previous Attempts
We previously hired two outside agencies to build custom syncing/merge logic.
Both attempts resulted in significant issues, including:
  • Data fields being stripped from Salesforce
  • Incorrect industry assignments being pushed back into HubSpot
  • Unpredictable sync behavior between the systems

Due to these problems, custom-coded solutions have proven risky.

Constraints / Requirements
  1. No loss of Salesforce data during any merge or sync process.
  2. Industry and other key company attributes must remain accurate after merging.
  3. Solution must work within or around HubSpot/Salesforce limitations without breaking the sync.

If a custom solution is required, it must include rigorous safeguards and/or staged testing to prevent previous issues from recurring.

Ideally, the solution should:
  1. Allow bulk or automated duplicate company merging
  2. Allow bulk contact deduplication
  3. Maintain sync integrity between platforms

Desired Outcome
A clear, safe, and maintainable approach to:
  • Eliminating thousands of duplicate companies and contacts
  • Ensuring clean, accurate master records
  • Maintaining a reliable and predictable HubSpot ↔ Salesforce sync going forward
proposal_description:
Enhance Data Reliability: Resolve Duplicates and Prevent Data Pollution


# Introduction

We bring best-in-class Go-To-Market operations to transform tech stack efficiencies, unify cross-functional teams, and enable business growth.

Cracks and breaks in operations for B2B SaaS often start from misaligned teams, siloed data, and outdated processes. Tech debt slows business growth and frustrates customers. We've spent over 15 years as relentless practical learners, partnering with VC and PE-backed SaaS leaders to simplify GTM efficiency and create sustainable business and revenue growth.

Every setup is unique, but we adhere to core principles: fostering collaboration across teams, simplifying processes, leveraging AI, and prioritizing human-centered experiences. 

We believe in embracing imperfections. Like the art of kintsugi, we mend what's broken to create something stronger and more beautiful. Let’s transform your revenue operations into your competitive advantage. 

Our strategic expertise ensures your organization navigates this cleanup effectively, with safeguards to prevent past pitfalls.

We are committed to guiding you every step, delivering clarity and results.

Thank you for considering  this proposal.

# Strategy Brief

Client: HubSpot Customer
As a HubSpot user relying on integrated CRM systems to drive revenue, your organization faces data quality hurdles that impede efficiency and decision-making.

We're passionate about making HubSpot your strategic powerhouse, aligned with Salesforce for unified operations.

Goal: Prioritize data integrity and efficiency to fuel business momentum
  • Resolve ~9,000 duplicate companies and ~10,000 duplicate contacts in HubSpot without manual effort or data loss.
  • Implement bulk/automated merging that preserves data quality, ensures accurate attributes (e.g., industry), and maintains sync reliability.
  • Establish preventive measures for ongoing deduplication, reducing future pollution and building trust in your tech stack.
  • Minimize mean time to repair (MTTR)—the average time between detecting a duplicate and resolving it—to enhance operational efficiency.

Tech Stack: HubSpot, Salesforce
As your GTM consultants, we will learn and understand your business operations. We’ll analyze your integration to optimize data flow, and mend sync gap.

# Scope of Services

Kintsugi Marketing agrees to provide the following services: 30 hours of Strategic Support and Data Management

Services include but are not limited to:

Strategic Support and Data Management
  • Conduct a thorough audit of duplicates in HubSpot
  • Map Company and Contact data in HubSpot and Salesforce for safe merging
  • Identify broader data anomalies that could impact integration and data integrity
  • Implement Koalify (HubSpot-integrated deduplication app) 
    • A native HubSpot app to merge company records and contact record, and configure custom rules to match by key identifiers
  • Develop staged testing protocols: Preview merges, simulate sync impacts, and verify no data loss or attribute type mismatches.
  • Create automated workflows for ongoing deduplication
  • Create HubSpot dashboard to monitor data anomalies 
  • Provide training and documentation on merge best practices 
  • Ensure all actions prioritize constraints: 
    • No Salesforce data loss
    • Accurate key attributes
    • Intact sync behavior
  • If needed, explore low-risk custom middleware (e.g., via Zapier or HubSpot Operations Hub) with rigorous safeguards, but prioritize off-the-shelf solutions to minimize risks.

# Timeline

Start: December 8, 2025
End: December 31, 2025
Engagement: 3 weeks
Phases: Audit & Plan (week 1), Staged Cleanup (week 2), Verify & Sustain (week 3)

# Investment

Based on previous experience and our current understanding of the situation, we anticipate providing 30 hours of Strategic HubSpot Support and Data Management: $3,750.00 (USD) Total.

The Koalify Pro Plan will cost $20 per month for 20,000 records to deduplicate.
