Grant access to HubSpot and Salesforce (read/write as needed), purchase Koalify, provide integration details, designate a point of contact, and review previews and deliverables promptly.

Consultant
Adhere to deadlines, implement with zero-tolerance for data risks, and deliver high-quality, robust solutions aligned with business goals.

  2.  Review and provide feedback on this proposal
  3.  Sign-off on the proposal
  4.  Complete the consulting agreement
  5.  Schedule kick-off call for Monday, December 8th

job_title: HubSpot to Netsuite connection
job_description: We are working with a client to establish a sync from HubSpot deals to Netsuite invoices. The client has 6 business units and each unit will require some differences in the deal and associated data to be pushed to netsuite - though all 6 would trigger at the same deal stage. Line items need to be included. We also require the status of invoice to write back to the HubSpot deal, showing when sent/paid.
proposal_description:
Hello, My name is Bill Barlas, I am an integration expert with years of experience working on integrations.

My proposed plan will allow you to use the standard HubSpot <> NetSuite integration (secure, free), while making use of 1 workflow (you will have configuration capabilities related to this) and 1 scripts (written in Javascript) to manage this integration.

High Level Architecture:

  • Within HubSpot based on Deal Status and flag dictating business unit create a Sales Order in NetSuite (This is an OOTB workflow feature of the HubSpot <> NetSuite marketplace)
    • We can use the deal data to determine what data to push to the Sales order
  • Use a SuiteScript (NetSuite Javascript function) to generate an invoice when the Sales Order is created Docs: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/chapter_4387172221.html 
  • Use the HubSpot <> NetSuite standard integration to sync invoices back to HubSpot & Associate them to the deal

(Diagram is attached)

Engagement Deliverables:

1. HubSpot Deal-to-Sales Order Workflow Configuration
  • Configure a standard HubSpot workflow that:
    • Triggers based on Deal Stage and Business Unit flags.
    • Pushes deal data to NetSuite to create a Sales Order via the OOTB HubSpot <> NetSuite connector.

2. SuiteScript 1: Sales Order to Invoice Automation
  • Develop and deploy a custom NetSuite SuiteScript that:
    • Listens for new Sales Order creation.
    • Automatically generates an associated Invoice in NetSuite.
    • Conforms to NetSuite API and deployment standards.

3. Invoice Sync Back to HubSpot
  • Leverage the existing NetSuite <> HubSpot connector to:
    • Sync invoices from NetSuite to HubSpot.
    • Automatically associate invoices with the originating deal in HubSpot.
    • Confirm bidirectional visibility of financial status.

4. Documentation & Knowledge Transfer
  • Provide a walkthrough document detailing:
    • Workflow trigger setup in HubSpot.
    • SuiteScript deployment steps in NetSuite.
    • Data mapping assumptions.
    • Support boundaries and future enhancement considerations.

5. Testing & Validation
  • End-to-end integration testing of:
    • Deal to Sales Order trigger.
    • Sales Order to Invoice generation.
    • Invoice sync and HubSpot association.

Required HubSpot Hubs & Tiers (HubSpot Side):

1. Operations Hub – Starter or Higher
  • Purpose: Enables the use of HubSpot’s Data Sync feature, which is essential for integrating with NetSuite.

Diagnostic Report

Solution Blueprint

Phased Roadmap

job_title: Properties, Workflows, & Sales Playbook
job_description: We need specific properties set up and workflows to ensure that all customized properties are filled out for company and contact records for detailed report and to be able to hyper target certain segments.  We also need to set up a sales playbook.
proposal_description:
Hi there, 

I have drafted the proposal below and have done this for a plethora of organizations including the likes of Alex Hormozi and Acquisition.com where I built all their marketing, sales and service automation directly into HubSpot.

Objective
Establish a clean, standardized data structure in HubSpot that ensures every Company and Contact record contains the right information for reporting, segmentation, and sales enablement. In parallel, create a Sales Playbook to guide reps through consistent qualification, outreach, and handoff processes. 

Scope of Work 
  1. Custom Property Framework
    • Identify and create all required custom properties for both Company and Contact objects (e.g., industry, lead source, region, lifecycle stage, intent score, etc.).
    • Standardize naming conventions, field types, and dropdown options to ensure data accuracy and reporting consistency.
    • Implement property-level requirements within record views (e.g., mandatory fields based on lifecycle stage or pipeline).
  2. Data Validation & Automation
    • Build workflows to automatically flag or notify users when key fields are missing or incomplete.
    • Automate data syncing between Company and Contact records (e.g., company type → contact persona alignment).
    • Set up internal notifications for record owners when data updates are required.
  3. Sales Playbook Setup
    • Create a HubSpot Sales Playbook accessible from within deals, including qualification questions, objection handling, and field entry guidance.
    • Integrate the Playbook into the existing deal pipeline(s) for structured call notes and standardized data capture.
    • Train users on how to access and use the Playbook effectively during discovery and proposal stages.

Deliverables
 
  • Fully configured property set for Company and Contact objects
  • Automated workflows for validation and field completion tracking
  • Active Sales Playbook integrated into HubSpot CRM
  • One recorded walkthrough and documentation for internal reference

Estimated Timeline: 2–3 weeks
Outcome: Cleaner data, improved reporting accuracy, and consistent sales execution that supports targeted marketing and leadership visibility.

 Month 3: Team training, testing, and optimization based on feedback

Let us know if you’d like us to share a sample scope or book a discovery session.

Thanks & Much Respect
Tim
HubSpot Certified Partner | Sales and Marketing Hub Experts

job_title: HubSpot Solutions Specialist (Long-Term Partnership Opportunity)
job_description:
We’re looking for an experienced HubSpot Solutions Specialist to become a long-term, trusted partner of our agency—someone capable of white-labeling under the TrueVyne name and supporting multiple client accounts across diverse industries.

This is not a task-taker role. We’re looking for a driver—a proactive consultant and strategist who takes ownership, communicates relentlessly, and delivers an exceptional client experience that builds trust, retention, and growth.

What You’ll Be Doing

You’ll lead and manage HubSpot client accounts across a mix of ad hoc support and ongoing retainers. Every account requires a hands-on, strategic, and consultative approach.

Your Responsibilities Include:

  • Serve as the primary HubSpot expert and relationship manager for assigned clients.

  • Conduct monthly account audits to identify gaps, errors, and opportunities for optimization.

  • Deliver proactive recommendations for improving HubSpot utilization, automation, and reporting.

  • Monitor and manage workflow errors, troubleshooting, and maintenance.

  • Lead the implementation of new features, betas, or integrations, and present the value to clients.

  • Identify opportunities for upsells, add-on projects, and new feature adoption to drive revenue growth.

  • Collaborate with internal developers on technical builds or integrations, providing clear direction and context.

  • Ensure proactive, consistent client communication- they should never have to ask for updates.

  • Provide clear project status reports, timelines, and “what’s next” visibility at all times.

  • Develop a deep understanding of each client’s business operations to make meaningful, data-driven recommendations.

Who You Are

We’re not looking for a typical HubSpot admin. We’re looking for someone who embodies ownership, curiosity, and creative problem-solving.

You might be a great fit if you:

  • Have 2–4+ years of HubSpot experience (Marketing, Sales, or Service Hub).

  • Understand technical and API functions conceptually (no coding needed, but you “get it”).

  • Thrive in a client-facing role with clear communication, documentation, and follow-through.

  • Are obsessed with proactive communication—you drive the relationship forward.

  • Love finding creative solutions to problems that don’t have an out-of-the-box answer.

  • Can balance strategic thinking with tactical execution—you both plan and deliver.

  • Are naturally curious, always learning, and excited to explore new HubSpot tools, betas, and workflows.

  • Take pride in keeping clients informed, confident, and never left to wonder.

  • Care deeply about client success, retention, and long-term relationships.

Working Style & Collaboration

This is a contract-based, flexible partnership role with room for steady growth.

You’ll start on a few client accounts, with the potential to expand as we grow together.

You’ll work closely with TrueVyne’s founder (Dan) and technical lead to ensure delivery excellence, but you’ll have autonomy and ownership over your clients. Communication and initiative are everything—we don’t micromanage.

We operate with:

  • Weekly internal syncs for priority alignment

  • Shared project management tools (ClickUp & HubSpot)

  • Transparent client reporting and documentation
  • Values-driven collaboration: humility, kindness, honesty, transparency

Compensation & Structure

  • Contract basis with flexible hours

  • Pay structured per project, per client, or ongoing retainer (based on engagement level)

  • Opportunity to grow into a long-term strategic partner role with recurring clients and revenue share potential
proposal_description:
Hi Dan,

This post honestly felt like you were describing exactly how we work!

I’m Nishant, a Senior HubSpot Specialist at OneMetric, an Elite HubSpot Partner that often works in a white-label model with agencies like yours. We quietly power HubSpot delivery behind the scenes for several partner agencies across the US, UK, and Australia, managing everything from full CRM implementations to ongoing retainers where we act as the HubSpot “extension” of their team.

Let me break down how we align with everything you’ve outlined:

How We Fit Your Vision Perfectly

You want a driver, not a doer:
That’s exactly how we operate. Every account we touch starts with a discovery audit and a clear quarterly roadmap, identifying gaps in data health, workflows, reporting, and user adoption. We don’t wait for tickets to come in, we create the to-do list before the client even realizes what’s missing.

Strategic + hands-on delivery:
Our internal model balances both! We act as the client-facing strategist, while our backend specialists (automation, data, CMS, and integration experts) handle execution. That means you get one consistent point of contact who drives strategy, while still having the bandwidth of an entire team when things get technical.

Ongoing optimization and audits:
We already run monthly audits for our existing white-label partners, covering:
  • Workflow performance & error handling
  • Form and pipeline health
  • Contact segmentation hygiene
  • Dashboard/reporting accuracy
  • Automation improvement recommendations
Each audit comes with a “next-steps tracker” we maintain inside ClickUp or HubSpot Projects — so your clients always know what’s happening next. Here's how we run our Audits - https://www.onemetric.io/hubspot/hubspot-audit

Communication & visibility:
We mirror your structure, weekly internal syncs, ClickUp task updates, HubSpot notes for transparency, and a clean Slack/Teams communication line. Our biggest compliment from agency partners has been: “You’re the only partner who makes us look good in front of our clients.”

Capabilities Snapshot
We’re comfortable across the entire HubSpot ecosystem:
  • Hubs: Marketing, Sales, Service, and CMS (Pro & Enterprise tiers)
  • Integrations: Salesforce, Apollo, Clearbit, Aircall, Typeform, Zapier, Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads, etc.
  • Automation & RevOps: Advanced workflows, lead scoring, lifecycle management, pipeline automation, lead routing, and campaign attribution
  • Data & Reporting: Custom dashboards, revenue attribution, activity tracking, and forecasting
  • Maintenance: Workflow monitoring, lead source attribution fixes, error alerts, and form sync troubleshooting
We even support tech handovers and documentation for agencies so delivery never depends on one person.

Working Style
We’re very aligned with how you operate:
  • Weekly syncs for priorities
  • Transparent reporting (we already use HubSpot + ClickUp)
  • Proactive status updates so that clients never chase us for progress
  • Kind, honest, no-ego collaboration
We’ve built long-term partnerships where agencies started with one account and within months, we were handling five or six client portals under their brand.

Partnership Structure
We’re flexible here, depending on what stage you want to start:

Option A – Retainer model:
Starts at $3500/month for up to 50 hours/month across 2–3 client portals (can scale as we grow together).
Option B – Per-client project model:
Fixed or hourly (at $70/hr), depending on whether it’s an implementation, cleanup, or ongoing support.
We’re happy to start with a single client account to establish trust, and then expand into a steady, recurring white-label partnership.

Why This Partnership Works:
  • You get strategic leadership + scalable delivery bandwidth without needing to hire full-time staff.
  • Your clients get a consistent experience, clear communication, and tangible ROI from HubSpot.
  • We get to build a long-term partnership based on transparency, mutual growth, and accountability.

If you’re open, I’d love to schedule a quick 30-minute sync this week so we can map out how to get started and which client account makes the most sense for the pilot.
Here’s my calendar - https://meetings.hubspot.com/nishant26

Looking forward to building something long-term and meaningful with TrueVyne,

Nishant

job_title: HubSpot RevOps Consultant (Enterprise)
job_description:
We’ve just moved from SugarCRM to HubSpot and purchased 10 Sales Hub Enterprise seats and 4 Marketing Hub Pro seats. We need an expert to migrate data, set up best-practice RevOps processes, and implement key integrations (Xero, PhoneBurner, Asana).

Objectives
- Migrate all SugarCRM data to HubSpot with clean mapping, dedupe, and validation.
- Build enterprise-grade automations for sales, marketing, and handoffs.
- Integrate Xero (billing), PhoneBurner (dialer/activities), and Asana (projects/tasks).
- Enable the team with permissions, playbooks, sequences, reports, and dashboards.

Scope of Work (deliverables)

1) Discovery & Design
Audit SugarCRM objects, fields, picklists, activities, attachments, and custom logic.
  • Define HubSpot data model: standard + custom objects, associations, required fields.
    Process design: Lead → MQL/SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won/Lost, pipeline stages, entry/exit criteria.
    Security model: teams, roles, permissions, record ownership rules.

2) Data Migration (SugarCRM → HubSpot)
Field mapping doc (1:1 + transformations), deduplication rules, lookup resolution.
Import activities (emails/calls/notes/meetings where supported), files, and lists.
Validation run + reconciliation report (record counts, error log, exceptions).
Delta cutover plan with blackout window and rollback.

3) Sales Hub Enterprise Setup
Pipelines (primary + any sub-pipelines), stage properties, required reasons.
Sequences, templates, snippets, playbooks, call outcomes, meeting links.
Lead scoring (behavioral + firmographic), SLAs and task queues.
Custom quotes, products, deal workflows (create tasks, set owners, update fields).
Forecasting setup, goals, and weighted pipeline definitions.

4) Marketing Hub Pro Setup
Lifecycle model, MQL definition, subscription types, double opt-in where needed.
Forms, CTAs, lists (smart/static), nurture workflows, and lead routing.
Email + landing page brand modules, compliance (GDPR/CCPA).
UTM + source tracking, campaign hierarchy.

5) Integrations
Xero: deal→invoice sync, invoice status back to HubSpot, revenue fields for reporting.
PhoneBurner: activity logging, call outcomes, contact sync, list handoff.
Asana: deal/contract approval → Asana project creation with key fields synced.
Webhooks/middleware (if needed) with retry + error handling.

6) Reporting & Enablement
Executive dashboards: pipeline health, velocity, forecast vs. quota, win/loss, campaign attribution.
Rep dashboards: tasks due, sequence performance, meetings booked, CAC/LTV basics.
Training: live sessions + Loom library; admin runbook; change log.

It's a long term engagement and you will be working with Sales, marketing & CS teams.
proposal_description:
Hey there,

Moving from a legacy CRM like SugarCRM into the HubSpot ecosystem while simultaneously standing up sales, marketing, and CS processes can feel like a mix of data clutter, process drift, inconsistent reporting, and teams not fully trusting the new system yet. I know exactly what that feels like.

We’ve stepped into this situation many times before, especially when companies upgrade to Sales Hub Enterprise and realize very quickly that the power is there but only if the foundations are right.

When the CRM becomes more of a chore than a tool, that’s usually a sign of misaligned data structures, unclear lifecycle stages, or integrations that don’t reflect real business logic. That’s the exact gap our team at OneMetric is built to solve.

As an Elite HubSpot Partner, we’re often brought in after a migration, a half-finished setup, or a rushed RevOps build. Recently, we:
-How we helped C&C Consulting achieve 30% week-on-week increase in calls by enhancing SDR efficiency and sales strategy with automation.

-How We Revitalized an Existing Database with Marketing Hub, Driving a 24% Monthly Increase in Leads Through a Content-Led Strategy

_We help you migrate from Salesforce to HubSpot with ease. From data migration to process setup, everything is planned, tested, and handled by experienced consultants.

Your project follows the same patterns complex, high-stakes, and touching every team and that’s where a structured, senior-led approach matters. 

How I’ll Approach This

Phase 0 - Discovery, Blueprint & Architecture
Before touching any data, we’ll map:
 • Your SugarCRM objects, fields, lists, and custom logic
 • Associations you rely on (especially between accounts/contacts/opportunities)
 • Required fields for pipeline transitions
 • Your ideal Lead → MQL → SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won/Lost journey
 • The security model your teams actually need (roles, ownership rules, visibility)
The outcome is a RevOps blueprint that prevents rework later.

Phase 1 - Data Migration & Core Build
This is where precision matters:

job_title: Hubspot email campaign setup

Hi there,

Thanks for posting this, I appreciate the opportunity to take a closer look.

I know exactly what this feels like. At some point, ongoing email campaigns stop feeling “strategic” and start feeling repetitive, brittle, and hard to trust. Lists grow messy, engagement plateaus, deliverability becomes a question mark, and the team isn’t fully confident that the right message is reaching the right audience at the right time. When HubSpot email becomes more of a chore than a lever for growth, that’s usually a sign of process drift not a lack of effort. We’ve stepped into this situation many times before.

I’m Todd, a Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric. We’re a HubSpot Elite Partner, and a big part of our work is steadying systems that have grown organically without a clear long-term email or automation strategy. Often, the “ongoing email campaign” question is really about establishing a sustainable, repeatable engine one that protects deliverability, drives consistent engagement, and doesn’t fall apart when the business evolves.

To give you quick context on how we help here:
  • Demand-to-pipeline setup for a B2B SaaS / complex-product company: “Build Foundations of Demand-To-Pipeline Engine https://www.onemetric.io/1mlabs/case-studies/build-foundations-of-demand-to-pipeline-engine

Our approach 

Phase 0 - Discovery & Blueprint
 Before building anything, we audit what’s really happening today: list health, engagement trends, deliverability signals, cadence, and how email ties into CRM and lifecycle data. The outcome here is a clear blueprint for what “ongoing” actually means in your context not just frequency, but logic, ownership, and guardrails.

Phase 1 - Core Campaign Framework
 This is where we implement the foundation: clean segmentation, smart suppression rules, reusable email templates, cadence planning, and (where appropriate) lightweight automation to support ongoing sends. The goal is consistency without rigidity — campaigns that can run week after week without degrading performance.

Phase 2 -Optimization, Reporting & Enablement
Once the engine is running, we fine-tune. That includes engagement-based tweaks, deliverability safeguards, simple dashboards for performance visibility, and clear documentation. We typically enable teams with short Loom walkthroughs or live working sessions so the system doesn’t become consultant-dependent.

To make sure we’re aligned, here are a few questions I’d want to explore early:

 Q4. What does “success” look like internally engagement, pipeline influence, or simply operational efficiency?

Our goal isn’t just to set up an ongoing email campaign it’s to make your team self-reliant on it, with a system that scales and stays healthy over time.

job_title: Hubspot Cleanup Project & Setting Up Proper Data Structure Processes To Limit Future Issues
job_description: The data in our current Hubspot implementation is a bit of a mess. We have Sales Hub Pro and Marketing Hub Pro. The Sales team came went Salesforce to Microsoft Dynamics to Hubspot and the data is definitely not clean. It's affecting our ability to get clear insights using reporting. We need someone to help us clean up the whole thing and then create SOPs/processes for us to follow to ensure data quality is maintained. We will also need ongoing support to ensure data stays clean.
proposal_description:
The Salesforce → Dynamics → HubSpot path tells me there's probably something deeper going on than dirty data. Organizations don't switch CRMs twice because of data quality — they switch because the system doesn't match how the business actually works.

We specialize in two things you mentioned: data architecture that actually fits your business model, and governance systems that keep it clean without requiring heroic effort.

A few questions to see if we'd be a good fit:

1. **What does "customer" actually mean in your business?** Do you sell to companies with multiple locations, or through dealers/distributors, or have ongoing service relationships? The answer shapes whether standard HubSpot objects work or need restructuring.

2. **What reporting are you trying to get that you can't get now?** Sometimes the gap is data quality. Sometimes it's that the data structure itself can't answer the question you're asking.

3. **Who owns data quality today?** If the answer is "no one" or "everyone," that's usually where the system breaks down. Good governance needs clear ownership and processes people will actually follow.

We work with organizations that have complex sales models — dealer networks, subscriptions, field service, inventory-driven sales. If your business model is straightforward, a standard cleanup partner would serve you well.

But if the real issue is that your CRM has never matched how you actually sell and serve customers — and you want a system that stays clean rather than another cleanup project in two years — that's what we build.

Happy to do a quick call to figure out which situation you're in.

Ryan Ginsberg
Unified Support Solutions

is_member (true / false)

job_title: HubSpot & Netsuite Integration
job_description:
We require someone with experience in HubSpot and Netsuite to facilitate an integration between systems.

You'll be working with the team here at Centralise (HubSpot partner agency) who are working with a client to enable a two-way sync of customer and financial data between systems. The team hear have extensive experience in HubSpot and will be able to create required fields and process here, but we require someone with experience in Netsuite and with building out an N8N flow to help join everything together.

The included SOW below details the full project details. Your job would be to own the requirements for any changes and information pulled from Netsuite, and to work with the team to build the N8N led triggers and actions.

SOW:

The purpose of this integration is to facilitate a bidirectional sync of Contact, Company & Deal data between HubSpot & NetSuite in order to provide visibility to the central Business Source of truth (NetSuite) and enable proactive marketing from HubSpot based on synced product/deal data from NetSuite. 


  1. System Responsibilities

System | Primary Responsibility | Description
NetSuite | Business Logic & Data Management | Source of truth for all business data
HubSpot | Customer Engagement & Marketing | Manages customer relationships and marketing campaigns

  1. Key Integration Points

  • Data Hierarchy: NetSuite serves as the master source of truth
  • Critical Updates: Deal synchronisation occurs every 5 minutes
  • Regular Updates: Non-critical data syncs daily or hourly
  • Real-time Events: Customer behaviour activity stream in real-time


  1. Data Flow Scenarios


Scenario 1: New Lead Created in HubSpot → NetSuite Customer Record

  • Trigger: A marketing-qualified lead is created in HubSpot.

  • Flow:

  • HubSpot creates a new Contact and associated Company record.

  • Integration validates required fields (e.g., email, company name).

  • A new Customer record is created in NetSuite, matching the Company and Contact.

  • Custom fields such as “Lead Source” or “Campaign” are mapped and transferred.



Scenario 2: NetSuite Sales Order → HubSpot Deal Update

  • Trigger: A sales rep creates a Sales Order in NetSuite.

  • Flow:

  • Integration identifies the corresponding Contact or Company in HubSpot.

  • A new Deal is created in HubSpot, or an existing Deal is updated.

  • Deal Stage is set to "Closed Won" or "In Fulfillment" based on NetSuite order status.

  • Order amount, line items (optional), and order date are passed to the Deal.



Scenario 3: Contact Updated in HubSpot → Sync to NetSuite

  • Trigger: A user edits a Contact record in HubSpot (e.g., updates email or phone number).

  • Flow:

  • Integration checks if a matching Contact exists in NetSuite (via email or unique ID).

  • Updates the existing NetSuite contact with the new data.

  • Fields such as job title, phone, and address are synced.

  • Audit logs record the change for compliance.


Scenario 4: Customer Record Updated in NetSuite → Sync to HubSpot

  • Trigger: A customer service agent updates billing details in NetSuite.

  • Flow:

  • Integration detects changes via polling or webhook.

  • Updates the corresponding Company and associated Contact in HubSpot.

  • Ensures consistency in address, payment terms, and other synced fields.

Scenario 5: Deal Created in HubSpot → Create Quote or Opportunity in NetSuite

  • Trigger: A sales rep creates a Deal in HubSpot marked as "Qualified to Buy."

  • Flow:

  • Integration generates an Opportunity or Estimate record in NetSuite.

  • Links to the appropriate Customer/Company record.

  • Product line items from HubSpot are mapped to NetSuite items (if SKUs align).

  • Estimated close date and probability fields are also transferred.

Scenario 6: Bi-Directional Sync of Lifecycle Stage or Status

  • Trigger: A record’s lifecycle stage is updated in either system.

  • Flow:

  • HubSpot lifecycle stage is mapped to a NetSuite customer status (e.g., Lead, Prospect, Customer).

  • Changes in one system trigger updates in the other, maintaining alignment.

  • Conflicts are resolved based on a configurable rule (e.g., latest update wins).

Business Rules for Multi-Region System Integration

  1. Customer Identification


Basic Rule: Each customer has ONE global identity but can shop in multiple regions.

How it Works:

  • NetSuite holds the master customer record
  • Customer email is the unique identifier across all systems
  • Each customer gets tagged with their primary region
  • The system tracks which regions they shop in

Example:

Customer: john@example.com

  • Primary Region: UK
  • Also buys in: US & Canada
  • One HubSpot contact record
  • Visible in all business units
  • Single NetSuite record


2. Deal Management

Basic Rule: Deals stay in their region of origin.

How it Works:

  • Orders are processed in the region they're placed
  • Customer can have orders in multiple regions
  • Order history shows all regions in HubSpot
  • NetSuite maintains separate order sequences per region

Example:

  • CAN Order: CAN-12345
  • US Order: US-12345
  • Same customer
  • Different order numbers
  • Process separately
  • View together in HubSpot


    3. Pricing and Currency


Basic Rule: Each unit handles its own pricing.

How it Works:

  • Each region has its own price list
  • Currency stays within its region
  • No automatic currency conversion
  • Prices sync from NetSuite to HubSpot

4. Marketing Communications

Basic Rule: Marketing is region-specific, but customer view is global.

How it Works:

  • Emails sent from customer's active region
  • Promotions are region-specific
  • Customer activity tracked globally
  • Marketing preferences per region

5. Data Updates


Basic Rule: Changes follow a specific update path.

How it Works:

  • NetSuite is always the master system
  • Customer updates flow NetSuite > HubSpot
  • Deals flow HubSpot <> NetSuite
  • Marketing data flows HubSpot <> NetSuite

job_title: HubSpot ↔ Abtrac Integration Build

job_title: HubSpot Implementation & Revenue Integration Specialist
job_description:
Project Overview:
We are seeking an experienced HubSpot implementation partner to help us optimize our CRM and revenue management processes. Our current setup lacks integration and automation, resulting in manual work and unreliable forecasting. We need a solution that connects HubSpot, QuickBooks, and our revenue tracking workflows to create a single source of truth for sales and revenue data.
Key Challenges We Need to Solve:
  • Unreliable Sales Forecasting: Current HubSpot forecasts are not trusted by the team.
  • Revenue Management Gaps: HubSpot is not being used effectively to manage and track revenue.
  • Mixed Sales Models: We have both ARR (annual recurring revenue for software licensing) and fixed-price sales.
  • Disconnected Systems: QuickBooks (finance), HubSpot (CRM), and manual Excel sheets for revenue tracking are not integrated, causing significant manual reconciliation.
  • Recurring Revenue Tracking: We struggle with quoting and tracking recurring revenue accurately.
Objectives:
  • Implement best practices in HubSpot for forecasting and revenue tracking.
  • Configure HubSpot to handle both ARR and fixed-price sales models.
  • Integrate HubSpot with QuickBooks and/or other tools to reduce manual reconciliation.
  • Create a reliable process for quoting and managing recurring revenue.
  • Establish a single source of truth for sales and revenue data.
Deliverables:
  • Audit of current HubSpot setup and recommendations.
  • Implementation of improved forecasting and revenue tracking processes.
  • Integration between HubSpot, QuickBooks, and revenue tracking tools.
  • Documentation and training for internal team.
Additional Info:
  • Looking for a partner who can provide strategic guidance and hands-on implementation.
proposal_description:
Hi,
Thanks so much for sharing the details of your project
— it’s exactly the type of work our team specialises in.
Based on what you’ve described, you’re dealing with challenges we see often when sales, finance, and revenue operations grow faster than the underlying systems: forecasting isn’t trusted, revenue is tracked in multiple tools, and ARR + fixed-price models aren’t fully supported inside HubSpot.
The good news is: all of this can be fixed with the right architecture.

Before jumping into implementation, we always start with a Scoping & Architecture Phase.
This ensures we truly understand your revenue model, your data sources, how QuickBooks is being used today, your quoting process, and how you want forecasting to behave in HubSpot.
It also helps prevent misalignment, rework, and costly integration mistakes later on.

Below is how we’d approach your project.

Phase 1 — Scoping & Revenue Architecture (2–3 weeks)
Goal: Define the right solution before building anything.

This phase includes:
1. HubSpot Audit & Revenue Process Mapping 
  • Review your current HubSpot setup, objects, pipelines, properties, and forecasting model 
  • Analyse your ARR vs fixed-price products, one-time vs recurring revenue, renewals, and expansion/cross-sell structure Document how revenue is currently tracked in Excel and QuickBooks 
  • Identify gaps in automation, lifecycle processes, and reporting 
  • Define your single source of truth 

2. Data Model & Revenue Architecture 
We design the architecture that will support reliable forecasting and reporting, including: 
  • Deal structure for ARR, renewals, and fixed-price projects 
  • Custom objects (if needed) such as Contracts, Subscriptions, or Revenue Snapshots (as we’ve implemented for other clients) 
  • Rules for maintaining data consistency
  •  How forecasting should behave per pipeline / revenue type 

3. Integration Strategy (HubSpot ↔ QuickBooks) 
  • Define what should sync, in which direction, and at what frequency
  • Determine if native integrations, middleware (Zapier/Make), or a custom API is the best approach 
  • Outline reconciliation rules: what data lives in finance vs CRM 
  • Produce a technical integration plan for your development/finance teams 

4. Reporting Framework 
  • Design the dashboards you need for: 
    • Forecast accuracy ACV/ARR tracking 
    • Renewal pipeline health 
    • Revenue recognition snapshots 
    • Expansion and contraction revenue 
  • Identify required datasets and calculations

Deliverables for Scoping Phase 
You receive: 
  • Full HubSpot Audit Revenue Architecture Diagram 
  • Data Model Blueprint (objects, associations, properties) 
  • Integration Strategy Document 
  • Reporting Specification 
  • Implementation Roadmap + Timeline + Investment Estimate 
This delivers a clear, validated roadmap before entering build mode.

Timeline: ~2–3 weeks
Hours: 16 hours
Investment:  $3,400 USD


Phase 2 — Implementation & Technical Execution
Once the scope is approved, implementation typically covers: Building the optimised data model in HubSpot Implementing the new forecasting and revenue tracking logic Configuring ARR, renewals, and fixed-price deal flows Setting up automations for revenue updates and lifecycle transitions Integrating HubSpot with QuickBooks (directly or via middleware) Creating the dashboards and revenue reports Testing, validation, and stabilisation period Documentation + training sessions (Loom videos included)
This mirrors our approach in past complex revenue projects, where we rebuilt their revenue model, created revenue snapshot automation, unified their pipelines, and established reliable forecasting structures.

Why We Recommend This Approach
Our experience shows that skipping the scoping phase creates gaps in forecasting, broken revenue reporting, and failed integrations between HubSpot and finance tools. Your mixed sales model (ARR + fixed-price) and the need for a single source of truth make upfront architecture essential.

Next Steps
If you'd like, we can schedule a 30-minute call to validate your needs and outline the scope in more detail.
From there, we can begin Phase 1.
Here’s my calendar: 👉 https://meetings.hubspot.com/lucila 

You also reach out to Martin Angeletti, 
here is his calendar: 👉 andimol.co/meetings/mangelet/30-minutes 

Happy to help your team finally get the forecasting, revenue visibility, and automation foundation you’ve been missing. 
Best, 
Lucy

Platinum Partner | HubSpot Academy Instructor

job_title: Custom Card Development
job_description:
We are looking to develop a custom integration card that connects PandaDoc with a custom object in HubSpot. This integration should enable seamless synchronization between the two platforms, ensuring efficient document handling and status tracking.
Key requirements for the integration include:
  1. Property Synchronization
    The integration must automatically sync custom properties from the specified HubSpot custom object to a pre-defined PandaDoc template. This ensures that all relevant data is accurately reflected in the generated document without manual input.
  2. Status Updates Based on Approval Workflow
    The system should monitor the approval status of the PandaDoc document. Based on this status (e.g., sent, viewed, approved, declined), the integration must update the corresponding pipeline stage of the custom object in HubSpot to reflect the current progress.
  3. PDF Generation and Storage
    Once the document reaches its final state (e.g., approved or completed), a PDF version should be generated and automatically stored within the associated custom object record in HubSpot for reference and recordkeeping.
The end goal is a fully integrated and automated workflow between PandaDoc and HubSpot that minimizes manual data entry, maintains up-to-date pipeline statuses, and ensures easy access to finalized documents.
Desired Time Frame: Within 2 weeks

job_title: Hubspot ADM Audience/Segmentation Creation
job_description:
Project Context 
  • HubSpot is our primary marketing automation platform.
  • Salesforce is our system of record for Accounts and Contacts.
  • SAP ECC houses all Sales Order data, which we are syncing into HubSpot via SFTP-delivered CSV files.
  • Sales Orders are mapped in HubSpot as custom objects, with associations to Accounts and/or Contacts when available.

  
We want to build advanced, dynamic segmentation in HubSpot for ABM and lifecycle campaigns. Example use cases: 
  • Segment Contacts based on their Account’s purchase history (e.g., product category, order frequency).
  • Create lists of Contacts at dormant Accounts (no orders in X days).
  • Build audiences of high-value buyers based on SAP order totals over time.Trigger campaigns based on Account Tier or Order Recency.

Association Model 
  •  We support multiple B2B order channels for our customers — including EDI, web ordering, customer service, and sales rep-assisted transactions. All orders are placed against a Sold-To account, which is mandatory. However, contact-level data is only available in some cases (ecommerce, customer service). 
 
This creates a mix of association scenarios: 
  • Some Sales Orders can only be linked to an Account in HubSpot.
  • Others can be associated with both the Account and a specific Contact, when data permits.
 
  
As a result, segmentation must account for these differences—ensuring that audiences can be built based on both Account-level order history and, when available, Contact-level engagement.
proposal_description:
Summary 
You have three critical data pillars — HubSpot (marketing automation), Salesforce (CRM of record), and SAP ECC (order history) — now integrated with custom objects in HubSpot. The goal is to leverage this rich dataset to build advanced, dynamic ABM and lifecycle segmentation that accounts for the complex relationships between Accounts, Contacts, and Sales Orders. 
HUSHinbound will design and implement a segmentation framework that works at both the Account and Contact level, enabling highly targeted campaigns for acquisition, retention, and reactivation.  

Understanding of Needs 
From your context, we understand: 
  • You have multiple order channels, with varying levels of contact-level detail.
  • Sales Orders are custom objects in HubSpot, associated with Accounts and sometimes with Contacts.
  • Segmentation needs to bridge Account-level and Contact-level data, depending on available associations.
  • Campaign examples include:
    • Contacts from Accounts with purchases in certain product categories.
    • Dormant Accounts with no orders in X days.
    • High-value buyers based on SAP order totals.
    • Campaign triggers from Account Tier or Order Recency.

The challenge: HubSpot’s native segmentation tools are powerful, but building truly dynamic, multi-object audiences requires careful data modeling, property mapping, and workflow design.


Proposed Solution

Phase 1 — Data & Association Model Audit
 
  • Review the current custom object schema for Sales Orders in HubSpot.
  • Validate Account ↔ Contact ↔ Sales Order associations and data completeness.
  • Identify key SAP order properties (e.g., category, frequency, value, recency) and ensure they’re mapped for segmentation.
  • Document scenarios where orders are Account-only vs. Account + Contact.

Phase 2 — Segmentation Framework Design
 
  • Develop Account-based properties for order recency, frequency, and value (RFV scoring).
  • Create Contact-level derived properties when orders are linked to Contacts.
  • Implement roll-up logic so that Account metrics can drive segmentation even for Contacts without direct order links.
  • Define dynamic list and workflow templates for common use cases.

Phase 3 — Implementation & Testing
 
  • Configure HubSpot calculated properties, workflows, and dynamic lists for target use cases:
    • Dormant Accounts (X days since last order).
    • High-value Accounts (total order value above threshold).
    • Product-category buyers.
    • Contacts linked to orders vs. inferred from Account.
  • Build campaign-ready audience lists that auto-refresh based on data updates.
  • Test segmentation accuracy against SAP source data.

Phase 4 — Enablement & Scalability
 
  • Deliver documentation for the segmentation framework.
  • Train your marketing and sales teams on how to create and maintain advanced segments.
  • Build a repeatable model that supports future ABM and lifecycle initiatives.

 
Why HUSHinbound
 
  • Deep expertise in multi-object HubSpot data modeling and custom object-driven segmentation.
  • Proven track record integrating HubSpot with ERP and CRM systems for ABM programs.
  • Ability to design data roll-up strategies that work seamlessly across Account and Contact records.
  • 5 star rated HubSpot Platinum Partner (HubSpot Directory listing).

Book a call to align on segmentation strategy: Schedule here

Why Us?

  • Shape the Future: This is your chance to be part of what will be Sweden’s fastest growing startup, directly influencing and solving problems for millions of consumers and companies. 

  • Mission-Driven Impact: You’ll be at the forefront of our digital transformation, working with top-tier founders and industry leaders to create a 7-star customer experience.

  • HubSpot Savvy: You have hands-on experience with HubSpot CRM and/or Marketing Hub.Whether through previous internships, projects, or personal initiatives - and understand its potential to transform customer engagement.

fast-paced environment, tackling challenges head-on and turning ideas into actionable solutions.

  • Passionate & Driven: You’re ready to disrupt the status quo, eager to learn, and excited to be a key player in a company that’s set on making history in the electrical service industry.

Your Responsibilities

Build & Configure HubSpot CRM: Architect and implement our CRM system - including customer databases, pipelines, and automated workflows - that becomes the backbone of our customer experience.

Execute on Strategy: Connect the needs of our three core departments: Marketing, Operations, and Electricians - by building functions that boost both quality and efficiency.

  • Define HubSpot data model: standard + custom objects, associations, required fields.
    Process design: Lead → MQL/SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won/Lost, pipeline stages, entry/exit criteria.
    Security model: teams, roles, permissions, record ownership rules.

2) Data Migration (SugarCRM → HubSpot)
Field mapping doc (1:1 + transformations), deduplication rules, lookup resolution.
Import activities (emails/calls/notes/meetings where supported), files, and lists.
Validation run + reconciliation report (record counts, error log, exceptions).
Delta cutover plan with blackout window and rollback.

3) Sales Hub Enterprise Setup
Pipelines (primary + any sub-pipelines), stage properties, required reasons.
Sequences, templates, snippets, playbooks, call outcomes, meeting links.
Lead scoring (behavioral + firmographic), SLAs and task queues.
Custom quotes, products, deal workflows (create tasks, set owners, update fields).
Forecasting setup, goals, and weighted pipeline definitions.

4) Marketing Hub Pro Setup
Lifecycle model, MQL definition, subscription types, double opt-in where needed.
Forms, CTAs, lists (smart/static), nurture workflows, and lead routing.
Email + landing page brand modules, compliance (GDPR/CCPA).
UTM + source tracking, campaign hierarchy.

5) Integrations
Xero: deal→invoice sync, invoice status back to HubSpot, revenue fields for reporting.
PhoneBurner: activity logging, call outcomes, contact sync, list handoff.
Asana: deal/contract approval → Asana project creation with key fields synced.
Webhooks/middleware (if needed) with retry + error handling.

6) Reporting & Enablement
Executive dashboards: pipeline health, velocity, forecast vs. quota, win/loss, campaign attribution.
Rep dashboards: tasks due, sequence performance, meetings booked, CAC/LTV basics.
Training: live sessions + Loom library; admin runbook; change log.

It's a long term engagement and you will be working with Sales, marketing & CS teams.

Madelyn Donovan
Founder, Basis Inbound
HubSpot RevOps Consultant & Systems Architect

job_title: HubSpot Campaign Standardization, Segmentation, and Workflow Optimization
job_description:
Penn Foster is seeking an experienced HubSpot expert to help us bring order, scalability, and efficiency to our marketing operations. We currently manage multiple channels in HubSpot (Enterprise, Partner, and Inside Sales) with over 200,000 contacts—many of which are not yet properly segmented by channel. Our Salesforce integration is active, but some logic and data flow issues remain.

Our goal is to standardize campaign setup, segmentation, and workflows so that our internal teams can operate more efficiently and confidently within HubSpot.

We’re looking for a Certified Pro who can help us identify where to start, guide the cleanup and standardization process, and leave us with a system that’s consistent, scalable, and easy to maintain.

What We Need Help With
> Campaign & Workflow Standardization: Develop reusable templates for campaigns, workflows, and emails so teams can “pull off the shelf,” clone, and execute campaigns faster—with consistent naming conventions and folder structures.
> Segmentation Framework: Establish clear best practices for creating, maintaining, and cleaning up campaign segments across channels.
> Data Cleanup: Audit and organize existing records to ensure data integrity, reliable segmentation, and accurate reporting. Address issues such as incomplete or inconsistent data, duplicate records, and unclear channel attribution.
> Standard Campaign Frameworks: Build a few go-to campaign types (for example, nurture, referral, product launch, seasonal) with pre-built assets, workflows, and automation logic.
> HubSpot Workflow and Automation Optimization: Streamline lead assignment, channel attribution, and workflow logic. We currently have roughly 100 overlapping workflows and need to reduce conflicts and redundancy.
> Salesforce Integration Alignment: Ensure Salesforce ↔ HubSpot sync behavior supports the new campaign and workflow logic without creating duplicates or misaligned data.

Preferred Skills & Experience
  • > Deep experience in HubSpot Marketing Hub and Salesforce integration
  • > Proven track record in data hygiene, workflow optimization, and campaign automation
  • > Strong understanding of segmentation strategy, lead routing, and attribution modeling
  • > Ability to design repeatable campaign frameworks and templates
  • > Excellent documentation and communication skills to support internal enablement


What Success Looks Like
  • > Teams can quickly and confidently launch campaigns using standardized templates
  • > Segmentation is consistent and channel assignment is reliable
  • > Workflow automation is simplified and error-free
  • > Data is clean, complete, and accurate across HubSpot and Salesforce
  • > Clear documentation and best practices empower internal teams to self-serve

job_title: HubSpot Workflow Optimizations
job_description:
We’re working to streamline and fix our HubSpot workflows to ensure cleaner, more automated, and data-driven processes. Specifically, we’re focusing on improving: 

  • Campaign templates – building consistent, scalable templates to standardize marketing campaigns and ensure accurate attribution.
  • Enrichment – enhancing lead data quality to enable better segmentation and personalization.
  • Scorecards – refining lead scoring logic to better identify high-quality leads and align with sales priorities. As well as connecting with external tools for reporting on these scorecards. 
  • Data management – cleaning and structuring data to eliminate duplicates, improve reporting accuracy, and drive better insights across teams.

Our overall goal is to make HubSpot more efficient, automated, and insightful for both marketing and sales teams. We'd prefer hourly but open to see what a project based cost is as well.
proposal_description:
Hey there,

When HubSpot becomes more of a chore than a tool, that’s usually a sign of process drift. I know exactly what that feels like. Data clutter creeps in, attribution gets murky, scoring becomes a black box, and workflows start solving yesterday’s problems instead of today’s needs.

We’ve stepped into this situation many times before. In most cases, teams are using HubSpot but they aren’t able to trust the data well enough to guide decisions. That’s where we come in.

I’m Todd, Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric - an Elite HubSpot Partner focused on helping B2B teams streamline demand generation, lead management, and RevOps inside HubSpot so both marketing and sales can operate with confidence.

How we approach this 

Phase 0 - Audit & Blueprint
• Review existing workflows, data model, scoring, enrichment sources, campaign structure
• Diagnose breakage, conflicts, and workflow chaos
• Build a prioritized roadmap with ROI-backed recommendations

Phase 1 - Fixes & Enhancements
 • Standardize campaign templates for clean attribution
 • Implement a lead enrichment and de-duplication strategy
 • Redesign scoring to align marketing efforts with sales priorities
 • Identify integration gaps and improve data syncs with external tools

Phase 2 - Optimization & Enablement
• Governance: workflow naming, foldering, documentation
• Reporting: dashboards for funnel health & team accountability
• Training: short Looms or live enablement to boost adoption

A few questions to ensure we start on the right path:
1. Which HubSpot hubs + tiers are you currently using (especially Ops Hub & Sales Pro/Enterprise)?
2. Do you already have external enrichment tools (Clearbit, ZoomInfo, Cognism) in play?
3. How is lead source and campaign attribution being reported today and who is relying on that data?
4. Do you have a shared lifecycle definition between marketing and sales?

Here are a few case studies showcasing how we have helped business to leverage Hubspot for their businesses -
1) Reengineering Sales in HubSpot for Qashio
2) Boosting Pipeline 83% with Lead Management

Happy to schedule a short exploratory call with your team to walk through possible approaches and module strategies. Let’s get on a call! 

Cheers,
Todd

Supporting multiple client accounts across diverse industries with a proactive, strategic approach can be demanding. It requires not just technical expertise, but a layer of ownership, clear communication, and ongoing optimization to ensure client success and retention. Agencies struggle with balancing tactical delivery and strategic growth, often facing constraints in bandwidth and specialized resources. And that where hands on HubSpot experience and a mindset rooted in ownership, curiosity, and strategic thinking comes in handy!

OneMetric currently works with over 30 HubSpot partners as their White Labelled Execution Arm- helping them turn their best client work into scalable, productized systems. 
As an Elite HubSpot Partner ourselves, we’ve seen both sides of the table - partner and delivery. 
At our core, we believe great partnerships are built on ownership. We don’t wait for directions, rather we take initiative, communicate clearly, and execute with accountability. 

Our Approach to Ownership
We work as your HubSpot and RevOps delivery team. 
You lead your client onboarding, we take care of communication, solutions, and delivery. This model ensures you remain the face of client relationships while we power your delivery with precision, scale, and strategic oversight.

Our people don’t just react, they lead conversations, identify gaps, and deliver proactive recommendations that strengthen your reputation for excellence. We fit right into your systems, processes, and tools, seamlessly operating under your brand.

job_title: HubSpot Implementation & Revenue Integration Specialist

  • Establish a single source of truth for sales and revenue data.

  • Audit of current HubSpot setup and recommendations.
  • Implementation of improved forecasting and revenue tracking processes.
  • Integration between HubSpot, QuickBooks, and revenue tracking tools.
  • Documentation and training for internal team.

job_title: Resolve HubSpot ↔ Salesforce Duplicate Company & Contact Data Issues
job_description:
Our current HubSpot–Salesforce integration does not support merging duplicate companies, leaving us with:
  • ~9,000 duplicate companies
  • ~10,000 duplicate contacts that would need to be merged manually, one by one
This volume makes manual cleanup unrealistic and risks ongoing data pollution.

Root Cause
HubSpot’s native integration does not sync merged company records to Salesforce, which prevents us from performing merges on the HubSpot side. The duplicates only live in HubSpot, as we have our own de-duplication system in Salesforce that manages all duplicates. There are no duplicates in Salesforce. 

Previous Attempts
We previously hired two outside agencies to build custom syncing/merge logic.
Both attempts resulted in significant issues, including:
  • Data fields being stripped from Salesforce
  • Incorrect industry assignments being pushed back into HubSpot
  • Unpredictable sync behavior between the systems

Due to these problems, custom-coded solutions have proven risky.

Constraints / Requirements
  1. No loss of Salesforce data during any merge or sync process.
  2. Industry and other key company attributes must remain accurate after merging.
  3. Solution must work within or around HubSpot/Salesforce limitations without breaking the sync.

If a custom solution is required, it must include rigorous safeguards and/or staged testing to prevent previous issues from recurring.

Ideally, the solution should:
  1. Allow bulk or automated duplicate company merging
  2. Allow bulk contact deduplication
  3. Maintain sync integrity between platforms

Desired Outcome
A clear, safe, and maintainable approach to:
  • Eliminating thousands of duplicate companies and contacts
  • Ensuring clean, accurate master records
  • Maintaining a reliable and predictable HubSpot ↔ Salesforce sync going forward
proposal_description:
Hey there,

I know exactly what this situation feels like. When you open HubSpot and see thousands of duplicate companies that you can’t safely touch because of Salesforce, HubSpot stops feeling like a system of record and starts feeling like a liability. Reporting becomes questionable, adoption drops, and every “quick” change risks making the mess bigger. The fact that two previous custom attempts caused data stripping and unpredictable sync behavior tells me this isn’t a tooling issue it’s a governance and strategy issue.

We step into this exact scenario a lot.

I’m Todd, Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric. We’re a HubSpot Elite Partner, and a significant portion of our work is stabilizing HubSpot–Salesforce environments after partial integrations, risky middleware, or well-intentioned custom logic that created more problems than it solved. When HubSpot becomes harder to trust than Salesforce, that’s usually a sign the sync needs to be rethought not patched.

A recent example:
We bring Salesforce and HubSpot together so everything just works. Your data, messages, and workflows stay in sync. And you get clarity and consistency without complexity.
-https://www.onemetric.io/integration/salesforce-to-hubspot

Here’s how I’d approach your situation.

Phase 0 - Discovery & Blueprint
Before touching merges or automation, we’d:
  • Audit the current HubSpot–Salesforce field mappings, sync rules, and object-level ownership
  • Identify which system is the true source of truth per field.
  • Analyze exactly how and where HubSpot-only duplicates are being created
  • Validate constraints around Salesforce’s dedupe process so we don’t fight it

Phase 1- Controlled Deduplication Strategy  
  • Isolate HubSpot-only duplicate sets using deterministic rules (domain + SFDC ID presence + lifecycle state)
  • Perform merges in staged batches with pre-merge field validation and rollback checkpoints
  • Lock critical fields (industry, segmentation, lifecycle) from being overwritten during merges
  • Use sandbox or mirrored test environments wherever possible before production runs

Phase 2 - Prevention & Sync Hardening
Once cleanup is stable, we:
  • Put guardrails in place so HubSpot can’t reintroduce the same duplicates
  • Adjust company creation rules, enrichment tools, and form behavior
  • Tighten sync triggers to reinforce Salesforce’s dedupe authority
  • Document “merge-safe” operating procedures so this doesn’t regress in six months

A few questions to make sure we architect this right:
  1. Which fields are currently mapped as bidirectional vs Salesforce → HubSpot only?
  2. Are HubSpot-only companies missing Salesforce IDs entirely, or were some de-synced historically?
  3. What tools are currently contributing to company creation (forms, enrichment, imports)?
  4. Do you have a Salesforce sandbox we can mirror for staged testing?
  5. Is leadership optimization more urgency-driven (clean now) or risk-driven (slow but bulletproof)?

Our goal isn’t just to eliminate duplicates it’s to give you a HubSpot–Salesforce relationship your team can finally trust.

Happy to walk through a similar before/after or start with a short audit conversation to pressure-test the safest path forward. Let's Talk

Cheers,
Todd

Hi! I’m Megan, HubSpot Certified Trainer with 10+ years of experience helping teams simplify their tech and get real value out of HubSpot. I’d love to learn more about what isn’t currently working with your custom email template.

I wanted to also let you know here that you generally cannot reliably use custom fonts in emails because most email clients (like Gmail, Outlook, and many mobile apps) do not support them. If you try to use a custom font, it will be ignored and replaced with a default font unless the font is rendered as part of an image (for example, a header graphic).

There are a few limited exceptions (like Apple Mail supporting some web fonts), but because support is inconsistent, best practice is to avoid custom fonts in email HTML.

job_description:

This role is execution-focused and operational. The goal is to restore data reliability, consistency, and usability so HubSpot can function as a trusted system of record.

The specialist will:
  • Improve overall data cleanliness and consistency in HubSpot
  • Reduce duplicate, incomplete, and unreliable records
  • Normalize properties so reporting and workflows function correctly
  • Support long-term data health through basic guardrails

     Scope of Work
    1. Data Cleanup & Normalization
    2. Property & Field Hygiene
    3. Lifecycle & Deal Stage Hygiene
    4. Marketing Data Cleanup (Operational)
    5. Reporting Support (Post-Cleanup)

    Deliverables
    • Cleaned and deduplicated CRM records
    • Standardized property values across key objects
    • Short summary of:
      • What was cleaned
      • What could not be fixed without process changes
      • Recommendations to prevent regression
proposal_description:
Hi there,

I know exactly what this situation feels like. HubSpot was meant to be a trusted system of record, but instead it’s become weighed down by data clutter, inconsistent properties, unreliable reporting, and low confidence across teams. When HubSpot starts feeling like a chore rather than a tool, that’s almost always a sign of process drift usually after a partial implementation, a migration, or well-intentioned changes layered on top of each other without governance. We’ve stepped into this exact scenario many times before.

I’m Todd, a Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric. We’re a HubSpot Elite Partner, and a big part of our work is coming in after audits to steady the system cleaning up what’s fixable, calling out what isn’t yet, and putting simple guardrails in place so teams don’t end up back in the same spot six months later. This role reads as execution-focused and operational, which is exactly where strong HubSpot data hygiene work needs to live.

How I’d approach your cleanup

Phase 0 - Discovery & Blueprint
Before touching data, I’d review the audit findings to understand where inconsistencies are coming from properties, imports, integrations, or human behavior. The output here is a prioritized cleanup plan that balances impact with risk (especially around active workflows and reporting.

Phase 1 - Data Cleanup & Normalization
  •  Deduplication across contacts, companies, and deals using clear merge rules
  • Normalizing key properties (lifecycle stage, lead status, deal stages, owner logic, core picklists)
  • Removing or archiving unused, conflicting, or misleading properties
  • Correcting incomplete or invalid records where rules can safely be applied

Phase 2 - Guardrails & Stability
Once the data is clean, I’d focus on preventing regression:
  • Property permissions and required fields where appropriate
  • Light workflow logic to enforce standards (without over-automation)
  • Clear definitions for lifecycle stages and pipeline usage

Phase 3 - Reporting Support & Handoff
Post-cleanup, I’d help validate that reports and dashboards are now trustworthy, flag where reporting still breaks due to process gaps, and provide a short written summary covering:
  • What was cleaned
  • What couldn’t be fixed without process changes
  • Practical recommendations to maintain data health
If helpful, I also document key decisions or record Loom walkthroughs so the team isn’t guessing later.

A few questions to align us quickly
  1. Which objects are currently causing the most pain contacts, companies, deals, or all three?
  2. Are lifecycle stages and lead statuses actively used by both sales and marketing today?
  3. Do you have any critical integrations (billing, product, enrichment tools) feeding data into HubSpot?
  4. Are there reports leadership has stopped trusting due to inconsistency?

Here are a few case studies showcasing how we have helped SaaS business, and more to leverage Hubspot for their businesses -
1) Reengineering Sales in HubSpot for Qashio
2) Boosting Pipeline 83% with Lead Management
3) Building a Partner Portal in HubSpot for Giift
4) Increasing Conversions by 19.5% with HubSpot Integration

Our goal isn’t just to fix HubSpot  it’s to make your team self-reliant on it again.

 I’m happy to start with a short audit discussion or walk through similar before-and-after examples to make sure this engagement stays focused. Let's talk!

Best,
Todd

job_title: Stripe → HubSpot Membership Data Architecture (Minimum Viable Setup)
job_description:
We’re a media business using HubSpot as our audience system of record. Our immediate goal is to establish a clean, minimum-viable membership data flow from Stripe → HubSpot so we can reliably distinguish paying members from non-members.
This is a focused Phase 1 project, not a full CRM rebuild.

Context
  • Newsletter-led business with multiple revenue streams (sponsors, paid membership, events)
  • Paid membership is managed in Stripe
  • HubSpot is our primary CRM


What we need help with (Phase 1 scope):
Stripe → HubSpot: Minimum Viable Membership Setup

We want to start with just three membership fields in HubSpot, sourced from Stripe:
is_member (true / false)
  1. member_status (active / canceled / trial)
  2. member_tier (optional — e.g. Pro vs other)
  3. 1. Stripe should remain the source of truth for payment.
  4. 2. HubSpot should reliably reflect membership state on the contact record.

What this should unlock immediately:

  • Avoid inviting existing members to “join” again
  • Target non-members for membership webinars or promotions
  • Message paid vs free audiences differently (email, events, lifecycle)

What is explicitly out of scope (for now):
  • Full subscription object modeling
  • Over-syncing data “just in case”
  • Sales funnel or lifecycle stage redesign

Event and webinar data may be a Phase 2 follow-on once membership signals are stable.

What we’re looking for:

  • A technical HubSpot operator (Rev Ops / Marketing Ops)
  • Experience with Stripe → HubSpot integrations
  • Someone is opinionated about what should vs should not live in HubSpot
  • Comfortable designing clean, minimal data models, not bloated ones
This is a project-based engagement, with potential follow-on work if Phase 1 goes well.
proposal_description:
Hey there,

I know exactly what this situation feels like. When Stripe is doing its job cleanly, but HubSpot starts to feel noisy or unreliable, the problem usually isn’t a lack of data it’s too much of the wrong data in the wrong places. We see this a lot with newsletter-led and media businesses  payment truth lives in Stripe, HubSpot becomes the audience system of record, and suddenly teams lose confidence because “member” means something slightly different depending on where you look. That’s where things like accidental re-invites, fuzzy segmentation, and hesitant campaign targeting creep in.

I’m Todd, a Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric. We’re a HubSpot Elite Partner, and a big part of our work is stepping in to steady systems after partial integrations, over-modeled data, or well-intentioned setups that outgrew their original purpose. We’re opinionated about what should live in HubSpot, what should stay elsewhere, and how to keep the system lightweight enough that teams actually trust and use it.

A few relevant example:
We helped a fintech company clean up a fragmented HubSpot setup by simplifying their data model and restoring HubSpot as a trusted system of record enabling better lifecycle visibility and monetization decisions. This mirrors the kind of minimal, opinionated data architecture you’re aiming for with Stripe → HubSpot membership signals.
 https://www.onemetric.io/resources/case-study/enabling-a-fintech-to-upsell-cross-sell-better

How I’d approach your Phase 1 work

Phase 0 - Discovery & Blueprint
We’d start by auditing the current Stripe setup, existing HubSpot properties, and any integration layer in between (native, middleware, or custom). The output here is a simple blueprint: exact definitions for is_member, member_status, and member_tier, plus clear rules for how and when they change. This is where we decide what not to sync as much as what to sync.

Phase 1 - Minimum Viable Membership Implementation
Implement a clean Stripe → HubSpot flow where Stripe remains the source of truth and HubSpot reflects state, not payment mechanics. This includes:
  • Mapping subscription events to your three core fields only
  • Guardrails to prevent historical or canceled data from polluting current status
  • Lightweight automation to keep contact records consistent and reliable

Phase 2 - Validation, Enablement & Handoff
We’ll pressure-test edge cases (trials, cancellations, tier changes), document the logic, and enable your team with short Looms or a live walkthrough. No black boxes. You should feel confident maintaining this without us.

A few questions I’d love to explore with you:
  1. Are you using Stripe subscriptions, payment links, or a mix for memberships today?
  2. How do you define “active” vs “canceled” immediately on cancel, or at period end?
  3. Do members ever hold multiple subscriptions or tiers simultaneously?
  4. Are there existing HubSpot properties or lists tied to “member” logic that need to be preserved or deprecated?


Our goal isn’t just to fix a Stripe sync it’s to give you a stable membership signal you can build on without fear of data creep.

Happy to start with a short audit conversation or walk you through similar before-and-after setups we’ve done. Let's talk!

Best,
Todd

job_title: I need to create Custom reports for our Sales Pipeline
job_description: We need a report that will track our prospects through various stages such as intro, reviewing docs, Signed Agreements, On File, Live. We need to be able to categorize each contact with one of these categories, then be able to pull a report that shows each prospect in those stages and when last contact was with them.
proposal_description:
Hey there,

I like what you’re aiming for, because that’s exactly where most HubSpot setups fall short. You’ve already got your sales stages mapped (Intro → Reviewing Docs → Signed → Live), but what’s missing is clarity. Seeing where every deal stands, how long it’s been there, and when someone last reached out.

The problem usually isn’t HubSpot itself, it’s how data flows between contacts, deals, and activities. Once those relationships are properly defined, HubSpot becomes incredibly powerful for reporting.

Here’s how I’d fix this for you:

  1. Deep-Dive Audit
    Review how your deals, contact associations, and activity fields are structured. I’ll pinpoint exactly why your current reporting can’t track stage movement or last contact correctly.

  2. Data Model Alignment
    Standardize how deal stages map to contacts, and ensure “last activity date” is pulling from the right source — not just emails but meetings and calls too.

  3. Custom Dashboards & Reporting
    Build reports that show deal distribution, time-in-stage, and engagement recency. You’ll know, at a glance, which deals are moving, which are stuck, and where follow-ups are slipping.

This kind of reporting isn’t just about charts, it’s about confidence in your pipeline data. We’ve done similar work for SaaS and service firms where these dashboards became their single source of truth for forecasting and accountability. I have attached few reports we built in HubSpot!

A few quick questions before we jump on a call:

  • Are those stages tied to deal pipelines or stored as contact-level fields right now?
  • How are you currently tracking “last contact”, manually or via HubSpot’s default activity logic?
  • Would you want this accessible to managers only, or across your sales team?

Happy to walk you through how we can get you full pipeline visibility and a reporting setup that actually tells the truth about your deals. Let's Talk!

Cheers,

Todd,
HubSpot Consultant

Supported by OneMetric – a HubSpot Elite Partner agency helping businesses design cleaner data models, smarter automation, and powerful reporting inside HubSpot.

proposal_description:
Proposal for HubSpot Email Deliverability Optimization & Contact List Segmentation

Prepared by: Walter Cervantes | Reforge Solutions

Objective

To optimize the delivery and performance of your monthly fact sheet email campaigns by:
• Organizing and segmenting your 6,000 contacts into strategic, high-performing lists in HubSpot.
• Implementing technical enhancements to reduce deliverability risks, including a full DNS setup (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) aligned with HubSpot’s email infrastructure.
• Ensuring your hedge fund’s email communications are compliant, highly deliverable, and positioned for successful ongoing engagement.

Scope of Work
1. Contact Audit & List Segmentation
• Audit of all 6,000 contacts for data hygiene (duplicate removal, invalid emails, opt-out status, etc.).
• Creation of tailored contact lists based on engagement levels, geography, or other custom criteria aligned with your needs.
• Recommendations for ongoing list management and segmentation best practices.
2. Deliverability Optimization & DNS Configuration
• Full technical setup for HubSpot email deliverability:
• SPF (Sender Policy Framework)
• DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail)
• DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting & Conformance)
• Collaboration with your domain registrar/hosting provider to implement and verify records.
• HubSpot backend configuration for large-scale email dispatch.
3. Testing & Quality Assurance
• Test campaigns to validate improved inbox placement and deliverability.
• Provide a deliverability report and future recommendations.

Timeline
• Kickoff & Discovery: 1-2 business days
• Contact audit & list creation: 3-5 business days
• DNS setup & verification: 2-3 business days (depending on registrar cooperation)
• Testing & final delivery: 1-2 business days
Total Estimated Time: ~8-10 business days from project start.

Investment

Total Project Fee:
✅ $2,800 USD (fixed fee)

Includes full audit, segmentation, DNS setup, and testing. Expedited delivery available upon request.

Why Reforge Solutions?
• Deep HubSpot Expertise: Proven track record working with financial services and hedge fund clients.
• Deliverability Focused: We go beyond the basics to ensure your emails land where they should — your clients’ inboxes.
• Agile & Fast: We understand the urgency and can prioritize this project for a quick turnaround.

Next Steps
1. Approval of proposal and project fee.
2. Access to HubSpot and domain registrar.
3. Kickoff within 24-48 hours of approval.

job_title: Restore Email, HubSpot Optimization & Team Enablement
job_description:
HubSpot Re-Implementation & Enablement (CREDE)

Goal: Fix email deliverability and re-start marketing campaigns; simplify and standardize our HubSpot CRM for adoption (including an acquired team); and enable reliable forecasting and attribution—then train our team to sustain it.
Background & Pain Points
  • Marketing emails paused after poor sender reputation (“HubSpot jail”): high bounces, list quality issues.
  • CRM is bloated (deal cards/fields, processes), causing low adoption—especially on the newly acquired team.
  • Inconsistent data capture from conferences/meetings; weak source attribution; limited visibility for forecasting.
  • Former in-house HubSpot owner left; we need an embedded expert/agency to steady the system and upskill us.
What We’re Looking For (Scope)
  1. Audit & Action Plan (phase 0): Quick technical/operational audit with a prioritized roadmap and effort estimate.
  2. Email Deliverability Recovery: Repair domain reputation (e.g., alias domains + warming tool), DNS auth (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), list health, segmentation, and sending best practices—so monthly nurture resumes.
  3. CRM Simplification & Governance: Streamline pipelines and deal cards (reduce required fields), standardize contact/account fields, list-upload rules, and create admin guardrails.
  4. Adoption & Training: Role-based training (BD, marketing, “doer-seller” teams), playbooks, and short Looms; show-and-do enablement.
  5. Attribution & Activity Capture: Improve meeting/note capture (Fathom already used; open to lightweight voice/capture tools), conference lead workflows, and reliable source attribution.
  6. Reporting & Forecasting: Clean dashboards for pipeline health and stage-based probability forecasting; executive roll-ups.
  7. Documentation & Handoff: Admin guide, governance doc, field dictionary, and 30/60-day operating checklist.
Deliverables
  • Audit + remediation plan; DNS/auth + warming implemented; cleaned/segmented send lists.
  • Simplified pipelines/deal cards and governed list-upload process.
  • Adoption toolkit: playbooks, role trainings, and recorded how-tos.
  • Standard attribution model + dashboards for pipeline/forecast accuracy.
  • Admin documentation and ongoing recommendations.
Success Looks Like
  • Marketing domain warmed and campaigns relaunching with healthy metrics.
  • BD and acquired team actively using HubSpot (measurable weekly activity uplift).
  • Fewer fields, faster data entry, cleaner data; clear visibility into pipeline and forecast.
  • Team can run the playbook without external help.
Engagement Model
  • Start ASAP with a paid audit, then execute in phases (email → CRM simplification → adoption/reporting).
  • Open to a vetted solo consultant or small agency; we value hands-on execution + teach-us-to-fish enablement.
  • Indicative budget: $5k–$15k depending on depth; begin with the audit and expand based on outcomes.
Key Tools/Context
  • HubSpot (Marketing & Sales), Fathom for meeting notes; considering simple voice/capture for in-person meetings.
  • Must keep all comms/activities tracked in HubSpot.
