The clients has HubSpot and Shopify (integrated so the orders are coming into the order object) and they want these reports (I told them we won't be able to do them all but we'd see how close we can get):

Sales & Revenue KPIs
  1. Total Revenue – Overall income from sales.
  2. Average Order Value (AOV) – Revenue per transaction.
  3. Conversion Rate – Percentage of website visitors who make a purchase.
  4. Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) – Estimated revenue from a single customer.
  5. Cart Abandonment Rate – Percentage of users leaving before checkout.

Marketing & Traffic KPIs
  1. Website Traffic – Number of visitors over time.
  2. Traffic Sources – Breakdown of organic, paid, direct, and referral traffic.
  3. Lead Conversion Rate – Percentage of leads turning into paying customers.
  4. Cost Per Acquisition (CPA) – Cost of acquiring a new customer.
  5. Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) – Effectiveness of paid campaigns.

Customer Engagement & Experience KPIs
  1. Customer Retention Rate – Percentage of repeat customers.
  2. Net Promoter Score (NPS) – Customer satisfaction and likelihood to recommend.
  3. Customer Support Response Time – Average time to respond to queries.
  4. Customer Reviews & Ratings – Volume and sentiment of feedback.
  5. Sample Request to Purchase Ratio – Percentage of quotes turning into sales.

Operational KPIs
  1. Order Fulfilment Time – Average time to process and deliver orders.
  2. Return Rate – Percentage of products returned.
  3. Stock Turnover Rate – How often inventory is sold and replaced.
  4. Live Chat Engagement Rate – Percentage of visitors using live chat.
  5. Customer Support Ticket Resolution Time – Efficiency in handling issues.
Desired Time Frame: 1-2 weeks
proposal_description:
Jean Christofferson Certified Profoundly Pro

  • Documentation as I go in a Google doc with call outs for any questions or issues

I look forward to our discovery call on Tuesday.

Jean Christofferson
jean@relevantspeed.com


Sales & Revenue KPIs
  1. Total Revenue – Overall income from sales.
  2. Average Order Value (AOV) – Revenue per transaction.
  3. Conversion Rate – Percentage of website visitors who make a purchase.
  4. Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) – Estimated revenue from a single customer.
  5. Cart Abandonment Rate – Percentage of users leaving before checkout.


With the Shopify integration that I worked with we had an issue regarding refunds.  Do you know if that is an issue that I need to look into and resolve?


For the conversion rate calculation of website visitors that make a purchase do they want to know ALL website visitors or just the ones for which there is a Shopify profile?

Marketing & Traffic KPIs
  1. Website Traffic – Number of visitors over time.
  2. Traffic Sources – Breakdown of organic, paid, direct, and referral traffic.
  3. Lead Conversion Rate – Percentage of leads turning into paying customers.
  4. Cost Per Acquisition (CPA) – Cost of acquiring a new customer.
  5. Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) – Effectiveness of paid campaigns.

For Lead Conversion Rate - is the lead status accurate?  Are leads considered contacts with a Shopify profile or any new contact in HubSpot.  Is there an issue with spam contacts

I am assuming advertising accounts are integrated.  Is it Google or does it include Meta as well?

One issue that I dealt with for the last client was that advertising campaigns, especially branded ones, were generating sales but the customer was a returning customer, so a new contact was not created.  Do we need to account for this?  Is the CPA that they want really what is the cost of acquiring a purchase?

Customer Engagement & Experience KPIs
  1. Customer Retention Rate – Percentage of repeat customers.
  2. Net Promoter Score (NPS) – Customer satisfaction and likelihood to recommend.
  3. Customer Support Response Time – Average time to respond to queries.
  4. Customer Reviews & Ratings – Volume and sentiment of feedback.
  5. Sample Request to Purchase Ratio – Percentage of quotes turning into sales.


Are they currently running NPS and CSAT surveys in HubSpot?

For support what type of support channels are they using?  Chat, email or other?  Do they have SLA’s setup for support?

How are samples requested?  Are the orders? Are they easy to differentiate from real orders? 



Operational KPIs
  1. Order Fulfilment Time – Average time to process and deliver orders.
  2. Return Rate – Percentage of products returned.
  3. Stock Turnover Rate – How often inventory is sold and replaced.
  4. Live Chat Engagement Rate – Percentage of visitors using live chat.
  5. Customer Support Ticket Resolution Time – Efficiency in handling issues.


Is this the last priority for reporting?  

Order fulfillment time and return rates should be available but I need to know more to determine how to handle stock turnover rate.  

Live chat engagement requires pulling data from both Shopify web traffic data and the chat performance.

Customer support ticket resolution time can be extracted from service, especially if they have SLAs setup.


Billing
My usual rate is $150 per hour.
After our discovery call I will be able to commit to a fixed price bid. Right now I think it will be in a maximum of 17 hours, $2,550.  It may be less after I see what is already there.

job_title: Configuring Hubspot Pipelines for accurate tracking
job_description:
Ultimately what we want to do is accurately track sales made by our sales team. This is impossible for multiple reasons, but for right now, we would like to have one pipeline close deals and copy over deal values to another pipeline so my sales team can track their sales.

We have 2 deal pipelines. 1 (HS - Hubspot Sales) is our sales team who customers interact with. 2. (BC Order Pipeline) which imports our sales from BigCommerce where all of our sales live. Not all of our HS Pipeline deals convert and not all our BC sales are from customers with existing HS sales. so its not a perfect 1-1. 

What we need is some sort of workflow that checks if someone has an existing HS deal open when their BC order is created. then it would close out their HS deal and copy of the data from the BC deal so that my sales team knows the deal they were working on with a customer has closed. 

This will allow for better tracking and performance of our sales team.
proposal_description:
Hey there,

I completely understand the challenge you’re facing. When two pipelines one for HubSpot Sales and one for BigCommerce orders aren’t aligned, it’s hard to know which deals your reps actually closed. Reporting gets messy, the team can’t trust the data, and HubSpot starts feeling more like a chore than a helpful tool. I know exactly what that feels like, and we’ve stepped into this situation many times at OneMetric.

We’re a HubSpot Elite Partner with extensive experience helping B2B companies regain control over pipelines and integrations. A strong example from our work is our Tracera case study: Building a Scalable Data Architecture in HubSpot
We audited fragmented pipelines across multiple teams, standardized processes, and implemented automation to ensure deal attribution and accurate reporting, giving leadership confidence in their numbers.

Here’s how I’d approach your project:

Phase 0 - Discovery & Blueprint
  • Audit both HS and BC pipelines and the current integration method.
  • Map the key fields to be synced (amount, products, order ID, close date).
  • Identify how to reliably match BC orders to existing HS deals.

Phase 1 - Workflow & Automation
  • Build a workflow triggered by BC order creation.
  • Check for an open HS deal for the same contact/company.
  • If found: close the HS deal, copy over key values from BC, and mark it for the rep.
  • If not found: let the BC deal stand alone.
  • Add safeguards to prevent accidental overwrites or duplicates.

Phase 2 - Reporting & Enablement
  • Create dashboards showing properly attributed revenue and sales performance.
  • Document the workflow and new process so reps understand the system.
  • Provide short Loom videos or a quick live session for team enablement.

To ensure the solution meets your exact needs, I’d like to clarify a few points:
  1. How is BigCommerce currently connected to HubSpot native app, custom API, or middleware?
  2. Do BC orders include Contact or Company IDs for matching?
  3. Does your team typically maintain one active deal per customer or multiple?
4. What reports/dashboards are most important for your leadership team?

Here are a few case studies showcasing how we have helped business to leverage Hubspot for their businesses -
1) Reengineering Sales in HubSpot for Qashio
2) Integrating Hint Health with HubSpot
3) Unifying sales, marketing & onboarding for an AI platform with HubSpot
4) Boosting Pipeline 83% with Lead Management
5) Building a Partner Portal in HubSpot for Giift

Our goal isn’t just to fix HubSpot it’s to make your team confident in the data and self-reliant. We can start with a 15-minute  call to walk through exactly how we’d set this up based on what we’ve done for other clients.

Cheers,
Todd

job_title: HubSpot Dashboard Buildout
job_description:

    - Ability to translate business requirements into clean, actionable dashboards
    - A short onboarding call to align on definitions and naming conventions
    - Delivery of dashboards that leadership can use for weekly forecasting

    Our goal:
    To consolidate all GTM performance into a single set of dashboards that allow us to see quota pacing, funnel health, channel performance, and efficiency at a glance.
proposal_description:
This is one of my favorite types of projects to work on: building HubSpot dashboards that actually function well and look good doing it. 

My team and I specialize in this kind of work: translating business logic into clean HubSpot reporting infrastructure that gives teams clarity and confidence in their data. We've built similar dashboard systems for B2B companies tracking multi-channel attribution, quota pacing, and funnel efficiency, and we focus on getting the definitions right upfront so the dashboards stay accurate without constant manual intervention.

Two Options for Dashboard Buildout
We're proposing two approaches depending on your timeline, budget, and level of customization needed:

Option 1: Core Dashboard Build — $2,500

This option focuses on building the three dashboards using your existing HubSpot structure with minimal custom property work. Best for teams with clean data and straightforward attribution logic already in place.

What's included:
  • Brief onboarding call (30–45 min) to align on definitions, naming conventions, and attribution logic
  • Build of all three dashboards using existing HubSpot properties and standard reporting tools: 
    • 2026 Channel Quota Pacing Dashboard
    • Funnel Conversion & Efficiency Dashboard (CPQM)
    • Channel Owner Scorecards
  • One round of revisions based on your feedback
  • Light walkthrough (30 min) to review dashboards and answer questions
Timeline: 2–3 weeks from kickoff
Best for: Teams with existing HubSpot properties for channel tracking, deal attribution, and owner assignment already configured; straightforward CPQM calculations; minimal need for custom fields.

Option 2: Full Dashboard Build with Custom Infrastructure — $4,500–$6,000

This option includes everything in Option 1, plus deeper discovery, custom property development, calculated fields, and more robust documentation and training. Best for teams who need custom attribution logic, new data structure, or more complex reporting needs.

What's included:
  • Phase 1: Discovery & Data Mapping (Week 1)
    Focused onboarding call to align on business definitions, attribution logic, and naming conventions. We'll audit your current HubSpot setup to identify any gaps in properties, deal stages, or data structure that could impact dashboard accuracy. If custom properties or calculated fields are needed (e.g., for CPQM or pacing calculations), we'll map those out during this phase.
  • Phase 2: Dashboard Build (Weeks 2–3)
    • We'll build the three dashboards in HubSpot, using custom reports, datasets, and calculated properties as needed: 
      • 2026 Channel Quota Pacing Dashboard: Quota attainment vs. plan by channel, with monthly and quarterly views, trend lines, and contribution breakdowns.
      • Funnel Conversion & Efficiency Dashboard (CPQM): Full-funnel conversion tracking from meeting booked through closed-won, CPQM by channel, efficiency metrics, and historical trending to identify bottlenecks.
      • Channel Owner Scorecards: Individual performance views showing pipeline sourced, meetings booked, opportunities created, and revenue attributed to each channel owner, with red/green pacing indicators.
    • Each dashboard will be built with automation in mind—no manual updates required once the structure is in place.
  • Phase 3: Review & Handoff (Week 4)
    We'll review the dashboards together to confirm they match your forecasting workflow and leadership expectations. My team will walk your team through how to read each dashboard, where the data is coming from, and how to troubleshoot if something looks off. We'll also provide documentation covering the logic behind each report, custom properties used, and any filters or date ranges that should be adjusted seasonally.
Timeline: 4 weeks from kickoff
Best for: Teams that need custom properties built, calculated fields for CPQM or attribution, more complex multi-channel logic, or comprehensive documentation and training for long-term dashboard maintenance.

How We Work

My team and I work transparently with shared Google Docs for planning and HubSpot for real-time progress visibility. You'll have access to dashboards as we build them, so you can provide feedback before final delivery. Depending on the project phase, you may work directly with me or with other members of my team—but I'll be involved throughout to ensure everything aligns with your goals.

Why Basis / Why Our Team

We've been building HubSpot reporting systems for B2B companies for several years, with a focus on RevOps, attribution, and executive-level visibility. Our background combines technical HubSpot expertise with an understanding of how GTM teams actually use data—so the dashboards we build aren't just accurate, they're designed around your team's decision-making rhythm.
We're particularly experienced in multi-channel attribution, funnel efficiency tracking, and building reporting structures that stay accurate as your business scales. And honestly? We love making dashboards that are both functional and visually clean—leadership dashboards need to be immediately readable, no digging required.

Next Steps

If either of these options feels aligned, I'd suggest we start with a brief discovery call to review your current HubSpot structure and confirm which approach makes the most sense. From there, we can lock in scope, timeline, and pricing.

Looking forward to helping you build the visibility layer your 2026 GTM plan deserves!

job_title: all things hubspot

proposal_description:
Hey there,

It’s very common to reach a point where HubSpot exists in the business, but no one feels confident using it end-to-end. What usually starts as a well-intentioned setup slowly turns into data clutter, low adoption, inconsistent reporting, and integrations that don’t quite line up with how the team actually works.

I know exactly what that feels like. We’ve stepped into this situation many times before often when HubSpot has become more of a chore than a tool. And in almost every case, the issue isn’t HubSpot itself; it’s process drift. As the business evolves, the system doesn’t, and suddenly teams hesitate to touch anything because they’re unsure what’s connected to what or whether reports can be trusted.

I’m Todd, a Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric. We’re a HubSpot Elite Partner, and a large part of our work is helping B2B teams steady and simplify their HubSpot setup after partial implementations, rushed rollouts, or DIY configurations that have outgrown the business.

A couple of quick examples to give you context:
A. Case Study: Boosting SDR Calls with HubSpot Automation
B. Case Study: Driving 24% Lead Growth for Getix Health
C. Case Study: Full Funnel Visibility With HubSpot

Here’s how I’d approach your HubSpot setup, even if we start small:

Phase 0 - Discovery & Audit (Blueprint)
We begin with a structured audit of your current HubSpot portal: data model, pipelines, properties, automation, reporting, and integrations. Alongside this, we map how your sales, marketing, and service teams actually operate. The outcome is a clear, prioritized roadmap that shows what’s broken, what’s missing, and what’s simply noise.

Phase 1 - Core Fixes & Foundations
Based on the audit, we stabilize the essentials:
  • Simplifying pipelines and lifecycle stages
  • Cleaning up property sprawl and inconsistent data
  • Fixing or introducing workflows where they genuinely save time
  • Closing key integration gaps so data flows cleanly

Phase 2 - Optimization, Reporting & Enablement
Once the foundation is solid, we optimize. This includes building dashboards your team can trust, documenting core processes, and enabling the team through short Loom walkthroughs or live working sessions. Adoption improves when people understand why things work the way they do.

A few questions to better understand your situation:
  1. Which teams are currently expected to use HubSpot, and where do you see the most confusion or resistance?
  2. What reports or metrics do you wish you could trust today but currently don’t?
  3. Are there any tools (CRM, ads, forms, support, billing) that should be better integrated with HubSpot?
  4. What would “HubSpot working well” look like for you 90 days from now?

Our goal isn’t just to fix HubSpot it’s to make your team confident and self-reliant on it, with clean data and clear processes that support growth.

Happy to start with a short audit discussion or walk you through relevant examples. Let's talk!

Cheers,
Todd!

job_title: HubSpot Workflow Optimizations
job_description:
We’re working to streamline and fix our HubSpot workflows to ensure cleaner, more automated, and data-driven processes. Specifically, we’re focusing on improving: 

  • Campaign templates – building consistent, scalable templates to standardize marketing campaigns and ensure accurate attribution.
  • Enrichment – enhancing lead data quality to enable better segmentation and personalization.
  • Scorecards – refining lead scoring logic to better identify high-quality leads and align with sales priorities. As well as connecting with external tools for reporting on these scorecards. 
  • Data management – cleaning and structuring data to eliminate duplicates, improve reporting accuracy, and drive better insights across teams.

Our overall goal is to make HubSpot more efficient, automated, and insightful for both marketing and sales teams. We'd prefer hourly but open to see what a project based cost is as well.
proposal_description:
Hello,

Thanks for reaching out. I'm interested in this—and I think the right first step is a 30-minute call rather than a long proposal.

Here's why: HubSpot workflow optimization usually looks simple on the surface (fix campaign templates, clean data, rebuild scoring), but the real complexity is invisible in the UI. When you have workflows across campaigns, enrichment, scoring, and data management all running simultaneously, they're almost always conflicting in ways you can't see—enrichment updating fields that scoring depends on, campaign templates triggering against stale data, duplicates running in parallel.

I built a custom workflow audit tool along with a object and property audit tool specifically to surface these conflicts. It maps your complete workflow ecosystem, flags dependencies, identifies duplicates, and prioritizes what to fix first. Most teams try to optimize blindly. This tool shows you exactly what's breaking and why before we touch anything.

We've used this approach managing workflow orchestration at Streamline over five years—coordinating across hundreds of automated processes with dependencies that break when they're not explicit. The difference between fixing workflows in isolation (which fails) and fixing them architecturally (which sticks) comes down to understanding your complete system first.

On a call, I want to understand:
  • What's breaking most frequently right now?
  • Where is your team spending the most time troubleshooting vs. optimizing?
  • Which systems do these workflows touch beyond HubSpot?

From there, I can run the audit, give you real guidance on scope, and figure out whether hourly or project-based makes sense for your situation.

Here is my calendar link to schedule a call --> https://calendly.com/unified-support-solutions/meeting?back=1&month=2025-11&duration=30

Ryan Ginsberg
Founder, Unified Support Solutions
HubSpot Certified Pro

job_title: Implement Loop Marketing Playbook
job_description:
I just booked time with you today at 3:00 to discuss HubSpot and how we can implement the new Loop Playbook. Before we connect, I wanted to give you a little context on who we are and what we’re looking for:
About Us
We’re a family-owned winery established in 1981, growing and producing wines and selling 100% direct-to-consumer (B2C). Today we produce 23 wines plus 4 grower champagnes (soon expanding to 12). Our team is 23 employees total:
  • Vineyard: 8
  • Winery: 6
  • Sales/Tasting Room: 3
  • Reservations/Logistics: 2 (currently using a customized HubSpot setup)
  • Admin/Marketing: 2
We also have strong brand guidelines and a compelling marketing story, though we haven’t been consistent in social media or digital execution.
Sources of Revenue
  • Wine Clubs (largest driver)
    • Society of Smugglers (3x/year, 6 unique wines)
    • Winesteins (2x/year, Rocket Science label)
    • Ballers Society (2x/year, signature high-tier wines)
    • Champagne Club (launching soon, expanding our grower champagne program)
  • Tasting Room Sales
  • JFC on the Road – bringing the experience directly to clients with a 20-case minimum expectation
  • Barrel Program – premium membership requiring a ~24-case commitment ($34K)
  • Online Sales – present, but not yet a major revenue driver
Current Tools & Operations
We use:
  • Ecommerce & Clubs: Shopify, Stripe, Bloom (4 wine clubs), Sovos (compliance)
  • Content & Storage: Dropbox, Experience App, Paperless Post
  • Communication: Zoom (phones), Red Chirp (text), Read.ai (for meeting insights) – exploring Slack
  • Events & Community: Wine events via Experience App, considering Reddit wine community
We need more cohesive reporting on club performance (signups, holds, skips, upsells).
Current HubSpot Setup
  • Content Hub Professional (3 core seats + 10,000 additional marketing contacts, 1 brand, 100 embeddable videos)
  • Marketing Hub Enterprise (5 core seats + 10,000 contacts)
  • Considering Data Hub (new Operations Hub)
  • Plan to cancel Breeze (too overwhelming and not ideal for B2C)
  • Exploring Wistia, Canva, and better integration for video/content
Our HubSpot renewal is October 8.
What We’re Looking For (Prioritized)
  1. Content & Storytelling Expansion
    • Implement more video content, leveraging my husband as a rich source for living history of Napa Valley wine
    • Repurpose content across multiple platforms (currently only Instagram + Facebook; interest in TikTok, LinkedIn, others)
    • Build processes to consistently showcase our brand voice, story, and customer advocacy (“raving fans”)
  2. Data Foundation & Loop Playbook
    • Begin with data cleanup and consolidation across spreadsheets, tools, and systems
    • Use HubSpot to better pull reports from our client and staff communications, helping us see where we’re doing well and where we need improvement
    • Centralize years of contacts and data into one place, as mentioned at INBOUND with their Data Hub or am I able to do that in a service I already have?
    • We know we have a lot of cleanup to do and are excited to hear your proposal on how to get us streamlined and moving in the right direction
    • Streamline reporting on wine club performance (signups, holds, skips, upsells)
    • Make surea HubSpot is configured to support both B2C and premium programs (e.g., JFC on the Road, Barrel Program)
  3. Discoverability & Digital Growth
    • Shift from SEO-only focus to AI-driven discoverability (priority for us to be found in AI summaries/search)
    • Expand online sales, which are currently minimal but represent a growth opportunity
  4. Operational Efficiency
    • Increase efficiency across our lean team by better using HubSpot and integrations
    • Replace or optimize tools where needed (ex: Breeze, Slack, video hosting platforms)
Renewal Priorities
With our renewal coming up October 8, we’d like your help to audit what we’re paying for and determine what’s truly needed now versus what can wait until we’re ready to fully leverage it.
For example, we signed up with Breeze but never used it. I’d like to understand the steps needed to pause or remove that service until the timing is right. Having your guidance on prioritization will help us make the most of HubSpot while avoiding being overwhelmed. Do you happen to have workflows of each of the services or know who I need to connect with to provide them for me?
We’re excited to dive in with you and get a clear plan in place.
Joy

Goal: Fix email deliverability and re-start marketing campaigns; simplify and standardize our HubSpot CRM for adoption (including an acquired team); and enable reliable forecasting and attribution—then train our team to sustain it.
Background & Pain Points
  • Marketing emails paused after poor sender reputation (“HubSpot jail”): high bounces, list quality issues.
  • CRM is bloated (deal cards/fields, processes), causing low adoption—especially on the newly acquired team.
  • Inconsistent data capture from conferences/meetings; weak source attribution; limited visibility for forecasting.
  • Former in-house HubSpot owner left; we need an embedded expert/agency to steady the system and upskill us.

  1. Audit & Action Plan (phase 0): Quick technical/operational audit with a prioritized roadmap and effort estimate.
  2. Email Deliverability Recovery: Repair domain reputation (e.g., alias domains + warming tool), DNS auth (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), list health, segmentation, and sending best practices—so monthly nurture resumes.
  3. CRM Simplification & Governance: Streamline pipelines and deal cards (reduce required fields), standardize contact/account fields, list-upload rules, and create admin guardrails.
  4. Adoption & Training: Role-based training (BD, marketing, “doer-seller” teams), playbooks, and short Looms; show-and-do enablement.
  5. Attribution & Activity Capture: Improve meeting/note capture (Fathom already used; open to lightweight voice/capture tools), conference lead workflows, and reliable source attribution.
  6. Reporting & Forecasting: Clean dashboards for pipeline health and stage-based probability forecasting; executive roll-ups.
  7. Documentation & Handoff: Admin guide, governance doc, field dictionary, and 30/60-day operating checklist.
Deliverables
  • Audit + remediation plan; DNS/auth + warming implemented; cleaned/segmented send lists.
  • Simplified pipelines/deal cards and governed list-upload process.
  • Adoption toolkit: playbooks, role trainings, and recorded how-tos.
  • Standard attribution model + dashboards for pipeline/forecast accuracy.
  • Admin documentation and ongoing recommendations.
Success Looks Like
  • Marketing domain warmed and campaigns relaunching with healthy metrics.
  • BD and acquired team actively using HubSpot (measurable weekly activity uplift).
  • Fewer fields, faster data entry, cleaner data; clear visibility into pipeline and forecast.
  • Team can run the playbook without external help.

  • Start ASAP with a paid audit, then execute in phases (email → CRM simplification → adoption/reporting).

  • HubSpot (Marketing & Sales), Fathom for meeting notes; considering simple voice/capture for in-person meetings.

job_title: Create a shipping PDF from CRM info
job_description:
We'd like to make a feature where we can create a shipping note pulling in properties from a deal.

It would contain things like the items ordered, address and name.

Inside of HubSpot we'd like to generate the PDF so we can print and give to our warehouse team.

We've seen some marketplace apps which can do this but most have a per order cost which makes it extremely expensive over a year so open to either someone building the function themselves or someone finding a lower cost fixed monthly fee marketplace integration and customising it for us.
proposal_description:
Hello,

Thanks for sharing the scope for the shipping note feature — it’s a well-defined use case and very achievable within HubSpot using a custom-built solution.

Overview

You’re looking for a feature that allows users to:

Pull deal properties (e.g. items ordered, shipping address, recipient name)

Generate a printable PDF shipping note

Trigger this from within HubSpot (ideally via a button)

Avoid ongoing per-document costs charged by most marketplace apps


Solution

I've implemented very similar systems in HubSpot before — for example:

Phocas Software: Form-to-PDF generation with the file uploaded to the deal record

Adams Outdoor Advertising: Proposal document builder using HubSpot UI Extensions and serverless functions


Approach for Your Project:

1. Custom UI Extension
Adds a “Generate Shipping Note” button on the deal record.


2. Serverless Function
Pulls the relevant deal data (e.g. line items, shipping address) and generates a well-formatted PDF.


3. PDF Handling
The PDF is either automatically attached to the deal or made available for direct download/print.


4. Design Flexibility
PDF layout and formatting can be customized based on your internal warehouse team’s preferences.


Cost

Depending on the design complexity and number of data points involved, the full implementation cost would be in the range of $2,400 (fixed fee) — which includes:

Initial discovery session

UI extension setup

Serverless backend logic

PDF generation & formatting

HubSpot file upload or download logic

Light testing and deployment

Basic documentation or handoff session


Optional: Cost-Effective Third-Party App Customization

If you'd prefer to go with a marketplace tool and just need help customizing it, I'm happy to vet some options and set up the workflow — though many tools still charge per document, so a custom build is likely your best ROI long-term.

Let me know if you'd like to move forward — happy to set up a quick call to scope PDF requirements and styling preferences.

Best regards,

Ryan Ginsberg
Unified Support Solutions

4. Lead Capture & Conversion Setup

  • Standardize thank-you page and follow-up email patterns.

5. Marketing Automation

  • Set up automated nurtures (welcome, warm-up, content-based, re-engagement).
  • Implement operational workflows for data cleanup and enrichment.

6. Email Marketing
  • Authenticate domains (DKIM/SPF) and configure send settings.
  • Create branded templates for newsletters, nurture, and campaigns.
  • Establish A/B testing and deliverability best practices.

7. Campaign & Channel Setup
  • Connect ad platforms (Google Ads, LinkedIn, Meta) with tracking templates.
  • Set up the Campaigns tool for multi-asset campaign reporting.
  • Configure blog, social publishing, and SEO tools if in scope.

8. Reporting & Attribution
  • Ensure tracking across ads, web, email, and automation.
  • Build dashboards for channel performance, lead acquisition, email engagement, and attribution.
  • Validate that conversion paths and campaign influence are recorded correctly.

9. QA, Launch & Handoff
  • Test all forms, workflows, scoring rules, CTAs, and emails.
  • Run a pilot campaign to validate data flow and attribution accuracy.
  • Provide training for Marketing Hub users and deliver documentation.

Some of this might not be relevant to your client, but I want to make sure you know what I can offer. I can scale back the plan based on your priorities and budget. I see this taking ~2 weeks to implement depending on what all you want to do, access to the platform and feedback from stakeholders. 

Note: My rate is $90/hr. I'm estimating 25 hours of work, so $90 * 25 = $2250.00 for the work described above.

Best,

job_title: Hubspot ADM Audience/Segmentation Creation
job_description:
Project Context 
  • HubSpot is our primary marketing automation platform.
  • Salesforce is our system of record for Accounts and Contacts.
  • SAP ECC houses all Sales Order data, which we are syncing into HubSpot via SFTP-delivered CSV files.
  • Sales Orders are mapped in HubSpot as custom objects, with associations to Accounts and/or Contacts when available.

  
We want to build advanced, dynamic segmentation in HubSpot for ABM and lifecycle campaigns. Example use cases: 
  • Segment Contacts based on their Account’s purchase history (e.g., product category, order frequency).
  • Create lists of Contacts at dormant Accounts (no orders in X days).
  • Build audiences of high-value buyers based on SAP order totals over time.Trigger campaigns based on Account Tier or Order Recency.

Association Model 
  •  We support multiple B2B order channels for our customers — including EDI, web ordering, customer service, and sales rep-assisted transactions. All orders are placed against a Sold-To account, which is mandatory. However, contact-level data is only available in some cases (ecommerce, customer service). 
 
This creates a mix of association scenarios: 
  • Some Sales Orders can only be linked to an Account in HubSpot.
  • Others can be associated with both the Account and a specific Contact, when data permits.
 
  
As a result, segmentation must account for these differences—ensuring that audiences can be built based on both Account-level order history and, when available, Contact-level engagement.

job_title: This is NOT a drill: HubSearch AI Tool for Website
job_description:
Hey Pros!! Looking for immediate help scoping and building the following:

Context:

Referral from HubSpot reps (AE's, CS, PDM, etc..) is our most valuable client acquisition channel. I need to build something akin to a "referral portal" and it needs to actually add value. Can't just be lead capture for us. The idea here is that a CS rep at HubSpot hears something about "needing to hire a full-time employee that knows HubSpot" and they can offer this gated page as a solution. Rep might say "Do you know HubSearch? They're a HubSpot specific recruiting org and they offer this really cool HubSpot Job Description service free for anyone we send their way. I'll send you the link, use my email address as the password to access, add some detail about what you need in your hire and they will build you a job description for you to post".

Obvious benefit for HubSearch - we get the referral and the data from the company and of course will have some great campaign on the back end of this. Company gets a custom built job spec, that ideally looks like a member of HubSearch took the time to craft it. We also will get very important attribution info on which HubSpot rep sent that referral which we're planning to use to power a gifting platform (Loop & Tie).

The project:

  • Build the entry point. It needs to be gated (or at least appear to be gated with password. perhaps that password is <anything>@hubspot.com

  • Once in they hit a pretty landing page that talks all about the job description that we will send. Need this landing page with form created.

  • Need that AI component built. Thinking this is probably an AI workflow tool on the back-end that takes the input, creates the job spec using a set of JD's that we've trained it on, and sends the spec to a member of my team.

  • I'd want this tool to be flexible enough for interesting repurposing, ideally so that the thing could build anything that AI is well suited to build (HubSpot specific interview questions for example) so we can use it other ways.

We're on SalesHub Enterprise, MarketingHub Pro, legacy CMS Pro

Desired Time Frame: Now and complete by end of Jan
proposal_description:
A "referral portal" is a great way to drive referrals, but the idea of generating free really cool HubSpot Job Descriptions feels super premium. Is an epic lead magnet for HubSearch to add value to the customer. It also cleverly solves a problem that a customer MUST SOLVE before buying from HubSearch (needing to hire a full-time employee who knows HubSpot), making it the PERFECT lead magnet.

To bring this idea to life, the first step is a Technical scoping project.

A scoping project is the first step in tackling a technical or complex initiative. It’s a structured process to fully understand the problem, define the requirements, and evaluate the technical feasibility of different potential solutions. The goal is to map out what needs to be done, test ideas through a Proof of Concept (POC), and create a clear action plan for successful implementation.

This phase focuses on mapping out what needs to be done and establishing a clear plan of action, minimising risk and ensuring alignment before moving on to the full build phase.

What needs to be done

Based on your project goals, here’s an outline of how we’ll approach each key component:

1/ Create the entry point:

  • Create a gated page for HubSpot reps to refer customers to access the portal

  • Options for gating include password protection (available with your current licenses) or migrating to Membership Pages (Content Hub Pro).

  • Add referral tracking to link submissions back to the correct rep.

2/ Automate job descriptions:

  • Design a system to generate customized job descriptions based on user input.

  • Ensure the output is professional and consistent.

  • Explore options for automation, such as: 

    • Zapier/Make for simplicity and speed.

    • Third-party apps from the HubSpot Marketplace.

    • HubSpot Programmable Automation (requires Ops Hub).

  • Optionally expand AI functionality to include additional hiring tools, like adding suggested interview questions to the Job Description.

3/ Track referrals:

  • Set up attribution so you know who sent each referral.

  • Use this data to build gifting campaigns and strengthen relationships.


Proof of Concept in 10 Hours

In just 10 hours, we can:

  • Map workflows and requirements.

  • Test a proof of concept (POC) to show how gated access, referrals, and automation will work.

  • Deliver a clear plan to move from concept to full implementation.


This gives you a tested concept and a clear path forward, saving you time and ensuring the final tool works as planned.

☝️ The POC tests the feasibility of your solution at an early stage, but it's not uncommon for these POCs to be very close to the final solution. If not, you'll be fully informed of any technical challenges and decisions, along with a clear recommendation on how to proceed.

job_title: HubSpot Contact Clean Up
job_description: Hello! We put in place a faulty workflow that jumbled up a ton of our contacts. We need help understanding what exactly happened, and putting measures in place so it does not happen again. I am the sales operations manager, and am happy to do the legwork; I just need some guidance on the best way to achieve contacts being associated with the right companies, and assigned to the correct salesperson. We only have 4 sales reps.
proposal_description:
Hello, 

Appreciate the context. Sounds like a workflow fired in a way that caused unintended changes across your contact records. You’re not alone—this kind of thing happens more often than people admit. The good news is we can help you get to the bottom of it and put the right safeguards in place. 
 
At Unified Support Solutions, we work directly with HubSpot’s APIs and property history tools to diagnose what happened and, when possible, revert changes cleanly. Since you’re handling Sales Ops and willing to do the legwork, we’ll keep this efficient and focused on what matters. 
 
Here’s how we’d approach it: 
 
Phase 1: Figure Out What Broke 

  • Review the faulty workflow(s)
  • Use property history to identify what changed and how widespread the issue is
  • Pinpoint the root cause—whether that’s ownership, company association, or something else
 

Phase 2: Clean It Up
 

  • Revert changes where we can using HubSpot’s property history
  • Build logic to restore data where rollback isn’t possible
  • Handle this in bulk, safely
 

Phase 3: Make Sure It Doesn’t Happen Again
 

  • Refactor the workflow (or rebuild it) with proper safeguards
  • Add guardrails to catch future issues early
  • Document what happened and how to avoid it moving forward

 
This isn’t a massive engagement—just the right amount of help to get things stable again. We can work on a flat fee or hourly depending on what makes more sense for you.
 
Let me know if you want to schedule a call or jump straight to a scoped proposal. We’re ready when you are.

 
Best,
 
Ryan Ginsberg

Unified Support Solutions

Owner | Technical CRM Consultant

818-645-5832

unifiedsupportsolutions.com

job_title: HubSpot CRM Audit, clean-up and future proofing project!
job_description:
Hey friends (this is Jason) - posting this on behalf of customer referred by HubSpot. I just got off a call with their CRM Admin and typed up the following description. She is looking for "her HubSpot partner" that she can lean on anytime they need help. Global manufacturing org. I have a proposal review meeting with her next Weds so get proposals in ASAP! I'll make sure she comes out of that meeting having booked calls with her top choices. 

Details (cleaned up by GPT):

I need help from a sophisticated HubSpot CRM Admin

I’m the CRM admin at a global manufacturing company with 5 facilities — 2 in the U.S. and the rest in Europe. We’ve been in HubSpot since 2022, and our entire global sales team operates from one HubSpot instance.

Things are messy.

We’re dealing with complex, overlapping sales team data. It’s been tough to keep domains and territories clean and separate. We have missing data, unclear lead qualification, and no reliable way to know who we should market to, who’s qualified, or who owns what account. We just rolled out sales territories and it’s been a real challenge.

My role has expanded fast, and I need a real HubSpot expert to step in and help me get this right.

Here’s what I need:

  • A full audit of our HubSpot instance to show me where we’re exposed and what needs to be fixed
  • Cleanup of existing data and setup improvements
  • Long-term structure that future-proofs our system so incoming data stays clean
  • Clear account ownership rules so every sales rep knows exactly who and what they’re responsible for — no more record crossover

I’m open to working with an individual or an agency. I do not want hourly billing — I want clear project pricing with an all-in cost and time frame. I’m also open to breaking this into smaller phases to get started and build momentum.

Target budget: $8,000–$10,000 total for initial scope

Ultimately, I want to find our HubSpot person — someone who understands complex sales orgs and knows how to design a CRM that actually supports the team using it.

Let’s fix this together.
proposal_description:
Hello, and thank you for your submission to Profoundly!!

Initial Disclaimer (to avoid sticker shock): I submitted this quote assuming it will be broken out in Phases, and my submission is for Phase 1, outlined in detail below.


Intro:

I am a solopreneur that specializes in additional HubSpot support in areas that I have high level of experience in, and everything you outlined below falls well within my sweet spot. I have dealt with manufacturing and distribution companies, I have created and managed complex sales territories based on various criteria like geography, product categories, seniority, etc., and I obsess over data hygiene and integrity for every system I've operated, some with dozens of complex integrations that create/update records hundreds of times a day. 

Scope:

My recommendation would be breaking this project into at least 4 distinct project engagements based on the bullets you outlined. This will help you get the most bang for your buck and maintain control over how this project gets executed and how time gets spent. It also keeps you from being tied to one provider for the entirety of the engagement if you dislike how one component gets resolved. So, even if you go with someone else here, that would still be in your best interest.

Phase 1: 
  • A full audit of our HubSpot instance to show me where we’re exposed and what needs to be fixed.
    • This is table stakes for everyone here and should be the least complex variable in the whole equation.
      • Quote: $2,000
      • Timeline: Completed 1-2 weeks from HubSpot Access provided
      • Deliverable(s): Audit document and presentation deck (examples below)

Phase 2:
  • Cleanup of existing data and setup improvements
    • You'll likely see a bit more variance in pricing here based on number of records and number of properties involved, but it still should be straight forward engagement.
    • Removed the "setup improvements" from your initial bullet to make Phase 2 simpler and put most of the complex processes in Phase 3.
      • Quote: $1,500 - $3,000 (locked in upon completion of Phase 1)
      • Timeline: Completed 1-3 weeks from Phase 1 Completion and Approval
      • Deliverable(s): Change Order document with high level description of changes, csv file of pre-cleaned data (for possible restoration if needed), object library map
 
Phase 3:
  • Setup Improvements & Long-term structure that future-proofs our system so incoming data stays clean
    • This one is the most complex to provide a Quote for right now because it is entirely dependent on what is uncovered and created in Phases 1 and 2, and on timeline and preference for any training or iterating upon what is built. 
      • Quote: $2,000 - $4,000 (locked in upon completion of Phase 2)
      • Timeline: Completed 2-8 weeks from Phase 2 Completion and Approval
      • Deliverable(s): Training materials, integration data map, workflow importance hierarchy

Phase 4:
  • Clear account ownership rules so every sales rep knows exactly who and what they’re responsible for — no more record crossover
    • This can be simple or extremely sophisticated and depends on your needs/preferences, but either way the technical work to be completed is similar, sophistication just means more branches or processes (example: do you need an account retention policy automation to remove accounts after x days of ownership or not activity? and is there account sharing or not?).
      • Quote: $1,500 - $2,500 (locked in upon completion of Phase 3)
      • Timeline: Completed 2-3 weeks from Phase 3 Completion and Approval
      • Deliverable(s): Training materials, ownership process documentation.

All In With Rylee:

Sharing the low end and high end estimate of this project for managing expectations. In reality, it will likely be somewhere in between the two.

Low End (simple: not a lot of records/Hubs/Tiers/integrations in play, manageable improvement wish list, moderate sales ownership requirements, little/no training)
  • Quote: $7,000
  • Timeline: 6 Weeks

High End (complex: many records/Hubs/Tiers/integrations in play, long improvement wish list, extremely nuanced sales ownership requirements, lot of training)
  • Quote: $11,500
  • Timeline: 16 Weeks



Next Steps:

I submitted this quote assuming it will be broken out in Phases, and my submission is for Phase 1.

I included "half-up-front", but in reality it's just $1,000 down payment for all new clients. Remainder and all future projects/phases will be paid-in-full upon your final review and approval of all completed work. 

I find it best to do a project review/proposal finalization call before we officially kick things off. I am free all next week between 10 am - 3 pm EST if you'd like to jump on a free, no obligation call to discuss this project. If those times don't work for you, feel free to suggest something that fits.

If you proceed with me following that call, then next steps will be granting access to your HubSpot and I Start Phase 1 Audit documentation.


Example screenshots below:

Audit Raw Document
130 assessment points in total (redacted text in certain columns for client anonymity)

Audit Findings Presentation
outlines how system is configured, what it is currently doing well, what it is currently doing that it should be/is suboptimal, and areas that are underutilized considering your current subscription tiers (with recommendations for how to maximize usage and/or how to optimize costs)

The total fee for this engagement is a flat rate of $2,000, covering up to 15 hours of work over 7 days. This budget-conscious approach ensures maximum value within your financial constraints.

This engagement will mend your HubSpot account into a unified, efficient platform that reduces drag on revenue momentum and strengthens your competitive advantage. job_title: Restore Email, HubSpot Optimization & Team Enablement
job_description:
HubSpot Re-Implementation & Enablement (CREDE)

Goal: Fix email deliverability and re-start marketing campaigns; simplify and standardize our HubSpot CRM for adoption (including an acquired team); and enable reliable forecasting and attribution—then train our team to sustain it.
Background & Pain Points
  • Marketing emails paused after poor sender reputation (“HubSpot jail”): high bounces, list quality issues.
  • CRM is bloated (deal cards/fields, processes), causing low adoption—especially on the newly acquired team.
  • Inconsistent data capture from conferences/meetings; weak source attribution; limited visibility for forecasting.
  • Former in-house HubSpot owner left; we need an embedded expert/agency to steady the system and upskill us.
What We’re Looking For (Scope)
  1. Audit & Action Plan (phase 0): Quick technical/operational audit with a prioritized roadmap and effort estimate.
  2. Email Deliverability Recovery: Repair domain reputation (e.g., alias domains + warming tool), DNS auth (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), list health, segmentation, and sending best practices—so monthly nurture resumes.
  3. CRM Simplification & Governance: Streamline pipelines and deal cards (reduce required fields), standardize contact/account fields, list-upload rules, and create admin guardrails.
  4. Adoption & Training: Role-based training (BD, marketing, “doer-seller” teams), playbooks, and short Looms; show-and-do enablement.
  5. Attribution & Activity Capture: Improve meeting/note capture (Fathom already used; open to lightweight voice/capture tools), conference lead workflows, and reliable source attribution.
  6. Reporting & Forecasting: Clean dashboards for pipeline health and stage-based probability forecasting; executive roll-ups.
  7. Documentation & Handoff: Admin guide, governance doc, field dictionary, and 30/60-day operating checklist.
Deliverables
  • Audit + remediation plan; DNS/auth + warming implemented; cleaned/segmented send lists.
  • Simplified pipelines/deal cards and governed list-upload process.
  • Adoption toolkit: playbooks, role trainings, and recorded how-tos.
  • Standard attribution model + dashboards for pipeline/forecast accuracy.
  • Admin documentation and ongoing recommendations.
Success Looks Like
  • Marketing domain warmed and campaigns relaunching with healthy metrics.
  • BD and acquired team actively using HubSpot (measurable weekly activity uplift).
  • Fewer fields, faster data entry, cleaner data; clear visibility into pipeline and forecast.
  • Team can run the playbook without external help.
Engagement Model
  • Start ASAP with a paid audit, then execute in phases (email → CRM simplification → adoption/reporting).
  • Open to a vetted solo consultant or small agency; we value hands-on execution + teach-us-to-fish enablement.
  • Indicative budget: $5k–$15k depending on depth; begin with the audit and expand based on outcomes.
Key Tools/Context
  • HubSpot (Marketing & Sales), Fathom for meeting notes; considering simple voice/capture for in-person meetings.
  • Must keep all comms/activities tracked in HubSpot.

proposal_description:

We’ve led and supported similar multi-entity rollouts before, where the goal wasn’t just to implement HubSpot, but to build a unified commercial infrastructure that gives every brand shared visibility and consistent control.

Most recently, we worked with a European enterprise operating five brands across two geographies. We architected a single HubSpot CRM with cross-brand data governance, localized quote templates (EN/FR), brand-level dashboards, and automated attribution, very close to the PSG model outlined here.

Here’s how we’d approach this collaboration 

Phase 0 — Discovery & Implementation Blueprint ($2000 fixed)

We’ll start by validating your multi-brand structure and SOW assumptions:
  • Review proposed data schema across Contacts, Companies, Deals, Products, and Brand metadata.
  • Assess MS Dynamics import plan, dedupe logic, and field mapping to HubSpot objects.
  • Evaluate pipeline standardization, property dependencies, and governance rules.
  • Validate proposed quote logic (line-item grouping, pricing overrides, umbrella SKUs).
  • Identify required custom associations, permission groups, and SSO structure.
Deliverable: A Blueprint Deck covering architecture, effort estimates per Hub, quick wins, and risk mitigations, fully aligned with Fresh Nous’ client-facing plan.

Phase 1 — Platform Configuration & Implementation

A. Data Hub Starter

  • Design a unified data model (Contacts, Companies, Deals, Products) with cross-brand deduplication logic.
  • Implement brand-tagging via custom properties and object associations.
  • Handle one-time import from MS Dynamics (one brand), including schema alignment and cleansing scripts.
  • Deliver data governance documentation - mapping tables, backup policy, and user access model.

B. Sales Hub Enterprise

  • Configure multi-brand CRM hierarchy using Parent-Child Company structures and shared pipelines segmented by brand milestones.
  • Implement stage automation: auto-task creation, owner assignment, and Outlook calendar sync via Microsoft 365 connector.
  • Create brand-specific quote templates (localized EN/FR), linked to unified product library and custom quote logic (grouped items, MRP identifiers).
  • Enforce role-based permissions, 2FA, and hierarchical team visibility.

job_title: Enrich Contact records in Hubspot from Wealth Engine API
job_description: We need to create an integration between HubSpot and Wealth Engine to enrich a filtered set of contact records.
proposal_description:
Hi,
Thank you for sharing this requirement.

To create an integration between HubSpot and Wealth Engine for enriching records, there are a set of best practices and technical considerations that we follow.
The integration is typically achieved through API connections, middleware (like Zapier), custom workflows, and careful data handling.

Key Points & Best Practices -

  • Filtered Enrichment - My team always filter and validate records to enrich only those that justify the cost and effort. Avoid bulk enrichment of the entire database.​

  • Data Quality - Audit and cleanse HubSpot data before enrichment to avoid errors

  • Field Mapping -  Clearly mapping of Wealth Engine enrichment fields to HubSpot contacts ensures consistency and prevent loss of data.​ This can prove to be the make or break for most enrichments.

  • Automation & Manual Checks: Combining auto enrichment (via workflows) with periodic manual reviews is good for accuracy and compliance.​

  • Reporting: Develop dashboards in HubSpot to track which contacts have been enriched and what impact the added data has on engagement or conversion.


Proposal - HubSpot<>Wealth Engine Integration

Here’s how my team will take care of the integration would be approached for maximum value and operational reliability:

  1. Discovery & Audit Phase
    First, my team will collaborate to confirm the business requirements, identify exactly which HubSpot contacts need enrichment, and clean existing data to ensure a high-quality foundation for integration. This ensures only the most relevant contacts are processed, optimizing cost and impact.​

  2. Technical Design
    My team will then design a secure and scalable integration between HubSpot and Wealth Engine, using middleware like Zapier or direct APIs. 
    Each filtered contact will be sent to Wealth Engine to retrieve enrichment data, including wealth indicators, giving capacity, and engagement scores or any other fields as per requirement, which will be mapped to dedicated HS fields.​

  3. Field Mapping & Workflow Automation
    Then we will set up automated workflows so that contacts meeting specific filters in HubSpot trigger enrichment requests, with returned data updating your CRM in real time. 
    Custom properties will be defined to store these insights cleanly and securely.​

  4. Quality Assurance & Compliance
    The process will include periodic manual checks, reporting dashboards, and compliance reviews for data privacy and accuracy. Continuous monitoring and maintenance ensure lasting business impact and operational peace of mind, though optional or your own team can take care of it.

  5. Documentation & Training
    We will provide detailed documentation of the integration, field mappings, and workflows, along with user training so your team can sustain and expand the process with minimal support.​

This approach ensures that your HubSpot contacts are enriched efficiently, accurately, and securely using Wealth Engine data, delivering actionable insight to your CRM.

job_title: HubSpot Salesforce Selective Sync Integration Expertise Needed for an Existing Integration. Updating Contact Account Relationship in Salesforce
job_description:
Hello, 

We are looking for a HubSpot agency with deep Salesforce integration experience. 

Currently in Salesforce our contact records are related to one account record and our existing business practice creates duplicate contacts when employees move throughout the industry. The HubSpot integration is currently configured with this contact model under a selective sync with Salesforce sharing rules and permission restrictions. 

We are actively evaluating shifting our Salesforce data structure to change the relationship between the contact and account object. 
- Current Relationship: 1 contact to 1 account 
- New Relationship: 1 contact to many accounts - with employment dates and multiple email address. Some contacts will be actively employed at multiple companies and some will have only 1 active account. 

This restructure would allow us to improve data quality and reporting capabilities in Salesforce. However, we need to understand what impact this would have in HubSpot. We will be reconciling years of duplicate contact in Salesforce, cleaning up our data and this will impact the integration to HubSpot. 

We're looking for an expert partner agency that will: 
1. Consult on the impact to HubSpot for this data shift - what needs to be done to any integration settings configured in Salesforce or HubSpot 
  • 2. Walk our HubSpot Administrator/Salesforce administrator through any updates needed
  • 3. Explain additional impacts to reporting or personalization such as updates that will be needed to workflows or personalization (Smart content) if a contact has multiple account relationships instead of 1. 
  • 4. Potentially join a meeting or 2 with our Salesforce consulting agency to ensure the systems are aligned based on expected updates and timelines for project changes. 

HubSpot Hub - Marketing Enterprise with one brand. 

Our goal is to determine the impact this project would have to determine best next steps as well as required budget. We want to have a better idea of the scope for this project over the next few weeks to have it ready for either December or 2026.
proposal_description:
Hi ,
I’m Lucy from Andimol — we help companies navigate complex HubSpot + Salesforce integrations to make sure both systems stay aligned as their data model evolves.

Your upcoming change from a 1:1 contact-to-account relationship to a 1:many structure (with multiple employments and emails per contact) is a smart move — but it’s also one of the most technically sensitive changes you can make. Our goal is to help you understand exactly how it will affect HubSpot before you move forward.


This short engagement is designed as a 10-hour Technical Scoping Project — a focused phase to uncover potential risks, test integration behavior, and build a clear, low-risk plan for implementation.

The project would be led by Martin Angeletti, a HubSpot Accredited Solutions Architect and Certified Trainer with 12+ years of experience bridging Salesforce, ERP, and HubSpot data models.


🎯 What We’ll Cover

  1. Integration Audit
    • Review current selective-sync rules, sharing logic, and field mappings.
    • Identify dependencies between Contact ↔ Account relationships and HubSpot properties, lists, and workflows.

  2. Impact Analysis
    • Evaluate how the new 1:many model (Employment or Affiliation object) would affect HubSpot syncing, deduplication, and reporting.
    • Map potential risks for smart content, personalization, and lifecycle logic.

  3. Sync & Ownership Strategy
    • Define which system (HubSpot or Salesforce) will be the source of truth for key fields.
    • Recommend adjustments to mapping, association labels, and custom objects.

  4. Deliverables & Next Steps
    • Integration Impact Summary (PDF)
    • High-level HubSpot–Salesforce Data Model diagram
    • Prioritized next-phase roadmap for your Admins and Salesforce partner

🧭 Timeline & Investment

Week 1 | Discovery & Audit | 4
Week 2 | Analysis & Recommendations | 6

Total: 10 hours
Investment: $2,000 USD (fixed)

This phase gives you a tested concept and a clear path forward, ensuring no data loss, sync conflicts, or broken workflows when restructuring your Salesforce relationships.


🗓 Next Steps

If this approach aligns with your needs, we can schedule a quick call so you can meet Martin and we can walk through the scope together.

Looking forward to collaborating on this,

When deals are long, buying journeys are non-linear, and revenue shows up over time, even small process gaps compound quickly. We’ve stepped into this exact scenario many times before, usually when HubSpot has become more of a chore than a tool.

I’m Todd, Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric. We’re a HubSpot Elite Partner, and a large part of our work is “steadying the system” after partial implementations, legacy process carryover, or growth that outpaced governance. We’re often brought in when leadership no longer trusts pipeline or forecast numbers not because HubSpot can’t support the model, but because the portal wasn’t designed to reflect how the business actually sells.

If we work together, I’d approach this in clear, practical phases.

Phase 0 - Audit & Blueprint

The output is a prioritized roadmap that explicitly calls out what’s breaking signal quality and executive confidence and what to fix first.

Phase 1 - Core Fixes & Structural Alignment
Based on the audit, we’d implement targeted improvements: lifecycle and lead status corrections, pipeline and forecasting logic updates, data cleanup rules, workflow refactoring, and integration hardening. The goal here is reliability making HubSpot a dependable system of record again.

Phase 2 - Optimization, Reporting & Enablement
Once the foundation is sound, we fine-tune automation, rebuild dashboards that leadership can actually trust, document governance rules, and enable your team through short Looms or live sessions. Our goal isn’t just to fix HubSpot it’s to make your team self-reliant on it.

job_title: HubSpot Contact Clean Up
job_description: Hello! We put in place a faulty workflow that jumbled up a ton of our contacts. We need help understanding what exactly happened, and putting measures in place so it does not happen again. I am the sales operations manager, and am happy to do the legwork; I just need some guidance on the best way to achieve contacts being associated with the right companies, and assigned to the correct salesperson. We only have 4 sales reps.
proposal_description:
Hi There,

I'd love to help clean up your HubSpot! I put together the following proposal assuming affected contact volume is reasonable (~<10,000 needing updates) and no significant workflow interdependencies that require custom dev work. 

Proposal: Fixing Contact–Company Associations & Strengthening Workflow Safeguards 
Objective
Resolve issues caused by a misconfigured workflow that led to incorrect contact–company associations and rep ownership. I'll implement fixes and introduce guardrails to prevent this from happening again—keeping your data clean, your workflows safe, and your sales team set up for success.  

Scope of Work 
1. Audit & Root Cause Analysis 
  • Review recent contact-based workflows to pinpoint where things went off track—especially around ownership assignments and company associations.
  • Analyze a sample of affected records to confirm when and how incorrect data was applied.

2. Fix & Reassociate
Contact–Company Associations
  • Auto-associate contacts to companies using domain-matching logic where possible.
  • For unmatched records, build a static list and reimport with corrected associations.

Sales Rep Ownership
  • Group affected contacts by key traits (e.g., region, lead source).
  • Use either a manual bulk assign or a round-robin workflow across the four sales reps.

3. Workflow Safeguards
  • Rebuild existing workflows with smarter logic—use conditions like “assign only if unknown” and exclude contacts already tied to a company or owner.
  • Lock down permissions so only key users can edit workflows.
  • Implement a test protocol using internal contacts before anything goes live.

4. Optional (But Recommended) Enhancements
  • Set up anomaly alerts using Operations Hub tools for proactive issue detection.
  • Create a repeatable QA checklist for future automation work to keep things clean and safe.

 
Expected Outcome
  • Contacts will be accurately associated with the right companies and sales reps.
  • Workflows will run with built-in logic to avoid overwriting valid data.
  • CRM records will be reliable, and your sales process will be back on track.
 
 I estimate this project would take 5 days with about 2-4 hours per day to audit the workflows, build lists of affected contacts, design the re-association logic, and fixing the ownership with QA as well. My usual rate is $80/hour so I'm open to fixed price for the project.

job_title: Hubspot data clean up, we have alot of duplicate companies and contacts we would like cleaned up
job_description: Hubspot data clean up, we have alot of duplicate companies and contacts we would like cleaned up, over 2000 companies and 4000 contacts
proposal_description:
🔧 $1,500 HubSpot Data Cleanup – Scope & Deliverables

Project Objective

To clean and deduplicate approximately 2,000 Companies and 4,000 Contacts in your HubSpot CRM to ensure more accurate reporting, segmentation, and automation.

Scope of Work

1. Pre-Cleanup Audit

Identify key sources of duplication (e.g., form submissions, imports, integrations)

Run initial analysis for:
  • Exact matches (email, domain, name)
  • Fuzzy matches (typos, similar company/contact names)


2. Flagging Duplicates

Use a mix of HubSpot dedupe tools, custom logic, and/or external scripts/tools to flag likely duplicates

Create a dedupe spreadsheet report for:
  • Exact match pairs
  • Fuzzy match pairs
  • Ambiguous/needs-review records


3. Merge Plan & Client Review

You receive a spreadsheet with:
  • Suggested merges (auto-approved)
  • Manual review items (you approve/reject)
  • We’ll also document any record where we defer the merge due to unclear associations or risk


4. Final Merge & QA

Merge confirmed records

Review associations (e.g., primary contacts on deals, parent-child companies, etc.)

Spot check 10% of final merged records for accuracy


5. Post-Cleanup Report

Summary of:
  • Duplicates removed
  • Contact & company count before/after
  • Risk flags
  • Recommendations to prevent future duplicates (e.g., form logic, integration sync settings)

Timeline

Duration
Pre-audit & analysis | 2 business days
Deduplication flagging | 3–5 business days
Review & confirmation | Client dependent
Final merge & QA | 2–3 business days
Total: Approx. 7–10 business days

Pricing

Fixed Fee: $1,500
(Includes up to 6,000 records)

Reforge Solutions is a HubSpot-certified, alumni-led consultancy trusted by startups and enterprise teams alike. We’ve cleaned up thousands of records across messy, long-neglected CRM systems. We don’t just push a button, we combine RevOps strategy with smart tooling and manual QA to deliver clean, reliable data you can build your business on.

Hola!
Gracias por la oportunidad de presentar una propuesta para la auditoría de su portal de HubSpot. Entendemos lo importante que es asegurarse de que están aprovechando al máximo la plataforma en la que invierten año tras año. Nuestro objetivo es sencillo: ayudarles a descubrir valor oculto, optimizar procesos y dar a su equipo claridad y confianza en el uso de HubSpot.

Entendimiento de sus necesidades
Ustedes buscan una auditoría completa del uso de HubSpot en su empresa para confirmar que están sacando el máximo provecho de las funcionalidades. Sabemos que el marco de tiempo es flexible, pero que quieren asegurarse de que las herramientas que pagan estén alineadas con sus metas de negocio y preparadas para el éxito a largo plazo.

Nuestro enfoque
Adoptamos un enfoque estructurado y colaborativo para las auditorías de HubSpot:

  1. Descubrimiento y revisión de accesos
    • Reunión con su equipo para entender los objetivos del negocio.
    • Revisión de la configuración actual de HubSpot, incluyendo roles de usuario, permisos e integraciones.

  2. Auditoría y análisis
    • Estructura de datos: contactos, empresas, negocios y propiedades.
    • Embudos y etapas: claridad, alineación y eficiencia.
    • Flujos de trabajo y automatización: oportunidades para reducir tareas manuales.
    • Informes y tableros: precisión y utilidad.
    • Integraciones: confirmar que HubSpot esté conectado con las herramientas adecuadas.

  3. Hallazgos y recomendaciones
    • Informe claro con fortalezas, brechas y áreas de mejora.
    • Hoja de ruta priorizada con acciones rápidas y mejoras a largo plazo.

  4. Implementación opcional
    • Si lo desean, también podemos ejecutar las mejoras:
      • Organización y limpieza de propiedades, listas y embudos.
      • Optimización de flujos de trabajo y automatización.
      • Creación de tableros personalizados según sus KPIs.
      • Capacitación para su equipo, asegurando que sepan usar las mejoras.

Entregables
  • Informe completo de auditoría de HubSpot
  • Plan de acción priorizado (acciones rápidas + largo plazo)
  • Hoja de ruta de implementación
  • Opcional: Ejecución de mejoras + capacitación del equipo

Cronograma
  • 2–3 semanas desde el inicio hasta la entrega del informe completo de auditoría (dependiendo de la complejidad y accesos).
  • La implementación puede iniciarse inmediatamente después si así lo desean.

Inversión
Su presupuesto estimado de $1,000–$5,000 encaja perfectamente.
  • Auditoría + recomendaciones: tarifa fija dentro de ese rango.
  • Implementación opcional: costo flexible según el alcance y las prioridades que decidan abordar.

¿Por qué Eternal Works?
  • Partner de Soluciones HubSpot Platinum con más de 25 años de experiencia.
  • Líderes de dos Grupos de Usuarios de HubSpot, profesores de HubSpot Academy e instructores de Bootcamp.
  • Amplia experiencia ayudando a empresas a simplificar HubSpot, optimizar el rendimiento y obtener resultados medibles.

Próximos pasos
Nos encantaría programar una llamada de inicio para confirmar accesos, objetivos y cronograma. A partir de ahí, comenzaremos el proceso de auditoría de inmediato y mantendremos a su equipo informado en cada etapa.
Muchas gracias nuevamente por considerar a Eternal Works. Esperamos con entusiasmo la oportunidad de ayudar a su empresa a aprovechar al máximo HubSpot.

Saludos cordiales,
Tim Jones
Fundador & CEO, Eternal Works


P.D.

job_title: HubSpot Implementation & Support Specialist – Ongoing Project-Based Role
job_description:
At Inovautus Consulting, we help accounting and consulting firms implement and optimize HubSpot Professional-tier Marketing, Sales, and CRM Hubs. We’re looking for a skilled and dependable HubSpot professional for 3–10 hours of ongoing work per week, with the potential to expand as our firm grows.

As our consulting work continues to grow, we’re reaching the limits of our internal capacity to manage multiple HubSpot implementation projects and ongoing client support.

Our lead consultant handles strategy, discovery, and some client communication — but we need a capable professional to help turn those plans into action. With several clients in different stages of onboarding and implementation, the work involves juggling multiple HubSpot environments, ensuring deliverables stay on track, and maintaining consistent progress across accounts.
This role exists to help us bridge that gap — bringing structure, execution, and follow-through to our HubSpot projects. 

What You’ll Work On
You’ll support both HubSpot implementation and ongoing client account management through a mix of strategic setup and tactical execution. Responsibilities include:
  • Collaborating with our lead consultant to map and manage implementation plans
  • Providing project management support—tracking tasks, managing timelines, and ensuring deliverables are met
  • Customizing pipelines, deal stages, and properties for both Marketing and Sales Hubs
  • Designing and maintaining automated workflows and lead scoring models
  • Managing CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) features within HubSpot to streamline proposal, invoicing, and payment workflows
  • Supporting ecommerce and payment integrations in HubSpot (e.g., configuring payment links, quotes, and integrations with accounting systems)
  • Importing and managing contact, company, and deal data
  • Building dashboards and reports to support financial, sales, and marketing  visibility
  • Setting up and maintaining brand kits, templates, and branded design assets in HubSpot
  • Reviewing existing client accounts for workflow or data issues and recommending solutions
  • Handling HubSpot support tickets and requests for client accounts
  • Monitoring HubSpot feature releases and identifying opportunities to enhance client accounts
Bonus points if you have basic development experience for landing page setup, module customization, or styling.
Our HubSpot Environment
We primarily support accounting and consulting firms using:
  • HubSpot CRM (core contact, company, and deal management)
  • Marketing Hub Professional (email marketing, forms, workflows, CTAs, landing pages)
  • Sales Hub Professional (pipelines, automation, quotes, payments, and reporting)
  • HubSpot CPQ and Payments (proposal-to-invoice automations and payment workflows)
You’ll help ensure these systems work seamlessly together to support both internal and client-facing business processes.

About You
You’re organized, reliable, and comfortable managing multiple projects. You take initiative, communicate clearly, and can confidently move work forward from direction or recordings. You’re experienced in HubSpot and familiar with its tools across CRM, Marketing, Sales, and Operations.
You’re comfortable on client calls, reviewing notes and recordings, and translating those into actionable plans that you can execute independently. Ideally, you hold HubSpot certifications in areas such as Sales Software, Marketing Software, Reporting, Implementation, or CPQ.
proposal_description:
Hi Inovautus Consulting,

Thanks for the detailed brief. This role is a strong match for our team. We’re a certified HubSpot Partner agency with a dedicated HubSpot Implementation team that supports other agencies and B2B clients across the full suite of Hubs, including Sales and Marketing Pro, CPQ, and CRM customization.

We regularly work in a fractional support capacity for firms like yours who need a reliable partner to operationalize strategy, juggle multiple portals, and bring structure and consistency to execution. Our team includes certified HubSpot Solutions Architects, Implementation Specialists, and Project Managers, and we’ve supported over 100 onboardings and portal builds this year alone.

Here’s how we would support you:
  • Translating strategy into execution with clear documentation, task tracking, and accountability
  • Building and managing pipelines, lead scoring models, custom properties, and workflows
  • Supporting CPQ setup, including quotes, payments, and accounting system integrations
  • Cleaning and importing data, building dashboards, and improving visibility across funnels
  • Maintaining brand kits, templates, and assets across client portals
  • Identifying optimization opportunities during implementation or onboarding
  • Proactively monitoring feature releases and surfacing client enhancement opportunities

We work in close collaboration with other consultants and internal teams. We’re comfortable jumping in with partial briefs, working from recordings, or building off of Miro boards or strategy decks.

We’d propose starting with a flexible retainer of 10 hours per month that can scale as needed. This gives you consistent support without the need to re-scope each small task. Our standard rate is $150 per hour, with options for pre-purchased hours or project-based pricing depending on how you'd prefer to structure things long term.

If helpful, we’re happy to jump on a quick call to discuss your active accounts and how we can best plug in.

Looking forward to hearing from you.
