job_title: Restore Email, HubSpot Optimization & Team Enablement
job_description:
HubSpot Re-Implementation & Enablement (CREDE)

Goal: Fix email deliverability and re-start marketing campaigns; simplify and standardize our HubSpot CRM for adoption (including an acquired team); and enable reliable forecasting and attribution—then train our team to sustain it.
Background & Pain Points
  • Marketing emails paused after poor sender reputation (“HubSpot jail”): high bounces, list quality issues.
  • CRM is bloated (deal cards/fields, processes), causing low adoption—especially on the newly acquired team.
  • Inconsistent data capture from conferences/meetings; weak source attribution; limited visibility for forecasting.
  • Former in-house HubSpot owner left; we need an embedded expert/agency to steady the system and upskill us.
What We’re Looking For (Scope)
  1. Audit & Action Plan (phase 0): Quick technical/operational audit with a prioritized roadmap and effort estimate.
  2. Email Deliverability Recovery: Repair domain reputation (e.g., alias domains + warming tool), DNS auth (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), list health, segmentation, and sending best practices—so monthly nurture resumes.
  3. CRM Simplification & Governance: Streamline pipelines and deal cards (reduce required fields), standardize contact/account fields, list-upload rules, and create admin guardrails.
  4. Adoption & Training: Role-based training (BD, marketing, “doer-seller” teams), playbooks, and short Looms; show-and-do enablement.
  5. Attribution & Activity Capture: Improve meeting/note capture (Fathom already used; open to lightweight voice/capture tools), conference lead workflows, and reliable source attribution.
  6. Reporting & Forecasting: Clean dashboards for pipeline health and stage-based probability forecasting; executive roll-ups.
  7. Documentation & Handoff: Admin guide, governance doc, field dictionary, and 30/60-day operating checklist.
Deliverables
  • Audit + remediation plan; DNS/auth + warming implemented; cleaned/segmented send lists.
  • Simplified pipelines/deal cards and governed list-upload process.
  • Adoption toolkit: playbooks, role trainings, and recorded how-tos.
  • Standard attribution model + dashboards for pipeline/forecast accuracy.
  • Admin documentation and ongoing recommendations.
Success Looks Like
  • Marketing domain warmed and campaigns relaunching with healthy metrics.
  • BD and acquired team actively using HubSpot (measurable weekly activity uplift).
  • Fewer fields, faster data entry, cleaner data; clear visibility into pipeline and forecast.
  • Team can run the playbook without external help.
Engagement Model
  • Start ASAP with a paid audit, then execute in phases (email → CRM simplification → adoption/reporting).
  • Open to a vetted solo consultant or small agency; we value hands-on execution + teach-us-to-fish enablement.
  • Indicative budget: $5k–$15k depending on depth; begin with the audit and expand based on outcomes.
Key Tools/Context
  • HubSpot (Marketing & Sales), Fathom for meeting notes; considering simple voice/capture for in-person meetings.
  • Must keep all comms/activities tracked in HubSpot.
proposal_description:
Hi there,
Thanks for the thoughtful post — this is exactly the kind of multi-phase stabilization and enablement work we specialize in.

We’ve supported several teams through email deliverability recovery, CRM cleanup (especially post-acquisition), and HubSpot re-implementation efforts focused on long-term scalability and internal enablement. Your goals are well aligned with our approach: hands-on execution paired with structured training so your team can confidently run things without ongoing external support.

One quick note on email repair
This isn’t a quick fix. Effective deliverability recovery takes patience, consistency, and discipline. The most important success factor is sending only to actively engaged, permission-based lists — and sticking to that. We’ll help you implement warm-up tactics, list segmentation, and DNS authentication, but building trust again with inbox providers is a process that requires focus and time.

How we typically structure this:
Phase 0: Paid Audit & Action Plan ($1,500 fixed rate) Quick-turn review of your current setup with a prioritized remediation roadmap and effort estimate.

Phase 1: Email Infrastructure & Deliverability Repair SPF/DKIM/DMARC setup, segmentation, sender reputation reset strategy, list hygiene.

Phase 2: CRM Simplification Pipeline and deal card cleanup, property governance, import rules, admin guardrails.

Phase 3: Attribution & Forecasting Lead capture, source attribution, and dashboard setup for accurate pipeline tracking.

Phase 4: Training & Adoption Role-based training, playbooks, recorded Looms, and admin documentation.

 We generally scope this as a 3-month project:

Month 1: Audit and foundational fixes
Month 2: System rebuilds and early adoption
Month 3: Training, optimization, and handoff

Happy to start with the audit and build from there. If you’d like to talk it through, you can book a call here:  https://www.eternalworks.com/meetings/timjones/discovery-call 

Looking forward to hearing more.

We need to have the integration done with our HubSpot so that every person on a Zoom or Teams, has a HubSpot contact created and the AI notes from the meeting are attached to the contact in HubSpot automatically.
proposal_description:
Hey how's it going? The technical integration you're describing is straightforward – we can absolutely connect Firefly to HubSpot for automatic contact creation and note attachment. But here's what I've learned from similar projects: the integration is the easy part. The hard part is making sure those notes actually get used.

I worked with a services company last quarter that had a perfect AI notetaker → HubSpot setup. Six months later, they had 400+ meeting notes sitting in contact records that nobody ever referenced. Their AEs still asked customers to repeat themselves, deals stalled because handoff context was lost, and managers couldn't coach effectively because they weren't reviewing call insights. The integration worked perfectly – the workflow didn't.

Before we jump into the technical setup, a few questions would help me understand if you need just the integration or a more complete solution:

1. What business outcome drove the decision to buy Firefly in the first place? (Better client handoffs, faster onboarding, coaching sales reps, something else?)

2. Right now, when someone has a meeting and Firefly captures notes, what happens next? Who's supposed to do what with that information?

3. Once these notes are in HubSpot, what will trigger someone on your team to actually open and use them? (I'm asking because "it's in the system" rarely equals "it gets used.")

The reason I'm asking: we can deliver the technical integration in your budget range, but if you want the notes to actually drive behavior change (better follow-ups, improved handoffs, data-driven coaching), that's a slightly different scope – and worth discussing upfront.

Want to jump on a quick 15-minute call to clarify what success really looks like here? I can map out whether you need just the integration or the full workflow design.

job_title: Fix HubSpot Tracking and Attribution for Webinar Landing Pages (Demio Form Integration)
job_description:
Background:
Our law firm hosts weekly educational webinars using Demio as our webinar platform. Registrations for these webinars are collected via landing pages that include an embedded Demio registration form (iframe). We promote these webinars through paid ads, directing leads to the landing page.
Currently, contact records created from these webinar registrations in HubSpot are missing key attribution data — specifically: Original Source Drill-Down 1, Original Source Drill-Down 2
This data loss prevents us from accurately tracking the effectiveness of our marketing campaigns and understanding the true source of leads.

Problem Statement:
The issue appears to stem from the Demio form being embedded via an iframe, which likely blocks HubSpot’s tracking scripts from capturing visitor and UTM parameters. As a result, HubSpot attributes these contacts incorrectly (or not at all) when they sync through the Demio integration.

Goal:
Ensure that all lead tracking and attribution data (UTMs, Original Source, Drill-Downs, etc.) flows correctly into HubSpot when visitors register for webinars, allowing accurate campaign performance reporting.
Additionally, ensure that the number of contacts coming from our paid (“money”) ads are all properly accounted for, so we can measure our ad performance more effectively and accurately.
proposal_description:
Hey there, I’d handle this by passing UTMs and the HubSpot cookie from your landing page into the Demio iframe, then syncing registrations through the HubSpot Forms API with the right context fields so Original Source and Drill-Downs populate correctly. That ensures every webinar signup carries full attribution into HubSpot for clean campaign and ad performance reporting. 

I’ve done similar setups for clients using iframe-based forms, and this type of setup restores accurate tracking without changing your current workflow. Happy to walk you through the implementation or just handle the full setup end to end.

job_title: Hubspot dashboard set-up
job_description: Trying to build dashboards for individual users to manage their own activities as well as provide a high level view for myself as the manager.
proposal_description:
Hi there,

I know exactly what this feels like. You’re trying to give your team clarity in HubSpot dashboards that actually help reps manage their day while also needing a clean, trustworthy view for yourself as the manager. Instead, you end up with too many reports, inconsistent numbers, and dashboards that people either ignore or don’t fully trust. 

We’ve stepped into this situation many times before.

I’m Todd, a Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric, a HubSpot Elite Partner. We work with B2B teams who already have HubSpot in place, but need it simplified, aligned, and made usable again especially around activity tracking, pipeline visibility, and leadership reporting.

A lot of our work is helping teams regain confidence in their dashboards.
For example, we helped a consulting firm replace weekly spreadsheet reporting with real-time, activity-driven dashboards inside HubSpot. Individual reps could finally see calls made, meetings booked, and next actions in one place, while managers had a roll-up view of performance without chasing updates. Adoption went up simply because the dashboards reflected reality.

How I’d approach your dashboard setup

Phase 0 - Discovery & Audit
We start by understanding how HubSpot is used today:
  • How individual users track activities and manage their day
  • Where reporting breaks down or feels inconsistent
  • What you, as the manager, actually need to see to run the team

Phase 1 - Core Dashboard Build
  • Action-focused dashboards for individual users (tasks, activities, deal next steps)
  • Management dashboards with clean roll-ups (activity trends, pipeline health, risks)
  • Standardized reports with consistent filters and logic so numbers mean the same thing everywhere

Phase 2 - Optimization & Enablement
  • Fine-tuning once dashboards are in real use
  • Short Loom walkthroughs so users understand why each dashboard exists
  • Light documentation to prevent future drift

A few questions to make sure this is done right
  1. Which roles need dashboards today reps, managers, leadership, or all three?
  2. Are activities and deal updates being logged consistently across the team?
  3. Do you want managers focused more on coaching visibility or performance tracking?
  4. Are custom properties or lifecycle stages already in place that affect reporting?
  5. What decisions should these dashboards support on a weekly basis?

Our goal isn’t just to build dashboards it’s to make HubSpot something your team actually relies on, without constant cleanup or confusion.

Happy to start with a short audit or walk you through similar before-and-after examples to show what “good” really looks like. Let's talk!

Best,
Todd

Project Objective:

We need a HubSpot expert to build an automated workflow for a Start Up Subscription Plans that supports multiple plan purchases by the same contact, allowing customers to purchase infinite plans (e.g., for different teams or departments) and seamlessly move through an onboarding process. The workflow should trigger on plan purchases, update deal properties (e.g., Company + Division), and handle both “Ready to Get Started” and “Not Ready” responses through form submissions. With the structure in place, I’ll implement workflows that:
  • Automatically move records from lead to deal status based on specific triggers
  • Assign owners or tasks based on lead type or pipeline stage

I’ll create custom reports and dashboards that provide visibility into:

We also require foundational reporting dashboards and training enablement material (such as Loom videos of the automation buildout) to support our team in adopting the enhanced setup.

Objective

The specialist will leverage workflow automations and sales sequences to guide sales members through a clearly defined customer journey—from initial prospect outreach through follow-up, pipeline progression, and ultimately to closed deal. This project aims to enhance operational efficiency, ensure consistent execution, and improve visibility into sales performance. 

Create a custom sales workflow leveraging Hubspot's workflow automations and sequences to guide a salesperson through the following workflows:

Initial Outreach to Newly Created Accounts
Notification/priority on newest accounts
  • Round robin assignment 
  • Defined Lifecycle Stages 
  • Task automation based on call counts on a contact 
  • Associated email sequences
  • Reporting and visibility for sales management 

Lead Nurturing Based on New Account Spend
  • Email sequences based on account revenue (will need API integration from our internal app to receive this data)
  • Task automation based on account activity 
  • Build out of new fields to track contact’s time within a given Lifecycle stage 

Reactivation Flow (Marketing Hub Automation)
  • Review our current Reactivation Email Automation to ensure it’s setup optimally and doesn’t interfere with the newly created workflows mentioned above.

Reporting & Dashboards
  • Performance Metrics Per Sales Team Member
  • Contact Counts per Lifecycle stage
  • Avg days to Close 
  • Avg speed to lead 
  • Avg touchpoints before a prospect responds 
  • Revenue opportunity per stage

Access & Support
You’ll have full access to our Hubspot and context to the project. The EVP Of Sales will be your main point of contact with support from a Project Manager and Developer. 

About Us
NextGen Leads is a leading provider of Health Insurance leads with a focus on lead quality and customer support.

We’ve stepped into this situation many times before at OneMetric. As a HubSpot Elite Partner, we’re often brought in after a company buys Enterprise with the intention of elevating their sales operations but quickly realizes the power is locked behind architecture, automation, and adoption. Your need for clean workflows, lifecycle governance, API-fed data, and sales reporting is exactly the sort of system stabilization we handle every week.

Here’s how I would approach your engagement in a structured, consultative way:

Phase 0 — Discovery & Blueprint
  • Review your visual workflow map and translate it into a HubSpot architecture blueprint.
  • Audit your current HubSpot setup (objects, properties, lifecycle definitions, task queues, sequences, existing automation).
  • Clarify what data will flow in from your internal app and define how revenue/spend will trigger nurturing.

Phase 1 — Buildout of Core Sales Workflows
Initial Outreach Workflow
  • Round robin assignment config, prioritization of new accounts, and lifecycle stage enforcement.

  • Embedded sequences for email outreach and follow-up.
Lead Nurturing Based on New Account Spend

Phase 2 — Reporting, Dashboards & Enablement
  • Dashboards for lifecycle velocity, rep performance, speed-to-lead, touchpoint analysis, and stage-based revenue opportunity.

Thanks for sharing your project. A subscription launch is an exciting moment, and setting up the right onboarding, retention, and winback workflows is one of the best ways to create a strong, automated customer experience right from day one. We would be happy to help you get this in place. 

You mentioned that you have the strategy outlined but want an expert to implement the automation in HubSpot. That is exactly where our team comes in. We are a HubSpot Solutions Partner with certified specialists in workflow automation, lifecycle journeys, and multi-channel communication. 

What We Can Help You Build 

Based on your goals, here is what the engagement would include: 
  • Mapping workflow logic for onboarding, retention, and winback 
  • Building automated email and SMS sequences 
  • Creating lists and enrollment conditions for subscriber status 
  • Ensuring automations support your subscription lifecycle events 
  • Testing to confirm each path works as expected 

How This Fits Your Budget 

Your target budget is under $1,000. For full onboarding, retention, and winback automation, the scope typically runs higher. To keep things efficient and aligned with your target, here are two options:

Option 1: Phase 1 Quick Build ($1,050)
  • One onboarding workflow
  • One retention or winback workflow
  • Up to 3 to 4 emails or SMS messages total
  • Core list and enrollment logic

This keeps the work lean while giving you the automation needed to launch confidently.

Option 2: Full Lifecycle Automation (quoted after discovery) 
If you prefer the full three workflow system with more messaging and branching logic, we can map that out once we review your subscription platform setup and integration. We anticipate the total cost would be between $1,500 to $3,750.

A Few Details We Will Confirm 

Some of the answers below can significantly impact the scope and cost. These details help us determine the right workflow structure, the number of hours required, and whether any integration or copywriting work is needed.

  1. Which subscription platform are you launching, and how does it pass subscriber events into HubSpot?
    If the platform already sends clean events into HubSpot, workflows stay simple. If not, we may need custom properties, integration setup, or logic adjustments, which increase the scope.

  2. Are you already set up with an SMS provider inside HubSpot?
    If SMS is already configured, we can quickly plug it into the workflows. If setup or integration is still needed, that adds time.

  3. Are you supplying the email and SMS content, or would you like support with copy?
    Using your finalized content keeps the project inside the lower budget range. If you need us to write messaging, the effort increases.

  4. What HubSpot tier are you on?
    Certain automation features are only available in specific tiers. This can affect how complex the workflows can be and whether we need alternate methods to achieve the same behavior.

  5. What needs to happen inside HubSpot when someone signs up, cancels, or becomes inactive?
    Simple triggers fit within your budget. More advanced logic, branching, reactivation paths, or multiple lifecycle states may require additional workflows and, therefore, more hours.

Why Teams Work With Us 

We are a HubSpot Solutions Partner with certified expertise across Marketing Hub, workflows, and customer journey automation. Our team frequently supports workflow-driven launch projects like this and helps clients build systems that are simple to manage and easy to scale as the subscription grows.

job_title: Custom Card Development
job_description:
We are looking to develop a custom integration card that connects PandaDoc with a custom object in HubSpot. This integration should enable seamless synchronization between the two platforms, ensuring efficient document handling and status tracking.
Key requirements for the integration include:
  1. Property Synchronization
    The integration must automatically sync custom properties from the specified HubSpot custom object to a pre-defined PandaDoc template. This ensures that all relevant data is accurately reflected in the generated document without manual input.
  2. Status Updates Based on Approval Workflow
    The system should monitor the approval status of the PandaDoc document. Based on this status (e.g., sent, viewed, approved, declined), the integration must update the corresponding pipeline stage of the custom object in HubSpot to reflect the current progress.
  3. PDF Generation and Storage
    Once the document reaches its final state (e.g., approved or completed), a PDF version should be generated and automatically stored within the associated custom object record in HubSpot for reference and recordkeeping.
The end goal is a fully integrated and automated workflow between PandaDoc and HubSpot that minimizes manual data entry, maintains up-to-date pipeline statuses, and ensures easy access to finalized documents.

job_title: Speed to Lead Calculation
job_description:
I am looking to measure speed to lead.

Some leads are autoamatically generated in our pipeline, AB carts from Shopify, form filled from website

Looking to calculate average time spent in first stage of pipeline

Looking to calculate time till first phone call made


This from HS customer Support.

Hi Todd!

I triple checked our resources and verified the available info with our team. I confirmed that this is indeed a Product Limitation, and currently, the Time Between Calculation properties will consider all calendar dates. It is not possible to exclude weekends from this default calculation at this time.

I tried to look for workarounds, and so far, the other alternative is to use custom code to achieve that goal. Either via the API, custom integration, or custom code workflow action (Ops Hub Pro+), with the assistance of a developer, you would have to get the properties "Date entered [stage]" and "Date exited [stage]" to make the calculation and use the code to exclude the weekends, then populate the result into another custom date property. In case you're interested in this approach, you may get assistance from one of our Solutions Partners or Profoundly Pros.

I understand that this is not the expected answer, but I hope it provides clarification for the current limitations of the tool. As per my scope, I'll submit this feedback internally and submit a roadblock for your account, so a feature like this gets consideration from our Product Team.

Kind regards,

We use the deal records as the "lead record"

-Shopify
-Netsuite
-Make.com

proposal_description:
Custom HubSpot Theme Development Proposal 


Executive Summary 

REMA TIP TOP’s digital presence relies on delivering a consistent, high-quality experience across all platforms. While the main website is hosted on WordPress, HubSpot is the hub for landing pages, blogs, and campaigns. To ensure a seamless brand experience, we propose creating a fully custom HubSpot theme that mirrors the design, branding, and functionality of the existing WordPress site (staging link provided). 
As an experienced HubSpot developer, I specialize in creating responsive, easy-to-manage HubSpot themes that align perfectly with brand guidelines. This project will deliver a flexible, scalable, and user-friendly theme designed to match your WordPress site while giving your team complete control over content creation without heavy coding requirements. 


Key Objectives 

  1. Brand Consistency: Match the visual design, typography, and color palette of the WordPress site to ensure a seamless visitor experience across platforms. 
  2. Responsive & Mobile-Friendly: Optimize all templates and modules for performance on any device. 
  3. Flexible Content Management: Build customizable HubSpot modules for landing pages, website pages, and blogs, enabling quick updates. 
  4. Reusable Templates: Provide pre-built templates for landing pages, blog listings, and single blog posts. 
  5. Ease of Use: Ensure marketing teams can create and update content without technical support. 


Project Plan of Action 

Part 1: Discovery and Scoping 
  • Review your WordPress staging site for design elements, layouts, and branding standards. 
  • Identify required modules and templates for HubSpot (landing pages, blogs, headers, footers, etc.). 
  • Confirm functionality requirements, including CTAs, forms, and blog configurations. 

Part 2: Design & Development 
  • Develop a custom HubSpot theme to match the WordPress staging site. 
  • Build responsive templates for landing pages, website pages, and blogs. 
  • Create reusable modules for text, images, sliders, CTAs, and other content types. 
  • Implement global header and footer for consistent navigation. 

Part 3: Implementation & Training 
  • Test theme across devices and browsers for responsiveness and compatibility. 
  • Provide video walkthroughs for your team.

proposal_description:

  • Applying your brand tokens, fonts, and colours across a solid base theme

  • One branded landing page build that includes all core modules so you can easily see how they work with your brand
  • A test blog setup (listing and single post) aligned to your site’s style
  • Ensuring responsiveness and editor-friendly controls

Advanced animations, complex custom modules, HubDB or API integrations, content migration, multi-language setup, and extensive QA. These can be added later if needed.

Approximately one week from receiving brand assets and HubSpot access.

job_description:
I just booked time with you today at 3:00 to discuss HubSpot and how we can implement the new Loop Playbook. Before we connect, I wanted to give you a little context on who we are and what we’re looking for:
About Us
We’re a family-owned winery established in 1981, growing and producing wines and selling 100% direct-to-consumer (B2C). Today we produce 23 wines plus 4 grower champagnes (soon expanding to 12). Our team is 23 employees total:
  • Vineyard: 8
  • Winery: 6
  • Sales/Tasting Room: 3
  • Reservations/Logistics: 2 (currently using a customized HubSpot setup)
  • Admin/Marketing: 2
We also have strong brand guidelines and a compelling marketing story, though we haven’t been consistent in social media or digital execution.
Sources of Revenue
  • Wine Clubs (largest driver)
    • Society of Smugglers (3x/year, 6 unique wines)
    • Winesteins (2x/year, Rocket Science label)
    • Ballers Society (2x/year, signature high-tier wines)
    • Champagne Club (launching soon, expanding our grower champagne program)
  • Tasting Room Sales
  • JFC on the Road – bringing the experience directly to clients with a 20-case minimum expectation
  • Barrel Program – premium membership requiring a ~24-case commitment ($34K)
  • Online Sales – present, but not yet a major revenue driver
Current Tools & Operations
We use:
  • Ecommerce & Clubs: Shopify, Stripe, Bloom (4 wine clubs), Sovos (compliance)
  • Content & Storage: Dropbox, Experience App, Paperless Post
  • Communication: Zoom (phones), Red Chirp (text), Read.ai (for meeting insights) – exploring Slack
  • Events & Community: Wine events via Experience App, considering Reddit wine community
We need more cohesive reporting on club performance (signups, holds, skips, upsells).
Current HubSpot Setup
  • Content Hub Professional (3 core seats + 10,000 additional marketing contacts, 1 brand, 100 embeddable videos)
  • Marketing Hub Enterprise (5 core seats + 10,000 contacts)
  • Considering Data Hub (new Operations Hub)
  • Plan to cancel Breeze (too overwhelming and not ideal for B2C)
  • Exploring Wistia, Canva, and better integration for video/content
Our HubSpot renewal is October 8.
What We’re Looking For (Prioritized)
  1. Content & Storytelling Expansion
    • Implement more video content, leveraging my husband as a rich source for living history of Napa Valley wine
    • Repurpose content across multiple platforms (currently only Instagram + Facebook; interest in TikTok, LinkedIn, others)
    • Build processes to consistently showcase our brand voice, story, and customer advocacy (“raving fans”)
  2. Data Foundation & Loop Playbook
    • Begin with data cleanup and consolidation across spreadsheets, tools, and systems
    • Use HubSpot to better pull reports from our client and staff communications, helping us see where we’re doing well and where we need improvement
    • Centralize years of contacts and data into one place, as mentioned at INBOUND with their Data Hub or am I able to do that in a service I already have?
    • We know we have a lot of cleanup to do and are excited to hear your proposal on how to get us streamlined and moving in the right direction
    • Streamline reporting on wine club performance (signups, holds, skips, upsells)
    • Make surea HubSpot is configured to support both B2C and premium programs (e.g., JFC on the Road, Barrel Program)
  3. Discoverability & Digital Growth
    • Shift from SEO-only focus to AI-driven discoverability (priority for us to be found in AI summaries/search)
    • Expand online sales, which are currently minimal but represent a growth opportunity
  4. Operational Efficiency
    • Increase efficiency across our lean team by better using HubSpot and integrations
    • Replace or optimize tools where needed (ex: Breeze, Slack, video hosting platforms)
Renewal Priorities
With our renewal coming up October 8, we’d like your help to audit what we’re paying for and determine what’s truly needed now versus what can wait until we’re ready to fully leverage it.
For example, we signed up with Breeze but never used it. I’d like to understand the steps needed to pause or remove that service until the timing is right. Having your guidance on prioritization will help us make the most of HubSpot while avoiding being overwhelmed. Do you happen to have workflows of each of the services or know who I need to connect with to provide them for me?
We’re excited to dive in with you and get a clear plan in place.
Joy

proposal_description:
Hey,

I really liked how clearly you described the current state. It’s a familiar crossroads for growing teams on HubSpot.
From what I understand, you’ve got two business lines (Advisory & Platform), multiple customer states (Live, Churned, Prospect), and shared Accounts, but HubSpot isn’t yet structured to mirror that complexity.

What usually happens in setups like this is:

  • Data and ownership get split between Sales, CS, and Marketing.
  • Teams lose visibility into “who’s owning what” at the Account level.
  • Renewal or upsell opportunities fall through the cracks because there’s no single view of relationship health.

The real fix here isn’t just cleaning data, it’s building HubSpot around your business model, not the other way around.

Here’s how I’d approach it, if we work together:

  1. Discovery + Blueprint 

    • Map how Accounts, Contacts, and Deals interact across both business lines.
    • Redefine the Account architecture to support dual lifecycles (Advisory & Platform).
    • Identify key touchpoints for BD, onboarding, and renewal handoffs.
    • Build the foundation for your “360° Account view”, where every stakeholder, service pitched, and last interaction is visible in one place.

  2. Implementation Roadmap

    • Create a structured system for your Advisory BD team to add new Contacts & Accounts easily.
    • Automate newsletter and webinar sign-ups to flow into HubSpot with proper segmentation.
    • Configure the HubSpot email plug-ins so BD conversations are tracked automatically.
    • Set up Customer Success workflows for renewals, retention, and churn analysis.

I can already see a clear end-state here: A system where your team can open any Account and instantly know who they are, what they’ve bought, what stage they’re in, and when they were last engaged.

That’s when HubSpot truly becomes a revenue command center, not just a CRM.

 I just want to quickly check a few things.

  1. Is your current HubSpot plan Starter or Professional?
  2. Do you have both business lines using the same portal right now?
  3. Are there any must-have integrations (like Typeform or your SaaS platform)?
Once I know that, I can outline the exact blueprint flow we’d run and give you a cost range for Phase 1.

By way of background, I’m Nishant from OneMetric, a HubSpot Elite Partner agency that helps SaaS and service businesses unify their sales, CS, and marketing inside HubSpot.
We’ve done similar dual-model builds for SaaS-plus-consulting companies, where the goal was to unify their entire customer lifecycle in one clean structure.

If this sounds aligned, I’d love to schedule a short chat this week, even 20 minutes to walk you through how we’d architect this with your team. Here's my meeting Link - https://meetings.hubspot.com/nishant26

Cheers!

Nishant

Objective
To engage a certified HubSpot Expert or Agency Partner to audit, optimize, and evolve our HubSpot instance to fully support a unified, data-driven customer journey — from pre-lead awareness through client renewals.

job_title: HubSpot Implementation & Support Specialist – Ongoing Project-Based Role
job_description:
At Inovautus Consulting, we help accounting and consulting firms implement and optimize HubSpot Professional-tier Marketing, Sales, and CRM Hubs. We’re looking for a skilled and dependable HubSpot professional for 3–10 hours of ongoing work per week, with the potential to expand as our firm grows.

As our consulting work continues to grow, we’re reaching the limits of our internal capacity to manage multiple HubSpot implementation projects and ongoing client support.

Our lead consultant handles strategy, discovery, and some client communication — but we need a capable professional to help turn those plans into action. With several clients in different stages of onboarding and implementation, the work involves juggling multiple HubSpot environments, ensuring deliverables stay on track, and maintaining consistent progress across accounts.
This role exists to help us bridge that gap — bringing structure, execution, and follow-through to our HubSpot projects. 

What You’ll Work On
You’ll support both HubSpot implementation and ongoing client account management through a mix of strategic setup and tactical execution. Responsibilities include:
  • Collaborating with our lead consultant to map and manage implementation plans
  • Providing project management support—tracking tasks, managing timelines, and ensuring deliverables are met
  • Customizing pipelines, deal stages, and properties for both Marketing and Sales Hubs
  • Designing and maintaining automated workflows and lead scoring models
  • Managing CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) features within HubSpot to streamline proposal, invoicing, and payment workflows
  • Supporting ecommerce and payment integrations in HubSpot (e.g., configuring payment links, quotes, and integrations with accounting systems)
  • Importing and managing contact, company, and deal data
  • Building dashboards and reports to support financial, sales, and marketing  visibility
  • Setting up and maintaining brand kits, templates, and branded design assets in HubSpot
  • Reviewing existing client accounts for workflow or data issues and recommending solutions
  • Handling HubSpot support tickets and requests for client accounts
  • Monitoring HubSpot feature releases and identifying opportunities to enhance client accounts
Bonus points if you have basic development experience for landing page setup, module customization, or styling.
Our HubSpot Environment
We primarily support accounting and consulting firms using:
  • HubSpot CRM (core contact, company, and deal management)
  • Marketing Hub Professional (email marketing, forms, workflows, CTAs, landing pages)
  • Sales Hub Professional (pipelines, automation, quotes, payments, and reporting)
  • HubSpot CPQ and Payments (proposal-to-invoice automations and payment workflows)
You’ll help ensure these systems work seamlessly together to support both internal and client-facing business processes.

About You
You’re organized, reliable, and comfortable managing multiple projects. You take initiative, communicate clearly, and can confidently move work forward from direction or recordings. You’re experienced in HubSpot and familiar with its tools across CRM, Marketing, Sales, and Operations.
You’re comfortable on client calls, reviewing notes and recordings, and translating those into actionable plans that you can execute independently. Ideally, you hold HubSpot certifications in areas such as Sales Software, Marketing Software, Reporting, Implementation, or CPQ.
proposal_description:

I have a marketing background, so I can be equally strategic as I am with implementation. In addition, I worked across many of the HubSpot hubs, ensuring that as a person moves through the sales process, they are getting the right communications in the right format, with relevant and timely information to continue moving them forward.

Implementation and ongoing HubSpot support is the exact service I provide to majority of my clients. I value learning about how you or your clients need HubSpot to work, and making it happen. I primarily work independently and support multiple clients in a white-label fashion, so your clients see me as part of your team when they interact with me. I have availability to take on up to 10 additional hours of work per week.

  • HubSpot Implementation - I have helped many clients set up their instance of HubSpot. From ensuring settings are updated, to pipeline creation and automation, to designing marketing templates, and more.
  • Ongoing HubSpot Support - I regularly manage multiple HubSpot portals at various phases of usage, from early setup to messy portals that need cleanup, to regular support.
  • Project Management - I support clients with ensuring that specialists are completing their work, meeting deadlines, and have the information they need to work independently. I am very task-oriented and deadline-driven.

I would be happy to work with you in an hourly capacity. With 3-10 hours of work per week, I would expect the monthly cost to land somewhere between $1000 to $3200 depending on how much support you need from me. job_title: Hubspot Account-Based Marketing Campaign Workflows Setup
job_description:
Need help developing and setting up (in Hubspot) automation workflows for a B2B ABM campaign. We have defined buyer personas and content to get started, but we need an expert in workflow development and setup to optimize the campaign to the full extent. 

Would prefer someone located in Eastern or Central U.S. time zones to provide easy connections with our team.
proposal_description:
Hey,

Thanks for posting this job! I took a look at the brief, and it’s clear you’ve already built the foundation of a strong ABM motion: defined personas, clear content, and intent to scale through HubSpot. Where most teams get stuck is turning that strategy into automated, measurable engagement across accounts and personas; that’s where we specialize.

Here’s how we usually approach this:

1. Discovery & Alignment
We’ll start by understanding how your personas interact with your content and sales touchpoints. The goal is to identify what engagement signals actually matter (page visits, webinar signups, ad clicks, etc.) so we can automate follow-ups and alerts around those high-intent actions — not just generic “opens or clicks.”

2. Workflow Design & Automation
 We’ll build smart workflows that do the heavy lifting for your team:
 • Persona-driven nurtures that adapt based on role and funnel stage, so messaging feels personal, not templated.
 • Account-level scoring combining firmographics and behavior, so your SDRs know which accounts are warming up and why.
 • Slack or email notifications when key personas engage, allowing sales to strike while interest is highest.
 • Lifecycle automation that moves contacts through MQL, SQL, and Opportunity stages with clear, measurable criteria, so there are no manual updates.

3. Reporting & Optimization
We’ll build ABM dashboards that give you a 360° view, which accounts are engaging, what content drives conversions, and where leads are stalling. This allows your marketing and sales teams to collaborate with data instead of assumptions.

I just have a few questions to help us tailor this properly:

 • Are you already using HubSpot’s ABM features (Target Accounts, Buying Roles), or would you like us to structure that from scratch?
 • How do you currently identify “engaged accounts”? Is it via activity in HubSpot, ad interactions, or CRM updates?
 • Do SDRs/AEs handle follow-ups directly in HubSpot, or through external tools like Salesloft or Apollo?

In terms of coverage, we’re available daily until 2 PM EST and coordinate with U.S. teams heavily via Slack and Microsoft Teams for quick turnarounds, daily syncs, and live workflow reviews, so collaboration stays tight and real-time.
At the end, our goal is simple: make sure every buyer persona and target account experiences a connected, data-driven journey and your sales team has clear visibility into who to talk to, when, and why.

I lead HubSpot programs at OneMetric, a HubSpot Elite Partner agency that helps B2B teams operationalize ABM and RevOps inside HubSpot. We’ve helped multiple companies like Syncron, Akraya, and Allied Revenue build persona-based automations, scoring logic, and cross-team visibility that directly improve pipeline velocity.
Feel free to go through a couple of our case studies - https://www.onemetric.io/services/demand-generation/account-based-marketing

Would love to set up a short call to align on your campaign goals and share how we’d structure this within your HubSpot instance. Here's my meeting link - https://meetings.hubspot.com/nishant26

Best,

Nishant

job_title: HubSpot CRM Setup for Irish Accounting Practice
job_description: Looking for assistance with set up. I have a small accounting practice in Ireland and wish to use a new CRM system to integrate outlook/teams/whatsapp/meeting notes etc into one streamlined platform. I like the look of HubSpot but would like assistance with set up etc.
proposal_description:
Hi,

I can totally relate to what you’re describing.
Running an accounting practice often means you’re buried under messages, client emails, Teams calls, and random WhatsApp notes, all scattered across different places. It works when things are quiet, but once client volume picks up, it’s chaos trying to remember who said what and where.

That’s exactly where HubSpot fits in beautifully for accountants.
It can become your single workspace, where every client email from Outlook, Teams meeting, or even WhatsApp chat is logged automatically. Before replying to anyone, you’ll see their entire conversation history and all open items in one place.

Here’s how that could look for your practice:

  • Outlook sync → Every client email gets saved under the right record automatically.

  • Teams & meetings → Notes and recordings are stored right inside HubSpot.

  • WhatsApp → We can connect it using tools like Make or Twilio so even client chats don’t get lost.

  • Client pipelines → Track each engagement (bookkeeping, audit, VAT filing) through clear stages — no more guessing who’s due for what.

job_title: HubSpot Campaign Standardization, Segmentation, and Workflow Optimization
job_description:
Penn Foster is seeking an experienced HubSpot expert to help us bring order, scalability, and efficiency to our marketing operations. We currently manage multiple channels in HubSpot (Enterprise, Partner, and Inside Sales) with over 200,000 contacts—many of which are not yet properly segmented by channel. Our Salesforce integration is active, but some logic and data flow issues remain.

Our goal is to standardize campaign setup, segmentation, and workflows so that our internal teams can operate more efficiently and confidently within HubSpot.

  • > Deep experience in HubSpot Marketing Hub and Salesforce integration
  • > Proven track record in data hygiene, workflow optimization, and campaign automation
  • > Strong understanding of segmentation strategy, lead routing, and attribution modeling
  • > Ability to design repeatable campaign frameworks and templates

What Success Looks Like
  • > Teams can quickly and confidently launch campaigns using standardized templates
  • > Segmentation is consistent and channel assignment is reliable

  • > Data is clean, complete, and accurate across HubSpot and Salesforce
  • > Clear documentation and best practices empower internal teams to self-serve

job_title: Fix HubSpot Tracking and Attribution for Webinar Landing Pages (Demio Form Integration)
job_description:
Background:
Our law firm hosts weekly educational webinars using Demio as our webinar platform. Registrations for these webinars are collected via landing pages that include an embedded Demio registration form (iframe). We promote these webinars through paid ads, directing leads to the landing page.
Currently, contact records created from these webinar registrations in HubSpot are missing key attribution data — specifically: Original Source Drill-Down 1, Original Source Drill-Down 2
This data loss prevents us from accurately tracking the effectiveness of our marketing campaigns and understanding the true source of leads.

Problem Statement:
The issue appears to stem from the Demio form being embedded via an iframe, which likely blocks HubSpot’s tracking scripts from capturing visitor and UTM parameters. As a result, HubSpot attributes these contacts incorrectly (or not at all) when they sync through the Demio integration.

Goal:
Ensure that all lead tracking and attribution data (UTMs, Original Source, Drill-Downs, etc.) flows correctly into HubSpot when visitors register for webinars, allowing accurate campaign performance reporting.
Additionally, ensure that the number of contacts coming from our paid (“money”) ads are all properly accounted for, so we can measure our ad performance more effectively and accurately.

job_description:

job_title: HubSpot Salesforce Selective Sync Integration Expertise Needed for an Existing Integration. Updating Contact Account Relationship in Salesforce
job_description:
Hello, 

We are looking for a HubSpot agency with deep Salesforce integration experience. 

Currently in Salesforce our contact records are related to one account record and our existing business practice creates duplicate contacts when employees move throughout the industry. The HubSpot integration is currently configured with this contact model under a selective sync with Salesforce sharing rules and permission restrictions. 

We are actively evaluating shifting our Salesforce data structure to change the relationship between the contact and account object. 
- Current Relationship: 1 contact to 1 account 
- New Relationship: 1 contact to many accounts - with employment dates and multiple email address. Some contacts will be actively employed at multiple companies and some will have only 1 active account. 

This restructure would allow us to improve data quality and reporting capabilities in Salesforce. However, we need to understand what impact this would have in HubSpot. We will be reconciling years of duplicate contact in Salesforce, cleaning up our data and this will impact the integration to HubSpot. 

We're looking for an expert partner agency that will: 
1. Consult on the impact to HubSpot for this data shift - what needs to be done to any integration settings configured in Salesforce or HubSpot 
  • 2. Walk our HubSpot Administrator/Salesforce administrator through any updates needed
  • 3. Explain additional impacts to reporting or personalization such as updates that will be needed to workflows or personalization (Smart content) if a contact has multiple account relationships instead of 1. 
  • 4. Potentially join a meeting or 2 with our Salesforce consulting agency to ensure the systems are aligned based on expected updates and timelines for project changes. 

HubSpot Hub - Marketing Enterprise with one brand. 

Our goal is to determine the impact this project would have to determine best next steps as well as required budget. We want to have a better idea of the scope for this project over the next few weeks to have it ready for either December or 2026.
proposal_description:
Hey there,

The situation you’re describing is something I’ve stepped into many times before especially the combination of data clutter, duplicate contacts, inconsistent reporting, and the added complexity of a HubSpot ↔ Salesforce integration that was built around an old data model. When teams start feeling like HubSpot is becoming more of a chore than a tool, it usually signals that the underlying object structure or sync rules have drifted from how the business now operates.

At OneMetric (HubSpot Elite Partner), we’re often the team brought in to steady the system when orgs are planning structural CRM changes or trying to predict the HubSpot impact of a Salesforce schema overhaul. Recently, we helped:

 • A strategic integration of HubSpot and Salesforce to streamline operations, enhance lead nurturing, and increased sales productivity 
https://www.onemetric.io/resources/case-study/financial-advisor?t=1763356205770

So the situation you’re in is very familiar and solvable with the right planning.

How I’d approach this project

Phase 0 — Integration Blueprint & Impact Assessment
This is where we map the ripple effects of your proposed Salesforce change.
 • Evaluate your current selective sync, permission sets, and HubSpot-side integration settings.
 • Model what a “multi-account” contact looks like from HubSpot’s perspective (since HubSpot doesn’t support native multi-account associations the same way Salesforce does).
 • Identify what breaks: personalization tokens, workflows referencing account fields, lifecycle mappings, reporting assets, and any smart content rules driven by company attributes.
 • Build a technical brief outlining required adjustments, risks, and sequencing.

Phase 1 — Configuration Updates & Admin Support
Here we guide your Salesforce and HubSpot admins through the actual changes:
 • Reconfigure sync rules to support the new relationship structure (including custom objects if needed for employment history).
 • Adjust duplicate management, de-duplication sequencing, and record reconciliation logic to prevent HubSpot from re-creating or merging unintended records.
 • Update workflows, lead assignment rules, and any personalization logic referencing company-level data.
 • Validate engagement and subscription consistency as contacts consolidate.

Phase 2 — Reporting, Personalization & Governance
Once the core sync is stable:
 • Rebuild or adjust HubSpot dashboards that rely on account data, associated deals, or company properties.
 • Review smart content logic that assumes one company per contact redesign rules so they remain reliable even with multi-account contexts.
 • Build lightweight documentation and a rules-of-engagement guide so your team understands how contacts will behave post-migration.

A few key questions to get this scoped correctly:
  1. Are you planning to use Salesforce’s Account Contact Relationship object or a custom junction object to represent employment?
  2. How many duplicate contacts do you estimate will be reconciled, and do any contain critical subscription or compliance-based data in HubSpot?
  3. Beyond Marketing Hub Enterprise, are you using Sales or Service Hub features that depend on company data?
  4. Do you intend for HubSpot to be aware of all account relationships or only the primary/active one?
  5. Are you currently using smart content, dynamic personalization, or segmentation that relies on account-level fields?

If helpful, I’m happy to walk you through similar examples from past integrations or start with a short impact assessment call. The goal here is simple: Let's talk
 
Make sure this Salesforce evolution strengthens HubSpot not destabilizes it.

Looking forward to connecting,
 
Cheers!
Todd

At that point, HubSpot stops being a growth engine and starts feeling like a liability.

We’ve walked into this exact situation many times where the CRM looks full on the surface, but underneath it’s messy enough that reporting doesn’t add up, automation misfires, and teams quietly lose confidence in the data. The good news: this is completely fixable with the right cleanup approach and a disciplined data governance layer.

At OneMetric, we’re a HubSpot Elite Partner with extensive experience helping B2B companies clean and optimize large CRM databases. A great example is our GetixHealth case study: Revitalizing a 440K-Record CRM Database
In this project, we audited outdated imports, removed duplicate contacts, and standardized inconsistent field values. After the cleanup, the marketing and sales teams had a CRM they could trust, and within months, they saw a meaningful lift in qualified leads simply because the data foundation was fixed. 

Here’s how I’d recommend approaching your project:

Phase 0 - Discovery & Audit
• Audit your existing HubSpot contact database, identifying duplicate patterns, field inconsistencies, and enrichment gaps.
• Review your current ZoomInfo file to ensure it’s clean, mapped correctly, and ready for replacement.
• Define the “source of truth” rules ZoomInfo vs. historical HubSpot data.
• Assess workflow risks so nothing breaks when the new data comes in.

Phase 1 - Data Cleanup & Replacement
• Take a safe archive export of the existing database.
• Clean, normalize, and validate the ZoomInfo list (formatting, mapping, required fields).

• Replace outdated contacts with the sanitized ZoomInfo dataset in controlled batches.

• Set up governance rules to prevent future duplicates or bad imports.

job_title: Simple Hubspot CPQ Setup plus Xero Integration
job_description: We have Commerce Hub Pro setup. We currently have a quote template setup. We are looking to take payments (payments is setup) on a quote, create an invoice and sync invoice with Xero. Simple product book - 5 fixed price services and 3 subscriptions. Want to build workflow to ensure quote to invoice is automated.
proposal_description:
Hey there,

I know exactly what this situation feels like. You’ve invested in Commerce Hub Pro, quotes are set up, payments technically work but the moment you try to move from quote → payment → invoice → Xero, things start to feel more manual and fragile than they should be. When HubSpot becomes more of a checklist than a system you can trust, that’s usually a sign of process drift not a bad tool. 

We’ve stepped into this exact scenario many times before. The core pieces are there, but without a clean automation layer, teams end up issuing invoices twice, reconciling payments manually, or losing confidence that what’s in HubSpot actually matches Xero. 
I’m Todd, a Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric, a HubSpot Elite Partner. We’re often brought in to steady Commerce and RevOps systems that were partially implemented or set up without a tight operational blueprint especially when accounting integrations like Xero are involved. 

A couple relevant examples:
We bring QuickBooks and HubSpot together so everything just works. Your data, messages, and workflows stay in sync. And you get clarity and consistency without complexity.
-https://www.onemetric.io/integration/quickbooks-to-hubspot

How I’d approach your setup 

Phase 0 - Discovery & Blueprint
 Before touching workflows, I’d audit: 
  • Your current quote template and payment configuration
  • Product library structure (5 fixed services + 3 subscriptions)
  • How invoices are currently generated and passed to Xero
  • Where manual steps or edge cases exist

 Phase 1- Core Implementation & Automation
 This is where the real value happens:
 
  • Finalize product objects and billing terms for services vs subscriptions
  • Configure quote-based payments so payment status reliably triggers next steps
  • Build workflows to:
    • Generate invoices automatically from paid quotes
    • Sync invoices to Xero with the correct tax, line items, and customer mapping
  • Validate lifecycle and deal stage alignment so reporting stays consistent

 Phase 3- Optimization, QA & Enablement
 Once flow is live:
 
  • Test edge cases (partial payments, subscription renewals, failed payments)
  • Add guardrails and alerts so issues surface early
  • Document the system with short Loom walkthroughs so your team knows exactly how it works and how to troubleshoot

 A few questions to make sure we design this correctly 
  1. Are invoices currently created inside HubSpot, Xero, or both and which system do you want to be the source of truth?
  2. Do subscriptions renew automatically via Commerce Hub, or are renewals quote-driven?
  3. Are taxes handled in HubSpot, Xero, or split across both?
  4. Do you need invoices generated on payment received or on quote acceptance?

If this resonates, I’m happy to start with a quick 15-minute audit conversation or walk you through a similar before/after setup we’ve done for other B2B services teams. Let's talk!
 
Our goal isn’t just to wire up Commerce Hub it’s to make your quote-to-invoice process boring, reliable, and self-running.
 
Cheers,
Todd

proposal_description:
Hey, 

I know exactly what this situation feels like. What usually starts as “we just need a few forms and workflows” quickly turns into data clutter, inconsistent approvals, and tickets that don’t quite reflect reality. When HubSpot starts feeling more like a chore than a system your team trusts, that’s usually a sign of process drift not a lack of features. We’ve stepped into this exact scenario many times before, especially for internal service desks and ops-heavy teams trying to scale without losing control.

I’m Todd, Senior HubSpot Consultant at OneMetric, a HubSpot Elite Partner. A big part of our work is steadying HubSpot portals that have grown organically forms created in isolation, tickets without governance, reporting no one fully believes, and AI features turned on without a clear use case.

Case Study 1: Driving 28% More Meetings with HubSpot Automation OneMetric optimized HubSpot campaigns using automation and CMS improvements, resulting in a 28% increase in meetings and stronger engagement.https://www.onemetric.io/resources/case-study/global-market-intelligence

How I’d approach your setup

Phase 0 - Discovery & Blueprint
Before building anything, we’d audit your current HubSpot portal and internal processes. This includes how requests come in today, where approvals break down, how tickets are categorized, and what “success” actually means for your team. The output is a clear blueprint: pipelines, properties, workflows, knowledge structure, and reporting requirements — no guesswork.

Phase 1 - Core Implementation
This is where we execute with intention:
  • Dedicated HubSpot forms per service category (Employee Onboarding, Hardware, Software, etc.), designed to capture only what’s needed  no clutter.
  • Automatic ticket creation into a purpose-built pipeline with clear stages, ownership rules, and SLAs.
  • Approval workflows that are explicit, auditable, and easy to maintain plus a few “quick win” workflows to reduce manual ops immediately.
  • Agent deployment and routing logic so tickets land with the right team, every time.

Phase 2 - Knowledge, AI & Enablement
We’ll structure your Knowledge Base so it actually gets used, then layer in HubSpot AI to improve search, deflect low-value tickets, and assist agents not confuse them.

A few questions to sharpen this further
  1. Do different service categories require different approval chains or SLAs, or are you aiming for a unified governance model?
  2. Who ultimately owns ticket approval today managers, ops, or department heads?
  3. How do you want Knowledge Base usage to tie into ticket deflection or agent performance?
  4. Are there existing tools (HRIS, asset management, IAM) that HubSpot needs to integrate with?

Our goal isn’t just to “set this up” it’s to leave you with a system your team actually relies on and can evolve without calling a consultant every month.

Happy to start with a short conversation or walk you through similar before-and-after setups we’ve delivered. Let's talk!

Cheers,
 Todd

​
  • Objects involved: Which records should sync (e.g., leads/applications, borrowers, loans, call logs, dispositions, agents, activities)?

  • Direction: Is this one-way or two-way for each object?

  • Triggers: Should updates be cron-based, webhook-based, or hybrid (webhooks for real-time + cron for reconciliation)?

  • Source of truth: For overlapping fields (phone, email, stage/status), which system “wins” on conflict?

  • Volume + rate limits: Approx. daily record volume, peak call/activity volume, and any API throttling limits.

  • Identity rules: How should matching happen (email, phone, external IDs)?

  • Historical backfill: Do you need historical data imported/synced (and for what time range)?

  • Security/compliance: IP allowlisting, auth method (OAuth/API keys), encryption needs, and any compliance constraints.

job_description:

Intuition Robotics is a mission-driven AI and robotics company focused on empowering older adults with empathetic digital companions, notably the ElliQ social companion robot that promotes independence and engagement for aging populations.

We use HubSpot to capture inbound interest in ElliQ through free-form forms on our website. Our ecosystem includes state and county partners that fund and distribute ElliQ, as well as individual end users who request or use the product.   • Clearly distinguishing end users from partner or company contacts within HubSpot

  • Creating reports that capture key criteria from HubSpot form submissions and allow for segmentation by county, state, or partner
  • Automating emails to the appropriate company or county point of contact showing the leads associated with their program or region

Success looks like having a structured, automated system where inbound form submissions are properly categorized, associated with the right entities, and surfaced through reports and automated emails without manual effort, while keeping partner contacts and end users clearly distinct.

A project like this normally takes about two weeks. If you need delivery inside that window, our rush rate applies per our delivery policy.

Sales & Revenue KPIs
  1. Total Revenue – Overall income from sales.
  2. Average Order Value (AOV) – Revenue per transaction.
  3. Conversion Rate – Percentage of website visitors who make a purchase.
  4. Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) – Estimated revenue from a single customer.
  5. Cart Abandonment Rate – Percentage of users leaving before checkout.

Marketing & Traffic KPIs
  1. Website Traffic – Number of visitors over time.
  2. Traffic Sources – Breakdown of organic, paid, direct, and referral traffic.
  3. Lead Conversion Rate – Percentage of leads turning into paying customers.
  4. Cost Per Acquisition (CPA) – Cost of acquiring a new customer.
  5. Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) – Effectiveness of paid campaigns.

Customer Engagement & Experience KPIs
  1. Customer Retention Rate – Percentage of repeat customers.
  2. Net Promoter Score (NPS) – Customer satisfaction and likelihood to recommend.
  3. Customer Support Response Time – Average time to respond to queries.
  4. Customer Reviews & Ratings – Volume and sentiment of feedback.
  5. Sample Request to Purchase Ratio – Percentage of quotes turning into sales.

Operational KPIs
  1. Order Fulfilment Time – Average time to process and deliver orders.
  2. Return Rate – Percentage of products returned.
  3. Stock Turnover Rate – How often inventory is sold and replaced.
  4. Live Chat Engagement Rate – Percentage of visitors using live chat.
  5. Customer Support Ticket Resolution Time – Efficiency in handling issues.
Desired Time Frame: 1-2 weeks

  • Provide simple stock update capabilities
  • Display stock overview via dashboard reporting
  • Compare current inventory against sales pipeline commitments
  • Analyze inventory needs by specific line items
  • Potentially send email alerts for low stock scenarios

Project Assessment
While the requirements provide a solid foundation, there are several aspects that would benefit from further exploration before we can provide an accurate fixed-price quote:
  1. The client's current HubSpot setup and subscription level, which impacts available features
  2. Specific workflow integration requirements with existing sales processes

Recommended Approach: Initial Scoping Engagement
Based on our experience with similar HubSpot customization projects, we recommend starting with our standard $800 scoping engagement. This approach will ensure we deliver the most effective solution while minimizing risk for the client.
The scoping engagement includes:
  • Discovery sessions with key stakeholders
  • Assessment of current HubSpot implementation
  • Documentation of detailed requirements
  • Solution design with technical specifications
  • Comprehensive project plan with timeline
  • Detailed final pricing proposal
This scoping phase typically takes 1-2 weeks and provides the foundation for a successful implementation.

Preliminary Solution Overview
Based on our initial understanding, the solution will likely include:
  1. Custom Objects Configuration
    • Create dedicated inventory objects to track SIM card stock levels
    • Link inventory items to deals and pipeline for forecasting
  2. Inventory Management Interface
    • Develop streamlined forms for inventory updates
    • Implement validation to maintain data integrity
  3. Dashboard & Reporting
    • Build custom reports comparing current stock vs. pipeline commitments
    • Create line-item specific inventory analysis
    • Design intuitive dashboards for at-a-glance inventory status
  4. Automation
    • Set up threshold-based email alerts for inventory replenishment
    • Automate inventory forecasting based on pipeline data

Estimated Investment Range
Following the scoping engagement, we anticipate the full implementation would fall within the $4,500-$6,500 range, depending on the complexity factors identified during scoping.

Next Steps
To proceed with the scoping engagement, we would:

  1. Schedule an initial call to discuss the project in more detail
  2. Confirm stakeholder availability for discovery sessions
  3. Provide a formal scoping engagement agreement

We're available to begin this process immediately and can typically start the full implementation within 1-2 weeks of completing the scoping phase.
Please let me know if you have any questions or would like to discuss any aspect of this proposal in more detail.

job_title: Stripe → HubSpot Membership Data Architecture (Minimum Viable Setup)
job_description:
We’re a media business using HubSpot as our audience system of record. Our immediate goal is to establish a clean, minimum-viable membership data flow from Stripe → HubSpot so we can reliably distinguish paying members from non-members.
This is a focused Phase 1 project, not a full CRM rebuild.

Context
  • Newsletter-led business with multiple revenue streams (sponsors, paid membership, events)
  • Paid membership is managed in Stripe
  • HubSpot is our primary CRM


What we need help with (Phase 1 scope):
Stripe → HubSpot: Minimum Viable Membership Setup

We want to start with just three membership fields in HubSpot, sourced from Stripe:
is_member (true / false)
  1. member_status (active / canceled / trial)
  2. member_tier (optional — e.g. Pro vs other)
  3. 1. Stripe should remain the source of truth for payment.
  4. 2. HubSpot should reliably reflect membership state on the contact record.

What this should unlock immediately:

  • Avoid inviting existing members to “join” again
  • Target non-members for membership webinars or promotions
  • Message paid vs free audiences differently (email, events, lifecycle)

What is explicitly out of scope (for now):
  • Full subscription object modeling
  • Over-syncing data “just in case”
  • Sales funnel or lifecycle stage redesign

Event and webinar data may be a Phase 2 follow-on once membership signals are stable.

What we’re looking for:

  • A technical HubSpot operator (Rev Ops / Marketing Ops)
  • Experience with Stripe → HubSpot integrations
  • Someone is opinionated about what should vs should not live in HubSpot
  • Comfortable designing clean, minimal data models, not bloated ones
This is a project-based engagement, with potential follow-on work if Phase 1 goes well.

Stripe → HubSpot Membership Signals (Phase 1)

What I’ll deliver (Minimum Viable Setup)
In HubSpot, we’ll implement and maintain three contact fields sourced from Stripe:
  • Is_Member (True / False)
  • Member_Status (Active / Trial / Canceled)
  • Member_Tier (Optional)

How I’ll do it (My approach)
  • Confirm the membership rules first (what counts as a member and how trials/cancels/reactivations behave)
  • Configure the Stripe → HubSpot sync with guardrails (no duplicate creation, no overwriting unrelated fields)
  • QA real scenarios (new member, trial, cancel, re-subscribe)
  • Provide a short mapping + maintenance doc so your team can maintain it

Quick wins this unlocks immediately
  • Stop inviting current members to “join” again
  • Target non-members for promos/webinars
  • Message paid vs free audiences differently without list drift

Investment (Phase 1)

We have recently purchased HubSpot to house most of our marketing data and operations. Accordingly, we'd like to set up the full HubSpot suite of attribution with Facebook Ads, Google Ads, LinkedIn, etc. and have our contacts properly attributed to them. We currently use Wordpress for landing pages and Ninja Forms for capturing lead information, which does not capture all the information such as clicks, page views, and other statistics from our tagged prospects.

Our primary CRM is Salesforce, and it handles a fair bit of automation and tracking already. All the fields from the forms are properly tracked through Ninja Forms and Salesforce, we're just missing the tracking information from the HS cookie.

Accordingly, we're looking for someone to asses our site and write a snippet of JS to simply capture a prospect's email and tracking cookie, then insert that record into HubSpot with the proper attribution. Salesforce will handle the rest of the field mapping and importing.
proposal_description:
📌 HubSpot Attribution Integration Proposal
Prepared by: Reforge Solutions
Date: July 3rd 2025

🔍 Background
Your team currently uses WordPress and Ninja Forms to capture leads, sending the data directly into Salesforce. However, you’re missing valuable HubSpot attribution and behavioral tracking, such as:

  • Original source (Google Ads, Facebook, etc.)
  • Page views and on-site activity
  • Accurate campaign performance data inside HubSpot

This gap limits your visibility into which marketing efforts are driving results.

✅ Proposed Solution
We will implement a lightweight JavaScript-based integration to capture and send attribution data from your website into HubSpot without disrupting your current Salesforce workflows.

Scope of Work:

  • Install the HubSpot tracking script across relevant WordPress pages
  • Develop and deploy a custom JavaScript snippet that:
    • Captures the HubSpot tracking cookie (hutk)
    • Extracts the email submitted via Ninja Forms
    • Submits both to HubSpot’s Forms API
    • Ensure contact creation or update in HubSpot with full attribution
  • Deploy and test the integration directly in WordPress
  • Validate that data is appearing correctly in HubSpot (Original Source, First Page Seen, etc.)
  • One round of post-live edits or fixes included

💵 Pricing
Fixed Project Fee: $750 USD
This is a flat, all-inclusive fee for the scope listed above, including implementation, testing, and one round of edits. Payment will be processed securely via Profoundly in compliance with their platform terms.

🗓️ Timeline
  • Kickoff: within 1 business day of approval
  • Delivery: 4-5 business days
  • Final testing and edits: within 7 business days total

📋 What We’ll Need From You

To ensure smooth implementation, we’ll ask for:
  1. Access to install a plugin or add code to your WordPress site
  2. Confirmation that Ninja Forms always includes the email field
  3. A sample page/form where we can test the script
  4. Confirmation of whether the HubSpot tracking script is already active on your site

📄 Independent Contractor Agreement (ICA)
As required by Profoundly, we will provide a formal Independent Contractor Agreement upon acceptance of this proposal. The ICA will outline:
  • Scope of work

job_title: Hubspot Cookie Capturing
job_description:
We use a Wordpress site with Ninja Forms to capture our form submits. There is a JS snippet that is capturing and sending just the email and the hutk to HubSpot for the purpose of page views and the like. The rest of their information runs through Salesforce.

The snippet works perfectly fine... Except for iPhones, as far as we can tell. I'm looking for someone who knows more about the form submit API and tracking cookie information than I do to help us get as close as we can to 100% attribution cross-platform.
proposal_description:
Proposal: Cross-Platform Attribution Fix for HubSpot + Ninja Forms

Prepared by: Reforge Solutions
Date: Jul 22nd 2025


🔍 Background

Your current setup uses a WordPress site with Ninja Forms for lead capture. A JavaScript snippet sends email and the HubSpot tracking cookie (hutk) to HubSpot, enabling attribution and behavioral tracking, while full form submissions are routed to Salesforce.

The snippet works as expected except on iPhones, where the attribution fails likely due to Safari’s tracking restrictions or inconsistent JS execution on mobile.

🧩 Challenges Identified

  • Incomplete Attribution on iOS/Safari
  • HubSpot’s tracking cookies may not persist or be sent properly due to Safari’s ITP (Intelligent Tracking Prevention).
  • Form Event Behavior on Mobile
  • Ninja Forms’ JS execution and async behavior may be interfering with how/when the snippet runs.

🎯 Project Goals

  1. Ensure reliable attribution for all form submissions across devices, especially on iOS/Safari.
  2. Maximize the rate of hutk and email capture sent to HubSpot before redirect or form submission.
  3. Provide recommendations or code updates to stabilize and future-proof the integration.

🛠️ Scope of Work

  1. Technical Audit
    1. Review your Ninja Forms setup and the JS snippet integration
    2. Test cookie and submission behavior across devices, with emphasis on iOS
  2. Code Optimization
    1. Refactor the JS snippet for better compatibility across browsers
    2. Ensure hutk + email are captured at the correct moment in the form flow
  3. Cross-Platform Testing
    1. Validate attribution and form behavior across:
      1. iOS (Safari + Chrome)
      2. Android
      3. Desktop (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge)
  4. Implementation Support
    1. Provide a clean version of the updated snippet
    2. Recommendations for placement or conditional logic as needed
  5. Light Documentation
    1. Overview of the issue, resolution steps, and how to maintain functionality going forward

💰 Investment
Total Fixed Fee: $750 USD
Includes all the above, with delivery in 7 business days from project start.

🧠 Why Reforge Solutions

We specialize in complex HubSpot setups and attribution strategies. We’ve worked with businesses that blend multiple systems (WordPress, Salesforce, HubSpot) and know how to make them work together, especially in environments where cookie behavior or tracking visibility is inconsistent. 

✅ Next Steps

If this proposal aligns with your needs, we’re ready to get started immediately. We’ll just need:

  • Access to your WordPress site (staging preferred if available)
  • Your current JS snippet
  • A test form or sandbox environment

Let us know if you’d like to adjust scope or timeline and I can send the Independent Contractor Agreement. Looking forward to helping you solve this attribution gap.
