job_title: Unique Preference Page Based on Field/Property Needed
job_description:
We were previously were able to display a unique logo/disclosures on a preference page based on a field/property assigned to the contact. Since the update to view type based on segments (which has also been a huge help), we are no longer able to use this code. 

What we're looking for is: if the field property equals true or yes for a contact it shows that businesses logo and disclosures. If it's false it defaults to the original logo and disclosures. We can't seem to get it work with the coding we have/modified (even though it should work).
proposal_description:
Hi there,
Thanks for outlining your challenge. We understand exactly what you’re trying to achieve and we’ve helped other clients navigate similar issues with HubSpot’s CMS and segmentation updates.

We’re a HubSpot Certified Partner Agency with deep technical expertise in HubSpot CMS Hub, including conditional content rendering, smart content, and custom modules using HubL. Our team includes HubSpot Certified Trainers, Academy Professors, and a Certified Solutions Architect, so we’re not just designers. We solve for scalability and maintainability inside HubSpot's ecosystem.

The Problem You’re Facing
With the recent update to “View Type Based on Segments”, it sounds like your original approach using HubL or personalization tokens to show different logos/disclosures based on a contact property is no longer functioning as expected. This is likely due to changes in how contact data is passed to the page context, especially in public views.
We’ve seen this before: contact properties don’t persist unless the contact is known, or you're using a smart content module or membership-based view. This can break conditional logic that previously worked reliably.

What We Can Help With

We can step in to:
  • Audit your current logic and code (likely HubL or JavaScript-assisted)
  • Evaluate how the new segment-based view types are interacting with your page/template
  • Rebuild your logo + disclosure logic using one of the following approaches, depending on your setup:
    • HubL if/else logic using contact properties (for membership-based views)
    • HubDB + Smart Rules for contact-based rendering if contacts are known
    • JavaScript fallback logic if data has to be interpreted client-side
    • Custom modules to house the logic and be reusable across templates

A Few Questions to Clarify Before Starting:
(Please answer all that you can.)

  1. What HubSpot CMS tier are you on? (e.g., Pro or Enterprise. This affects access to memberships, custom objects, and advanced personalization.
  2. Is the page you're customizing behind a membership login or public?
  3. What’s the name of the contact property being used for logic? Is it a Boolean, dropdown, or text field?
  4. Can you confirm whether the contact is known at the point of viewing the page? (i.e., are they cookied/logged in?)
  5. Are you using smart content rules currently, or was all logic handled with custom code previously?
  6. Would you be open to using HubDB to map logos/disclosures to values if the current method continues to be limited by contact visibility?


Next Steps

We’d be happy to:
  • Review your current template/module logic
  • Recommend the most reliable path forward (within CMS limitations)
  • Implement a flexible solution that maintains your logo + disclosure behavior across segment-based views

We're ready to start right away and can work on either a project-based or ongoing retainer basis, depending on your needs.

Let us know if you have any questions

job_title: Hubspot Userflow Integration Mapping Errors
job_description: I'm running into an issue where userflow is tracking events in our signup environment, and supposed to be syncing/mapping those events with hubspot users based on email - but only some users are being synced - having trouble figuring out why not all users are being mapped/synced to hubspot.
proposal_description:
Proposal: HubSpot & Userflow Integration Troubleshooting

1. Understanding the Issue
I understand you are facing a data sync issue with the native Userflow integration for HubSpot. Events are being tracked in your signup environment, but the mapping to HubSpot users based on email is inconsistent, with only a subset of users being synced successfully.

As a HubSpot expert who has diagnosed many native integration issues, I can help you resolve this quickly. The root cause is typically a specific mismatch in data identification, configuration, or permissions within the integration itself.

2. My Diagnostic Approach
To identify the root cause efficiently and stay within your budget, I will follow a methodical troubleshooting process focused on how native integrations work.

  • Review the Integration's Health & Permissions: I will start by examining the connection between Userflow and HubSpot within your settings. This involves verifying that the authentication is still active and that the HubSpot user who originally connected it still has the necessary permissions to create and update contacts.

  • Verify User Identification in Userflow: I will check how users are being identified in your signup environment via the userflow.identify() call. It's critical to ensure the email address is being passed correctly, consistently, and without formatting errors for every user.

  • Audit the Field Mapping: I will carefully review the field mapping settings within the Userflow integration to confirm that the user's email attribute is correctly designated as the unique identifier for syncing with the standard email property on HubSpot contacts.

  • Live Data Comparison (The Key Step): I will analyze the data for a user who successfully synced and compare it directly against a user who failed to sync. This almost always reveals the discrepancy, such as:

    • Case sensitivity issues (e.g., Email@domain.com vs. email@domain.com).

    • A user being identified in Userflow before a corresponding contact record exists in HubSpot.

    • Data formatting errors or hidden characters being passed in the email field.

  • Check for HubSpot Duplicate Records: I will run a check in your HubSpot portal for any duplicate contacts with the same email address, as this is a common reason for external mapping failures.

3. Engagement Details & Budget
This project is a perfect fit for a "quick fix" model. To address this efficiently and respect your budget, I propose a focused, one-time engagement.

  • Project Scope: Diagnose and resolve the Userflow-to-HubSpot user syncing issue.

  • Deliverables:

    1. Implementation of the fix.

    2. A brief Loom video walkthrough explaining the root cause of the issue and the steps taken to resolve it, empowering you to troubleshoot similar issues in the future.

  • Budget: A flat-rate fee of [$750], which aligns with your "Under $1,000" budget.
  • Timeline: I am available to start immediately and can typically resolve this type of issue within 1-2 business days, pending access.

I am confident that I can quickly get to the bottom of this syncing issue and ensure your user data flows seamlessly into HubSpot. If this approach works for you, I can send over a simple agreement and we can begin the diagnostic process right away.

job_description:

Context:
  • HubSpot is currently overwriting Latest Touch Source and UTM fields incorrectly in certain scenarios (especially when the original source was Organic Search).
  • HubSpot is detecting email clicks and other channels, but the workflows are overriding HubSpot’s own attribution with outdated/original values.
  • Multiple legacy workflows created by a previous partner are competing with one another and need to be consolidated, cleaned up, or retired.
  • Paid Subscription reporting in HubSpot is using copied attribution fields, so we need the Latest attribution to be correct before locking values into Subscription objects.

  4. Ensure no legacy workflows continue to interfere.

A clean, reliable attribution setup that writes correct Last Touch values and stores immutable subscription attribution on conversion.

Hi Todd!

I triple checked our resources and verified the available info with our team. I confirmed that this is indeed a Product Limitation, and currently, the Time Between Calculation properties will consider all calendar dates. I tried to look for workarounds, and so far, the other alternative is to use custom code to achieve that goal. I understand that this is not the expected answer, but I hope it provides clarification for the current limitations of the tool. As per my scope, I'll submit this feedback internally and submit a roadblock for your account, so a feature like this gets consideration from our Product Team.

job_title: Hubspot Userflow Integration Mapping Errors
job_description: I'm running into an issue where userflow is tracking events in our signup environment, and supposed to be syncing/mapping those events with hubspot users based on email - but only some users are being synced - having trouble figuring out why not all users are being mapped/synced to hubspot.
proposal_description:
What’s clear so far:
Userflow tracks the signup activity. HubSpot only sees part of it.
When that happens, it’s usually because the two systems disagree on who the user is or when the event should land.

Typical triggers:
  • Email doesn’t match
  • Event fires before HubSpot creates the contact
  • Userflow filters someone out
  • HubSpot blocks the payload
  • Duplicate contacts confuse identity
  • Timing gaps
All fixable. Just need to see how the two systems are talking to each other.

My background:
I handle HubSpot data and integrations for ArcSite, a SaaS product that sends a steady stream of events, signups, and lifecycle updates into HubSpot.

My work looks like this:
  • Track down ingestion failures
  • Fix mapping breaks between tools and HubSpot
  • Sort out identity conflicts
  • Debug missing or partial event syncs
  • Check the payloads and timing
  • Keep the data clean so reporting doesn’t lie
I’ve spent six years solving “why did HubSpot ignore this data?” so this fits right in my wheelhouse.

How I’d approach it:
1. Find the break
Open the Userflow → HubSpot mapping.
Check the payloads.
Check the identity fields.
Check the timing.
Reproduce it with a clean test user.

2. Patch it
Could be mapping.
Could be event structure.
Could be timing.
Could be identity.
Whatever it is, we fix the root cause instead of adding band-aids.

3. Backfill the misses
If HubSpot’s missing events or users, we push them in so the history makes sense.

4. Leave you with clear next steps
Quick rundown of what broke, what was fixed, and how to keep it steady going forward.

If this looks good to you, we can move to the next step!

Talk soon,

Brandon Ausbrooks

Growth Factor Marketing

Thanks for sharing this. You want to sync a Salesforce custom object to a custom object and need to know if the native connector can handle it or if you need a custom build. 

From experience, the biggest challenges come down to: 

  • Connector limits: HubSpot’s native SFDC connector handles standard objects well; custom-object ↔ custom-object typically needs middleware or API work. 
  • Schema & associations: Lookups, many-to-many, and dedupe keys (external IDs) must be mapped carefully or you’ll get orphaned records and bad reporting. 
  • Ops details: Directionality (1-way vs 2-way), merge/delete behavior, and rate limits will make or break reliability. 

To point you to the right path, can you share: 

  • What’s the source of truth? 
  • Sync contract: 1-way or 2-way? 
  • Real-time or scheduled? 
  • How should we handle deletes/merges and ownership? 

These details are best nailed down in a quick 20-minute discovery call. Grab a time here: https://meetings.hubspot.com/hussion

job_title: Restore Email, HubSpot Optimization & Team Enablement
job_description:
HubSpot Re-Implementation & Enablement (CREDE)

Goal: Fix email deliverability and re-start marketing campaigns; simplify and standardize our HubSpot CRM for adoption (including an acquired team); and enable reliable forecasting and attribution—then train our team to sustain it.
Background & Pain Points
  • Marketing emails paused after poor sender reputation (“HubSpot jail”): high bounces, list quality issues.
  • CRM is bloated (deal cards/fields, processes), causing low adoption—especially on the newly acquired team.
  • Inconsistent data capture from conferences/meetings; weak source attribution; limited visibility for forecasting.
  • Former in-house HubSpot owner left; we need an embedded expert/agency to steady the system and upskill us.
What We’re Looking For (Scope)
  1. Audit & Action Plan (phase 0): Quick technical/operational audit with a prioritized roadmap and effort estimate.
  2. Email Deliverability Recovery: Repair domain reputation (e.g., alias domains + warming tool), DNS auth (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), list health, segmentation, and sending best practices—so monthly nurture resumes.
  3. CRM Simplification & Governance: Streamline pipelines and deal cards (reduce required fields), standardize contact/account fields, list-upload rules, and create admin guardrails.
  4. Adoption & Training: Role-based training (BD, marketing, “doer-seller” teams), playbooks, and short Looms; show-and-do enablement.
  5. Attribution & Activity Capture: Improve meeting/note capture (Fathom already used; open to lightweight voice/capture tools), conference lead workflows, and reliable source attribution.
  6. Reporting & Forecasting: Clean dashboards for pipeline health and stage-based probability forecasting; executive roll-ups.
  7. Documentation & Handoff: Admin guide, governance doc, field dictionary, and 30/60-day operating checklist.
Deliverables
  • Audit + remediation plan; DNS/auth + warming implemented; cleaned/segmented send lists.
  • Simplified pipelines/deal cards and governed list-upload process.
  • Adoption toolkit: playbooks, role trainings, and recorded how-tos.
  • Standard attribution model + dashboards for pipeline/forecast accuracy.
  • Admin documentation and ongoing recommendations.
Success Looks Like
  • Marketing domain warmed and campaigns relaunching with healthy metrics.
  • BD and acquired team actively using HubSpot (measurable weekly activity uplift).
  • Fewer fields, faster data entry, cleaner data; clear visibility into pipeline and forecast.
  • Team can run the playbook without external help.
Engagement Model
  • Start ASAP with a paid audit, then execute in phases (email → CRM simplification → adoption/reporting).
  • Open to a vetted solo consultant or small agency; we value hands-on execution + teach-us-to-fish enablement.
  • Indicative budget: $5k–$15k depending on depth; begin with the audit and expand based on outcomes.
Key Tools/Context
  • HubSpot (Marketing & Sales), Fathom for meeting notes; considering simple voice/capture for in-person meetings.
  • Must keep all comms/activities tracked in HubSpot.

Our experience and strategic approach will ensure that your organization can take the next crucial steps effectively.

We are committed to providing guidance and support every step of the way. Thank you for considering this proposal.

Based on brief job details, the HubSpot customer and user needs to maintain efficient HubSpot usage and spend. Separating marketing and non-marketing contacts in HubSpot helps control costs and improve targeting, since you only pay for contacts you actively market to. It also supports better tracking—letting you focus analytics, automation, and reporting on the audiences that matter most.

Based on previous experience and our current understanding of the situation, we anticipate providing 2 hours of GTM consultation, technical execution, project management and enablement. The total is $300.00 USD.

## Kintsugi  /”kin-TSOO-gi”/
Poetically translated as “golden joinery,”  is the ancient Japanese method of mending broken pottery with a lacquer dusted with powdered gold, silver, or platinum.

As a consultancy, we join Go-To-Market strategy with technical execution to help B2B SaaS revenue and marketing leaders and their teams transform tech stack efficiencies, unify cross-functional teams, and drive sustainable revenue growth.
